H.M. penal settmt., Massaruni, 2nd June, 1891; clk., Bartica pol. mag. court, 27th Oct., 1891; issuer, penal settmt., Massaruni, 30th May, 1892; clk., ditto, 18th Aug., 1892; clk. and issuer, Georgetown prison, 12th June, 1894.

BLACK, GEORGE GOW.—B.1851; ed. High Schl., Glasgow; Univ. Coll. Schl., London; and Glasgow Univ.; clk., audit dept., W. Australia, 1892; audit examr., 1894; ch. clk., P. W. Dept., 1896; ag. under-sec., P.W., Jan. to Dec., 1902; is a J.P.

BLACKMORE, EDWIN GORDON, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1837; ed. at King Edward VI. Gram. Schl., Bath; served with the Taranaki Rifle Volrs. in the New Zealand war, 1863-64, and was present, in reserve, at the action of Poutoko, 2nd Oct., 1863, and at the storming and capture of the rebel Maori strongholds at Ahunahau and Kaitake, Mar., 1864 (medal); parly. librnr. to the legis. of S. Austral., Oct., 1864; clk. asst. and serj.-at-arms, house of assem., Dec., 1869; clk. of the house of assem., May, 1886; clk. leg. coun. and clk. of parltys., May, 1887; clk. of Austral. convention which met in Adelaide and Sydney, 1897, Melbourne, 1898, to agree to a bill for the federation of Australia; clk. of the constitutional comttee. to draft the bill; reed. thanks of conven.; clk. of sen. and clk. of parliaments, Austra. Commonwealth., 1901; several years examr. and lecturer in hist. for Univ. of Adelaide; organising sec. of movement to raise a Bushmen’s corps for service in S. Africa, and mem. of sub-comttee. to fully equip the corps; author of “The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Denison and Mr. Speaker Brand on Points of Order, Rules of Debate, and the Gen. Practice of the House of Commons, 1857-84”; “The Decisions of Mr. Speaker Peel, 1884-95”; “Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the House of Assembly of S. Australia”, “Manual of the Practice, Procedure, and Usage of the Legis. Coun. of S. Australia”; and the “Law of the Constitution of S. Australia.”

BLACKWELL, LIONEL NORTON.—2nd lieut. 4th Batt. King’s Shropshire L.I., Nov., 1888; lieut., Sept., 1889; transferred to Thames Mil. Div., R.E., Mar., 1894; instructor of musketry, Apr., 1895; asst. inspr., Lagos Housa Force, Nov., 1895; ag. dist. comsrr., Oct., 1896; captain, July, 1898; received reward (50l.) after examination in the Yoruba language, 1898; Lagos representative—delimitation of the bounaries; 1; cal. Lagos-Northern Nigeria, July to August, appointed District Commissioner of the and Acting Railway Commissioner, Augus. Bar (In. Temp.), 1897; dep. judge advoc., S. Africa, Mar., 1900, to June, 1901; sec. to gov., O.R.C., 1901 to Mar., 1903; ag. col. sec., O.R.C., May to Sept., 1902; asst. ditto, 1903; ag. ditto, Apr. to Aug., 1903, Dec., 1903, to Sept., 1904, and Mar., 1905, to Jan., 1906; col. sec., Barbados, 29th Oct., 1907.

BLADEN, JAMES BUXTON.—B. 1866; apptd. elk., store and timekeeper, harbour wks., Antigua, Jan., 1896; ag. 2nd elk., P.O., Oct. to Dec., 1896; ag. 1st elk., 1897; acctnt. of savings bank, Jan. to Oct., 1898; cashier of ditto, Oct., 1898 to Mar., 1902; ag. 1st indoor treasy. offr., Aug., 1901; ag. 2nd indoor ditto, on sev. occasions, 1899, 1900 and 1902; ag. 3rd indoor ditto (or cashier), 1901 and 1902; ag. 3rd outdoor ditto, and excise offr., in 1899 to 1902; jun. elk., audit dept., May, 1902; apptd. 2nd treasy. offr., and quarantine offr., Montserrat, Apl., 1903; sec. of quarantine bd., 1903-6; govt. analyst for testing petroleum, 1903-4; elk. to comnrs. of valuation, 1905-6; 1st treasy. elk., Apr., 1907; ag. asst. treas., July-Aug., 1907.
BLAINE, SIR CHARLES FREDERICK, KNT. BACH. (1889).—Deleg. for Cape Colony to S. African Customs Union Conf., 1882.

BLAINE, HERBERT FRANCIS, K.C.—Ed. at Harrow and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; called to Bar (In. Temp.), 1881; admitted advoc., sup. ct. of Cape, 1881; M.A., 1889; twice ag. sol.-gen. East dists. ct.; att.-gen., O.R.C., May, 1901; mem. I.C.C., 1903; ag. atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1905, and 1906-7; mem. exec. and legis. couns.

BLAIR, ALEXANDER HAMILTON.—B. 1872; ed. at Oxford Mil. Coll.; 2nd lieut., 4th batt., Norfolk rgt., 1892; lieut., 1893; resigned, 1894; re-apptd. lieut., 4th batt., Norfolk rgt., 1898; capt., 1898; Hythe certif., 1898; Shoeburyness, 1900; asst. inspr., Lagos Hausa force, 1899; on active serv., Ashanti, 1900, relief of Kumasi (medal and clasp, ment. in desps.); spec. serv., E. Coast of Africa, 1900-1; dist. comnrs., Lagos, 1904; spec. serv., Jebu Ode, 1904.
BLAIR, HON. JAS. WM.—B. 1871; called to the bar, Queensland, 1894; M.L.A. for Ipswich, Queensland, since Mar., 1902; atty.-gen. since Sept., 1903; and atty.-gen. and sec. for mines since Apr., 1904.

BLAKE, THE HON. EDWARD, K.C., M.P.—B. 1833; called to the bar of Upper Canada, 1850; created Q.C., 1864; entered Canadian parl. 1867, and has also sat in Ontario assem.; was prime min. of Ontario from 20th Dec., 1871, until 25th Oct., 1872, when he resig.; sworn of the priv. cot. 7th Nov., 1873, but did not hold office; re-entered the cabinet, 19th May, 1875, as min. of justice, a position he exchanged in Sept., 1877, for that of pres. of the coun.; ret. from the cabinet, 31st Jan., 1878; declined the chancellorship of Ontario, Dec., 1862, also the ch. justiceship of the sup. ct. of the Dominion, May, 1875; is chancellor of the Univ. of Toronto, and Pres. of the Law Soc. of Upper Canada; leader of Liberal party in Canada, 1880-7; has sat in the Parl. of U.K. since 1892; mem. of S. Afr. Comtce., 1897.

BLAKE, SIR ERNEST EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1845; apptd. to a clerkship in the C.O., 4th July, 1863, after a compet. exam.; 1st jun. class, 20th Nov., 1869; 2nd class elk., 30th Sept., 1872; asst. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 1st Oct., 1872; priv. sec., 27th Jan., 1874; 1st class elk. and head of the gen. dep., 1st May, 1879; crown agt. for the cols., 1st Jan., 1881.

BLAKE, SIR HENRY ARTHUR, G.C.M.G. (1887), K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1887), F.R.G.S. —B. 1840; cadet, Roy. Ir. Constab., Feb., 1861; sub-inspr., Mar., 1859; apptd. res. mag., Polar 1876; in Jan., 1882, was one of the five special mags. selected by Govt. to concert and carry out measures for the pacification of a large portion of Ireland; apptd. gov. of the Bahamas, Jan., 1884; Newfoundland, 1887; Queensland, Nov., 1888, but did not take up apptmt.; gov., Jamaica, Dec., 1888; gov., H. Kong, 1898; gov., Ceylon, 1903; ret. July, 1907.
BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—Ck., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apl., 1898; ag. registr.-gen., May, 1906, to Mar., 1907; 1st cls. elk., regitr.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1907.

BLAKELY, JAMES FRANCIS.—B. 1877; 3rd elk., col. sec.'s off., B. Honduras, Feb., 1894; ag. 2nd elk. Apl., 1895; elk. to survr.-gen., Nov., 1896; asst. keeper, King's warehouse, May, 1897; elk. to col. engrnr., Jan., 1899; ag. 2nd grade cust. and ex. offr., Mar., 1904; 1st cls. elk., treasy. and cust., Feb., 1906; ag. ch. elk., treasy. and cust. house, Jan., 1907.

BLAKENEY, MAJOR JOHN EDWARD CHARLES, 4th Batt. Essex Regt.—B. 1886; ed. at St. Peter's Coll., Camb.; asst. inspr. S. Leone frontier pol., 1895; inspr., 1897; attached to Anglo-French bndry. comn., 1896; ag. dist. comnrs., Panjuma dist., S. Leone, Aug., 1897, to Jan., 1899; commanded expedit. against Lofus and Bandis, 1898; recd. thanks of gov't. and S. of S.; served throughout S. Leone rebell. of 1898-99; commg. at Panjuma during siege of that post; served in operations at Liberian frontier, Dec., 1898; West African med. and clap., 1898-9; ag. dist. comnrs., Falaba dist., 1899; adjt., frontier force, 1899; ag. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1900; held this apptmt. until re-organisation of West African forces, when apptd. maj. commg. S. Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., June, 1901; 2nd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Apr., 1904.

BLANCHARD, VICTOR PHILIPPE.—B. 1868; elk., govt. office, Roseau, Dominica, 1889; elk. to valuation comnrs. and comnrs. of assessed taxes, Feb., 1895; ag. ch. outdoor offr., treasy. and quarantine offr., July, 1895, to 1896; ag. sec. quarantine bd., 1896; treasy. offr., 1897; col. postmtr., 1899.
BLANCHET, J., K.C.—Provl. sec. and registr. Quebec; puisne judge, King's Bench div., sup. ct., Quebec, 1891.

BLAND, ROBERT NORMAN.—B. 1859; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Cheltn. Coll.; B.A. Trim. Coll., Dub.; Cadet, S. Settlns., Oct., 1882; passed in Malay, May, 1884; collr. and mag. Sri Menanti, June, 1886; dist. offr. southern dist., P.W., 1888; collr. of land rev., Penang, 1889; ditto, Singapore, 1890; offr. in charge, Sunjie Ujong, Dec., 1894; ditto, Negri Sembilan, Jan. to Apr., 1895; ag. offr. assignee of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Stltlns., Mar., 1897; sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1897; inspr. of prisons, July, 1897; ag. res. coun., Malacca, April to Sept., 1900, Nov., 1901, to June, 1903, and Oct., 1903; treas., S.S., July, 1904, but continued to act as res. coun., Malacca; confirmed, Jan., 1905; res. coun., Penang, 17th Feb., 1907.

BLAYNEY, J. ALBERT, L.R.C.P., Edin.; L.R.C.S., Edin.; L.F.P.S., Glas.—Govt. med. offr. Br. New Guinea; res. mag. central dist., 1893; mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1896; ag. registr.-gen., and collr. of customs, Oct., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ch. med. offr., Oct., 1899.
BLENKIRON, JAMES ERNEST.—B. 1870; 3rd acctnt., B. Cent. Africa Prot., Dec., 1893; 1st asst. treas., 1901; ag. treas., Apl. to Dec., 1902, Nov., 1904, to June, 1905, and Mar. to Oct., 1907.

GLINSETT, HENRY FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1874) B.I.B., 1847; clk., Roy. Ordnance Factories at Portsluith, Jan., 1867; asst. comr., African commiss. clk., Aug., 1869; priv. sec., 1870-1, to the admnr. of the G. Coast, and clk. of coun., 1872; served throughout the Ashanti Exped., of 1873-4, as control offr. with Capt. Glover's force; mentioned in despatches, and medal; acted on several occasions as auditor-gen. of the W. A. Stttms. from 1875 to 1881; comsir. to examine the acts. of the Falkland Is., 1882; sent on special service to the G. Coast as comsir. of acts., 1884.
BLOUNT, THE REV. CHAS. K., M.A.—Assistant minister, Falkland Is., 1901.

BLOW, HORATIO JOHN HOOVER.—B. 1855; arrived, N. Zealand, 1872; entered govt. service (P.W. dept.), 1873; record clk., 1874; ag. asst. under sec., Jan., 1883; asst. under sec., Oct., 1885; ag. under sec., June, 1891; under sec., Oct., 1891.

BLUNDELL, FREDERICK MOSS.—B. 1869; atty., notary, and conveyancer of the Transvaal; priv. sec. to mil. gov., J'burg., during S. African war; dist. regis. of mining rights, J'burg., 14th Feb., 1901, to 2nd Aug., 1903; asst. sec., mines dept., Transvaal, 3rd Aug., 1903; ag. sec., ditto, 14th Nov., 1904, to 15th May, 1905; asst. sec., ditto, 14th May, 1905.
BOAK, THE HON. SIR ROBERT, KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1822; Pres. of legis. coun., Nova Scotia; resigned, 1904.

BOAST, HENRY W.—Ck. and Zulu interp., Greytown, Natal, July, 1876; clk. to res. mag., Ladysmith, May, 1878; clk. and Zulu interp., Estcourt, Dec., 1880; ditto, Ixopo, 1886; regist., native high ct., Apr., 1887; admnr. of native law, Tugela Valley, Jan., 1890; mag., Krantzkop, 1894; mag., Polela div., Oct., 1899; mag., New Hanover, 1905.
BODEKER, HENRY ALBERT, M.B.C.M., Glasgow.—Ed. at Glasgow Univ. and in Germany; med. offr., Uganda Prot., 1st Sept., 1899; med. offr., East Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1902.
BOLTON, JOHN GEORGE Elliott, M.R.C.S.—Eng. Govt. med. offr. and comsir. in lunacy, Mauritius, 21st Jan., 1870; sanitary warden, 15th Nov., 1875; ag. med. inspr. in 1900 and 1902.

BOLTON, WM. FERRALL.—Appd. to crown agent's dept. 20th Nov., 1900; on spec. serv. under F.O. as ag. treas. of Somaliland, 22nd Nov., 1902, to 19th July, 1903; African gen. serv. medal and clasp for operations against Somali Mullah, 1902-4; asst. treas. East Africa Prot., 11th Jan., 1904; asst. govt. emigr. agt. at Calcutta for Trinidad, Jamaica, Mauritius, and Fiji, 21st Sept., 1904; passed exam. in Hindustani, June, 1905; ag. emigr. agt., 28th Mar., 1906, to 30th June, 1907; ag. emigr. agt. for B. Guiana and Natal, 1st July, 1907.
BOLUS, PERCY REGINALD, M.B. (Lond.).—Asst. col. surgeon, Falkland Is., 1904.

BOND, THE HON. SIR ROBERT, P.C. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1901), Hon., L.L.D., 1902.—B. 1857; Speaker, Newfoundland house of assm., 1884; mem. of exec. coun. and col. sec., 1889 to 1894; official deleg. to London on fisheries question, 1890, and to Washington on reciprocity question in the same year; again col. sec., 1895 to 1897; deleg. to Ottawa in connection with the confederation negotiations, 1895, and subsequently negotiated a loan for the col. in London in that year; prime min. and col. sec., 1900; specially invited by H.M.'s govt. to attend confce. in relation to French treaties question, 1900; represented Newfoundland at col. confce., 1902; negotiated Hay-Bond treaty with U.S.A., 1902; represented Newfoundland at Impl. confce., 1907.

BONHAM-CARTER, ARTHUR THOMAS.—Ed. at Winchester Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1890; called to the bar, 1894; Inner Tem. and West Circ.; S. African war, Queen's medal and four clasps; asst. res. mag., Transvaal, 1902; mag., E. Africa Prot., 9th Apr., 1905; judge of the High Ct., E. Africa Prot., 29th July, 1906.

BONSER, RT. HON. SIR J. W., P.C. (1901), KT. BACH. (1894), M.A.—B. 1847; formerly fellow of Christ's Coll., Camb., sen. classic in 1870; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 18th Nov., 1872; att.-gen., S. Stttlts., 1883; ch. justice, 1893; ch. justice, Ceylon, 1893; mem. jud. comtce., priv. coun., 1901.
BOON, GEOFFREY PEARL.—Ck. and inspr. of wks., P.W.D., St. Kitts, Aug., 1907.

BOOTH, LEONARD, WM.—B. 1856; writer, Ceylon service, 1878; pol. mag., Avisawella, 1880; office asst. to govt. agt., Uva, 1886; forest settltn. offr., 1891; agt. govt. agt., Manaar, 1891; pol. mag., Kandy, 1895; resumed duties as forest settltn. offr., Dec., 1895; agt. asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Mar. to Oct., 1896; special offr. under Ordinance No.1 of 1897; govt. agt. Sabaragamuna, June, 1899; govt. agt. N.C. Province, Feb., 1900; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Nov., 1905; govt. agt., Uva, Dec., 1905; prin. collr. of customs, May, 1906; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., June, 1907.

BOOTH, ROBT. MALCOLM.—Cadet, Fiji, Nov., 1883; attached to col. sec. office, Feb., 1884; ditto stip. mag. office, Tai Levu, July, 1884; at Navua, Nov., 1884; att.-gen.'s office, June, 1885, to Oct., 1888; passed cadet, 1887; stip. mag., Tai Levu, 1888; ditto, Loma Viti, 1893; ditto, Savu Savu and Bua, 1893; confirmed as stip. mag. and comm. of sup. ct., 1894; called to the bar Mid. Term., 1899; stip. mag., sub-coll. customs and tax inspr., Lautoka, 1901; comsir., Naitasiri Prov. and S.W. Rewa, Apr., 1906; M.L.C., Apr., 1906.

BOOTHSBY, JOSEPH, C.M.G. (1878).—B. 1854; clk. col. sec.'s office, S. Australia, 1853; clk. in audit office, 1854; ch. clk., 1856; ch. clk. in ch. sec.'s office, 1859; also govt. statist and supt. of census, 1860; asst. sec. and govt. statist, S. Australia, 1866; and under-sec. and govt. statist, 1868 to 1880; elected corrpdg. mem. of the statistical soc., Lond., 1869; trustee, savings bk., S. Australia, 1869; a comsir. for international exhibts., 1872; joint edit. of a work "S. Australia; its History, Resources, and Productions," pub. by authority of govt., 1876; exec. comsir. representing S. Australia at the Paris univl. exhib. of 1878; elected hon. mem. of roy. geogl. socs. of Berlin and Vienna, 1878.

BORDEN, THE HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1902), B.A., M.D.—B. 1847; grad. of Harvard Med. Schl.; has represented King's in the House of Commons since 1874, with the exception of one term, being defeated at the gen. elections of 1882; min. of Militia and Defence in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; attended Impl. confce., 1907.

BORN, EDWARD TURNER.—B. 1872; M.B., Dunelm; honours, 1st, 2nd and 3rd M.B.; prelim. sci., and regist. exams.; asst. col. surgeon, St. Lu., 1901; ditto, Falklands, 1903; col. surgeon, of hd. of health, pub. vaccinator, and... Falklands, 1904; mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1904.

BOSHOFF, HENRI GUILLAUME, J.P. (Natal).—Advoc. of the sup. ct., atty. and notary public; 2nd puisne judge, native high ct., Natal, 15th June, 1899; acted as 1st puisne judge, 18th Nov., 1900, to 30th Sept., 1901; from 1st Dec., 1903, to 18th May, 1904; and from 1st to 30th June, 1904; and as judge pres., from 19th to 31st May, 1904; 1st puisne judge, 1st July, 1905; ag. judge pres., 1906.

BOSMAN, JOHANNES JACOBUS.—Examiner of diagrams, surv.-gen.'s office, Cape, Aug., 1892; examnr. of candidates for practical land surveying; geodetic offr., Jan., 1903; cir. of secondary triangulation of Cape Colony, Dec., 1904.

BOSTON, REV. NATHANIEL HENRY.—Ed. C.M.S. Gram. Schl., Freetown, and Fourah Bay Coll., S. Leone; Durham Univ., L.H., 1878; B.A., 1890; deacon, 1880; priest, 1881; sec. of S. Leone C.M.S., 1880-1887; missionary and supt., Bullom mission, 1880-1898; delegate of S. Leone native church to England, 1885; J.P., S. Leone, 1890; editor of "Missionary Friend Magazine," 1876-1881; supt. of Laccé church and dist., 1898-1899; minister of St. Paul's church, Tasso Is., Jan.-Apr., 1900; priest in charge, St. Henry's parish, York, S. Leone, Apl. to Sept., 1900; ast. col. chaplain and regis. of deaths, Cape Coast, 1st Sept., 1900; ag. col. chaplain 23rd Sept., 1900, to 7th Apr., 1901; also from 10th May, 1902.

BOTHER, RIGHT HON. GEN. LOUIS, P.C. (1907), LL.D. Edin.—B. 1863; mem. of 1st Volksraad of the S. African Republic, representing Vryheid; acted as veldt-cornet for Vryheid at commencement of S. African war, 1899; was comdr.-in-chief of the Boer forces during part of the campaign; one of the signatories to the terms of peace signed at Vereeniging, 1902; visited England to confer with Mr. Chamberlain, 1902; premier of the Transvaal, 1907; represented the Transvaal at Impl. confce., 1907.

BOTTOMLEY, W. C.—B. 1878; ed. Owens Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., entrance maj. schlr., 1896; schlr., 1898; B.A. and 3rd wrangler, 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. clkr., 22nd Oct., 1901; sec. to col. surv. comte., 1905; sec. to inter-deptl. pensions comte., 1907.

BOUCAUT, THE HON. SIR JAMES PENN, K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1831; entered parlmt., S. Australia, 1862. Held office in several ministries as atty.-gen., Oct., 1865, to Mar., 1866; and thence to May, 1867; as atty.-gen. and premier, and again as atty.-gen. in 1872; as premier and comsnr. of crown lands and pub. wks., from June, 1875, to June, 1876; as premier and treas., from 26th Oct., 1877, to 25th Sept., 1878, when he accepted a seat on the bench of the sup. ct.; ret. from bench, 1905.

BOUCHER, CYRIL HANSLEWOOD.—Headqrs. offr., armed nat. constab., B.N. Guinea, Apr., 1905; ast. res. mag., central div., Jan., 1906; mag. for native affairs, Jan., 1906; J.P., Jan., 1906; visiting justice, Port Moresby gaol, Mar., 1906.

BOULTON, J. F., Assoc. M.I.C.E. (1884).—Exec. engineer, p.w. dept., H. Kong, Apr., 1889; ag. ast. dir. p.w., July, 1902, to May, 1903, Apr., 1904, to Mar., 1905, July to Sept., 1905, and Mar. to Nov., 1907.

BOURKE, SOUTHWELL G. J.—Ent. Royal Navy as cadet, 1864; served in "Aurora," Canada, during Fenian raid (medal); in "Satellite" during Abyssinian campaign (medal); in "Orontes" during Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal, Khedive's star); called to bar (Mid. Tem.), 1887; stip. mag., Br. Guiana, 1897; interpr. in French (1st class, Navy); ag. pol. mag., Georgetown, Oct., 1900.

BOURNE, HUGH CLARENCE, C.M.G. (1906).—M.A. (Balliol Coll., Oxford).—B. 1858; ast. sec. charity organization soc., 1881-84; called to the bar (Inner Tem.), 1884; regt.-gen. and examnr. of titles, Trinidad, 1893; acted as judge of sup. ct., 1896 and 1898; audr.-gen. Trinidad and Tobago, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1899, 1901, and 1902; admstd. govt., 10th to 13th Nov., 1901; ag. 1st class clkr., C.O., Oct., 1902; col. sec., Jamaica, June, 1904; admstd. govt., Sept., 1904, and May, 1907.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLENEY, KT. BACH (1902).—B. 1854; Gilchrist scholar (London Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with 2nd class honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Linc. Inn, Nov., 1876; solr.-gen. and escheator-gen. of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of comm. to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1886; ag. legal asst., C.O., Oct., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902.

BOVELL, JOHN REDMAN, F.L.S., F.C.S.—B. 1855; parochial treas. and collr. of rates, parish of St. John, Barbados, Apr., 1882; supt. reformatory and industrial schl., Apr., 1883; mem. loc. comte., Col. and Ind. exhib., 1885, and other local exhib., comtes.; supt. Botan. Stn., 1886; went to Antigua, 1890, to start Skerton training schl.; vice-pres., Windward dist. agric. soc., 1890; mem. of comm. to inquire into sugar cane borers, Jan., 1893; J.P., Mar., 1893; chairman emigr. comte., Apr., 1895; seconded for supt. of reformatory to act as agric. supt. for sugar cane experiments, Barbados, Oct., 1898; went to Antigua and St. Kitts to start sugar cane experiments, Jan., 1899; one of the Barbados representatives at the various agric. confce., held by Impl. comsnr. of agric.; sent to St. Vincent in June, 1907, to report on damage done in Carib country by volcanic eruptions of 1902-3.
BOVILL, ALFRED KARLSKAKE.—Clk. of wks. pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1882-5; ast. to dir. of survey and principal forest officer, 1886-1886; inspr. of agric. industries, 1892-96; prin. forest offr., 1895.

BOWELL, THE HON. SIR MACKENZIE, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1823; emigrated from Suffolk to Canada in 1833; well known in Canada as a journalist; has sat in Canadian House of Commons since 1867; sworn of the privy coun., 19th Oct., 1878, and apptd. min. of customs; min. of militia and defence, 1892; min. of trade and commerce, Dec., 1892; on special mission to Australia in connection with the development of inter-col. trade, 1883; pres. of col. confce. at Ottawa, 1894; prem. of Canada and vice-president of council, 1894-96.

BOWEN, ALFRED SYDNEY.—B. 1860; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, and Newton Coll., S. Devon; 3rd clkr., crown lands off., Trinidad, 18th Oct., 1878; 2nd clkr., crown lands office, July, 1879; 4th clkr., rec. gen.'s office, July, 1881; branch savings bk. clkr., Jan., 1883; ch. clkr., surg-en.'s office, Jan., 1889; ch. clkr., G.P.O., May, 1889; warden St. Ann's and Diego Martin, Dec., 1889; J.P., 1890; ag. priv. sec. to Sir F. Broome, Sept., 1896; supt. of pastures, Jan., 1901; ag. town comsnr., 1902; inspr. of prisons, 1903.

BOWEN, ERNEST F. S., F.S.I., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; clk. audit office from Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1889; clk. post office from May, 1889, to Sept., 1890; ch. clkr. and draughtsman, pub. wks. office from Oct., 1890. to Jan., 1895; supt. pub. wks., Barbados, Feb., 1895.

BOWER, Sir Graham John, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1848; ret. comdr., R.N.; entered navy, 1861, and served on Mediterranean, E. coast of Africa, E. Indian, Home, and Australian stations, until 1880, when he was apptd. priv. sec. to Sir Hercules Robinson, gov. Cape of Good Hope, and high commr. for S. Africa; imperial sec. to the high commr., 1884-97; colonial sec., Mauritius, 1898; admstd. govt., July, 1900, to 1901, and Nov., 1903, to Aug., 1904.

BOWES, Frederick, B.A. (Oxon).—Exhibtnr., Wadham Coll., Oxford; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., 9th Oct., 1891; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, 1891; additional pol. mag., Badulla, 1892; ag. pol. mag., Haldummulla, 1892; ag. office astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., 1893; ditto, N.C. Prov., 1894; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakacheri, 1895; office astt. to govt. agt., Uva, 1895; ag. ditto, Cent. Prov., and ag. fiscal, Cent. Prov., 1897; ag. supt. of pol., Colomba, 1898; officer of class IV., 1899; landing survr., customs, Colomba, 1899; officer of class III., 1901; astt. govt. agt., Matara, 1901; pol. mag., Kandy, 1902; ditto, Colomba, and adit. dist. judge, Colomba, 1903; pol. mag., Kandy, 1903; astt. govt. agt., Puttalam, 1st May, 1904; ditto, Matale, Feb., 1907.
BOWIE, Robt.—P.C. certif., 1892; M.A., Edin., 1893; Normal Coll., Cape Town, 1896-1900; vice-principal, Normal Schl., Bloemfontein, 1st Jan., 1901; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., 1st July, 1904.
BOWLEY, Francis Bulmer Lyon.—Solv. of sup. ct., England, 1890; solv. of sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1893; notary public, 1895; ag. Crown solv., May 1899; Crown solv. and Queen’s Proctor, 1900.

BOWRING, Charles Calvert.—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; col. audit branch of exchequer and audit dept., 1890; Hong Kong, 12th Dec., 1892; loc. aud., B.C. Africa, 7th Sept., 1895; loc. aud., East Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., 5th June, 1899; treas., East Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1901; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., East Africa Prot., 1907.

BOWRING, Walter Andrew.—Ed. at Eton; clk., col. audit branch, 1894; served in Cyprus, 1896; astt. auditor, East Africa Prot., 13th Feb., 1899; ag. local auditor, Uganda Prot., 1899; local auditor, ditto, 31st May, 1902.

BOXWELL, W.—B.A., Trin. Coll., Dublin, 1872; ensign, 20th ret. of foot, 1872; lieut., 51st L.I., 1873-78; adjt., Bord. horse, S. Africa, during Sekukuni campaign (medal with clasp), 1879-80; Natal mtd. pol., 1881-86; sub-inspr. Basutoland mtd. pol., 1886-1901; astt. commr., Basutoland, 1901.

BOYCE, Ernest Peller.—Supernumerary clk., G.P.O., Barbados, June, 1878; notarial clk. col. sec.’s office, Apr., 1879; sec. to commrs. to inquire into the working of the G.P.O., 1879; ch. clk., pub. library, 1880; ag. librn., Sept., 1882; astt. clk., petty debt ct. of St. Michael’s, Nov., 1882; ag. ch. clk., May, 1883, to June, 1884; inland rev. offr., 1887; clk., astt. ct. of appeal, 1901.

BOYD, A. D.—Teacher, preparatory dept., Dominica Grammar Sch., Sept., 1894; junr. govt. off. treas., Jan., 1895; clk. govt. off., Portsmouth dist., Jan., 1896; govt. off. treas., Roseau, Sept., 1896; quar. off., Oct., 1897.
BOYD, A. H.—B. 1865; entered C.O. after exam., Apr., 1887; astt. clk., June, 1892; supplementary clk. in registry, Mar., 1900.

BOYLE, Alexander George.—B. 1872; ed. at Charterhouse, Clifton Coll. and R.I.E.C., Cooper’s Hill; priv. sec. to senior puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., 1893; resig., 1895; astt. treas., Uganda Prot., 18th Sept., 1895; ag. sec. to H.M. Commr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apr., 1898; ag. treas., Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., 1st Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admstr., May, 1901, to Jan., 1902; apptd. collr. with seniority of 18th Sep., 1895, 1st Apr., 1902; astt. sec., 1st Apr., 1902; ag. sub-commr. E. Prov., 1st Nov., 1903; sub-commr., 1st Jan., 1905; ag. dep. commr., 15th Apr. to 28th Oct., 1907.

BOYLE, Sir Cavendish, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1849; ed. at Charterhouse; dist. mag., Leeward Is., Dec., 1879; col. sec. of Bermuda, and registr.-gen., 1882; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1888; govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1894; admstd. govt. at various times, 1894-1900; was also chairman of the Br. Guiana bk., Apr., 1897, to Nov., 1898; govrn. of Newfoundland, Jan., 1901; govrn. Mauritius, 15th Apr., 1904.
BOYSE, V. H.—B. 1883; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902.

BRABANT, Major-General Sir Edward Yewd, K.C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1839; Mem. of the legis. assem. for E. London, Cape of Good Hope; retired from Cape mounted rifles, as captain, 1870; field comdt. of col. forces, 1878; col., Cape yeomanry, 1879; in command of local forces in the S. African war, 1899-1901; ret. 1904; M.L.A. for East London, 1904.

BRACE, George Knight Kirkwood.—Dispenser of medicines and gen. asst. N. P. asylum, Bahamas, 1885 to 1890; clk., regis. of records off., 1890 to 1896; 3rd clk., recvr.-genl.’s and treasr.’s dept., 31st Dec., 1896; ag. regis. of records, 26th Aug. to 10th Nov., 1901; ag. 2nd clk. and book-keeper, recvr.-genl.’s and treasr.’s dept., Nov., 1903, to March, 1904, and from 5th May, 1904.

BRACE, Thomas Evan Duggan.—B. 1863; dispenser N. P. Asylum, Bahamas, Sept., 1882; clk. pol. office, Aug., 1883; 2nd clk. col. sec.’s office, Sept., 1887; clk. to bd. of pub. wks., New Providence, Mar., 1900; ch. clk., col. sec.’s dept., Mar., 1904; ag. col. sec., Sept.-Dec., 1905, and Sept.-Oct., 1906.
BRADDELL, Thomas de M. L.—Barrister-at-law; puisne judge, S. Settlmts. and jud. commr., F.M.S., July, 1907.
BRADFORD, M. G.—Asst., P.W.D., Sarawak, May, 1895; supt., July, 1901; ag. commr., May, 1902.

BRADLEY, John Thos., L.R.C.P.E., L.R.C.S.E., L.F.P. and S.G.—B. 1870; ed. at Queen’s Coll., Belfast, and Lond. Schl. Trop. Med. (1901); undergrad. in arts, Royal Univ., Ireland; prizeman in med., midwifery, and diseases of women and children, Queen’s Coll., Belfast, 1897-8; gold medallist in practical midwifery, Clifton st. Mater. Hosp.; 1st prizeman in med. and surg. diseases of children, Children’s Hosp., Queen st., Belfast; late astt. med. offr., Glamorgan Co. Asylum, 1901; astt. med. offr., Seychelles, 26th Aug., 1901; J.P. and chmn. of loc. bd. of health, 1st and 2nd div., South Mahé; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Seychelles, 1st Mar., 1906.

BRADNEY, George Preston.—Ed. at Oratory Schl., Edgbaston, and Trinity Hall, Camb.; B.A., Cantab.; honours in history, 1899; col. audt. off., June, 1901; astt. aud., B.C. Africa, 8th Feb., 1902; astt. auditor, East Africa Prot., 26th Sept., 1904.

BRADY, Alfred Barton, M.I.C.E.—Pupr. and afterward ch. draughtsman, ch. engrnr.’s dep. L. and Y., rly., Manchester, Jan., 1872; astt. G. W. Stevenson, M.I.C.E., 1879; survr. Docking Union and Hunstanton urban sanitary authority, Mar., 1881; engnr. and survr., Maldon sanitary dist., Essex, Oct., 1882; ast. engnr., rly. dept., Brisbane, Qnsld., Jan., 1885; ast. engnr. of bridges (rlys.), July, 1887; engnr. for bridges (main roads), P.W.D., June, 1889; govt. architect and engnr. for bridges combined, Sept., 1891; under-secretary for public works, govt. architect and engnr. for bridges combined, Feb., 1901.
BRADY, JOHN BANKS.—Head mast., Grey Coll. Schl., 1st Dec., 1901; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., 1st Feb., 1904.

BRAITHWAITE, H. W.—Petroleum locker and asst. excise locker, San Fernando, Trinidad, 1873; ch. clk. sub-treasury, 1874; supt. regis. of births, 1878; J.P.; elected mun. coun., 1888; now ch. clk. med. dept.; sec. quarantine, and sec. bd. of health.

BRAMSTON, SIR JOHN, G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1897), D.C.L., C.B. (1886).—B. 1832; graduated B.A. at Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1854; elected fellow of All Souls, 1855; D.C.L., 1863; barrister, Mid. Temp., 1857; went to Queensland, 1859, as priv. sec. to Sir George Bowen; resig., 1861; was mem. of the legis. coun. of Queensland, 1863 to 1869, and of the exec. coun. 1863 to 1866. While in England in 1867, was ast. boundary commr. for Devon and Cornwall under the Reform Act of that year. Returned to Queensland, 1868, and became asty. gen., 3rd May, 1870; resig., 31st Dec., 1873; represented the Burnett district in the legis. assem. of Queensland, Apr., 1871, to Dec., 1873; apptd. asty. gen. of Hong Kong, 1873; was ex officio mem. of the legis. and exec. couns.; acted as judge of the sup. ct. of Hong Kong, Feb. to May, 1874; apptd. an ast. under-seer. of state, C.O., 30th June, 1876; employed on a mission to Berlin in connection with the Angra Pequena negotiations, July 1886; regis. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, Feb., 1892; ret. 14th Nov., 1897; sent out as roy. consnr., in conjunction with Admiral Sir James Erskine, K.C.B., to inquire into matters relating to French treaty rights in Newfoundland, 1898; mem. roy. consnr. for Paris Exhibn. of 1900.

BRANCH, CHRISTIAN WM.—B. 1870; ed. at St. Kitt's high schl. and Antigua gram. schl., Edin. Univ., M.B., C.M., 1893; Leeward Is. med. offr. in charge, Virgin Is., 1894; M.L.C., Virgin Is., 1894; ag. consnr., V.I., 1894; med. offr. dist. 4, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1895; mem. bd. of health, St. Kitts, 1895; ag. supt. Cunn. Hosp., ag. surg., gaol, and ag. analyst, vital statistics, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1896; ag. med. offr., dists. 7 and 8, 1897; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis volrs., 1897; ag. ast. col. surg., St. Lucia, 1899; pub. phys., Inagua, Bahamas, 1900; ag. res. just., Inagua, 1901; med. offr., Kingstown dist., med. offr., col. hosp., and supt., pauper asylum, St. Vincent, 1902; also ag. med. offr., dist. I., surg., gaol, and health offr., 1902; J.P., 1902; ag. mem., legis. and exec. couns., 1903; mem. bd. of educn., 1904; del. to W. J. Quarantine Conf., 1904.

BRANCH, EDMUND RALPH.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin.; dist. med. offr., Dominica, 1902; ditto, Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1902; med. supt., Cunningham hosp., and surgeon of the gaol, St. Kitts, Feb., 1903; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, 1904; capt., 1905.

BRANCH, E. ST. J., B.A., B.C.L., Durham Univ.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1892; ag. chief mag., St. Vincent, 1895 to Jan., 1898; asst. to asty. gen., Leeward Islds., May, 1898; ag. atty.-gen., July to Nov., 1899; mem. legis. coun. and exec. coun., Dominica; mem. general legis. coun., Leeward Is., Dec., 1899; asty.-gen., Leeward Is., 12 Sept., 1902; ag. exec. coun., 10 Oct., 1902.

BRANCH, E. W. R., M.B., C.M. (Edin.), B.A. (Durham).—Med. offr., Virgin Is., Oct., 1889; acted as consnr., Virgin Is.; J.P. and M.L.C., Virgin Is.; med. offr., Montserrat, 1890; M.L.C., Montserrat; med. offr., St. Kitts, 1894; dist. med. offr., Antigua, Jan., 1900.
BRANDT, FRANCIS ROBERT, M.R.C.V.S.—Ed. at St. Peters, York; govt. vet. surg., C. of G. H., 1897; vet. inspr., bd. of agric., England, 1903; ast. vet. offr., East Africa Prot., 2nd Apr., 1904.

BRASSEY, LORD, 1st baron (created 1886), SIR THOMAS BRASSEY, G.C.B., K.C.B., D.C.L (Hon.) and M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1830; called to the bar Linc. Inn, 1866; M.P. for Devonport, 1865, and for Hastings, 1868 to 1886; lieut. comdg. roy. naval volrs., 1873; hon. comdr., 1880; col. 2nd Cinque Ports art. volrs., 1891; dep. lieut. and J.P. for Sussex; was civil lord of the admty., 1880 to 1884, and sec. to the admty., 1884-5; pres. of the statistical soc., 1879-80; comdr. of the legion of honour, 1889; chairman of the roy. comsn. on opium traffic, 1894-5; lord-in-waiting, 1895-9; gov. of Victoria, 1895-1900.

BRATT, JAMES HICKS DAVSON.—B. 1866; 5th cl. clk., audit dept., British Guiana, 1889; 4th, 1892; 3rd, 1895; 2nd, 1897; sec. to comte of inquiry into travelling expenses, 1893; sec. to comte on pensions, 1898; ast. loc. auditor, N. Nigeria, Feb., 1901; loc. auditor, Sept., 1901; ch. ast. treas., Jan., 1903; ag. treas., Feb., 1905; Aug., 1904, Feb., 1906, and Aug., 1907.
BRAYN, RICHARD FYSHER, B.A., Cantab.—Cltk., col. and branch, E. and A. dept., 5th June, 1905; ast. aud., Hong Kong, 14th April, 1906; ag. loc. and, 13th Aug. to 9th Nov., 1906.

BREARLEY, L. J.—B. 1882; passed Lond. matric. and Oxford sen. local exam., June, 1886; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to ld. chamberlain's office, Feb., 1900; transferred to C.O., 18th Feb., 1900.

BREMNER, BRUCE LIVING.—Ed. at Craigmount house schl., Edin., and premium apprentice, L. & N.W. rly. shops, Crewe, 1880 to 1884; L. & N.W. rly. loco. dept., 1894 to 1897; ast. loco. supt., Uganda rly., 1898.
BRETT, F. W.—B. 1864; Ent. service of United African Co. (afterwards Roy. Niger Co.), Mar., 1882; priv. sec. to Sir G. Goldie, 1883 to 1890; transferred to C.O. as supplementary clk., Jan., 1900.

BREW, SAM. HENRY.—B. 1865; ed. Wesleyan high schl., Cape Coast; clerical ast., govt. office, G. Coast, Apr., 1887; clk. to bd. of educn., 1st Oct., 1890 to 1st Jan., 1898; jun. clk., Jan., 1891; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; 2nd clk., 1st Jan., 1894; 1st clk., 24th Dec., 1899; ast. ch. clk., col. exec. office, 1st Jan., 1905.

BREWIN, ARTHUR WINBOLT.—Ed. at Winchester; cadet, Hong Kong, 1888; passed cadet, Dec., 1890; ag. asts. registr.-gen., May, 1891; Mar., 1894, and again 1895 to 1897; J.P., 1894; insp. of schools, 1897; registr.-gen., 1901; M.L.C., 1903.

BREWSTER, EDWARD JOHN.—Ed. at Brewster and Chelt.; ast. dist. offr., Matang, Perak, 1878; ast. supt., Lower Perak, 1878; ast. mag., Kristal, 1879; ditto, Salama, 1880; ag. dist. offr., Krian, 1883-4; ditto, Larut, 1885; ditto, Knita, 1888; dist. offr., Krian, 1889; mag., Krian, 1889; stat. comsr., of lands, and regist. of mines, Jan., 1892. ag. state auditor, Apr., 1893; dist. mag., Lower Perak, Aug., 1893; ditto, Kinta, Sept., 1904.

BRIDGER, JAMES FREDERICK EDMUND, M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.)—Camb. certif., 1st cl. hons.; inter. M.B. (Lond.) 2nd cl. hons. in pharmacology; prize in clinical surgery and hons. in practical surgery and materia medica, St. Mary's Hosp., Lond.; late asst. demonstrator in physiology in St. Mary's Med. Schl.; civil surg. attached to 3rd batt. Northd. Fus., Antigua, 1902; med. offr. and supt. in charge of small pox statn., and med. adviser to gen. bd. of health, Barbados, during epidemic of 1902-3; commr. apptd. by govt. of Barbados to visit and report on system of quarantine practised by port sanitary authorities of New York, also to Trinidad to report on epidemic of eruptive fever in Port of Spain; received thanks of govr., hse. of assem., and gen. bd. of health for services rendered to Barbados; port health offr., Barbados, 1905; ex-officio mem. of quarantine bd.

BRIDGES, W. F.—Sub adminstr.-gen., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1885; ag. adminstr.-gen., Feb., 1886, to Jan., 1887; commr. to inquire into road arrears under rd. ordinance, May, 1889; and intoland titles, Oct., 1890; adminstr.-gen., Br. G., Apr., 1899.
BRIDGMAN, ALFRED H.—B. 1860; entered C.O. after exam., Oct., 1889; apptd. asst. clk., May, 1892; shorthand writer to C.O., sept., 1896-1900; asst. supt. of printing, Mar., 1900.

BRIGHT, CHARLES E., C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1829; a leading merchant in Victoria; has been chairman of the harbour trust and commr. for the Melbourne exhib.; has also been commr. for Victoria at exhibs. in Europe.
BRILL, JOHANNES.—Lit.D., Utrecht Univ.; rector of Grey Coll., O.R.C., 6th Mar., 1873.

BRINTON, ALFRED JOHN—Ck., col.sec.'s off., Cape Colony, May, 1884; 3rd cls. clk., July, 1885; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1892; prin. clk. statistical branch, July, 1897; ag. registr-gen. of statistics, July to Dec., 1904, and Apr. to June, 1905; registr-gen. of statistics, July, 1905.

BRISBANE, LORD ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. ST. CLAIR GEORGE ALFRED DONALDSON.—B. 1863; late scholar of Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (1st cls. trip.), 1885; 1st cls. theol. trip., 1887; M.A., 1889; hon. D.D., 1904; Wells Coll., 1887; deacon, 1888; priest, 1889, Cant.; consecrated Lord Bishop of Brisbane, 28th Oct., 1904; Archbishop, 1905.
BRISTOW, CHARLES EDWIN.—Govt. printer, S. Australia, Aug., 1890.

BRISTOWE, LEONARD SYER, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1857; called to the bar, In Temp., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1903; joint author of "Law of Charities and Mortmain"; author of "The Mortmain and Charitable Uses Act, 1891," and of "The Hospital Legal Handbook"; joint editor of 2nd edtn. of "MacSwiney on Mines."

BRISTOWE, LINDSAY W.—Ck. survr.-gen.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1880; in treas. and cust. dept., 1883-4; sec. crown lands bd., 1884; qualified land survr., clk. regsr.'s and record office, clk. of the petty debt ct., July, 1884; clk. of coun., 1885; regsr. V.A. court, 1886; provost-marshal, 1887; regsr. and keeper of records, 1887-1890; offl. admstr., 1890; dist. commr. Toledo dist., 1892; Belize dist., 1893; notary and commr. of the sup. ct.; editor Br. Honduras handbook, 1888-93; dist. commr. G. Coast, 1893; assigned to treas., Apl., 1894; ag. ch. asst. treas., Aug., 1894, to Jan., 1895; dist. commr., Accra, 1894; Elmina, 1895-96; chief regsr. and sheriff, 1897; dist. commr., Chaima, Sekondi, 1898; Salt Pond, 1899; Winnebah, 1900; 3rd asst. col. sec., Oct., 1901; 2nd ditto, 1902; 1st ditto, 1903; clk. ex. and leg. couns., Mar., 1904; ag. ch. asst., 9th Feb. to 3rd Mar., 15th July to 2nd Dec., 1904, 20th June to 12th Nov., 1905, 3rd to 31st Dec., 1905, and 1st Jan. to 30th June, 1906.

BRITTEN, HENRY.—Apptd. probationer, Dec., 1895; 3rd cl. clk., magistrate's office, Ixopo, June, 1896; clk. and Zulu interpreter and sub-distributor of stamps, Ixopo, Apr., 1897; ag. clk. of the court on several occasions; served through the Boer War, 1899-1900, with the Border mounted rifles, including the siege of Ladysmith.
BRITTEN, CHARLES ALFRED.—Master, Royal Coll., Mauritius, 15th Nov., 1876; sec. ditto, 17th Feb., 1893; sec., college comte., 12th May, 1896; inspr. and sec. to comtees, 19th Oct., 1903.

BROADRICK, EDWARD GEO.—B. 1864; ed. at Sherborne schl.; clk. lower divn. H.M.'s office of works, Nov., 1884; cadet S.S., 1887; dist. offr., Neboq Tebal, P.W., June, 1890; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1895; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Indian imigrts., May, 1896; in sole charge of dept., Nov., 1896; dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1897; ag. collr. land rev.; offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, May, 1897; ag. sen. dist. offr., P.W., Sept., 1898; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Dec., 1898, to Nov., 1900; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Dec., 1901; ag. inspr. of prisons, S.S., Feb., 1902; capt. Singapore volr. rifles, July, 1900; p.s. cert., schl. of instruction, Chelsea barracks, July, 1901; major and ag. commt. Singapore volr. corps, May, 1902; sen. dist. offr., Province Wellesley, Aug., 1902; mag. for, and visited Cocos Is., Nov., 1902; ag. 1st mag., Sing., May to Aug., 1903; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sept., 1903; ag. commt. Singapore vol. corps, 6th Nov., 1903; pres., mun. comms., Singapore, 11th June, 1904; commt., Singapore vol. corps, 1st Mar., 1905; lieut.-col., Singapore vol. corps, 30th Nov., 1906.

BROCKMAN, EDWARD LEWIS.—B. 1865; Cadet, S. Stlttns, 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; ag. supt. of educn., Penang, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; ag. col. of land rev., Penang, May, 1889; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1892; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1895; 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Aug., 1897; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Feb., 1899; commr., court of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1902; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Sep., 1903; ag. col. sec., Apl., 1905, to July, 1906; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr., 1907; ag. res. gen., May, 1907; confirmed as fed. sec., continuing to act also as res. gen., July, 1907.
BROCKMAN, FREDERICK SLADE.—Surv., lands and survey dept., W. Aust., 1884; surv., P.W. dept., 1887; surv., lands and survey dept., 1890; inspecting surv., ditto, 1891; chief ditto, ditto, 1894.

BRODEUR, HON. LOUIS PHILIPPE LL.D., Laval, K.C.—B. 1862; ed. at the coll. of St. Hyacinth, Laval Univ.; called to the bar, 1884; K.C., 1899; editor of Le Noir, 1896; elected to H. of C., Canada, as mem. for Rouville, 1891, 1896, 1900 and 1904; deputy-speaker, 1896-1900; speaker, 1900-1904; min. of in. rev., 1904-1906; min. of marine and fisheries, 1906; attended Im. Conf., 1907.
BRODHURST, HENRY WM., FRED. COTTHAM.—B. 1856; Ed. at Cheltenham Coll.; writer, Cey...
BRODIE, D. E.—Ed. at Winchester; entd. serv. of British South Africa Company, 1897; asst. sec., 1902; sec., Aug., 1906.
BRODIE, H. M.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur.; sworn ld. survr., Maur., Apr., 1900; asst. survr., Seychelles, May, 1901; town survr., 23rd Dec., 1901; land survr., Fiji, Feb., 1906.

BROOK, HERBERT ARTHUR, I.S.O. (1905).—B. 1855; ag. clk., pol. office, Nassau, N.P., Bahamas, May to Oct., 1874; librn., Nassau pub. liby., reading room and museum, Oct., 1875, to Oct., 1879; clk., pol. office, Oct., 1879; to June, 1883; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., May to Nov., 1881, and Oct. to Nov., 1882; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Mar. to Apr., 1883; ag. 3rd clk. treas., June, 1883; priv. sec. to admstr., Jan. to Feb., 1884; clk. in charge, col. sec.'s dept., Mar., 1885; ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept. and clk. bd. of pub. works, June, 1883, to Aug., 1887; regsr. of records, Sept., 1887 (still holding same); ag. stipy. and circ. mag., Aug., 1895, and Aug., 1897; ag. provost-marshal and ag. comdt., Oct. to Nov., 1898; ag. col. sec. and tempy. mem. ex. coun., Aug. to Sept., 1899; supt., Bahamas census, 1891 and 1901; is a mem. of leg. coun.; mem. of bd. of educn.; a J.P.; army dist. paymr., Bahamas; and edits the annual statutes of the Bahamas.
BROOKE, HIS HIGHNESS SIR CHARLES JOHNSON, G.C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1829; Rajah of Sarawak.

BROOKE, GILBERT E.—Ed. Pem. Coll., Cam., and Lond. Hosp.; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1901; L.R.C.S. and L.R.C.P. Edin., L.F.P.S. Glas., D.P.H., Scottish Conj.; Fell. Roy. Inst. Pub. Health; F.R.G.S.; late surg. Clan Line and Furness S.S. Co.; govt. med. offr. and med. offr. of health, Cockburn Harbour, Turks and Caicos Islds.; ag. G.M.O. and M.O.H., Turks Isld., Aug., 1899; J.P. for the col., June, 1899; ag. asst. comsnr., Cockburn Harbour, Dec., 1899; May, 1900; dist. comsnr., Caicos Is., June, 1901; port health offr., Singapore, Jan., 1902; ag. govt. analyst, Mar. to Oct., 1906; lecturer in hygiene and examnr. in chem. and physics to Straits and F.M.S. med. schl., Singapore.

BROOKS, G. L.—Joined Lond. and Metropol. pol. force, 1885; inspr. Sierra Leone, Oct., 1894; supt., sheriff and provost-marshal, July, 1896; ag. pol. mag., coroner and judge of ct. of requests for Freetown, July to Dec., 1897.

BROOME, WILLIAM.—Entered Natal civil service, 1875; clk. exec. coun. and sec. defence comte., 1878; ch. clk., atty.-gen.'s office, 1879; advoc., sup. ct., 1882; sec. coun. of educn., 1885; R.M., Newcastle div., 1889; acted several times as asst. col. sec., 1886-88; J.P. for the col., 1889; mem. of coun. of educn., 1891; master and regsr. sup. ct., 1889; offices of master and regsr. separated 1897, retained that of master; mag. Durban, 1898; judge of spec. treas. ct., 1900; resumed duty as mag., Durban divn., 1902; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1904; 3rd puisne judge, Dec., 1904.
BROWN, SIR CHARLES GAGE, M.D., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889), Hon. LL.D.—B. 1826; was for many years med. adviser to the Col. Office; retired 1897.
BROWN, H. Y. L.—Govt. geologist, S. Australia, Dec. 1882.
BROWN, JOHN FRANK.—Imperial service, 10th Sep., 1873; P.M.G., Natal, 14th Sep., 1900; lent to Transvaal govt., 1st Oct., 1902; P.M.G., Transvaal, 3rd April, 1903; M.L.C.

BROWN, JONATHAN RICHARD.—Prison offr., B. Guiana, 1st Feb., 1878; promoted 1st cls. and served at penal settm., Massaruni, 18th Nov., 1884, to 30th Dec., 1887; ag. keeper, Fellowship pris., West Coast, Demerara, 3rd May to 3rd Nov., 1890; principal warder, penal settm., Massaruni, 19th June, 1894, confirmed; ag. chief warder, Georgetown pris., 4th July to 8th Dec., 1895, 22nd Dec., 1895, to 30th Mar., 1896, and 3rd Nov., 1897, to 12th July, 1898; ag. keeper, New Amsterdam prison, Berbice, 9th to 21st Dec., 1895; ag. chief warder and asst. supt. at penal settm., Massaruni, 15th June to 14th Sept., 1897, and 12th Mar. to 16th June, 1900; keeper, Suddie pris., Essequibo, 1st Aug., 1900, to 31 Aug., 1903 (confirmed 1902); ch. warder, Georgetown pris., June, 1903, confirmed, Mar., 1904); ag. supt., Georgetown pris., 14th Nov., 1903, to 13th Apr., 1904, ditto, Dec., 1904, to Apr., 1905, and in 1907; ag. asst. supt. and ch. warder (combined), Massaruni penal settm., May to Aug., 1905.

BROWN, JOSEPH.—Served 14½ years in R.H.A.; passed course of instr., schl. of gunner, Shoeburyness, and manufacturing branches of Royal Arsenal, 1878-79, granted certifa.; embarked for India, 1880; passed in Hindustan; passed long course, Artil. Coll., Woolwich, 1891-92; spec. ment. in Royal Laboratory and Royal Gun Factory; awarded a certif.; sub-insp. of pol., Gambia, 1892; ag. supt. of pol. on several occasions; J.P. and comsnr. of ct. of requesta, Gambia, 1892; ag. sheriff, Mar. to Sept., 1893; ag. inspr. of pria., Mar. to Sept., 1893; mem. of quar. bd., May, 1893; mem. of bd. of health from July, 1893; supt. of pol., Oct., 1894; ag. col. engr., Apr. to Dec., 1894, and from July to Nov., 1896; inspr. of pris., Mar., 1895; inspr. of weights and meas., Dec., 1895; successfully commnd. punitive exped. in Fogni agst. two Jobal chiefs—Wawo and Jaja—in 1898 (ment. in desps. and thanked for services); commd. artillery, Gambia field force, 1901 (ment. in desps., medal and clasp); in charge of secretariat, Gambia, July to Dec., 1902, and July to Dec., 1905; sheriff, Jan., 1903; ag. treas., collr. of cust., postmr., and chrmn., navigation and pilotage bd. on various occasions; commnd. detachment W.A.F.F., Jan. to Apr., 1903; ag. curator of intestate estates, Apr. to July, 1903; mem. of navigation and pilotage bd., June, 1904; ag. ch. mag. on several occasions; mem. of bd. of management, "McCarthy Square," July, 1905; ag. col. sec., Gambia, Feb. to May, 1907; capt. and in commd. of Gambia artil. vol. force, June, 1907; market inspr., May, 1907; provanl. mem. of ex. and leg. couns. on many occasions.

BROWN, RICHARD MYLES.—Ed. Liverpool Coll. Instn., and Royal Coll., Mauritius (1st English scholarship); called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1869; counsel to govt., Seychelles, 1872 to 1877; practised before sup. ct., Mauritius, 1877 to 1884; mem. of comsn. on laws and rules affecting civil jurisdiction and procedure of dist. cts. in Mauritius, Apr., 1882; dist. judge, Seychelles, Nov., 1884; judge of Seychelles, and sen. mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1889; adminst. govt., Seychelles, Nov., 1892 to May, 1892; judge sup. ct., Mauritius, Aug., 1892.
BROWN, W.—Ck. of wks. for post and telegraph extension wks., Bloemfontein, O.R.C., 1st Sept., 1902.

BROWNE, ALBERT, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1860; apptd., after a compet. exam., to be clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., Sept., 1877, to Oct., 1880; elk. in ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 21st Oct., 1880; returned to duty in C.O., Jan., 1883; apptd. to act for three years as ast. acctnt. to the high commnr. for S. Africa, Apr., 1891; has acted also as auditor, Basutoland, from Oct., 1892; mem. of bd. of inquiry into postal and telegraph adminstrn., Bechuanaland, Apr., 1892; ag. Imperial sec., Jan. to Mar., 1895, and (jointly with Capt. Dawkins) Mar. to Aug., 1896; ast. to Imperial sec., and acctnt., Aug., 1895; ag. Imp. sec., Apr. to June, 1900; finan. advr., O.R.C., August, 1900; col. treasur., O.R.C., Dec., 1901; rettd., 1907.
BROWNE, ALFRED.—B. 1860; apptd. ast. harbour-master, Barbados, 1890.

BROWNE, ARTHUR EDWARD.—Served in S.A. Camp., 1900 to 1902 with 16th Co. (Worcester) L.Y.; Queen's Medal and three clasps, King's medal); clk., land settlmt. dept., O.R.C., June, 1902; chief clk. and registr., July, 1903; ag. ast. sec., July to Dec., 1904; Dec., 1904, to Jan., 1905; ag. sec., May to Nov., 1906.

BROWNE, FRANK.—Student in Pharmaceut. Soc.'s Schl. in 1887; medallist in chemistry; elected research schlr. in 1888; apptd. a demonstrator in practical chemistry laboratories, 1889 to 1893; apptd. ast. apothecary and analyst, Hong Kong, 1893; govt. analyst, 1898; sec. to liquor commnr., 1898; is Ph.C. and F.C.S.; J.P., 1903; mem. of comttee. for evening continuation classes, 1906; author of several scientific papers.
BROWNE, ROBERT.—Gen. Post Office, Barbados, 1890; customs, 1894; 2nd cls. supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, 1900; ag. ast. treas., Gold Coast, July to Sept., 1900; 1st cls. supervisor, May, 1902.

BROWNING, JOHN GRANT, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Cutford Schl.; res. engrnr. Eastwood and Gresley sewage scheme; exec. dist. engrnr., P.W.D., Selangor, Malay States, Feb., 1896; engaged upon Pahang Trunk rly. construction.
BRUCE, LORD, EDWARD JAMES.—B. 1881; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; lieut., Forfar and Kincardine R.G.A. Militia; ast. priv. sec. to Earl of Elgin, 1st Feb., 1906.

BRUCE, SIR CHARLES, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1837; ed. at Harrow; author of "Die Geschichte von Nala und Damayante" (Sanscrit text, published by the Imp. Acad. of St. Petersburg), 1862; poems, 1866, and other wks.; ast. libr., Br. Museum, 1863; prof. of Sanscrit, King's Coll., 1865; rector Royal Coll., Mauritius, 1868; dir. of pub. instruction, Ceylon, 1878; col. sec., Mauritius, 1882; adminstd. the govt. in 1883; lieut.-gov. and govt. sec., Br. Guiana, 1883; gov., Windward Is., 1893; prof. of Mauritius, 1897; ret., 1903.

BRUCE, ROBERT RANDAL.—B. 1808; elk., Saint Helena, Mar., 1886; ch. elk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; dep. registr. of births and deaths, Sept., 1886; J.P., Sept., 1896; dep. harb. mast., Oct., 1898; collr. of dues, 1887 to 1901; col. treas. and harb. mast., Jan., 1902; shipping mast., Jan., 1906.
BRUCE, THOS. ROBERT.—B. 1862; 2nd elk., col. sec.'s off., St. Helena, Jan., 1898; postmaster, St. Helena, Apr., 1898.

BRUMELL, JOHN.—Admitted to Mid. Tem., Oct., 1872; called to the bar, June, 1875; practised his profession in Br. Guiana until Apr., 1882, when he was apptd. a stip. justice of the peace, stip. justice for New Amsterdam, and sheriff for the county of Berbice, 1883; acted on several occasions as pub. prosecutor, on two occasions as judge of the inf. crim. ct.

BRUNKER, HOWARD M.—2nd lieut., Donegal Artillery (militia), 1890; lieut., 1891 (passed schl. of instrn., Woolwich, 1893); resig., 1892, to join Bechuanaland border pol.; served in ditto, Oct., 1892, to Nov., 1895; served through Matabale campaign, 1893-4 (medal); apptd. sub-inspr. of pol., B. Guiana, 12th Feb., 1896; promoted dist. inspr. 6th April, 1900; acted adjutant and musketry instr. to B. G. militia and police, April, 1901, to April, 1902; apptd. capt. in Donegal Artillery (militia), 18th June, 1902; passed schl. of musk., Hythe, 1902; promoted county inspr. of pol., B. G., 19th Nov., 1903.

BRYAN, HERBERT, C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1865; 2nd lieut., Line regt., 18th June, 1892; lieut., 16th Jan., 1894; capt., Manchester regt., 26th July, 1899; brev. major, 29th Nov., 1900; served in W. Africa, 1897-8; employed in Lagos Hinterland; also in operations on the Niger, including expeditn. to Bassema (ment. in desp., medal with two clasps); served in N. Nigeria, 1900, slightly wounded (ment. in desp., clasp); served in operations in Ashanti, 1900; on the staff, D.A.A.G. (ment. twice in desp., medal); chief staff offr., Gambia expedit., 1901 (ment. in desp., medal with clasp); staff offr., W.A.F.F., 16th Oct., 1901; employed at C.O., 8th May, 1902, to 14th Nov., 1903; ag. col. sec., G. Coast, 29th Nov., 1903; ag. gov., 9th Feb., 1904, to 3rd Mar., 1904; apptd. col. sec., G. Coast., 17th Feb., 1904; ag. gov., 10th May to 11th Nov., 1905, and 3rd Apr., to 1st Sept., 1906.
BRYAN, HUGH, M.A.—Provl. ast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Natal, 9th Dec., 1902; ast. mast., 1st Jan., 1903; inspr. of schl., 11th Nov., 1904.

BRYANT, ALFRED THOMAS.—Ed. Tonbridge schl. and Wad. Coll., Oxon.; cadet, S. Stltlnts., 1883; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1886; dist. offr., Malacca, 1887; ag. ditto, P.W., 1889; dist. offr., Dindings, June, 1890; ag. collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treasv., Malacca, Mar., 1894; inspr. of schl., S. Stltlnts., Apr., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, 1898; ag. 1st mag., Sing., Aug., 1905; ag. aud.-gen., June, 1906; treas. S. Stltlnts., Feb., 1907.

BUCHANAN, ALEXANDER.—B. 1848; ed. St. Peter's, Adelaide, S. Australia, and Hofwyb, Switzerland, and Glasgow; admitted to S. Australian bar, 1884; stip. mag. Port Adelaide, 1891; master sup. et., registr. in admv., registr. of companies, industrial and provident societies, and trades unions, 1891; industrial registr. under Conciliation Act, 1895; registr. of probates, Aug., 1903; dist. registr. of high ct. of Aust., 1903; dep. industrial registr. of commonw. ct. of conciliation and arbitn., 1907.

BUCHANAN, SIR EBENEZER JOHN, LL.D., Cantab., Knt. Bach. (1901).—B. 1844; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1873; advoc. of sup. ct. of Cape Col., of high ct. of prov. of Griqualand West, and of vice-admiralty ct., Cape Col.; elected mem. for Worcester in house of assem., 1877; capt. D.E.O.V. Rifles, 6th Mar. 1878; active serv. in Transkei, 1879; extra A.D.C., gov. and comdr.-in-chief, 1879; ag. atty.-gen. Griqualand West, Nov., 1879; M.L.C. at coun. of province; apptd. a puisne judge sup. ct. of the Cape Col., Apr., 1880; assthe ct. of the eastern dist., May, 1880, and to sup. ct., June, 1887; ag. ch. just. of Cape Col., 1894, 1896, 1897, 1900, 1901 and 1905; and as such pres. of legis. coun. of colony; mem. of univ. coun. since 1888; vice-chancellor r of univ. of Cape, 1901-5; pres. of special tribunals (treason ct.) 1902-4; pres. war losses review coun., 1904-5; Knt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, 1901; pres. Cape socy. for aid of sick and wounded in war, 1899-1902; mem. of Lord Mayor's relief fund comte. during Transvaal war; chmn., Red Cross comte. of Cape; editor of sup. ct. law reports, etc., Cape Colony.
BUCKELL, CHAS. PHILIP.—Govr.'s messenger and telegraph clk., Portland prison, 1st Mar., 1877; warden, Singapore pris., S. Stithons, 29th Mar., 1883; supt. of telegraphs, Penang, 23rd Dec., 1892.

BUCKNILL, JOHN ALEXANDER STRACHEY, F.Z.S.—Ed. at Charterhouse schl., Godalming, and Keble Coll., Oxford; science exhibt., Charterhouse, 1891; B.A. (hons., chemistry) 1894; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., mid. circuit, 1896; M.A. Oxford, 1897; advoc. of sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902; J.P., 1902; M.L.C., 1904; mem. of prisons advisory board, 1905; legal adviser to atty.-gen., 1906; chmn. of comtees of legis. coun., 1906; consnr. of patents, registr. of trade marks, and registr. of companies, Transvaal, 1902-7; chmn. of many govt. coms., and has on several occasions received thanks of Transvaal admn.; sole consnr. to enquire into allegations of immorality against Chinese labourers; sole consnr. to enquire into non-publication of detailed mining statistics by Transvaal dept. of mines; King's advoc., Cyprus, 1907; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1907; author of "The Birds of Surrey," and other ornithological works.

BUKO, T. B.—Ed. at C.M.S. gram. school, Lagos; entd. civ. serv., Feb., 1888; clk. to ch. registr., sup. ct., Jan., 1890; transf. to customs dept., Apr., 1891; dep. registr. and interpreter, Leckie, east. dist., May, 1892; clk. to atty.-gen., Mar., 1901; 3rd cl. clk. col. sec. s off., May, 1901.
BULMER, J. A.—I.S.O. (1903); Postm., Cyprus, 27th July, 1878; postm.-gen., Trinidad, 1883; ret., 1903.

BULWER, SIR HENRY ERNEST GASCOYNE, G.C.M.G. (1883), K.C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1836; was one of the offl. residents under the lord high consnr. in Ionian Is., from Oct., 1860, until the withdrawal of the Br. protectorate in June, 1864; apptd. treas. and rec. gen. for the Is. of Trinidad, in Feb., 1866; admin. the govt. of Dominica, Leeward Is., from Mar., 1867, to Jan., 1869; apptd. gov. of Labuan, and H.M. consul-gen. for Borneo, in Aug., 1871; lieut.-gov. of Natal, Aug., 1875, to 1880; gov. of Natal and special consnr. for Zulu affairs, Dec., 1881; high consnr. Cyprus, 1885; ret. 1892.
BULYEA, GEORGE HEDLEY VICARS.—Lieut.-gov. of prov. of Alberta, Canada, 1st Sept., 1905.

BUNDEY, THE HON. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, KT. BACH. (1904), K.C. B. 1838; atty.-gen. of S. Australia, Sept., 1878; entered parlmt. in 1871; held office as min. of justice and educn., July, 1874, to Mar., 1875; 3rd judge of the sup. ct., 1884; also pres. of state bd. of conciliation, Jan., 1895, to Apr., 1896; ret. 1904.

BURBIDGE, HON. G. W., LL.D., K.C.—Called to the bar, New Brunswick, 1871; sec. to comm. to consolidate statutes of New Brunswick, 1876-7; dep. min. of justice, Canada, and solr. for Indian affairs, May, 1882; one of the comsrs. to revise the consolidated statutes of Canada, June, 1883, to June, 1885; judge, exchequer ct. of Canada, Oct., 1888.

BURCHELL, H. C.—Mem. Can. Soc. C.E.; mem. Am. Inst. M.E.; Mount Allison and McGill univs.; asst. engr. to prov. engr. of Nova Scotia, 1875-8; govt. engr., Newfndld., 1884; manager govt. telegraphs, 1889; man. Newfndld. rly., 1897; dep. min. and ch. engr., P.W., and chmn. St. John's municipal coun., 1898; supt., pub. wks., Br. Hond., 31st July, 1905, but did not take up apptn.

BURKE, HENRY LARDNER, K.C.—B. 1850; ed. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford, 2nd cl. classical mods., 1871; B.A., 1873; M.A., 1901; LL.B., of Cape univ., 1880; admitted as an advocate of east. dist. ct., 1880, and of sup. ct., 1886; examiner in literature at Cape univ., 1888-1890; ag. solicitor-gen., 25th June to 25th July, 1895; 22nd Dec., 1896, to 28th Mar., 1897, and from 8th May to 12th June, 1897; apptd. crown prosecutor, Griqualand West, 14th June, 1897; apptd. K.C., 13th May, 1898; sol.-gen., 1st Mar., 1903; ag. puisne judge of sup. ct., July to Sept., 1904, and Nov.-Dec., 1905.
BURKE, S. C.—Ed. at Harrow Schl.; B.A. Jesus Coll., Camb., 1890; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1890; clk. of cts., Jamaica, 1898.

BURLEY, E. B.—B. 1876; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd divn. civ. ser., and assigned to registr.-gen.'s office, 21st Jan., 1896; transf. to C.O. 20th June, 1896; special service in office of high consnr., South Africa, July, 1900, to Jan., 1902; minor staff offr., Feb., 1903.
BURLEY, H. D.—Apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and temporarily employed in the B. of T., Dec., 1906, to June, 1907; assigned to C.O., July, 1907.

BURMESTER, CHARLES MANSEL.—Ed. at Dulwich Coll.; ent. serv. of Norddeutsche-Lloyd SS. Co., Bremen, April, 1898; res. Dec., 1898; joined 58th Co. (Berks) Imp. Yeom., Jan., 20th, 1900; served in S. Afr. camp, 1900-1901; clk. to sec. O. R. C. Admin., Mar. 20th, 1901; clk. to col. sec., June, 1902; ag. ch. clk., Mar., 1905, to Jan., 1906, Aug. to Dec., 1906, and from 8th Apr., 1907.

BURNS, ALAN C.—Revenue offr., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., Mar., 1905; ag. 3rd rev. offr. and harbour., June to Oct., 1906; ag. clk. treasy., May, 1905, to Feb., 1906, Oct., 1906, to May, 1907, and June to Sept., 1907.

BURNS, R. E.—2nd clk., registr.'s off., St. Kitts, June, 1898; ag. clk., town consnr., Basseterre, June to Dec., 1899; clk. P.O., Oct., 1901; extra rev. offr., treasy., Dec., 1899; ag. clk., treasy., May, 1902, to Mar., 1903; ag. govt. clk., clk., ex. and legis. couns., clk. bd. of health, Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1904, June, 1904; ag. clk., treasy., St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1904; tariff clk., treasy., St. K.-N., Oct., 1904; ag. audit clk., May, 1905, to Feb., 1906, and Oct., 1906, to May, 1907; 2nd cls. supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1907.

BURNSIDE, SIR BRUCE LOCKHART, KT. BACH. (1884).—B. 1833; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, Apr., 1856; solr.-gen., Bahamas, May, 1864; was ag. atty.-gen. in 1865, 1866, 1869, and 1875; mem. of exec. coun. in 1866; and of legis. coun. in 1872; mem. of the local legislature from 1859; and was speaker of the House of Assem. in 1866; made Q.C. in 1874; atty.-gen. coun., 1875; Queen's advoc., Ceylon, 1879; ch. justice, 1883; ret., 1893.

BURNSIDE, NIGEL BUTCHER.—New Providence, Bahamas, 3rd clk., survr.-gen.'s dept., 1879 and 1881; ag. clk. bd. of pub. wks., 1880 to 1882; clk. legis. coun., 1881 to 1888; 2nd clk. survr.-gen.'s dept., 1882 to 1883; 2nd clk., col. sec. s dept., 1883 to 1887; priv. sec. to Admin. E. B. A. Taylor, C.M.G., 1886; clk., col. sec.'s dept., and clk., bd. of pub. wks., 1887 (still holding same); ag. col. sec., July to Nov., 1889; July to Oct., 1890, and in 1891; ag. recvr.-gen., Jan. to Mar., 1898, May to Oct., 1898, and May to Dec., 1899; ag. col. sec., Feb. to Mar., 1899, audr. of pub. accts., 1904.
BURNSIDE, R.B.—Called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1884; admtd. to W. Australian bar, July, 1884; usher of black rod, legis. coun., Dec., 1890; crown solr., July, 1894; 3rd puisne judge, Dec., 1902.

BURROWES, THOS. FRASER.—B. 1874; qualified for call to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1900; served in treas., B. Guiana, 1893-1901; sec. to excise bd., Aug., 1898, to Sept., 1899; transf. to S. Nigeria in 1901, and served in custs. and P.O.; ag. collr. of cust., Lagos, on spec. serv. to inquire into discrepancies in collection of duties, and to reorganise dept., Sept., 1904, to Feb., 1905; ag. collr. of cust., S. Nigeria, Jan. to Apr., 1906; ag. P.M.G. in addition to ordinary duties, S. Nigeria, Feb. to Apr., 1906, and Nov., 1906, to Apr., 1907; prov. collr., Lagos, 1st May, 1906, ag. prov. treas., Lagos, with supervision over custs., Aug. to Oct., 1906; drafted custs. management ordce., 1907; sen. asst. col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1907; extraordinary M.L.C. to advise on custs. ordce., Dec., 1907.

BURROWS, STEPHEN MONTAGU, M.A.—Scholar of Eton, scholar of Exeter Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1876; graduated in classical hon., 1879; writer, Ceylon service, Oct., 1889; priv. sec. to Lieut.-Gov. Douglas, June, 1881; J.P. and additional coroner for Colombo, Nov., 1881; attached to the Queen's advoc. dept., June, 1882; J.P. and additional pol. mag., Galle, Aug., 1882; ag. office asst. to the govt. agt., N.W. prov., Apr., 1884; extra office asst. to the govt. agt., central prov., May, 1884; ag. office asst. to the govt. agt., north-central prov., and additional dist. judge, coisnr. of requests and pol. mag., Anuradhapura, Aug., 1884; ag. office asst., Badulla, July, 1886; ag. asst.-agt., Matale, June, 1887; ag. dist. judge, Kegalle, 1882; ag. fiscal agt., C.P., 1882; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, 1893; do., Nuwara Eliya, 1896; dir. of pub. instruction, 1899; govt. agent, N.W.P., 1902; author of "The Visitor's Guide to Kandy and Nuwara Eliya," and "The Buried Cities of Ceylon."

BURSLEM, W.—Ed. Manchester gram. schl. and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1879; M.A., 1882; asst. mast., Harrison Coll., Barbados, 1880; 2nd mast., Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 1891; head master, Lodge schl., Barbados, 1892; principal, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, 1895.

BURT, ALFRED EARLE.—B. 1852; Clk. to ch. justice, W. Australia, June, 1871; clk. col. sec.'s office, Dec., 1873; to gov., 1874; in audit office, Jan., 1875; draughtsman, marine survey dept., 1875; and in crown lands dept., Sept., 1877; acted as regisr. of deeds and titles, Jan., 1880, to Mar., 1881; regisr. of titles and deeds, June, 1890.

BURT, OCTAVUS, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1849; clk. in gov. office, W. Australia, May, 1872; Jan., 1874, clk. to exec. comm. and priv. sec. to gov.; Apr., 1874, to Dec., 1874, priv. sec. to Gov. Weld; May, 1875, clk. to exec. comm. and priv. sec. to Gov. Sir Wm. Robinson, K.C.M.G.; Sept., 1877, ch. clk. and keeper of records in survey office; res. mag., Newcastle, Apr., 1880; ditto, York, Apr., 1887; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1887; ag. col. sec., 1890, with seat in exec. comm., under-secr. for W. Australia under responsible govt., 1891; acted as sec., agent-gen.'s off., London, Apr. to Oct., 1898; sheriff and comptroller-gen. of pris., W. Australia, 1901; acts also as dep.-marshal of high ct. of Australia.

BURY, FRED. W.—Junior clk., registr.'s off., B. Guiana, Feb. to Aug., 1887; librnr., govt. secretariat, Sept., 1887, to Feb., 1888; asst. clk., recvr.-gen.'s off., Aug., 1888, to Mar., 1889; 5th cls. clk., Apr., 1889, to May, 1892; 4th cls. clk., June, 1892, to May, 1897; 3rd cls. clk., June, 1897; ag. 1st cls. clk., Oct., 1900, to Mar., 1901; ag. asst. recvr.-gen. and senr. administrator, Berbice, Feb., 1904, to 23rd Dec., 1904.
BURY, ROBERT MAXWELL.—5th class clk., postal dept., B. Guiana, Dec., 1887; 4th class clk., ditto, 1st Aug., 1898; 3rd cls. offr., customs dept., 29th Jan., 1903.

BUSHE, ROBERT GERVAISE.—Ed. at King's Col., Camb., where he obtained an exhibn. on entrance; elected to a foundation scholarship, 1874; was 27th wrangler in the math. tripos, 1875; grad. B.A., 1875; 2nd master Queen's Roy. coll., Trinidad, 1878; inspr. of schools, 1890; auditor-general, 1903.

BUSHE, ROBERT JOHN SCOTT.—A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1861; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad. Malvern Coll., and Lancing Coll.; articled to late J. E. Tanner, M.I.C.E.; ag. and special draftsman, P.W. Dept., Trinidad, various occasions, 1882-6; draftsman light rly., Feb., 1889; survr. of loan wks., Grenada, Jan., 1890; ag. dir. pub. wks., 1891; engaged on loan wks., Trinidad, 1893; and in charge of S. div., P.W.D., 1894-6; draftsman, 1894; dist. offr., 1st grade, 1899; asst. col. civ. engnr., B. Guiana, 1902; ag. col. civ. engnr., May to Nov., 1903; July, 1906, to Feb., 1907, and from 20th Dec., 1907.

BUTLER, ERNEST ORMOND.—Served in Bech. Border police and Bech. mounted pol., 1891-1897; Matabele war, 1893-4; served in B.S.A. police, Bech. Prot. div., 1898-1902; S. African war, 1899-1902 (medal and clasp, defence of Matjikeng); ch. clk., headquarter off., Lt.-Gen. Baden Powell's force, 1900; customs offr., 1902-1903; asst. commr.'s clk., Bech. Prot., 1902; J.P., ditto, 1903.

BUTLER, F. G. A.—B. 1873; exhibitioner of Trin. Coll., Oxford, 1892; 1st class classical mods., 1894; 1st class final classical schools, 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in Admiralty, Oct., 1896; 2nd class clk. in C.O., Apr., 1897; sec. to W. Africa currency comittee, 1899; priv. sec. to Duke of Marlborough, Mar., 1904; priv. sec. to Mr. Winston Churchill, Dec., 1905; 1st class clk., 1st Jan., 1907; chmn., E.I.O., 1907.

BUTLER, PAUL JOHN HENRY.—B. 1844; lieut. 5th Roy. Lanes. Militia, 16th Nov., 1860; capt., 13th Nov., 1866; entd. Natal civ. serv., Jan., 1890; sergt.-at-arms, legis. assem., Natal, Nov., 1895; serv. in S. Africa war as lieut. with Hotchkiss gun detach. to Ladysmith, 1899-1900. (medal and clasp, defence of Ladysmith).
BUTLER, RICHARD, M.L.A., S. Aust. (1890).—Min. of ed. and agric., 1898-9; treas., 1901; treas. and consnr. of crown lands and immigr., 1902; also premier, 1st Mar. to 26th July, 1905.

BUTLER, CAPT. VERA ALBAN.—Late R.M.L.I. and 7th Roy. Lane. Militia; 2nd class inspr. of pol., Mauritius, June, 1889; pol. offr., with magisterial powers, Diego Garcia, Sept., 1886; reverted to former post on abolition of latter, Mar., 1889; ag. 1st class inspr. of pol., Jan., 1892; inspr., Sept., 1894; ag. ch. offr. of pol. on several occasions for Port Louis.

BUTLER, MAL.-GEN. SIR WM. FRANCIS G. (1906), K.C.B. (1886), C.B. (1874); B. 1838; in Canada in Red River expedit., 1870; in A. 1874; Zulu War, 1878-9; Egypt, 1882; with G. Relief exped., 1884-5; comdg. troops S. Africa, 1898; admstd. govt. Cape, Nov., 1898, to Feb., 1899; commanding troops W. Dist. England, 1899; do., Aldershot, 1900.

BUTTERWORTH, CAPTAIN ARCHIBALD WILLIAM.—B. 1866; gov't. storekeeper, Br. N. Guinea, Oct., 1890; headqrs. offr. armed constab., Feb., 1891; also port and harbour, and native mag., promoted commandant constab. and res. mag., Oct., 1894; active serv., Tugere expedit., 1896 (medal and clasp); volunt., for serv. in S. Afr., and joined 4th Q'land conting., Mar., 1900, as sen. lieut.; prom. capt. at Pretoria, Nov., 1900; ment. by Lord Roberts in cablegram to gov't. of Q'land for splendid behaviour, 2nd Dec., 1900; (Queen's medal with three clasps); resig. conting. and Br. N. Guinea govt. serv., Sept., 1901; 2nd lieut., Royal Lanc. Regt., Sept., 1901; capt. and company comdr., Lagos batt. W.A.F.F., Sept., 1901; active serv., Aro expedit., S. Nigeria, 1901-2 (medal and clasp); capt. 4th batt. Royal Lanc. Regt., Aug., 1904; dist. commsn., Lagos, Oct., 1904.

BUXTON, SYDNEY CHARLES, M.P.—Ed. at Clifton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; was mem. of the London schl. bd. from 1876 to 1882; hon. sec. to Mr. Tuke's Irish emigrn. fund, 1882-4; author of the "Handbook to Political Questions," the "Political Manual," "Finance and Politics," "An Historical Study, 1783-1883," &c., and editor of the imp. parlim. series; M.P. for Peterborough from June, 1883, to Nov., 1885, when he was unsuccessful; contested Croydon unsuccessfully in Jan., 1886; elected July, 1886, and re-elected in 1892, 1895, and 1900, for Poplar (Tower Hamlets), for which he still sits; parly. under-sec. of state for the cols., 17th Aug., 1892, to 28th June, 1895; postm. gen., Dec., 1905.

BUXTON, Sir THOMAS FOWELL, BART., G.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1895).—3rd Bart.; M.A., Camb.; B. 1837; dep. lieut. and J.P. for Essex and Norfolk; sheriff, 1875; M.P. for King's Lynn, 1865-68; lieut.-col., 2nd Tower Hamlets rifle volr. brigade, 1864; hon. col. since 1884; gov't. of S. Australia, 1895-9.

BYATT, HORACE A.—Class. exhibtn., Lincoln Coll., Oxford, 1894; B.A. (honours), 1898; asst. collr., B.C.A. Prot., Nov., 1899; Africa general serv. medal, 1899-1900; asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Aug., 1905; asst. dist. offr., Nov., 1905; sec. to adminstr., Somaliland, Aug., 1906.
BYRNE, M.—Sec. to law dept., Victoria, Oct., 1896; formerly ch. clk. in same dept.

CADE, CHARLES SHERWOOD.—Capt. in the W. Cork artillery militia; served through the Zulu war, 1878-9; present at the battle of Ulundi; asst.-inspr., G.C.C., Mar., 1880; inspr., 11th Dec., 1882; adjt. and musketry instructor of the G.C.C., Sept., 1882, to May, 1883; local comdt. of pol., Cyprus, Sept., 1883; also asst. to commsn., Nicosia, and gov't., central prison, Oct., 1884; commsn. Nicosia, 17th Oct., 1905.

CADMAN, JOHN, M.Sc., M.I.M.E., F.G.S.—B. 1877; ed. High Schl., Newcastle-under-Lyme, and Durham Univ. Coll. Sc.; Staff. C.C. scholar, 1895; B.Sc. (Dunelm.), hons., 1899; M.Sc., 1902; pres., Union soc., 1898; certif. colliery man., 1900; asst. gen. man., Silverdale coal and iron works, Staffs., 1900; asst. agt., Walter Scott, Ltd., Collieries, Durham, 1901; H.M. insp. of mines (H.O.) East Scotland, 1902; ditto, Staffordshire, 1903; govt. mining engr., Trinidad and Tobago, 1904; author of prize papers on "Iron Ore Deposit" before Inst. of Mining Engrs.

CADOGAN, 5TH EARL (Great Britain), GEORGE HENRY CADOGAN.—B. 1840; eldest son of the 4th earl, succeeded his father in 1873; was M.P. Bath for a few months previous to his succession to the title; parly. under sec. for war, May 1875, to 2nd March, 1878; parly. under sec. for the col., 2nd Mar., 1878, to 28th Apr., 1880; lord privy seal in Lord Salisbury's 1st and 2nd ministries; lord-lieut. of Ireland, 28th June, 1895-1902.

CAINE, LIONEL EDWARD.—Ed. at Queen Elizabeth's Gram. Sch., Cranbrook, Kent, and in Belgium; Eastern Tel. Co., 1883-1897; Perth-curnow, Carcavellos, Lisbon, Aden and Mombass; asst. supt. of tels., Uganda rly., 1st Dec., 1897; supt., Feb., 1902; supt. of tels., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 1st Apr., 1902; Nandi medal, 1900.

CALDER, CHARLES MACLEAR.—B. 1857; admitted solr., sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; clk. of cts., parish of Portland, Apr., 1888; ditto, parish of St. Mary's, Feb., 1889; res. mag., Trelawney, Jan., 1897; solr., sup. ct., 1st Aug., 1881; asst. clk., petty sess., Portland, 21st Oct., 1885; clk. of cts., Portland, 2nd Apr., 1888; ditto, St. Mary, Feb., 1889; res. mag., Trelawney, Jan., 1897; ditto, conjoint parishes of Westmoreland and Hanover, Jan., 1899.

CALVERT, JOHN J., I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1830; Clk., col. sec.'s office, N. S. Wales, 1853; clk. of select comtees, legis. assem., May, 1856; Ist clk. legis. coun., Apr., 1859; clk. asst., Jan., 1860; clk. of the parlms., Apr., 1871.

CAMERON, ALLEN DUNCAN.—B. 1874; ed. at Rathmines Sch., Dublin; cler. asst., inspr. of villages, and sec. to bd. of health, B. Guiana, 14th Apr., 1890; 6th cls. clk., inspr. of pris., Oct., 1895; 5th dtd., 9th Feb., 1896; govt. offr., dept. of mines, Bartika, 8th Dec., 1896; ag. navig. offr., 2nd Mar., 1897, to 8th Dec., 1898; ag. mag. as clk., Bartika, 1897-98; transf., with same appt., Korailo station, 19th Oct., 1899; J.P., 1899; mag. as clk., Corentyne jud. dist., 1st May, 1900; 3rd cls. clk., pol. dept., 1st Nov., 1901; lieut., B. Guiana militia, 21st Nov., 1901; capt., 4th Nov., 1901; ag. priv. sec. to gov't., 7th May to 23rd Dec., 1903; A.D.C. to gov't., Aug., 1902, to Sept., 1904; ag. govt. agt. and stip. mag., N.W. dist., 22nd Jan., 1904, to 17th Feb., 1905; ag. stip. mag., S. Essequibo jud. dist., 1st Nov., to 30th Dec., 1905; ag. asst. stip. mag., Berbice jud. dist., 1st Jan., to 27th Apr., 1906; and stip. mag., West Coast Demerara jud. dist., 17th Oct., 1906, to 29th Jan., 1907.

CAMERON, DONALD CHARLES.—Ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; entd. Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., secretariat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1893; 3rd class and despatch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. sec. to ag. gov't., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; ag. asst. gov't. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, principal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to gov't. of Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) whilst on leave, 1902; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, July, 1904; ag. col. sec., 19th Dec., 1904, to 26th Oct., 1905, and 14th Apr., to 13th Sept., 1906.

CAMERON, MAJ.-GEN. DONALD RODERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1834; entered R.A. 1856; capt. 1866, maj. 1875, lieut.-col. 1882, col. 1886; ret. 1888; instr. in gunnery, 1859-62, 15th Bde. R.A.; served throughout Bhootan campaign, 1864-66, as adjt. and as staff offr. of offr. comdg. R.A., Doonar Field Force (medal with clasp, thrice mentioned in despatches); adjt. 1st Bde. R.A., 1867-8; accompanied the Hon. W. McDougall, C.B., to Fort Garry, as a mem. of the exec. coun., N.W. Territories, 1869; was comanr. internat. bndry. coman. and supdt. the expedn. which marked the internat. bndry. from the Lake of the Woods to the summit of the Rocky Mountains, 1872-6; reported on the Br.-Alaskan bndry., 1874; sec. to Canadian delegation at the Paris internat. confce., 1883, for the protection of submarine cables; reported on the E. bndry. of Br. Columbia, 1884; and again on the Alaskan bndry. in 1886; priv. sec. to Sir C. Tupper, Canadian plenip. to the fishery confce., 1887; principal, Roy. Mil. Coll., Kingston, Canada, 1888; ret., 1896.

CAMERON, EDWARD J., C.M.G. (1905).—Ed. at Shrewsbury Schl., Clifton Coll., and Merton Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. to Sir C. C. Lees, gov., Bahamas, Jan., 1882; and as gov. of the Leeward Is., Jan., 1884; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, Nov., 1884; J.P. for the Stttnt., and visiting justice, Freetown Gaol; ag. col. sec. and treas., Dec., 1885, to July, 1886; pres., Virgin Is., Feb., 1887; mem. Leeward Is. legis. coun., 1887, and exec. coun., 1888; comanr., Turks Is., Mar., 1893; ag. judge sup. ct., May to Dec., 1893, Jan. to June, 1899, and Apl. to July, 1898; admstr., St. Vincent, May, 1901.

CAMERON, HON. EWEN HUGH.—Mem. of Shire coun., Victoria, since 1863; has been mem. and chmn. of rlws. standing comte.; M.L.A. for Evelyn, Victoria, 1874; min. for mines and water supply, 1902-4; comanr. of pub. wks. and min. of pub. health, Feb., 1904.

CAMERON, MAJOR MAURICE ALEXANDER, C.M.G. (1900), R.E.—B. 1855; first comanr. in Roy. Engrs., Aug., 1874; dept. col. engrnr., S. Stttntls., Dec., 1883, to Apr., 1892; during which he acted for about two years and a half as col. engrnr. and survr-gen., and was a mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; apptd. one of the crown agts. for the colonies, July, 1895.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD NEIL, F.R.G.S.—Capt. reserve of offrs., late R.A.; South Africa, 1897-1903; on spec. serv., 1st July, 1899, to 31st Dec., 1900, including spec. apptmnt. at H.B.M. consulate-gen.'s, Delagoa Bay, May to Dec., 1900; afterwards on intell. staff, army headqrs.; operations in Natal, 1899-1900, including actions at Colenso and Spionkop, and relief of Ladysmith (twice ment. in desps.; Queen's medal, 4 clasps; King's medal, 2 clasps); Anglo-Portuguese boundary delimit. comanr., East Africa, 1904-5.

CAMPBELL, CHARLES HOWARD.—Ed. at Repton Sch.; P.A.S.I., London, 1898; diploma, land ag. and survr.; asst. engrnr., survey and constn. staff, Buenos Ayres Great Southn. Rly., S. America, 1899-1904; survr., E. Africa Prot., 4th May, 1904; ag. asst. ch. survr. and land offr., May to Sept., 1905; asst. land offr., 18th June, 1906.

CAMPBELL, DOUGLAS GRAHAM.—2nd Surveyor, P.W.D., Selangor, S. Stttntls., 1883; ditto, land office, 1885; asst. dist. offr., Rawang, 1887; sec. to res., 1888; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 1890; ditto, Ulu Selangor, 1893; ditto, Klang, 1897; ditto and asst. Indian immigrtn. agt., 1898; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1899; sec. to res., 1901; res. of Negri Sembilan, 1904; has acted as res. on several occasions, and also as sec. to govt.

CAMPBELL, H.C., I.S.O. (1903).—Second clk. in the deeds registry office, Natal, Apr., 1861; 2nd clk. audit office, Jan., 1863; 1st clk. R.M. ct., co.
