Pietermaritzburg, Mar., 1868; R.M.; div. of Ixopo, Mar., 1876; ag. R.M. div. of Umgeni, Nov., 1876; R.M., Inanda, and J.P. for col., 1880; master and regir., sup. ct., 1882; col. audr., Apr., 1889; acted as judge native high ct., and as 2nd puisne judge and atty.-gen.; audit-gen., 1894; chmn. civ. ser. bd.; judge president, native high ct., 1899; judge, spec. treas. ct., 1900; resumed duty as judge pres., nat. high ct., 1901; chmn. of comanr. on prison reform and penology, 1905-6; and of native affairs comanr., 1906-7.

CAMPBELL, J. CATHEY.—Capt., R.Irish Rifles; served in traffic dept., Ceylon govt. rlws., 1890; Ceylon pris. dept., 1891; 1st class inspr., Ceylon harbour pol., 1893; transf'd. to land pol., 1897; asst. comanr., pol. and pris., N. Nigeria, 1901; sheriff and keeper of gaols, 1903; comdt. of volrs., customs offr. and san. inspr., Falkland Is., 1906.

CAMPBELL, LANCELOT FREDERICK.—B. 1866; temporary clk. of cus., Sierra Leone, May, 1883; warehouseman, Dec., 1885; landing waiter, 1889; baggage offr. and landing waiter, 1893; 3rd clk., 1895; 2nd clk. and statistician, 1899; 1st clk. in charge of statistics and shpg. branches, 1899; examg. offr., 1904; warehsepr., 1905; ag. ch. clk. of cust., June to Sept., 1906; acted as admeasurer of vessels under M.S. Act on several occasions.

CAMPBELL, W. TELFER.—Ed. at Rossall; passed entrance exam., Roy. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst, 1882; served with Methuen's Horse, Bechuanaland field force, 1884-85; reg.-gen.'s dept., Queensland, 1889; asst. res. mag., Eastern div., B.N. Guinea, 9th Oct., 1890; res. mag., S.E. div., ditto, Feb., 1891; res. mag. and comanr. of sup. ct., B.N. Guinea, 1893; res. comanr., Gilbert and Ellice Is. Prots., and dep. comanr., Western Pacific High Comanr., 29th Nov., 1905.

CAPE TOWN, ARCHBISHOP OF, MOST REV. WILLIAM WEST JONES, D.D.—Consec. 1874; metropolitan of the church of the prov. of S. Africa; formerly fellow of St. John's Coll., Oxon; preacher at Chapel Royal, Whitehall, and rural dean of Oxford.
CAPPER, THOMAS.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1876; B.A., Camb. (Math. and Nat. Sc. Triposes), 1879; inspr. of schools, Jamaica, Oct., 1889; offl. mem. of legis. coun., 1882.

CARBONE, SIR JOSEPH, LL.D. (1862), C.M.G. (1887), K.C.M.G. (1891), G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1839; received at the Malta Bar, 1863; reporter, ct. of commerce, 1868; crown advoc. and govt. legal adviser, 1880; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., ex officio; ch. justice, Malta, 1895; vice-pres. legis. coun., 1898.

CARDEN, J.—B. 1866; shorthand writer in office of asst. sec. (for India) to Roy. comanr. Col. and Indian Exhib., 1886; diploma for services; entered service of Roy. Niger Company, Jan., 1887; priv. sec. to Sir G. Goldie, Jan., 1887, to Dec., 1899; transf'd. to C.O. as supplementary clk., Jan., 1900.

CARDEW, CLAUDE AMBROSE.—B. 1870; asst. agt., Chinde, B. Cent. Africa Prot., June, 1883; collr., South Nyasa dist., 1894; jud. offr., 1895; 2nd class asst., Apr., 1902; dist. mag., Upper Shire, 1902; dist. ree., 1st class, Apr., 1906.

CARDEW, COL. SIR FRED., K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1839; engaged in N.W. Frontier of India campaign, 1863, and Zulu and Transvaal campaigns, 1879-81; D.A.Q.M.G. in S. Africa, 1879-80; asst. mil. sec., China, 1882-3; sub-comanr., Zululand, 1884-6; A.A.G., S. Africa, 1889; res. comanr., Zululand, 1890; gov. S. Leone, 1894 to 1900.

CARGILL, JASPER FARMER.—B.A., LL.B., Gorville and Caius Coll., Camb.; called to the bar Inner Tem., Jan., 1890; ag. asst. to atty.-gen. and clk. to legis. coun., Jamaica, 1895; law exam. to solr.'s comte., 1897; referee of titles, 1900; sec. to Montego Bay Riots comm., 1902; res. mag., Jamaica, Aug., 1904; ag. solr.-gen., and nom. M.L.C. on various occasions, 1896-1904.

CARLILE, EDWARD, K.C. - Gold medallist in law, Melbourne Univ., 1868; elk., census office, Victoria, May, 1861; regis.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1862; crown law offices, Mar., 1865; called to the bar, Victoria, 1871; parly. and professional asst. to crown law offrs., 1873; parly. draftsman, July, 1879; elk.-asst., legis. assem., Apr., 1882; re-apptd. parly. draftsman with higher status, Apr., 1889.

CARLING, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1893). - B.1828; mem. for London, Ontario, 1857-74; elected to Dominion house of commons on confederation, 1867; rec.-gen. of Canada, 1862; min. of wks. and agric., Ontario, 1867-71; again elected for London, 1878; sworn of the privy coun. and made postm.-gen. in Sir J. Macdonald's govt., 1882; min. of agric., 1885 to 1892; mem. of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; ret., 1894.

CARLSON, KNUT ALEXANDER. - B. 1863; forest probationer in the Knysna conservancy, Cape Colony, 5th Feb., 1888; probationary asst. to conservator of forests, Knysna, Sept., 1889; dist. offr., Kokstad, Transkeian conservancy, May, 1892; passed through course of forestry at R.I.E. Coll., Cooper's Hill, during 1894-5, and was awarded a diploma; dist. forest offr., Butterworth, Aug., 1895; transf. to O.R.C. as chief of forestry div., 1st Dec., 1903.

CARMODY, P. - Entered Imp. service after open competn., 1876; inland rev. laboratory scholarship, 1889; diploma (1st class) Roy. Schl. of Mines; asst. analyst and subsequently analyst, Somerset House, 1881-90; govt. analyst, Trinidad, and prof. of chemistry, Queen's Roy. Coll. and Coll. of the Immaculate Conception, June, 1890; mem. central agric. bd., 1890; mem. bd. of health, 1891; is F.I.C., F.C.S., mem. of soc. of chem. industry, and author of prize paper on "Recent Progress of Invention and Science," and other published papers on scientific subjects.
CARON, L. B. - Puissne judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1874.

CARON, THE HON. SIR J. P. R. A., K.C.M.G. (1885), K.C. - B. 1842; called to the bar of Lower Canada (now Province of Quebec) 1865; created a Q.C., 1876; entered the Canadian House of Commons, 1873, where he still sits; min. of militia and defence for the Dominion of Canada, 8th Nov., 1880; postm.-gen., 1892 to 1896; one of representatives of Canada at col. confe., Ottawa, 1894.
CARPMAEL, ERNEST. - Ed. at Dulwich and Trinity Hall, Camb.; scholar, 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., cadet, Weihiwei, 1904.

CARRENA, ALBERT EMANUEL E.O. - B. 1873; ed. at St. Gregory's R.C. gram. schl., Lagos; asst. elk. har. dept., 1894; elk. and storekeeper, 1895; messenger and copyist, Queen's advocate's off., 1896; 2nd elk. gen. registry, 1897; Queen's advoc.'s elk., 1897; assisted as elk. in the trade comsn., 1898; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the conduct of Dr. D. J. Jones, 1899; promoted 3rd class elk., govr.'s office, 1901; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the railway accident at Owowo, 1901; sec. to the comsn. of inquiry on the customs dept., 1901; acted as regis. of corres., secretariat, 1902; on spec. serv. to Ekiti countries, 1903; ag. conf. elk., elk. of exec. and legis. comns., 1904; 2nd cls. elk. secretariat, 1905; transf. to govr.'s office, 1905; 1st cls. elk., 1907.

CARRINGTON, EARL (created 1895), VISCOUNT WENDOVER (U.K.), BARON CARRINGTON (Great Britain, 1797, and Ireland, 1796), CHARLES R. W. CARRINGTON, P.C. (1881), G.C.M.G. (1885). - B. 1843; ed. at Eton and Trim Coll., Camb., B.A. 1863; late capt. Roy. Horse Guards, lieut.-col. 3rd batt. Oxford Light Infantry; capt. Queen's Body Guard, 1881 to 1885; gov. of N. S. Wales, 1885-90; lord chamberlain, 1892-95; pres. bd. of agric., Dec., 1905; joint hered. lord greatchamberlain of England.

CARRINGTON, MAJOR-GEN. SIR FREDERICK K.C.B. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1887), C.M.G. (1880). - B. 1844; ed. Chelten Coll.; 24th regt.; comdr. F. L. H. in the Transkei war, S. Africa, 1878-79; commanded Transvaal volks. against Sikukula, 1878-79; commanded left wing C.M.R., 1879 to 1883; commanded col. forces in Basuto war, severely wounded; commanded 2nd mounted rifles, Bechuanaland field force, 1884; comdt. of pol. Br. Bechuanaland, 1885; comdt. native forces, Zululand, in 1888; again in command Bechuanaland pol., Dec., 1888-89; and again on special service, Nov., 1893, in connection with Matabele war; comdg. brigade at Gibraltar, 1895; com- manded forces in Matabele war, 1896; resumed command at Gibraltar, 1897; commanded Rhodesian Field Force during S. Afr. war, 1900.

CARRINGTON, SIR JOHN WORRELL, K.T. (1897), C.M.G. (1888), K.C., F.R.G.S., D.C.L., LL.D. - B. 1847; ed. Codrington Coll., Barbados (S.C.C., 1866), and Line. Coll., Oxon (B.A., 1872; M.A., 1891); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1872; acted as judge, asst. of appeal, Barbados, 1874-5; J.P., 1874; mem. house of assem., 1874-8, and again 1881; M.L.C., 1878-81; solr.-gen., 1878; again acted as judge, asst. of appeal, 1879; and as atty.-gen., 1880; hon. D.C.L., Durham, 1879; hon. LL.D., St. Andrews, 1894. Was pres. of the educ. bd., and of the bd. of health, and received the thanks of govt. for services in connection with educn.; ch. justice, St. Lucia and Tobago, 1882; on special duty as ag. col. sec., Grenada, 1882; admnstd. govt. of Tobago, 1883 to 1885; prepared revised edition Tobago laws; ag. ch. justice of Grenada, 1886; in 1887-9 prepared a new edition of the laws of St. Lucia; atty.-gen. Br. Guiana, Dec., 1888; chancellor of the diocese, 1890; engaged, 1891-2, in drafting for the govt. of Br. Guiana bills relating to the etc., criminal law and procedure, etc., of that col.; chmn. central bd. of health, 1889, of the opening-up-the-country comsn., 1891-2, and of the spirits ordinance comsn., 1893-4; maj. in the Br. Guiana militia, 1893. In 1894 prepared a new and revised edition of the laws of Br. Guiana; ch. justice, Hong Kong, 1896; ret., 1902.
CARROLL, HON. JAMES. - Native min. and comsnr. of stamp duties, New Zealand.

CARRUTHERS, JOHN BENNETT, F.R.S.E., F.L.S. - B. 1869; ed. Dulwich Coll., Royal Schl. of Mines, London, and Griefswald Univ., Prussia; demonstrator of botany, Royal Vet. Coll., London, 1892; Prof. of botany, Coll. of Agric., Dowton, Wilts, 1894; proceeded to Ceylon to investigate cacao disease under joint auspices of govt. and Ceylon planters assoc., 1898; mycologist to govt. of Ceylon and asst. dir., Royal Bot. Gardens, 1900; dir. of agric. and govt. botanist, F.M.S., 1905.
CARRUTHERS, HON. JOSEPH HECTOR McNEIL M.A. - B. 1857; called to the bar, 1879; M.L.A. for Canterbury, 1887-1894, and since for reformed dist. of St. George, N.S. Wales; min. for pub.

CASSON, Joseph Charles.—Priv. sec. to Sir H. H. Johnston, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., H.M.'s commr., B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1894-1895; clk. in ch. acctn.'s off., Feb., 1896; asst. sec., B. C. A. admtn., Apr., 1901; supt. of native affairs, Aug., 1903.
CASTOR, C. F., M.B., C.M., F.R.G.S.—Asst. col. surg., G. Coast Col., Feb., 1879; med. offr., Br. Guiana, 1885; med. supt. leper asylum, 1887; is J.P. and coroner.
CATOR, Bertie Angelo.—Lieut., R.N. (retired); dep. mast. attendant, Singapore, Feb., 1907.

CATT, The Hon. A.—Mem. house of assem., S. Australia, 1881; commr. of crown lands, S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884; commr. of pub. wks., June, 1887, to June, 1889; chmn. of comites., legis. assem., 1890-1903.

CAVENDISH, Alexander.—B. 1878; ed. at Bedford and Hertford coll., Oxford (exhibn., 1901); B.A.; cadet, S.S., Jan., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang; ag. 4th mag., Singapore; passed final exam in Malay, May, 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Oct., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, 23rd Jan., 1904; ag. supt. of educ., Penang, Apr., 1905; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Singapore, 16th May, 1907.

CAYLEY, Sir Richard (Knt., 1882).—B. 1833; M.A. and scholar of St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, June, 1862; advoc. sup. ct. of Ceylon, June, 1863; dep. Queen's advoc., May, 1868; puisne judge of the sup. ct., 1873; Queen's advoc., 1876; ch. justice, 1879; resig., 1883; is a mag. for Rutland.

CELESTIN, Louis Albert.—Ed. govt. normal schl., Mauritius; teacher in edn. dept., 1874; ag. inspr. of schls., Nov., 1886, to Aug., 1889; dist. cashier, Dec., 1889, to Nov., 1893; ch. inspr. of distilleries, Nov., 1893; ag. supt. of distilleries, Apr., to Oct., 1895; dist. cashier, Flacq, June, 1898; Rose Hill, Jan., 1901; 2nd cls. clk., treasy., June, 1903.

CHADWICK, John Courtenay Chasman.—B. 1846; clk. and interp. to the R.M., Inanda div., Natal, Jan., 1868; clk. of the ct., Oct., 1870; acted as R.M. and admstr. of native law on various occasions, 1872-7, at Inanda; J.P. for the Inanda div., Aug., 1875; registr. of the native high ct., and sec. to native admtn. bd., Nov., 1876; admitted an atty. of the sup. ct., 1878; first class interp. on the staff of Maj.-Gen. Newdigate during Zulu War of 1879 (medal and clasp); admstr. of native law and border agt., Upper Tugela, 1880; R.M., Lions River div., and J.P. for the col., 1882; R.M. and admstr. of native law, Ixopo, Sep., 1886; advocate of sup. ct., 1893; mag., Lions River div., 1897-8; Umgeni divn., 1898; on spec. duty to Maputaland, Oct., 1898; mag. of city div., Pietermaritzburg, 1902; ag. ch. mag., Durban, Jan., 1904, to 1st Feb., 1906; judge, native high ct., July, 1906.

CHADWICK, Osbert, C.E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1834; has been employed by the C.O. to report on the drainage and water supply of various cols.; was consult. engr. for the Malta water wks. and Hong Kong water supply and drainage.

CHALMERS, Albert John, M.D., Ch. B. (Victoria Univ., Eng.), M.D. (Liverpool), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Cumb.).—B. 1870; asst. col. surg., G. Coast, 1897; ag. ch. med. offr., 1900, and during siege of Kumasi, 1900 (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); reg. med. coll., lect. on pathology, Ceylon, 22nd Apr., 1901; author of "Simple medical directory for officials in G. Coast Colony," 1901 (ditto, C. 3rd edtn., 1907; report on sanitation of Colombo, 1907; and various papers on medical subjects.

CHAMBERLAIN, THE RT. HON. JOSEPH, P.C. (1880), M.P., Hon. LL.D. (Camb.), Hon. D.C.L. (Oxford).—Eldest son of the late Mr. Joseph Chamberlain; born in London, 1836, and ed. at University Coll. schl., after which he was engaged in business in Birmingham till 1874, when he retired. In 1870 he was elected a mem. of the Birmingham town coun., and in the same year a mem. of the schl. bd., of which he became chmn. in 1873. In each of the three following years, 1874-5-6, he was elected mayor of the borough, and in June, 1876, on the retirement of Mr. Dixon, one of the three representatives of Birmingham, he was returned to Parlimt. At the general election of 1880 he was again returned with the late Mr. John Bright and Mr. Philip Muntz as his colleagues, and on the formation of Mr. Gladstone's adminstn. he was apptd. pres. of the bd. of trade, with a seat in the cabinet. While at the bd. of trade he prepared and succeeded in passing through Parlimt. the Bankruptcy Act, which is now in force, the Patents Act of 1883, and other measures. After the redistribution of seats in 1885 he was returned as mem. for W. Birmingham, and joined Mr. Gladstone's 3rd adminstn. in 1886, as pres of the local govt. bd., but resigned in consequence of his disagreement with the premier's home rule policy. At the general election of 1886 he was returned unopposed for W. Birmingham, and at the end of 1887 proceeded to the U. States as plenipotentiary to negotiate, in conjunction with H.M.'s min. there and Sir Charles Tupper, a treaty for the settlement of the Atlantic fisheries question. The result of these negotiations was the convention of 1888, and though it was rejected by the U.S. senate, the modus vivendi established by it still regulates the fisheries. On the defeat of Lord Rosebery's govt. in June, 1895, Mr. Chamberlain joined Lord Salisbury's govt. as sec. of state for the col., and continued to hold that office in Mr. Balfour's cabinet, 1902. At the general elections of 1895 and 1900, in both of which he took a very prominent part, he was again returned for W. Birmingham. At the end of 1902 he visited S. Africa, to deal with questions connected with the settlement of the country; Lord Rector, Glasgow Univ., 1896; Chancellor, Birmingham Univ., 1901; resigned office in connection with the question of a new fiscal policy, Oct., 1903.

CHAMBERLAYNE, MAJOR TANKERVILLE JAMES.—B. 1844; mem. of the Societe d'Histoire diplomatique of France, and of Karleian, Pipe Roll, and Kildare archeological societies; commendatore of the Order of S. Silvester for serv. rendered to the Vatican library, 1899; coronation medal, 1902; ed. Chelt. Coll.; ensign, Ceylon rifles, June, 1868; transf'd to 80th regt., Oct., 1873; fort adjt., Hong Kong, June, 1875, to Mar., 1877; ag.-brig.-maj., Nov., 1875, to Feb., 1876; officiated twice as asst. mil. sec. China command; served in Transvaal and throughout Zulu war, 1878-79; present at Ulundi (medal and clasp, 1878-79); A.D.C. to gov. of Natal (Sir H. Bulwer, G.C.M.G.), 3rd Feb., 1882; priv. sec., Mar., 1882; also ag. elk. exec. coun., July, 1885; priv. sec. to high commr. of Cyprus, and elk., exec. coun., Mar., 1886; thanked by S. of S. for serv. in connection with Cyprus deputation to London, 1889; local commt. of pol., 1892; col. of customs and excise and asst. to commr., Larnaca, 1894; ag. commr., Larnaca, June-Oct., 1895; Kyrenia, Nov., 1896-Apr., 1897; Nicosia, June-Nov., 1897; Kyrenia, Apr., 1898; commr. and local commt. of pol., Kyrenia, 1899; ag. commr., Larnaca, Ju.-OCT. 1900; do. Nicosia, Jan. to May, 1900, N. Great to Oct., 1901, and Aug., 1902, to Nicosia R. ditto, Kyrenia, from Dec., 1903; and—"Lacrimae Nicosienses," 1894, and "Le R. A. dou Morf," 1907.

CHAMBERS, CECIL HART.—B. 1876; cash St. John's savings bank, Antigua, 1893; acct. ditto, 1894; 4th out-door offr., treasv., and cu. dept., 1895; 3rd in-door offr., treasv., Antigua, 1897; audit clk., Dominica, 1900; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901; ag. chief audit clk. at federal acctn., Antigua, May, 1902, to Mz., 1903, and May, 1905, to Feb., 1906; ag. treasv. offr., Montserrat; M.I.C.; commr. for oaths, Nov., 1903, to Mar., 1904; financial ast., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1906.

CHAMBERS, JOHN RATCLIFFE.—2nd clk. to pres. St. Kitts, May, 1875; 3rd landing waiter, treasv., Dec., 1878; 1st clk. to regis. and provost-marshal, Apr., 1881; sec. of the commr. to inquire into the general condition of Dominica, Nov., 1881; priv. sec. to gov. Leeward Is., 1882 to 1883; clk. to mag., dist. C, St. Kitts, July, 1883; dep. coroner, May, 1885; qtrmr. with St. Kitts militia, June, 1885; J.P., and visiting justice of the gaol; ag. mag., dist. C, 1899, 1902, 1903 and 1904; escheator-gen., 1905; ag. mag., dist. D, St. Kitts, Apr.-Dec., 1907.

CHANCELLOR, ALEXANDER RICHARD.—Capt. 4th Batt. H.L.I., serv. in W. Indies, Dec., 1876, to June, 1902; asst. supt. of pol., S. Settlem., Oct., 1902; supt., Tanjong Pagar Dock, Nov., 1905; supt. of pol., Singapore, Sept., 1874.

CHANCELLOR, J. R., D.S.O.—Capt. R.F.; served with expedn. to Dongola, 1896, with force at Suakin (medal and Egyptian medal); in operations on N.W. frontier of India, 1897-8, with Tirah exped. force, action of Dargai, (ment. in desps.) sec. to col. def. comtee., Oct., 1906.

CHANDLER, WILLIAM KELLMAN, C.M.G. (1902), B.A., LL.D.—B. 1857; St. John's Coll. Camb. (1875-79); law tripes, 1879-80; called to the bar, Inner Temp., June, 1879; ag. escheator-gen. of Barbados, 1880-81; J.P., 1880; apptd. commr. of probates, Dec., 1880; ag. pol. mag. of Bridgetown, Aug. to Nov., 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1880, to Mar., 1881; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Mar., 1881, to June, 1883; master-in-chancery, May, 1882; judge of asst. ct. of appeal, June, 1883; mem. of house of assem., Mar., 1881; represented parish of St. Peter until Nov., 1884; M.I.C., 1884; represented Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connection with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; ag. col. sec., July, 1902, to Feb., 1903, and Apr. to Oct., 1903.

CHAPMAN, ARTHUR, V.D. (1907).—B. 1861; assessor of taxes, Hong Kong, 1889; ag. asst. supt. fire brigade, 1891-92; J.P., 1894; lieut. Hong Kong volunteer corps, 1893; capt., 1897; maj. 2nd in command, 1899; ag. commt. on several occasions; thanks and medal for services during plague, 1894; mem. of comte. on volunteer corps, 1898; commanded volunteers during disturbances in New Territory, 1899; col. aux. forces long serv. medal, 1902; commanded Hong Kong and China Coronation contingent, 1902; sec. to pub. health and bldgs. ordce. commn., 1906-7; commt. vol. corps, 2nd Apr., 1907; col. aux. forces (ffr.) decoration, 1907.
CHAPMAN, HON. AUSTIN.—B. 1864; M.I.A. of N. S. Wales for Braidwood, 1891-1901; M.P. for Eden-Monaro, 1901; retd. to 1st House of Rep., C. of A., 1901; re-elected, 1903; 1st gov't.

CHICHESTER, CHARLES RENFRIE.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll. and Dublin Univ.; B.A. Dub.; L.R.C.P. and L.R.C.S., Ireland; diplomate in public health; licentiate in midwifery, Rotunda, Dub.; dipl. in pub. vaccination; asst. col. surg., Gambia, Mar., 1896; ag. col. surg., Apr. to Nov., 1896, May to Dec., 1898; pres. of bd. of health whilst ag. col. surg.; mem. quarantine bd.; sen. med. offr., So. Nigeria, 1904.

CHILD, Lieut. Herbert A.—Entered H.M. navy as cadet, 15th July, 1883; speely promoted to midshipman, 23rd July, 1885; served in H.M. ships, "Conquest," "Triumph," "Calypso," "Minnotaur," "Duke of Wellington," "Pilot," and "Thrush"; speely promoted to lieut., July, 1891; landed with nav. brig. in Gambia expd., 1891-92 (West African medal and clasp); resigned comsn., Feb., 1894, to take up appt. as asst. supt. marine in N.C. P., 4th May, 1894; took part in naval operations against Chief Nana of Benin river, 1894 (mentioned in desps., 2nd clasp); took part in naval operations against Brass chiefs, 1895 (3rd clasp); sup. of marine, S. Nig., 15th Jan., 1897; took part in naval operations against King of Benin, 1897 (mentioned in desps., 4th clasp); took part in Aro expd., 1901-2, having organisation and charge of marine transport arrangements (mentioned in desps., African gen. serv. medal and clasp); comsn. as lieut. R.N. restored 21st Aug., 1902, on joining emergency list of retired officers, R.N.; took part in Bibiala expd., 1903 (ment. in desps., 2nd clasp); took part in Kwale expd., 1905 (3rd clasp); on amalgamation of Lagos and S. Nigeria apptd. dir. of marine, 1st May, 1906; ag. prov. comsnr., E. Prov., 25th May to 6th June, 1907.

CHIPPENDALL, GEORGE HERBERT.—Sub-lieut. 3rd foot, 1874; lieut. East Kent regt., 1874; capt. Nov., 1883; maj. W. Riding regt., Dec., 1893; lieut.-col., Nov., 1898; comdt. defence force, W. Australia, Dec., 1898; transf'd. to commonwealth govt., 1901.

CHITTY, Louis Ogilvy.—B. 1857; ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; asst. mtr., Harrison Coll., Barbados, 1883-91; headmnr. gram. schl., St. Vincent, Sept., 1891, to Dec., 1893; inspr. of schls., St. Vincent, Jan., 1894, to Dec., 1896; inspr. of schls., Jamaica, Jan., 1897, to Mar., 1898; audr. and inspr. of schls., and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Seychelles, Apr., 1898; ag. J.P., Pradlin, 4th Apr. to 12th June, 1901; audr. only, 1902; ag. treas. and collr. of cust., 1st Oct. to 11th Dec., 1906; ditto, 9th May to 10th Nov., 1907.

CHURCH, ARTHUR FREDERICK.—Ed. at King's Coll., London; asst. engrn., North Cornwall rly., 1893 to 1895; Southampton Dock, 1895 to 1896; Uganda rly., 1896; dist. engrn., Uganda rly., Apr., 1905; officiating supt. of way and works, Uganda rly.
CHURCH, THE HON. C. E.—Mem. for Lunenburg in Nova Scotian legis. assem.; comsnr. for pub. wks. and mines.

CHURCHILL, JOHN SPENCER, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1835; Ed. at Winchester; ensign, 46th regt., 1854; served at siege of Sebastopol (medal and clasp); capt., 36th regt., 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Lisgar, when lord high comsnr. of the Ionian Is., 1857; pres. of Virgin Is., 1879; ag. pres., New 1879, and of Dominica, 1882 to 1887; pres. Montserrat, 1887; comsnr. of St. Kitts-No., 1888; col. sec., Bahamas, 1895; ret., 1905.

CHURCHILL, RIGHT HON. WINSTON LEONARD SPENCER, P.C. (1907).—M.P. for Oldham, 1900-6; for N.W. Manchester, 1906; late lieut. 4th Queen's Own Hussars; ed. Harrow, Sandhurst; entd. army, 1895; served with Spanish forces in Cuba, 1895 (1st cl. (Spanish Order of Military Merit); served, attached 31st Punjab Infantry, with Malakand field force, 1897; present at operations in Bajaur, including actions of 16th and 30th Sept. (desps., medal with clasp); served as orderly offr. to Sir W. Lockhart with Tirah exped. force, 1898 (clasp); served, attached 21st Lancers, with Nile exped. force, 1898; present at battle of Khartoum (medal with clasp); contested Oldham (C.) 1899; served as lieut. South Africa Light Horse; acted as correspond., "Morning Post," South Africa, 1899-1900; taken prisoner, action 15th Nov., but escaped 12th Dec.; present at actions of Acton Homes, Venters Spruit, Hussar Hill, Cingolo, Monte Cristo, and at battles of Spion Kop, Vaal Krantz, and Pieters; also operations round Dewetsdorp, April, 1900; passage of Sand River, 15th May; engagements of Johannesburg and Diamond Hill, and capture of Pretoria (medal with six clasps); partly, under-sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1905; visited Uganda and E. Africa, 1907; author of "The Story of the Mahakand Field Force"; "The River War"; "Savrola"; "London to Ladysmith, via Pretoria"; "Ian Hamilton's March"; "Life of Lord Randolph Churchill."

CLANLIS, J. B.—Chief clk. Port dept., Gibraltar; supplementary clk., Port dept., Sept., 1880; 3rd cl. clk. and clk. to registrar of shipping, Feb., 1883; 2nd cl. clk., Jan., 1892; 1st cl. clk. and clk. to shipping mr., Jan., 1898.

CLARE, HENRY LEWIS.—B.1858; Ed. Rathmines schl. and Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A. (1879), M.B., and B. Ch. (1880); M.D., 1897; D.P.H.; jun. res. med. offr., pub. hosp., Kingston, Jamaica, Apr., 1881; dist. med. offr., Dry River dist. and hosp., July, 1882; transf. to Vere dist., 1883; to Chapelton, 1888; to Spanish Town, 1892; surg. to St. Catherine dist. prison, and to Middlesex and Surrey cty. gaol; dist. med. offr., Kingston, June, 1896; mem. cent. bd. of health; surg.-gen. and M.O.H., Trinidad, Aug., 1907.

CLARENCE, BEVERLEY CHARLES.—Clk. to registr., sup. ct., Natal, Apl., 1875; asst. clk. of the peace, and prosecutor of the native high ct., Natal, Apr., 1880; clk. of the peace, 1896; mag., Pietermaritzburg, 1905.
CLARK, ALFRED ALLEN.—Forester, Ceylon, 1st May, 1875; asst. conservator of forests, 1st Jan., 1889; ag. conservator, Jan., 1901, to June, 1902.

CLARK, HON. ANDREW INGLIS.—B. 1848; barrister, 1877; mem. of House of Assem., Tasmania, representing Norfolk Plains, 1878-1882; atty.-gen., 1887-92 and again in 1894; resign., 1897; one of the representatives of Tasmania in fed. coun. of Aust., 1888, 1889 and 1891; in fed. confce. at Melbourne, 1890; in fed. convention at Sydney, 1891; vice-chancellor of Tasmania Univ., 1901-3; judge of sup. ct., Tasmania, since 1898; author of "Studies in Australian Constitutional Law," 1901; 2nd edition, 1905.

CLARK, FRANCIS WILLIAM.—B. 1864; M.D. (Durham), 1900; M.B., 1892; D.P.H. (Camb.), 1891; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.I. (Lond.), 1886.—Ed. St. Paul's schl., and St. Barts. and Middlesex hosps.; mem. of Hon. Soc. of Mid. Temple; med. offr. of health and supt. fever hosp., Lowestoft, 1893; med. offr. of health and supt. of statistics, Hong Kong, 1895; Dean and lect. on forens. med., Hong Kong coll. of med.; mem. sanitary bd., 1895; president, 1905; J.P. 1896; mem. leg. coun., 1902; mem. exec. coun., 1905; P.C.M.O., 1905-6.

CLARK, GEORGE HARVEY.—B. 1873; C.M.G.; Brixton gram. schl., London, and Potsdam schl., Jamaica; clk., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, May, 1890, to July, 1891 (resigned); admitted sol. sup. ct., Jamaica, 17th Sept., 1897; clk. of ct. St. Mary, Jamaica, 2nd Nov., 1903; super. reg. mag., Dec., 1903, to May, 1904; ag. res. mag. St. Mary, 1st Aug. to 29th Nov., 1904; ag. mag., Clarendon, Aug. to Sept., 1905; ag. solr., sup. ct., England, 12th Aug., 1905; ag. mag., St. Mary, June, 1906; and July, 1906; Aug., 1907, and from 12th to 16th Nov., 1907.
CLARK, JOHN PERCIVAL.—Ed. at Alg. gram. schl.; works and inspection under E. Board of Agriculture; survr., E. Africa Prov., July, 1904.
CLARK, THOMAS.—B. 1884; apptd. compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of ct., and assigned to C.O., Apl., 1902.
CLARK, SIR WM. MONTMOR, KT. BACH., K.C.—Called to the bar, Ontario, 1861; 1887; senator of Toronto univ.; chrmn. of management of Knox Coll., Toronto, 1880; gov., Ontario, Apl., 1903.

CLARKE, SIR CHAS. MANSFIELD, Bart., G.C.M.G. (1901); G.C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1839; entd. 1856; served in N. Zeeland, 1861-66; Zulu war (promoted brevet-col. C.B. and clasp), 1879; Basutoland, 1880-81; com. gen. of col. forces at Cape of Good Hope, 1880-82; A.A.G., War Office, 1884; D.A.G., land, 1886-88; maj.-gen. commanding 3rd div. brig., Aldershot, 1889-92; D.A.G. at headqu., 1892-93; com.-in-chief and lieut.-gen. command. the Forces, Madras, 1893-98; quartermaster to the Forces, 1899; gov., Malta, 1903-1905.
CLARKE, ERNEST D.—Dep.-registrar, Trinidad.

CLARKE, SIR FIELDING, KT. BACH., LL.B.—B. 1851; called to the bar, Mid. Easter, 1876; N.E. circuit; atty.-gen. of F.G. ag. ch. just. and ch. judicial commr. for the Pacific, 1882 to 1883, and again 1884; commr., 1885; puisne judge, Hong Kong, 1888; ch. just., 1891; ch. just., Jamaica, 1896.

CLARKE, GEORGE HERBERT.—B. 1874; teacher of bd. schl., Calabash Bay, Andros, Bahamas, Feb., 1897, to Dec., 1902; asst. res. Dec., 1902; ag. asst. res. just., coroner, vaccinator for Berry Is., revising offr., res. births, deaths, and marriages, 1907.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. Sir GEORGE S., G.C.M.G. (1905), G.C.I.E. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1848; entered R. 1868; capt., 1880; major, 1887; served in R. 1882 (medal and star); in Suakin expd., mentioned in despatches (clasp); sec. col. decomttee., 1885-92; supt. of carriage in Woolwich, 1893; gov. Victoria, 1901-1904; as woon of a comte. to advise as to the crea. a Board for the administrative business of War Office, Nov., 1903; sec. to Imperial De- comttee., 1904; gov. of Bombay, 1907.

CLARKE, Lieut.-Col. Sir MARSHAL H., K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1880), (late R. B. 1841; res. mag., co. Pietermaritzburg, 1874; A.D.C. to Sir T. Shepstone, H.M. commr., S. Africa, 1876; polit. offr. and commr., Lydenburg, 1877; served in Transvaal campaign 1880-1, in command of Landdrost Potchefstroom; mentioned in despatches; res.

CLAYTON, REGINALD JOHN BYARD.—B. 1875; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1898; attached to land off., Batang Padang, July, 1899; in charge Kuala Kangsar treasy., Dec., 1899; passed final law exam., Feb., 1900; ag. registr., Taiping, Apl., 1900; ag. mag., Larut, May, 1900; ag. collr., land rev., Matang, Aug., 1900; in charge of Matang dist., Sept., 1900; passed final language exam., Nov., 1900; ag. collr. of land rev., Matang, Jan., 1901; ditto, Batang Padang, Feb., 1901; 1st cls. mag., Perak, Dec., 1901; asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Feb., 1904; ditto, Kinta, June, 1904; and dep. registr. of titles, Perak South, till June, 1905; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1906.
CLEARE, W. T.—Resident just. and collr. of cust., Bimini, Bahamas, Nov., 1899; ditto, Long Island, Mar., 1901; ditto, Green Turtle Cay., Abaco, 1906.

CLELAND, WILLIAM LENNOX, M.B., A.M., Edin. (1876).—B. 1847; Asst. med. offr., Derby county asyl., Eng., 1877; res. med. offr., Parkside lun. asyl., S. Australia, and asst. col. surg., 1878; res. med. offr., Adelaide and Parkside lun. asyls., and col. surg., S. Australia, 1896; ceased to be res. med. offr., Adelaide Lun. Asyl., which was closed in 1903.

CLEMENTI, C.—Ed. St. Paul's schl. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (demy, 1894; B.A., 1898; M.A., 1901), hon. mention Hertford schl., 1895; 1st cl. mods., 1896; hon. mention Ireland and Craven schl., 1896; Boden Sanskrit scholar, 1897; prox. acc. Gaisford (Greek Prose), 1897; 2nd cl. lit. hum., 1898; prox. acc. Chancellor's Latin essay, 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; passed cadet, 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., and ag. elk. of councils, Dec., 1900, to Oct., 1901; asst. registr.-gen., Aug., 1901; mem. bd. of examrs. in Chinese, Apr., 1902; seconded for spec. serv. under govt. of India, May, 1902; J.P., June, 1902; ag. asst. col. sec., and elk. of councils, Sept., 1902, to June, 1903; sec. interpretation bd., Dec., 1902; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, April to June, 1903; mem. of land etc., New Territories, Dec., 1903; asst. land offr. and pol. mag., New Territories, May, 1905, to Sept., 1906; asst. col. sec. and elk. of couns., Oct., 1907.

CLEVELAND, ROBT. ACHILLES.—B. 1864; ed. Univ. Coll. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (England); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.T.M.H. (Camb.); med. offr., Cayon dist., St. Kitts, 1890; ditto, Georgetown, Saint Vincent, 1892; dist. med. offr., Laraoca, Cyprus, 1894; ditto, Nicosia, 1895; ag. ch. med. offr., Cyprus, 1895 and 1900; mem. of municipal couns., Nicosia; passed colloquial standard exam. in mod. Greek; ag. ch med. offr., 1906.

CLIFFORD, HUGH CHARLES, C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1866; joined Perak service, 1883; passed in Malay, Feb., 1885; collr. land rev., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1885; on special service to Pahang, 1887; ag. gov.'s agt. there, 1887-8; supt., Clu Pahang, 1889; ag. Br. res. Pahang, Sept., 1890, to Mar., 1891, Apr. to Dec., 1891, and from Feb., 1893; took a leading part in suppression of Pahang rebellion, 1892; sec. to gov. Selangor, Dec., 1894, but continued to act res. of Pahang; led armed exped. into n. states of Trunggānu and Kēlāntan to capture of fugitive rebel leaders, Mar.-June, 1895; res., Pahang, July, 1896; gov. of Br. North Borneo, 1896-97. and Labuan, 1899 to 1901; Br. res., Pahang, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad and Tobago, Sept., 1903, confirmed, 14th Sept., 1904; admstd. govt., 26th Mar. to 30th Aug., 1904, and from 1st Apr. to 23rd Oct., 1906; col. sec., Ceylon, 3rd May, 1907.

CLIFT, HON. J. AUGUSTUS, K.C.—Called to the Bar, 1884; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1889; speaker of assembly, 1891; clk. of assembly, 1893-97; elected to assembly, representing Twillingate dist., 1900; re-elected, 1904; min. of agric. and mines and mem. of cabinet, 1904.

CLIFTON, ROBERT CECIL, I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1854; landing waiter and postmtr., Bunbury, W. Australia, 1873; 2nd clk., lands and survey dept., 1874; 2nd clk., customs, 1876; ch. clk., land and survey dept., Apr., 1880; under sec. for lands, 1891.

CLINCKETT, ROBERT JAMES.—Entered provost marshal's office, Barbados, as jun. clk. in 1869; acctnt. in 1870; acted as provost marshal from Feb. to Sept., 1879; again in 1880; resig. 1880; apptd. official assignee, 1st Oct., 1882; served as mem. of health and quarantine bds., from 10th Oct., 1899, to 6th May, 1901; acted as mast. in chancery, from Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; mem. of educn. bd.; gov. of "Harrison" and "Queen" Cols. and Combermere schl.; M.L.C., from 10th Oct., 1899, to 24th Mar., 1903.
CLUTSAM, S. H. O.—Called to bar, Bahamas, July, 1868; ag. sec. to bd. of educn., 1882; ag. stip. and circuit mag. in 1887; asst. clk. and sergt.-at-arms, house of assem., 1866; ch. clk., 1890.

COATES, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1856; clk., immigrn. dept., Fiji, 1889; inspr. ind. immigrn.; inspr. nat. lab., 1892; stip. mag., Savu Savu and Tavuni, 1900; provl. inspr., Macuata and Bau, 1901; agt.-gen., immigrn., 1904.

COBHAM, CLAUDE DELAYAL, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1842; ed. Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxon; B.A. (honours), 1866; B.C.L., M.A., 1869; late M.R.A.S. ass't consnr., Larnaca, Cyprus, Oct. 1, 1878; consnr. of Larnaca, Mar. 18, 1879; ag. ch. sec. to govt., on three occasions; ag. ch. collr. of customs, 1886; has edited a Bibliography of Cyprus, and "Excerpta Cypria," and translated Mariti's "Travels in Cyprus," the story of "Omm Haram," and Graziani's narrative of the siege of Nicosia and Famagusta.

COBLEY, WALTER HENRY, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1850; A.M.I.C.E.; Asst. engrn., Natal rly., Feb., 1876; ag. maintenance engrn., Jan., 1880, to 1881; maintenance engrn., 1882; dist. engrn., 1883; suptg. engrn. of surveys and construction, 1897.

COCHRANE, C. S., M.I.C.E.—Grad. at Trin. Coll., Dub., 1868; was employed on rly. wks. in S. America, 1871-74; asst. engrn. on the Cape Govt. rly., 1874; ag. dist. engrn., 1877; engrn. in the pub. wks. dept., Trinidad, 1878; engrn. in charge of surveys, 1879.

COCHRANE, HON. THOMAS, H.A.E., M.P.—Ed. at Eton; served in 33rd Highlanders and Scots Guards; hon. major, 4th Batt., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; J.P.; Fife; D.L. for Renfrewshire; M.P. for North Ayrshire; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, June, 1895, to Feb., 1901; on active service in the S. African War, 1900; U.S. of S., Home Office, 1902.

COCKBURN, THE HON. SIR JOHN ALEX., K.C.M.G. (1900); M.D. (Lond.).—B. 1840; mem. of house of assem., S. Australia, 1884-1898; min. of educn., 1885, to June, 1887; premier and cl. sec., 1889-90; again min. of educn. and agric., 1893; agt.-gen. for S. Australia in London, 1898-1901.

CODRINGTON, STEWART.—B. 1874; lec. S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; ag. sub-inspr. of sc. Malacca, May, 1899; ag. supt. Malay coll., May, 1900; ag. distr. offr., Malacca, Oct., 1900; dis. offr., Penang, Aug., 1902; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, June, 1907.

COGHLAN, T. A., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1856; agt.-gen. for N. S. Wales, Feb., 1905; ed. Sydney grammar schl.; apptd. to pub. wks. dept., 1873; assoc. mem. Inst. of Civil Engrs., 1882; asst. engrn. harbour and rivers dept., 1884; govt. statistician, 1886; registr. of friendly societies, 1892; honry. fellow roy. stat. soc., 1893; mem. of pub. ser. bd., 1896; justice of the peace, 1896; roy. commr. to inquire into the working of the marine bd., 1896; roy. commr. to inquire into working of Sydney water supply and sewerage bd., 1897; supervis. N. S. Wales census of 1891; pres. of the cd. of Australian statisticians to determine the population of the colonies, and to arrange for uniform census of Australasia, 1900; auth. "Wealth and Progress of N. S. Wales," "Seven Colonies of Australasia," "Children Study in Statistics," "Notes on the First Aspect of Australian Federation," "Report on the Eleventh Census of N. S. Wales," "Trends on the Deaths of Women in Childbirth," "Statistics N. S. Wales," "Child Measurement," "Discharge of Streams in Relation to Rain," "Australia and New Zealand" (with Hon. Ewing), "Progress of Australia in the Century," &c.
COHEN, HON. H. E.—Col. treas., N. S.W., Dec., 1877, to Dec., 1878; judge, metro. ct., May, 1881; min. of just., Jan., 1885; puisne judge, 1896.
COHEN, W. S.—Ed. at Clifton Col. Trin. Coll., Camb.; 2nd class tripes, 1893; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; sec., (temporary), O.R.C., 1902.

COLE, FRED. EDWARD.—Ed. at Marlbro. and King's Coll., Lond.; called to the bar, Tem., Feb., 1886; dist. consnr., G. Coas., May, 1888; clk. of cts., St. Elizabeth, Jan., Nov., 1890; ag. res. mag., Apr., 1891; app. mag., St. Thomas, Jamaica, 1902.

COLE, GEORGE.—B. 1840; entd. R. c. civ. ser. as public schoolmaster, Oct., 1890; to bd. of educ. and inspr. of schls., Sept., 1892; mem. of bd. of examiners for the civil colony, April, 1888; mem. of advisory on col. lights, Mar., 1900.

COLENBRANDER, HERMAN JAMES.—B. clk. for immigrn. purposes, Zulu border and Lower Tugela div., Natal, 3rd Nov., 1899; 3rd clk., and Zulu and Dutch interpreters, Umsinga, 26th Jan., 1887; clk. of mag., Umvoti, Aug., 1889; Dundee, July, 1889; River May, 1895; ch. clk. to mag., Durban, 1901; asst. mag., Durban, July, 1902; ag. Umlazi div. (Pinetown), Mar., 1905; has been ag. mag. on several occasions at Dundee, W. Ladysmith, Ixopo, Umgeni, Greytown, Newcastle and Alexandra divs.; mag. for colony, 1902; J.P. for the colony, 1904; through siege of Ladysmith.

COLES, THE HON. SIR JENKIN, K.C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1842; Mem. of house of assem., Australia, 1875-8, and since 1881; const. lds., June, 1884, to Feb., 1885; June, 1885, June, 1889; consnr. P.W., Feb. to June, 1889; speaker house of assem., S. Australia, 1890.
COLL, ANTHONY MICHAEL.—Ed. Haileybury and Exeter Coll., Oxon; M.A. and B.C.L.,...

COLLENS, JAMES HENRY, V.D. (1902).—Principal, govt. training school, Port of Spain, Trinidad, 1878, and govt. statistician, 1900; lieutenant, Trinidad volunteers, 1880; captain, 1885; major, 1902; elected on first bd. of directors, civil service widows' and orphan's fund, 1890; author of "Guide to Trinidad," and other works; ag. supt., govt. printing dept., Dec., 1891; ag. postmaster-gen., 1893, 1895, 1897, 1898; ag. insp. of schls., 1899, 1900, 1901, 1902; insp. of schls., Aug., 1903; commanded the Trinidad lt. inf. contingent at His Majesty's coronation, 1902.

COLLENS, COL. ROBERT JOSEPH, V.D. (1902).—Sub-storekeeper, pub. wks. dept., New Zealand, 1863-77; store audit, 1877-8; clk. in treas., 1878-9; 1st clk., treas., 1879-85; clk.-in-charge, 1885-90; acct. to treas., 1890-1906; asst. sec., 1903-6; sec. to treas., paymaster-gen., and recvr.-gen. since 1906; also finance mem., N.Z. coun. of defence since 1906; J.P., 1904; has served in N.Z. volunteers since 1865; colonel, 1907; lieut.-col., N.Z. militia, for services rendered in connection with enrolment of S.Africa contingent, 1902; exec. offr., N.Z. rifle assoc., since 1879; stat. offr., ditto, 1886-1903; ch. exec. offr., 1904; commdt., N.Z. Bisley rifle team (winners of Kolapore cup), 1904.

COLLET, WILFRED, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1856; ed. at Univ. Coll. schl., Lond.; LL.B., Lond., 1889; 1st cls. intermed., 1889, and final, 1903; provisional 2nd clk., native dept., Fiji, Jan., 1881; insp. of imgrts. and asst. native comsrs., Jan., 1883; sec. W. Pacific high comsrs., Oct., 1884; dist. comsrs., Cyprus, 1897; also British deleg. of Evqaf., 1901; ag. rec.-gen., 1900-01, and in 1902 and 1904; col. sec., Br. Hond., 18th Oct., 1905; admstg. govt., 18th Oct., 1905, to 12th Aug., 1906.
COLLIE, ALEXANDER.—Clk., accts. branch, repatriation dept., O.R.C., 14th July, 1902; examr. of accts., audit dept., 1st Dec., 1902; dist. examr. of accts., 1st July, 1905.

COLLIE, James.—Apptd. clk. in financial adviser's office, O.R.C., 6th June, 1900; examr. of accts., audit dept., 1st July, 1901; ch. clk. and acctnt., treas., 1st July, 1902; ag. asst. col. treas., Aug., 1905, to Feb., 1906, and May to Nov., 1906; asst. col. treas., 1st July, 1907.

COLLIER, FREDERICK WILLIAM, I.S.O. (1907).—Third clk., ext. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Nov., 1869; 2nd clk., Dec., 1870; record clk., Jan., 1877; ch. clk. and sec. to the quarantine bld., Dec., 1879; has acted as asst. govt. sec., and clk. to the ct. of policy and the combined ct. on several occasions since July, 1880; postmr.-gen., 1888.

COLLINS, A. E.—B. 1871; ed. at the City of Lond. schl. and Camb.; L. Rothschild scholar and foundation scholar of Trin. Coll., 1890; prizeman, 1891-2; 1st class classical tripus, 1893; B.A., 1893; M.A., 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., a clk. in office of S. of S. for Cola., June, 1894; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Edward) Wingfield, Mar., 1897; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Feb., 1898; 1st class clk., Nov., 1898; sec. of S.S. currency countee., 1902-3; prin. clk., May, 1907.

COLLINS, HON. GEORGE THOMAS.—B. 1839; called to the bar, 1861; M.L.C. for Tamar, Tasmania, since 1895; chief sec., min. of defence (until federation) and min. of agric., 1899-1903; pres. of cent. bd. of health, 1900-3; col. and late condg. offr. of Launceston Artv., V.D.

COLLINS, JAMES ALLISON.—B. 1835; served in 1876 and 1877 as priv. sec. to State prea. of O.F.S. and as clk. to treas.-gen.; after resigning rejoined serv. as asst. registr., high cts., O.F.S., 21st June, 1879; representative to orphan master, 9th Feb., 1885; landroost, Ladybrand, 19th Nov., 1887; ditto, Winburg, 9th Mar., 1894; under govt. sec., 23rd July, 1897; res. mag., Bloem., O.R.C., 17th Mar., 1900; asst. sec. to O.R.C. adminstr. (afterwards asst. col. sec.), 1st Sept., 1901; master of the high ct., 1st Mar., 1903.

COLLINS, JOSEPH THOMAS, M.A., LL.M.—Scholar and medallist of Trin. Coll., Melbourne; exhibitioner, scholar and honourman in arts and laws, Melbourne Univ.; barrister-at-law, Victoria, 1894; legal adviser to Roy. Comsln. on Local Govt. Law, 1902; mem. and subsequently chmn. of Roy. Comsln. on Unification or Federation of Municipalities in Victoria, 1903-5; asst. parly. draughtsman, Victoria, since 1906; author, in conjunction with Mr. C. H. Meaden, of "Local Government Law and Practice."

COLLINS, ROBT. MUIRHEAD, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1852; retired lieut. R.N.; entered R.N., 1866; served in flying squadrons in 1868 and 1872; sub-lieut., June, 1872; served on Channel and Australian stations to 1876; lieut., June, 1876; apptd. to Victorian naval forces (Australia), Dec., 1878; comdr. Victorian naval forces, 1884; sec. for defence for Victoria, 1888; sec. to dept. of defence, Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Mar., 1901; now in England representing Commonwealth govt.
COLLISSON, P. L.—Ed. at King's Coll., London; 2nd div. clk., E. and A. dept., 1901; examr., aud. branch, Oct., 1905; examr. of accts., audit dept., O.R.C., Jan., 1906.
COLLS, ARTHUR STANLEY.—Ed. Rugby; asst. engineer, Midland Rwy., 1883 to 1886; dist. engnr., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886; dist. engnr., Pretoria dist., Transvaal, 1902; ch. engnr. of buildings, 1905.
COLLYER, ARTHUR JOHN MONCE.—Ed. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 24th August, 1902; dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1907.

COLLYMORE, H. W.—Supernum. G.P.O., Barbados, Oct., 1876; ag. 3rd clk., 1878; 3rd clk., record branch, col. sec.'s office, Mar., 1879; 3rd clk., correspondence branch, Jan., 1881; ag. 2nd clk., 1883; ag. pub. libr., 1884; 2nd clk. correspondence branch, col. sec.'s office, July, 1884; ag. ch. clk., June, 1893, to Apr., 1894; Aug. to Dec., 1893, and July to Nov., 1896; sec. highways commsn., 1893-6; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. of legis. coun., Jan., 1898.

COLLYNS, JOHN MOORE, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (1900); M.B., Lnd., 1904; D.P.H., Lond., 1st—Ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, Devon. St. Bartholomew's Hosp.; certif. of Schl. Trop. Med., Lond., 1905; civil surg., S. Afr. Campaign; Queen's Medal with five clasmed. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 6th July, 1906.
COLMAN, Eugene Ernest.—B.A. Cantab.; cadet, S. Sttlts., Oct., 1902; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Oct., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1904; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., Mar., 1906.

COLMER, Joseph G., C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1856; priv. sec. to first high commr. for Canada, and sec. of emigr. dept. of Canadian govt. offices, Lond., 1880; apptd. sec. to office of high commr., 1881; acted as interim sec. to colonization bd. since its formation, Dec., 1888, and formally apptd. as such Nov., 1891; res., Mar., 1903.

COLOMBO, Sir John C. R., M.P., K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1838; was M.P. for Bow and Bromley, 1886 to 1892; formerly capt., R.M.A., in which corps he served 1864-69; has been variously employed with naval, military, and auxiliary forces; author of many works and lectures on imperial defence and protection of commerce in war; received the thanks of gov. of Br. Columbia, 1877.
COLOMBO, 4th Bishop of, Right Rev. Reginald Stephen Copleston, D.D. Formerly fellow and tutor, St. John's Coll., Oxford. Consec. Dec., 1875.

COLUMBIA, BRITISH, 2nd Bishop, Right Rev. William Willcox Perrin.—Trin. Coll., Oxon, B.A., 1870; M.A., 1873; D.D., 1893; Fellow, King's Coll., London, 1900; curate, St. Mary's, Southampton, 1871 to 1881; vicar of S. Luke's, Southampton, 1881-93. Consec. 1893.
COLVILLE, J. W.—Clerk, lands dept., Victoria, apptd. 1863; head of registry of that dept., Jan., 1878; cent. bd. of health, May, 1885.

COMBE, Ralph Molynex.—Ed. at Haileybury Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1897; Crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 4th Aug., 1905; currency commr., 1st Apr., 1906; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1907.

COMMISSIONG, Tyrell Mildmay.—Ed. at S.E. College, Ramsgate; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1895; joined Grenada bar, Oct., 1895; ag. pol. mag. on several occasions, 1898 to 1905; pol. mag., W. Dist., 1st Aug., 1905.
COMMISSIONG, W. S., K.C.—Admitted to the bar, Grenada, 1867; escheator-gen., July, 1872; has acted on various occasions as atty.-gen. and as ch. just.; is an unofficial member of legis. coun.

CONDELLA, Claude F., B.es. L., F.R.G.S.—Ed. Edinburgh Acad., Stonyhurst Coll. and Paris Univ.; honours, London Univ., matric.; certificat d'aptitude à l'enseignement des langues vivantes, Paris; cert. of regisn., column B, teachers' registn. coun.; lecturer on mod. languages and English literature, Paris, 1885; ed. dept. Malta, 1900; examr. in French and German, Malta R.A. exam.; mem. of bd. of examrs. for Malta civ. ser.; inspr. of schls., St. Lucia, 1905; govt. examr. of secondary schls., Grenada, 1905; ag. M.L.C., St. Lucia, 1906; govt. deleg. to agric. conf., Jamaica, 1907.
CONRAN, Joseph George.—Emigration agent at Madras, for Mauritius, 1st Jan., 1899.
CONROY, J. G., K.C.—Cent. dist. ct. judge, Newfoundland, 1880.
COOK, Alexr.—Auditor Br. N. Borneo, Oct., 1880; treas. and aud.-gen., Aug., 1881; mem. of coun., June, 1883.
COOK, Henry Austin.—Clerk of wks., P.W.D., O.R.C., June, 1903.

COOK, Wallace Haynes.—Clerk, Penal Settlement, Massaruni, Br. Guiana, Feb., 1881; steward, July, 1883; ag. sec. to poor law commrs. and supt. of girls' reformatory, Apr., 1890, to Jan., 1891, and May, 1895, to Feb., 1896; clk. of supplies prison dept. and sec. to bd. of prisons, Nov., 1890; supt. almshouse, Georgetown, June, 1892; amalgamated offices of supt. almshouse, and sec. to poor law commrs., May, 1899.

COOKE, John Hamilton, B.A., M.B., B.Ch.—Trin. Coll., Dub., honours in classics, 1882-3; in anatomy, 1885-6; sen. house surg. Western gen. dispensary, Marylebone, Lon., 1890-1; med. offr. dist. C., Dominica, 1891; dist. St. Kitts and Nevis, 1892; Yaws hosp., 1892-7; mem. bd. of health, 1893; surg.-cap. of local forces, 1898; dist. med. offr. Antigua, Jan., 1905.
COOKE, William Ernest, M.A., F.R.A.S.—Cadet, Adelaide observatory, 1878; B.A. Adel. Univ., 1883; ast. astronomer, Adel. observ., 1894; M.A., Adel. Univ., 1889; govt. astron., W.A., 1896.
COOM, John, M.I.C.E.—Engnr., N. Zealand. P.W.D., 1876 to 1881; res. engnr. working rly., 1881 to 1897; inspecting engnr. for rlys., 1897 to 1899; ch. engnr., 1899.
COOMBE, Ephraim Henry.—Mem. House of Assembly, S.Aust., 1901; chmn. of comtees, 1900.

COOMBS, W. H., F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S.—Comdr. Roy. Navy; assoc. inst. C.E.; entered R.N. 1866; served on the Mediterranean, N. Amer., W. Indies, China, and other stations; lieut., 1875; served under Indian govt., 1876 to 1884, as ast. sup't marine surveys, chiefly on harbours, Madras, Colombo, Chittagong, etc.; passed as interp. in Hindi, Urdu, and Persian, 1877-8; ret. as cd. R.N. 1894; apptd. by S. of S. for Cols. prot. imgrts., Trinidad, Feb., 1896; M.L.C. and J.J. for the isld.
COOPER, Archibald Samuel.—Ed. at Marlborough Coll.; ast. ch. acctnt., Uganda rly., 15th Mar., 1897.

COOPER, Francis Alfred, C.M.G. (1901), M.I.C.E.—B. 1860; fellow Sanitary Inst., Gt. Britain; ed. at Rossall and Loughborough gram schl., ast. to Mr. James Mansergh, C.E., Westminster, 1879; sanit. survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1887 to 1890; survr. to sanitary bd., 1888 to 1890; inspr. of buildings, P.W.D., 1888 to 1890; ag. ast. engnr., 1889; ag. survr.-gen., 1889; ag. ass't survr.-gen., 1889 to 1890; res. engnr. in charge of water and drainage dept., 1890 to 1892; dir. of pub. wis., Oct., 1891; mem. exec. and legis. couns., Mar., 1892; dir. pub. wks., Ceylon, 1897; pres. of sanitary bd., Hong Kong, 1895-7; rec'd. thanks of govt. for services in settlement of claims under the Taipingsham resumption ordn., 1895; and from S. of S. for Cols. for services during the plague, 1896; M.L.C.; commr. of rds., waterwks., engnr. and mem. of harb. bd. and mun. coun., Colonie, 1897.
COOPER, Sir Pope Alexander, Kt. (1904)—Atty.-gen., Queensland, Dec., 1880; northern judge, 5th Jan., 1883; 1st puisne judge, sup. cd. ch. justice, 1903.

CORBETT, Frederick H. M.—Hon. cor. phil. soc., etc.; priv. sec. to Mr. Justice Lawnt at various periods from 1885 to 1888; librnr. Colonists museum, June, 1886; hon. sec. to gen. comtees, Ceylon, for Paris Exposn., 1889; ag. sec., cent. irrigation bd., June, 1890, to May, 1891; resp. librship., Dec., 1893; has represented col. at Imp. Inst. as hon. exec. offr., since July, 1893; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Jan., 1897; rec'd. jub. gold med. of Ceylon, 1897.
CORBETT, Nigel Edward Frank.—Ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., 19th Apr., 1900; collr., 1st Apr., 1906.

DEAUX, CAPT. HARRY EDWARD SPILLER, C.M.G. (1904).—Ed. at Cheltenham, 1889; class tripos, 1892; passed in Hindu and Persian, 1895; Somali, 1899; B.A.; entd. Indian Army, 1894; probationer, ombay polit. dept. as 4th ast. polit. res., Aden, Apl., 1898; ast. res., Berbera, July, 1898; vice-consul, Berbera, Oct., 1898; consul, Nov., 1902; admstd. govt. of Somaliland Prot., Nov., 1900, to Mar., 1902; Oct., 1902, to Aug., 1903; Mar. to June, 1904; served in operations against the Somali Mullah, 1902-04, as polit. offr. (ment. in despa. medal and 3 clasp); dep. commr., Somaliland Prot., 1904; admstd. govt., June, 1905; commr. and comdr.-in-chief, 15th May, 1906.

CORK, PHILIP CLARKE, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1854; clk. imgrn. dept., Jamaica, May, 1875; Hindust. interpreter to the agt.-gen., 1876; inspr. of imgrts., 1877; prot. of imgrts., Grenada, 1881; ditto, Jamaica, 1884; comsrr. under the Kingston Improv't. Laws, 1894; nomtd. mem. of legis. coun., 1896; ast. col. sec., 1897; col. sec., Br. Hond., 1901; admstd. the govt. in 1903 and 1905; admstd., St. Lucia, Oct., 1906.

CORK, WM.—Clk. and locoer, Jamaica, Aug., 1868; 3rd class ast. collr. of taxes, Jan., 1869; 3rd class collr. of taxes and parochial treas., 1870; 2nd class collr. of taxes, 1879; 1st class collr. of taxes, Apr., 1895; supervisor of rev. offices, Dec., 1903.

CORNEY, BOLTON GLANVILL, I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1851; ed. at Fontainebleau, in London, and at Schwerin; St. Thomas's Hosp., 1870; M.R.C.S., Eng., 1874; entered the col. service in 1877 as govt. med. offr., Fiji; health offr., Suva, 1877; med. offr. for the imgrn. dept., 1881; agt. ch. med. offr., 1882-3; agt. agt.-gen. of imgrn., Jan., 1886, to Mar., 1887; M.L.C. and of native regulative bd., 1885; ch. med. offr., June, 1887; M.L.C., June, 1888, to present time; ag. sup. lum. asyl., 1887-1903; ag. rec. gen., Jan. to Mar., 1892; comsrr. to inquire into decrease of native popula., 1893; comsrr. to inquire into misgov't. of Prov. Tallevu, May, 1896; temp. mem. exec. coun., 1892 and 1895; comsrr. of cust., 1895; in 1897 rec. thanks of coral comte. of roy. soc. for assistance to the geol. expdn. to Funa-futi, Ellice Isds.; then from the roy. soc. for renewed services to expdn., 1898; agt. agt.-gen. for imgrn., Dec., 1902, to Mar., 1903.

CORNWALL, C.F.—Graduated B.A. Magdalen Coll., Camb., 1857; called to bar of Inner Tem., 1861; many years mem. of legislature of col. of Br. Columbia; on confederation of that prov. with Canada in 1871, summoned by H.M. to the Senate of the Dominon; lieut.-gov. of Br. Columbia, 1881-6.
CORNWELL, G. F.—Called to the bar (Linc.'s Inn), 1859; one of H.M.'s counsel for Gibr., 1885; coroner and J.P., 1895; inspr. of schools, 1902.

CORYNDON, ROBT. THORNE.—B. 1870; joined Bechuanaland Border Police, under B.S.A. Co., 9th Nov., 1889, and the Pioneer force for the occupation of Mashonaland, June, 1890; priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. C. J. Rhodes, 1896-7, and during parly. inquiry into Jameson Raid; served in Matabele War, 1893, and Matabele Rebellion, 1896 (medal and clasp); Brit. res. with Lewanika, and B.S.A. Co.'s representative in Barotseeland, June, 1897; took expdn. to Lealui, Upper Zambesi River, 1897; admstd., N.W. Rhodesia, Sept., 1900; dep. res. comsrr., Swaziland, 15th April, 1907.
COSTIGAN, THE HON. JOHN.—Mem. of Canadian house of commons and of privy coun.; min. of inland rev., 1885; sec. of state for Canada, Dec., 1892; min. of marine and fisheries, 1894 to 1896.

COTTLE, HENRY CHAS.—B. 1857; 2nd ast. govt. printer, Ceylon, Nov., 1885; ast. govt. printer, 1888; ag. govt. printer on several occasions, 1890-1904; ag. contr. of govt. stores, 1904 and 1906; govt. printer, 1906.

COULL, WM.—B. 1857; enrolled law student, Leeward Is., 1875; clk. col. sec.'s office, Leeward Is., Mar., 1878; acted on several occasions as ch. clk. and as clk. of the Antigua and federal exec. and legis. couns.; dep. coroner for dist. A, Oct., 1880; ag. mag., dist. B, Antigua, Jan. to Apl., 1882; mag. and coroner for dist. E, Roseau, and regis.-gen. of births, marriages, and deaths, Dominica, Aug., 1882; mem. of the bd. of poor law guardians; a visiting justice of the gaol, and nominated M.L.A., 1883; escheator-gen., 1885; mem. exec. coun., 1895; offl. mem. legis. coun., 1895; in charge of out-door poor relief, 1898; chmn. of quarantine bd. under new (Convention) Act, Jan., 1906.
COULTER, ROBERT MILLAR, C.M.G. (1907), M.D.—Deputy of postmr.-gen. of Canada, Aug., 1897.
COUNSEL, E. A.—Secretary for lands and surveyor-gen., Tasmania, 1889.
COUPER, SYDNEY.—Ed. at Kelvinside Acad. and Univ. Coll., Victoria Univ., Liverpool; served with L. & N.W. rly.; sen. ast. engr., Uganda rly.; dist. engnr., Uganda rly.

COURTNEY, JOHN MORTIMER, C.M.G. (1897), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1838; ast. sec. and accontnt. to the treasy., Canada, 1869; dep. min. of finance, dep. rec.-gen., and sec. to treasy., 1878; mem. of comsrr. of inquiry into the civ. ser. of Canada, 1891; ret., 1st Mar., 1906.

COURTNEY OF PENWITH, 1ST BARON (created 1906), RT. HON. LEONARD H. COURTNEY, P.C. (1889).—B. 1832; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. (2nd wrangler) 1855; called to the bar, Linc.'s Inn, 1858; repented Liskeard, Dec., 1876, to Dec., 1885, when he was returned for the co. div. in which that borough was merged until 1900; parly. undersec. for the home dept., Dec., 1880; parly. undersec. for the cols., Aug., 1881; sec. to the treasy., May, 1882; resig., 1885; chmn. of ways and means, 1886-92.
COUZENS, CHARLES.—B. 1861; Apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess. C.O., Jan., 1889; promoted 2nd cl. mess., Nov., 1899; King's mess., 1st Feb., 1902.

COVEY, ARTHUR.—B. 1870; solr., 1892; dist. comsrr., G. Coast, 1900; mentioned in Ashanti despatches and received medal and war gratuity; ast. col. sec., 1902; invalided, 1903; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1904; with Anglo-French bndry. comsrr., Sokoto Prov., 1906-07.

COWAN, JAMES.—B. 1848; Clk. to res. York, W. Australia, 1864; postmr. and c' pol. mag., Perth, 1870; clk. to legis. coun. regis. and master sup. ct., &c., Nov., 1877; curator of intestate estates, pol. mag. a mag., Guildford; mag. of debts ct. and Perth, 1890.

COWELL, H. RUSSELL, B.A.—B. 1877; ed. Malvern and Cambridge; scholar of Clare, 1896-9; apptd., after compet. exam., elk., sec.'s dept., inland revenue, Oct., 1901; 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 27th Mar., 1902; sec. to inter-deptmtl. comttee. on oil fields of W. Indies, 1904.

COWPER, SYDNEY, C.M.G. (1901)—B. 1854; elk. in office of comsrs. exhibn. of 1851, in 1871, in educn. dept. S. Kensington, 1875; 3rd class elk. in col. sec.'s office, Capetown, 1879; priv. sec. to premier, 1880-1; 2nd class elk., 1881; priv. sec. to premier, 1884-5; 1st class elk., 1885; sec. to prime min., 1887; ret. on abolition of office, 1891; priv. elk., dept. of agricult., 1892; sec. to premier (office re-established), 1897; ret., 1904; J.P. for Cape Colony; civ. ser. examiner (1899-1901), and art trustee; Basuto med. (1880); commdg. 4th batt. Cape Peninsula regt. (1901); major, P.A.O. Cape Field Artillery, 1903.

COX, CHARLES T., C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1858; Govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, July, 1874; sen. elk., 1883; ch. elk. and sec. to the quarantine bd., 1888; sec. to the royal comsn. on admnstr.-gen.'s dept. of B. Guiana, 1882; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1899; compiled "Index to Laws of Br. Guiana," 1887 (2nd edition, 1891), also "Some Notes for Govt. Officers," 1894; asst. govt. sec., and elk. to ct. of policy and combined ct., 1889; also elk. of exec. coun., 1892; ag. govt. sec., in 1893, 1894, 1895, and 1898, with seat in exec. coun. and legislature; chmn. of comttee. on pensions, 1898; ag. audr.-gen. on several occasions; admnstr. of St. Kitts and Nevis, 1899-1904; ag. gov., Leeward Is., July to Oct., 1903, and May to Sept., 1904; govt. sec. and ag. gov. B. Guiana, Sept., 1904, and Apr. to Oct., 1906.

COX, E. A. W.—Cadet, Sarawak civ. ser., Mar., 1885; asst. res., May, 1891; of Lundr., June, 1896; res., 2nd class, Mar., 1898, of Trusan; again May, 1899; in charge of Lawas, 16th Apl., 1905, in addition to Trusan.

COX, HUGH BERTRAM, C.B. (1902).—B. 1861; ed. at Westminster schl. and Chr. Ch., Oxford; 1st class classical mods., 1881; 1st class final class. schl., 1883; M.A. and B.C.L., 1884; called to the bar, 1885, and joined S. Wales circuit; assisted the attv.-gen. (Sir R. Webster) in parl. and offl. work, 1886-1897; jun. counsel to the treasv. in peirage cases, 1892, and to H.M. Customs, 1896; employed in connection with the Br. Guiana bndry. question, and as one of the jun. counsel in the preparations of the Br. case in the arbitrn.; legal asst. under-sec., C.O., Nov., 1897.

COX, SIR WILLIAM HENRY LIONEL, KT. BACH. (1896).—B. 1844; substitute procureur and advoc.-gen., Mauritius, Feb., 1880; puisne judge of the sup. ct., Aug., 1880; procureur and advoc.-gen., Aug., 1886; ch. justice, S. Sttlnts, 1893; ret., 1906.
CRAIG, WM.—Supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore; ag. sup. money order branch and savings bank, Oct., 1903, to June, 1905; ag. asst. P.M.G., Singapore, Mar., 1907.

CRAIGIE-HALKETT, MONTAGU C.—Ag. priv. sec. to gov. Falkland Islds., Mar., 1897; writer, col. sec.'s off., Apl., 1897; priv. sec. to admnstr., Apl., 1897; elk., col. sec.'s off., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898; treasv. elk., 1899.
CRAMPTON, DUDLEY RUSSELL.—Ed. at Aldenham Schl., Elstree, and in Germany and Switzerland; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 24th May, 1904.

CRAWFORD, HENRY LEIGHTON, C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1855; ed. at Clifton and Corp. Christi Coll., cmb. (scholar); writer, Ceylon service, 1877; pol. mag., dist. judge, and asst. govt. elk. various stations, 1880-1890; 2nd asst. col. pris. elk. of legis. coun., 1890; prin. asst. col. sec. Ju. Prov., 1891; comsrs. under "The Buddhist Temples Ordece," 1889, Nov., 1900; govt. agt., Prov., Nov., 1901; prin. collr. of cust., 10th Ch., 1902; ag. treas., June 1904; govt. agt., S. Sec., May, 1906; govt. agt., W. Prov., offr., 1906; ag. col. treas., Mar., 1907; controller, rev., July, 1907.

CRAWFORD, WALLACE.—4th elk., imigr. dept., Br. Guiana, Sept., 1880; 3rd elk., Aug., 1884; 2nd elk., Oct., 1885; 1st elk., Mar., 1893; imigr. agt., Jan., 1894; sen. imigr. agt., June, 1906; acted as emigr. agt. at Barbados, Oct., 1890, to Nov., 1891; awarded 1st class certif. in Hindustani, Mar., 1885.

CRAWSHAY-WILLIAMS, ELIOT, F.R.G.S.—B. 1879; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; entered army (R.F.A.), 1900; served in India, 1901-1903; seconded for duty under foreign office at Delhi Durbar; resig. comsn., 1903; travelled in Persia, 1903-04; apptd. asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Winston Churchill, U.S. of S. for Col., 1906.

CREAGH, C. V., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1842; barrister-at-law (Mid. Tem., called May, 1892); was asst. dist. supt. of the 1st class in the Punjab pol.; in 1866, passed the highest pol. examn., and the 2nd standard in Oriental languages; in 1867 raised the Sikh pol. for the Hong Kong govt.; dep. supt., 1867; J.P. in 1868; sheriff in 1874; received thanks of govt. for services in typhoon, Sept., 1874; acted as supt. of pol. in 1869-70 and 1877-78; ag. A.D.C. in 1878; supt. of fire brigade, 1878; acted as pol. mag. and coroner, 1878 to 1880; passed with credit the six exams. in Chinese colloquial prescribed by the govt.; in 1879, mem. of comttees of public gambling, and on the defence of Hong Kong; arbitrator under the opium ordinance (7 of 1879); asst. Br. res. judge, and mem. of state coun., Perak, Mar., 1883; gov. and comr.-in-ch. and ch. judicial offr., Br. N. Borneo, Mar., 1888, to 1895; also gov. Labuan, 1889 to 1895, when he ret.

CREASY, LEONARD, M.I.C.E.—Pioneer offr., Ceylon, 1874; supting. offr., 1877; dist. engrn., Feb., 1886; prov. engrn. N. Prov., July, 1890; financial and office asst., Nov., 1894; prov. engrn. E. Prov., Jan., 1895; ditto. Prov. Uva, May, 1895; on deputation to Seychelles, Nov. to Dec., 1896; prov. engrn., Cent. Prov. Kandy, Sept., 1897; ag. dir. pub. wks., 15th Mar., 1903.
CREED, HENRY.—B. 1871; apptd., after exam., 3rd class messenger in C.O., July, 1900; 2nd class, 1st Feb., 1904.
CREIGHTON, JAMES KEMBLE.—Ed. at Smythe's Schl., Southsea; Eastern Tel. Co., Mar., 1892, to 31st July, 1903; asst. supt. of tels., E. Africa Prot., 1st August, 1903.

CRESWELL, MARGARET S.—Apptd. postmistress of Gibraltar and supt. of govt. telegraphs, Imp. P.O., Sept., 1877, as a mark of the estimation of the services of her late father, the dep. postmtr.-gen. at Gib.; transf. to col. govt. in Jan., 1886, when the col. assumed the control of the local P.O.

CRESWELL, CAPT. WILLIAM ROOKE, C.M.G. (1897).—Joined R.N. Dec., 1865; lieut., 1873, for service in action at Laroot River (wounded); engaged in suppression of slave trade on African coast, 1875-7; ret. from Navy, 1878; apptd. to S. Australian naval force, 1885; comdr., 1891; post. capt., 1895; naval comdt., 1893-1900; commanded H.M.C.S. "Protector" to China Boxer outbreak, 1900; naval comdt. of Uganda rly., 1900-4; dir. of Commonwealth naval traffic dept., Bengal and N.W. rly., 9½ years; traffic man., Uganda rly., 9th Aug., 1897.

CRUISE, HENRY RICHARD.—B. 1894; ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Leeward Is., Feb., 1904; ag. 3rd outdoor offr., treasy. and cust., Antigua, May, 1904; 4th indoor offr., treasy., Apr., 1905; clk. to atty.-gen., Aug., 1905; clk., P.W.D., Nyassaland, B.C.A., July, 1906.

CUDDEFORD, WM.—Employed on financial mission in Bahamas, May, 1885, to May, 1886; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1887; local auditor, Lagos, under C. and A.G., London, Dec., 1889; ditto, S. Leone and Gambia, Sept., 1893; auditor, Windward Is., May, 1896.

CUMBERLAND, CHARLES RUSSELL, B.A.Oxon.—B. 1866; entd. Ceylon civ. ser., 4th Oct., 1889; attached to the secretariat, 1st Apr., 1891; pol. mag., Balapitiya, 29th Sept., 1893; ditto, Panadure, 16th Dec., 1895; offr. of cls. IV., 15th May, 1898; dist. judge, Kegalla, 13th Dec., 1899; asst. gov't. agt., Matale, 15th Aug., 1900; on special duty, camp for prisoners of war, Dijatalawa, 3rd Dec., 1900; offr. of cls. III., 9th June, 1901; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1st July, 1902; asst. gov't. agt., Matale, 30th Oct., 1903; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1905; asst. gov't. agt., Kalutara, Mar., 1906; prin. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1906; offr. of cls. II., Nov., 1906; has held numerous ag. appts.
CUNLIFFE-OWEN, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1847; for services in connection with the Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886.

CUNNINGHAM, J. FRANCIS, F.R.G.S., F.Z.S.—Ed. King's Coll., Lond., and privt.; sec. to B.C. Africa adminstr., 24th Sept., 1892; orgns. printg. dept., and was apptd. editor of offl. gazette, June, 1894; served as vol. in campaigns against Zarafla, etc., 1895 (reed. Central African medal); won gold medal offered by Zomba rifle club, 1897, and the Edwards Cup, 1898; ch. Br. commr. for delimitation of Anglo-Portuguese boundary in B.C. Africa, 24th May, 1899; sec. to Sir H. Johnston's spec. miss. to Uganda, 24th Aug., 1899; passed exams. at Mid. Temp. and admitted a law student, 29th June, 1901; admitted to Gray's Inn, 1st July, 1901; sec. to Uganda adminstr., 1st Apr., 1901; apptd. judl. offr., 19th July, 1900; carried on duties of ch. judl. offr. and regist. in Uganda, 10th Apr., 1900, to 19th Dec., 1900; acted at headquarters for spec. commr. during his tour of inspection, 28th May to 8th June, 1900; and during the dep. commr.'s absence with Nandi expdt., 9th Jan. to 16th Feb., 1901; present post, sec. to Uganda adminstr.; regist. gen. of marriages, 1st Nov., 1902; joined Uganda vol. reserve, 1903; elected sec. of corps (company comdr.), 24th Jan., 1905; won annual championship and cup, 24th Mar., 1905; leader of African Prots. vols., Bialey, 1907; author of "Uganda and its Peoples," "Notes on the Anthropology and Ethnology of Indigenous Races," 1905.

CUNYNGHAME, SIR PERCY F. BART.—B. 1867; cadet, Sarawak civ. ser., July, 1886; asst. res. of Trusan, May, 1890; res. (2nd class), Mar., 1896; lieut. 3rd battn. Middlesex Rifles, May, 1897; capt., Oct., 1901; ag. res., Limbang, May, 1899; res., 2nd class, Upper Rejang, Aug., 1901; commdt., Sarawak Rangers, July, 1903; res., lat div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.

CURLEWIS, JOHN STEPHEN, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Cape civ. serv., 1883-1888; advocate sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Oct., 1887; advocate high ct. (late S.A.R.), May, 1888; counsel Imperial govt. in arbitn. with late S.A.R. Asiatic question, 1895; ag. judge of high ct. (la... S.A.R.), 15th Apr., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1900; pres. of crim. ct. for Witwatersrand, 2nd Nov., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1900; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Sept., 1903.

CURLING, CLARENCE NAPIER.—B. 1868; served under educn. dept., Cape Colony, 1888-1890; B.S.A. pol., 1890-91; educn. dept., 1892-1893; headmnr. of diocesan gram. schl., Nassau, Bahamas, 1894-95; res. just. and collr. of Elenthus, Bahamas, Aug., 1895; ch. regisr., G. Coast, 12th Jan., 1899; dist. comsnr., Mar., 1899; ch. regisr. and sheriff, 1901; prov. comsnr., Jan., 1907.
CURMI, TANCREDO, M.V.O. (1907).—Asst. supt. of pol., Malta, 1890; senr. asst., 1902; supt., 1903.

CURRIE, SIR DONALD, G.C.M.G. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1825; chmn. of the Union-Castle Mail Packets Co. In July, 1876, at the time of the negotiations between the Earl of Carnarvon and Pres. Brand, he assisted by his good offices in bringing about the success of these negotiations, and thus ending the long dispute between the Br. Govt. and the Orange Free State in reference to the diamond fields; formerly M.P. for Perthshire.

CURRIE, HARRY AUGUSTUS FREDERICK.—Ed. at Glenalmond and Cooper's Hill; Fell. of Cooper's Hill, 1887; on Forth Bridge constr., wks., 1888; asst. engrnr., pub. wks., dept., India, 1888-1892; asst. man., N.W. rly., India, 1892-94; dep. consulting engrnr. for rlys., 1894-95; asst. sec. to govt. of India, 1894-97; dep. consulting engrnr. for rlys., 1897-98; lent to Pekin Syndicate for employment in China, 1898-1902; dep. man., Oude and Rohilkund rly.; man., Uganda rly., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns., E. Africa Prot., 1907.

CUSACK-SMITH, SIR BERRY, K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1859; ed. at Eton; barrister, Middle Temp., 1884; law scholarship, 1883; formerly lieut. 1st V.B. Essex regt.; apptd. H.M. Consul at Samoa, Jan., 1890, and H.M. dep. comsnr. for the W. Pacific at Samoa, May, 1890; proceeded in H.M.S. "Curacoa" to Union Group to report on land claims, Aug., 1892; dep. comsnr. for the Samoan and Union Groups, and authorised to perform the duties of res. in the Union Group, Oct., 1892; special judicial comsnr., Apr., 1893; apptd. to license and superintend employment of Gilbert Is. natives in Samoa, Jan., 1894; consul-gen., Valparaiso, 1897.

CUSCADEN, W. A.—B. 1853; Sen. Sophister, Trin. Coll., Dub.; capt. 4th Roy. Dub. Fus.; asst. inspr. of constab., G. Coast, 1879; civil comsnr., Tacquah, 1881; ch. inspr. of pol., Singapore, 1883; asst. supt. of pol., Penang, 1884; ag. pol. supt., Malacca, 1889, and July, 1897; Singapore, 1891, and Apr., 1893; asst. supt. of pol., P.W., 1893; supt. pol., Malacca, July, 1897; ag. supt. pol., Singapore, Apr., 1898; ag. inspr.-gen. pol., S.S., Mar., 1901, to Aug., 1902, and Nov., 1903, to Mar., 1904; supt. of pol., Sing., Aug., 1902; inspr.-gen. of pol., S.S., 18th Dec., 1906.

DAETH, JOHN, M.I.C.E.—Ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; asst. draughtsmen, P.W.D., Jamaica, Jan., 1873; ch. ditto, Mar., 1875; dist. engrnr., W. dist., Jan., 1883; engrnr., Kingston dist., Aug., 1886; S. dist., Apr., 1892; Kingston and E. dist., Apr., 1899; seconded for service as col. engrnr. and surv.-gen., Dominica, Jan., 1901; asst. D.P.W., Jamaica, Mar., 1902; ag. D.P.W., 1902, 1903, 1905 and 1907.

DALE, CHARLES ERNEST.—B. 1867; ed. Greenwich and King's Coll., London; fell. soc. accnts. and auditors, fell. roy. statistical soc.; apptd. dir. customs Niger Coast Protec., Jan., 1895; asst. postmr.-gen., Apr., 1896; ag. dir.-gen. customs and postmr.-gen., Feb. to Sept., 1896, Oct., 1897, to June, 1898, Oct., 1899, to Feb., 1900; Dec., 1900, to July, 1901; treas. Southern Nigeria, Feb., 1898; ag. sec., Feb. to Apr., 1900; Jan. to Apr., 1903, dir. pub. officers' guarantee fund, Apr., 1900; ag. treas., Lagos, Sept., 1904; financial comsnr. to combined admstn. of Lagos and S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906.

DALE, H. E.—B. 1875; ed. at St. Paul's Schol. and at Oxford; class. schol., Balliol Coll., 1894; Hertford schol., 1895; Craven schol., 1895; Ireland schol., 1896; 1st class mods., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1898; B.A., 1898; Fell. New Coll., 1898; Derby schol., 1899; M.A., 1901; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., O.C., Oct., 1898; sent on special mission to Br. Hond., Nov., 1903; priv. sec. to Sir F. Hopwood, Jan., 1907.

DALE, HAROLD ROSS.—B. 1863; entered atty.-gen.'s office, Cape, Dec., 1879; 2nd class clk., July, 1883; ch. clk. to solr.-gen., Grahamstown, Mar., 1885; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Nov., 1886; J.P. for the Colony; registr. of sup. ct. and taxing offr., 1st June, 1901.

DALLAS, FRANCIS HENRY.—Ed. Univ. Coll. School; asst. sec. Penang municipality, 1895; ag. sec., May, 1896; Sarawak civ. ser.; ag. editor Sarawak Gazette, and manager, Sarawak govt. printing office, 1897; ag. treas. and clk., supreme coun., June, 1902; treas. and recorder of sup. coun., 1903; mem. of comtece. of adminstr., 1903-4.

DALTON, THE REV. CANON JOHN NEALE, M.A., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1839; was gov. to T.R.H. Princes Albert Victor and George of Wales, and ag. chaplain to H.M.S. "Bacchante", chaplain in ord. to Her Majesty, 1886; canon of Windsor, 1885.
DALY, SIR MALACHY BOWES, K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1836; Lieut.-Governor of Nova Scotia, 1890.
DALY, THE HON. THOMAS MAYNE, K.C.—Mem. for Selkirk, Manitoba, in Dominion house of commons; min. of interior, Canada, 1892-6.

DANAVALL, JOHN MARTIN.—Barrister, Mid. Temp., 1877; ag. solr.-gen., Leeward Is., 1883; again in 1884; ag. mag. Dist. C. 1883, and again in 1885; 2nd puisne judge, Leeward Is., June, 1888; also dep. judge of the vice-admiralty etc., 1889.

DANDY, JOSEPH HENRY.—Ed. Oakes Inst., Liverpool, certif. as inspr. of nuisances and sanitary inspr. from sanitary inst., 1896; 1st cl. advanced hygiene, S. Kensington, 1897; surveying (1st cl.) and certif. of sanitary knowledge, Victoria Univ., 1898; certif. schl. of hygiene, Univ. Coll., 1898; practical bacteriology, Univ. Coll., 1899; mem. of san. inst., 1900; san. inspr., Liverpool, chief sanitary inspr., Hong Kong, June, 1899.

DANE, ROBERT, M.R.C.S., L.S.A. (1887), L.R.C.P. (1889).—House physician, Middlesex hosp., 1890; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, June, 1890; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1893; col. surg., Sing., Mar., 1901; ag. col. surg. res., Sing., May, 1905, to Jan., 1906; mem. of S. S. med. coun., July, 1905.

DANIEL, ROWLAND MORTIMER.—Ed. at St. Edmund's, Canterbury; 2nd lieut., Glam. artil. militia, 1893; certif. of musk. Hythe; capt. and instr. of gunnery, 1898; seconded sub.-inspr. B.S.A. pol., Div. I. (now Bech. Prot. pol.), 1898; served through S. African war, 1900-02, including siege of Mafeking; ment. in desps.; brig. sig. offr. and staff capt., 1900-01; ag. asst. comsnr., S. Bech. Prot., 1902; asst. res. mag. at Serowe, 1903; ag. asst. comsnr., N. Prot., 1904; ag. comsnr., ditto, Jan., 1907.

DAVIDSON, WALTER EDWARD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; scholar and exhibitioner of Christ's Coll., Camb.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., Aug., 1880; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, Dec., 1880; writer col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1881; transfd. to Colombo Kachcheri, Sept., 1881; attached to the Negombo pol. ct., May, 1882; acted as asst. agt., Negombo, July to Oct., 1882; extra office asst., Colombo Kachcheri, May, 1883; and ag. office asst., Nov., 1884; sec. to Ceylon coman., col. exhibn., 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Apr., 1888, Mar., 1892, and Feb., 1895; ag. pol. mag., Kalutara, Apr., 1899; Galle, Dec., 1890; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1887; Haldummulla, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1899; ag. govt. agt. and fiscal, prov. of Sabaragamuwa, July, 1896; ast. govt. agt., Kegalla, Oct., 1896; N. Eliya, Feb., 1899; chmn. mun. coun., and mayor of Colombo, Apr., 1898; reasig. as chmn. of mun. coun. on going on leave; ast. sec. to administrator, Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1902-3; gov., Seychelles, 15th Apr., 1904.

DAVIES, COLIN REES.—Ed. at Eton; admitted solr. of sup. ct., Sept., 1891; under-sheriff, Pembrokeshire, 1897; called to the bar, Bahamas, July, 1901; ag. ch. just., Feb., 1903; ag. prosecuting coun. for the Crown, May, 1903; coroner, Nov., 1904; reglar.-gen., Br. Hond., Apr., 1905; also reglar., Vice-Admiralty ct.; ag. atty.-gen. on various occasions, and mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; also mem. of bds. of educn. and quarantine; J.P. for col.; atty.-gen., 5th Feb., 1907; joined Inner Tem., July, 1907; chancellor of the diocese of B. Honduras and Cent. America.

DAVIES, HON. JOHN MARK.—M.L.C. for S. Yarra, Victoria, 1889; for Melbourne, 1899; min. for just., 1890-92; solr.-gen., 1899-1900, and 1902-3; min. of pub. instrn., 1903; atty.-gen. and solr.-gen., Victoria, 1903.

DAVIES, JOHN MURRAY.—B. 1878; ed. at Xavier's Coll., and Melbourne Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1902; office asst., Galle, 1903; pol. mag., Kurunegala, 1904; off. asst., ditto, 1905; dist. judge, Kegalla, 1906; seconded for serv. as ast. land attlt., offr., 1906.

DAVIES, HON. SIR LOUIS HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1845; called to the bar, 1866; Q.C. in 1880; was one of the Canadian counsel before the internat. fishery coman. at Halifax in 1880; solr. gen. of P.E. Is., 1869, and again in 1870. led the opposition in P.E.I. Legislature until Sept., 1876, when he became premier and atty.-gen.; resig. in Mar., 1879, and was defeated at the gen. elections which followed; was first returned to the House of Commons at the gen. elections in 1882, and has since sat for Queen's; min. of marine and fisheries in Sir W. Laurier's govt., June, 1896; mem. joint high comsn., Quebec and Washington, 1898-9; puisne judge of sup. court, 1901.

DAVIES, THE HON. SIR MATTHEW HENRY, K.T. BACH. (1890).—B. 1850; solr.; late mayor of Prahran; mem. exec. coun. Victoria; min. without portfolio, Feb., 1886; speaker, legis. assem., 1887-32; vice-pres. and an exec. comsnr. for the centennial internat. exhib., 1888.

DAVIES, THEOPHILUS ALFRED.—Temp. clk., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1885; ag. astt. examng. offr. of cust., Lavana, 1889; tide waiter, June, 1892; ag. astt. examng. offr. of cust., and postmtr., Mano Salija, Mar. to Nov., 1893; prev. offr., Bassa, 1895; ditto, Kukuna, 1896; on special mission to open cust. station at Saioniya, Nov., 1896; prev. offr., Saioniya, 1896 to 1902; insp. of health, Saioniya, Tambakka dist., 1900; offr. in charge of cust. and postmtr., Kambia, 1902, and 1903; sub-collr. of cust., sub-acctnt., postmtr. and money order clk., Mano Salija, July, 1903, to 1905; sen. warehouseman, Oct., 1905.

DAVIES, WM. REES.—Ed. Eton and Trin. Hall, Camb.; B.A., 1886; called to bar (Inner Tem.), 1887; went South Wales circuit; J.P. and dep. lieut. Pembroke dist.; J.P. for Haverfordwest; M.P. for Pemh., 1892-8; asst. priv. sec. to the late Sir W. V. Harcourt when chancellor of exchequer, 1893-5; atty.-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1897; administered the govt., June, 1901; ag. ch. just., May to Aug., 1902; King's advocate, Cyprus, 1902; atty.-gen., Hong Kong, 1906.

DAVIS, BECCLES.—Ed. C.M.S. grammar schl., Sierra Leone, and Fourah Bay Coll. (Dunelm); 1st class customs officer, 1894; ag. statistical officer of customs, Niger Coast Prot., Apr. to Oct., 1894; P.C.O. at Opobo, Brass and Bonny, 1894 to 1899; supervisor of customs, Southern Nigeria, 1900; transf. to pub. wks. dep., 1903; acct., P.W.D., 1903.

DAVIS, CHAS. GREY HOWICK.—B. 1871; Ent. col. ser. of Br.
