Guiana as astt. clk. audit office, Nov., 1887; 5th cl. clk., Apr., 1889; 4th Sept., 1889; 3rd Jan., 1894; sec. to comte. of combined court on col. expenditure, 1893-4; 2nd cl. clk., Nov., 1896; ag. 1st cl. clk., 1895-6; ag. acnt. to sup. ct., July, 1899, to Sept., 1900; 1st clk. and acnt., post office, Sept., 1900; ag. chrmn., poor law comsrs., 9th to 20th Dec., 1906, and from 16th Mar., 1907.

DAVIS, C. T.—B. 1873; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and Oxford; classical scholar, Balliol Coll., 1892-6; 1st class classical mods., 1894; Jenkyns exhibnr., 1896; 1st class lit. hum., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 1st cl. clk., acnt.-gen.'s dept., admiralty, Oct. 5th, 1896; transf. to sec.'s dept., inland rev., Mar., 1897; 2nd cl. clk., col. office, Apr. 15th, 1897; went to Gibraltar as sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir C. R. Thompson on a mission to inquire into the currency question, May and June, 1898; sec. Pacif. cable comttee., 1900; priv. sec. to Sir M. Ommanney, June, 1902; 1st cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1905.

DAVIS, LEOPOLD ALBERT RICHARD.—B. 1862; permanent supernum. aid waiter, cust. dept., B. Guiana, Oct., 1882; 5th cl., Apr., 1883; 4th cl., Oct., 1884; 4th cl. offr. of cust., Jan., 1886; 3rd cl., Oct., 1887; 2nd cl., June, 1896; ag. 1st gen. comsry. of taxation and offr. astt. to comptr. gen., cust. in excise matters, July, 1898, to Mar., 1899; 1st cl. offr., 8th Sept., 1905; ag. survr. of cl. 1st Nov., 1905, to 28th Feb., 1906; 9th Au., 8th Nov., 1906; and from 1st Feb., 1907.

DAVIS, NICHOLAS DARNELL, C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1846; was for some years in Grenada, in ge. priv. sec.'s office; priv. sec. to Admstr. Baynes for a short time. In Br. Guiana served as clerk in several depts.; Aug., 1870, to Feb., 1871, sec. to the comsrs. of inquiry into the treatment of imigrts.; sec. to roy. comsrs. of inquiry into the treatment of imigrts. in Mauritius, Feb., 1872, to Dec., 1873; comdt., Br. Sherbro, S. Leone, 1874; postmtr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1876; acted as rec.-gen., Dec., 1880, to Sept., 1881; comptroller of customs, 1st Oct., 1881; ag. auditor-gen., mem. of the ct. of policy, and sen. comsrr. of the Vissengen estate, 1882; mem. ct. of policy again in 1886, and since 1892 continuously; auditor-gen., 1st July, 1888; mem. exec. coun., Dec., 1896; has acted as govt. sec. on nine occasions, and three times as dep. govr.

DAVIS, STEWART SPENCER, F.R.G.S.—Extra rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; 4th ditto, 1895; 1st clk., treas., 1898; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, 1898; ag. adjutant, 1899; acctnt., treasy., G. Coast, 1901; astt. treas., 1902; ag. ch. astt. treas., Dec., 1904; student, Gray's Inn, 1904.

DAVSON, CHARLES SIMON, B.A.,LL.D., K.C.—Ed. at Westminster and Trin. Hall, Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Tem., Jan., 1881; admitted to bar of Br. Guiana, 1882; acted as solr.-gen. on many occasions; stip. mag., 1888; solr.-gen., 1898; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1898, to Oct., 1899; again, Oct., 1900, to Apr., 1901, and on several other occasions; puisne judge, Mauritius, 1905.

DAWE, HON. CHAS.—Newfoundland: M.H.A. Harbour Grace, 1878-1889; for Port-de-Grave since 1898; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1894, and again in 1897; elected leader of oppo. party in house of assem., 1906.

DAWE, HON. ELI.—B. 1843; M.H.A. for Harbour Grace, Newfoundland, 1889-1905; rly. comsrr., 1890; financial sec., 1893; chrmn., bd. of wks., 1895; min. of agric. and mines, 1900; mem. of cabinet, 1903; min. of marine and fisheries, 1904.
DAWSON, J. B. H.—M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond., 1893; St. Mary's Hosp. med. schl., Lond.; dist. med. offr., Leeward Islds., Oct., 1894; dist. surg. of Sierra Leone, Aug., 1893.
DAWSON, JOHN WILDING.—Asst. eng., drainage dept., Mauritius, 30th Nov., 1894; drainage authority, 15th August, 1898; asst. dir. of pub. wks. and surveys, 28th Dec., 1904.
DAWSON, SAMUEL EDWARD, C.M.G. (1906).—King's printer, Canada, and controller of stationery, Nov., 1891; doc. of letters, Laval Univ., Quebec, and pres. of roy. soc. of Canada.

DAY, H. R. A.—Cadet, Sarawak civ. service, July, 1886; asst. res. Lundu, June, 1881; asst. res., Bitulu, June, 1893; res. 2nd cl., Mar., 1896; res. of Lundu, 1898; res., 2nd cl., Upper Sarawak, Jan., 1902.
DEACON, A.—Apptd. after exam. 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 1907.

DEACON, THOMAS.—Apptd., after open compet. exam., for the home civ. ser., to the Impl. Post Office, July, 1884; postmtr.-gen., Gold Coast Colony, Sept. 2nd, 1899; lieut.-G. C. Rifle vol., 1899; capt., 13th Oct., 1901; ag. adjutant, 11th Apl., 1903.

DEAKIN, THE HON. ALFRED.—Comsnr. of pub. wks. and min. of water supply, Victoria, Mar. 8th, 1883; and also vice-pres. bd. of land and works; solr.-gen. Nov. 13th, 1883; pres. of roy. comsn. on water supply, Dec., 1884; ch. sec. and min. of water supply, 1886; resig., Nov., 1890; sen. deleg. to col. confce., 1887; one of the Austr. delegates sent to Eng. in connection with Austr. federation bill; joined 1st ministry of the Commonwealth as atty.-gen. and min. of justice, Jan., 1901; prime min. of Australia, 1903-4, again prime min., 1905; represented Australia at Imp. Conf., 1907.

DEALY, THOMAS KIRKMAN, Inter. Arts (Lond.), D.A.F. (Paris), F.R.G.S., F.C.S., F.R. Hist. S., M.R. San. I., P.—B. 1869; ed. at St. Mary's Coll., Hammersmith; Marquis of Ripon's prize and a double first (head of each list), 1880; matric. (1st div.), Lond. Univ., 1883; 1st B.A. (1st div.), 1884; Diplôme (degré supérieur), and Société perpétuel de l'Alliance Française (Paris), 1902; life mem. of Assoctn. Phonétique Internationale; mem. of the Malone Soc'y; headm. St. Mary's, Derby, 1881-84; asst. master, govt. central schl., now Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1884; hon. examr. in physics to Coll. of Medicine for Chinese, 1888; passed 2nd standard higher grade Chinese, Aug., 1890; ag. 2nd master, Victoria Coll., Apr., 1890, to Sept., 1891, Apr., 1894, to Apr., 1895, Mar., 1899, to Apr., 1900, May, 1900, to Oct., 1901, and Mar. to Sept., 1904; apptd. master in charge, pol. schl., Sept. 1st, 1900; resig. pol. schl., 30th Apr., 1906; apptd. 2nd mast., Queen's Coll., 16th Mar., 1906; apptd. to bd. of examns., 1906.

DE BOISSIERE, ARNAULD.—Entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 1891; trooper, light horse, 1897; sub-lieut., 1901; lieut., 1903; ag. chief of fire brigade, Mar., 1903; J.P. for colony, and inspr. of Indian immigts.

DE CELLES, ALFRED DUCLOS, C.M.G., (1907), Chevalier de la Légion d'Honneur (1904).—B. 1843; editor of "Le Journal de Quebec," 1867 to 1872; editor of "La Minerve," Montreal, 1872 to 1880; ast. librarian of parlmt., Canada, 1880; joint librarian, 1885; author of "Papineau" and "Cartier," and "Les États-Unis, origines, constitution et développement."

DE FENZI, CHARLES WILLIAM PERKS DOUGLAS.—Ck. ast. and librarian, legis. coun., Natal, 5th Feb., 1889; sec. to comsn. on stock importation, 1889-90; sec. to bot. gardens comsn., 1891; sec. to comsn. on scab and stock diseases, 1894-5; ck. of the legis. coun. on introduction of resp. govt., 10th Oct., 1893.

DE FREITAS, ANTHONY, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1869; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; ag. pol. mag., Grenada, 1900 and 1901; ag. atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1902; ag. registr., sup. ct., Grenada, 1902, to 1904; ag. ch. just., St Vincent, May to Oct., 1904; stip. mag. and coroner, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Oct., 1904; atty.-gen., St. Lucia, 4th Apr., 1907.
DE GRUCHY, A. NICOLLE, M.B., C.M., Edin.—Ast. med. offr., and J.P., Victoria, Seychelles, and outlying islands, 11th Sept., 1901; col. surg., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1906.

DE HAMEL, HARGRAVE BARRY.—Major, Londonderry Artillery; ast. supt. of pol., Straits Settnmts., June, 1897; seconded as ag. ast. comsnr. of pol., Kinta, Perak, Dec., 1905; supt. of pol., Penang, May, 1907.
DE KRETZER, EDWARD, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1864; employed in col. sec.'s off., Caylon, 8th May, 1872; ast. aud.-gen., 1st Jan., 1901; ast. cont. of rev., Mar., 1907.

DE LA CORNILLIERE, HENRI GASTON ERNEST.—Draughtsman, P.W.D. of late O.F.S., Nov., 1892, to Mar., 1900; architectural astt., P.W.D., O.R.C., Mar., 1900, to July, 1902; head of architectural drawing off., ditto, July, 1902.

DELAFAYE, SIR LOUIS VICTOR, KT. BACH. (1898).—B. 1842; called to bar Mid. Tem., 1863; mun. councillor, St. Louis, 1870; prov. judge, sup. ct., Mauritius, Mar., 1879; mem. prison bd., Jan., 1884; mem. civ. ser. inquiry comsn., Feb., 1884; mem. civ. ser. comsn., 1884-95; ag. puisne judge, 1884-5, and May, 1890, to July, 1892; Q.C., Aug., 1892; puisne judge, Nov., 1894; ag. chief justice, June, 1898; chief judge, Dec., 1898.
DE MARTIN, G. P.—B.A., Dublin; jun. astt. master, Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1904.

DE MELLO, ALOYSIUS.—B. 1880; B.A.; ed. at St. Patrick's Sch., Karashi, St. Peter's Coll., Camb., and in France; mem. of hon. soc'y. of Gray's Inn; law triplos, Camb., 1902; cadet, S. Stltnts, 27th Nov., 1903; astt. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, 13th Apr., 1904; proceeded to study Chinese at Canton, 6th Dec., 1904; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, 3rd Sept., 1906; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 3rd Oct., 1906; passed cadet, 21st Jan., 1907; ag. sheriff and dep. regiar., sup. ct., Penang, and ast. offl. assignee, Penang, 14th May, 1907; is mag. for Singapore and Penang.
DENHAM, HON. DIGBY FRANK.—B. 1859; M.L.A. for Oxley, Queensland, since 1902; min. for agric. and pub. wks., Sept., 1903; min. for agric. and rlyws., Jan., 1906; resigned, Feb., 1907.

DENHAM, EDWARD BRANDIS.—B. 1876; ed. at Malvern and Merion Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1889; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1899; extra offr. ast. govt. agt. West Prov., Dec., 1899; ast. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1902; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., May, 1904, and Feb.-Mar., 1905; ast. govt. agt. N. Prov. for pearl fishery work, Mar., 1905; 2nd ast. col. sec. and sec., agric. bd., priv. sec. to O.A.G., Sept., 1905; organising sec., agric. bd., Dec., 1905; landing survr., cust., Colombo, June, 1906.
DENMAN, ROBERT.—B. 1860; ed. Marl. Coll. and Guy's Hosp.; M.R.C.S. Eng., 1886; L.S.A. Lond., 1886; D.Ph., Cantab, 1905; gov't. med. offr., Sevchelles, 1896; ch. med. offr., 1900.

DENNETT, R. E.—Ed. at Marlborough; served in the Congo, 1879-1902; inspr. of forests, S. Nigeria, 1902; ast. conservator of forests, 1st grade, 1904; author of "Seven years among the Fjort," "Notes on the Folklore of the Fjort," and "At the Back of the Black Man's Mind."
DENT, FRANKLAND, M.Sc., Ph.D., F.I.C.—Ast. govt. analyst, Singapore, Aug., 1905; gov't. analyst and science lecturer, S. Settnmts., Mar., 1906.
DENT, CECIL WYNTER.—B. 1869; ed. at Winchester Coll.; inspr. repatriation accts., O.R.C., 1902; and., ditto, 1902; ch. clk., audit dept., O.R.C., July, 1905.

DENTON, SIR GEORGE CHARDIN, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1851; ensign, 57th regt., Oct., 1869; lieut., May, 1871; adjt., Aug., 1876; capt., Jan., 1878; chief of pol., St. Vincent, Apr., 1880; apptd. one of the comsnrs. to inquire into the pol. force, Barbados, Oct., 1890; mem. of exec. coun., Oct., 1881; represented St. Vincent at the telegraphic confce. at Barbados, May, 1886; admstd. govt. St. Vincent, May to July, again in 1886, and from Nov., 1887, to Mar., ag. col. sec., 1886 to 1888; ool. sec., Lagos, Mar., 1888; admstg. govt. on many occasions, 1889-1900; apptd. lieut.-governor, 1900; admnr. of Gambia, Nov., 1900; gov., Mar., 1901.
DE PIRO, GIUSEPPE LORENZO, MARCHESO, C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1858; late hon. sec., comtee. of privileges, Maltese nobility.
DERAVIN, EUGENE E.—Rev. offr., St. Kitts, 1893; 1st rev. and excise offr., 1897; ag. asst. treas., Sept., 1904, and June, 1905.

DERBY, 16TH EARL OF (England, created 1845), BARON STANLEY, 1832 (United Kingdom); BART. 1627 (England); BARON STANLEY OF PRESTON, 1886; G.C.B. (1886); A.D.C.—THE RIGHT HON. FREDERICK ARTHUR STANLEY, younger son of the 14th Earl of Derby, b. 1841; ed. at Eton; entered the Grenadier Guards in 1858, and was apptd. lieut. and capt. in 1862; retired from the army, and entered Parlimt. as mem. for Preston in 1865; was Lord of the Admiralty from Aug. to Dec., 1868; elected mem. for N. Lancashire at the gen. election of 1868, and again in 1874, 1880, and 1885; was financial sec. for war from Feb., 1874, to Aug., 1877, when he was apptd. financial sec. to the treasy., Mar., 1878, sec. of state for war, which office he held till the retirement of Lord Beaconsfield's admin., in 1880; S. of S. for Cols., June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; resig., Feb., 1886; pres. bd. of trade in Lord Salisbury's 2nd admin., Aug., 1886; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1888-93; succeeded his brother as Earl of Derby, 1893; Lord Mayor, Liverpool, 1895.

DE ROS, COLIN JOSEPH FERNANDES STANISLAUS.—Ed. at Catholic gram. schl., Georgetown, B. Guiana; served as a volunteer, customs, 1st Apr. to 31st Dec., 1885; 6th cls. offr., 1st Jan., 1886; 5th cls. offr., 16th Dec., 1886; 4th cls. offr., 1st Sept., 1892; 3rd cls. offr., 4th June, 1896; 2nd cls. offr., 14th Sept., 1900; ag. offr. asst. to comptroller in excise matters, 1st June to 31st Aug., 1902; ag. 1st cls. offr., 9th Aug., 1906.

DE SARAH, J.H., C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1844; writer to the govt. of Ceylon, 1865; pol. mag. of Balapiti-modara, 1866; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1867; comsr. of requests, Colombo, 1868; dist. judge, Kurunégala, 1875; regis.-gen., Colombo, 1886; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1883; registr.-gen., May, 1886; dist. judge, Jaffna, Aug., 1891; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1891; ditto, Kandy, Aug., 1893; ret.

DESHON, EDWARD, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1836; mem. of land bd., Queensland; ensign in 68th L.I., June, 1854; lieut., Dec., 1854; served with 68th L.I. at siege and fall of Sebastopol, from Nov., 1854, to end of the war; medal and clasp, and Turkish medal; instructor of musketry to the 68th L.I. from Nov., 1857, to Oct., 1861; passed compet. exam. for admission to Staff Coll., in July, 1861; ret. by sale of comsn. in Oct., 1861; audit-gen., Queensland, 1890; ret. 1901.

DE SMARAIS, HENRY EUGENE.—Called to the bar., Mid. Tem., Apr., 1866; pol. mag., Rodrigues, June, 1875; dist. judge and stip. mag., Seychelles, Nov., 1879; ag. ch. civ. comsnr. on several occasions; ag. mag., Moka, Apr. to Sept., 1882; dist. mag., Moka, Aug., 1884; ag. pol. and additional mag., Port Louis, Nov., 1884; dist. mag., Moka, Dec., 1885; ag. jun. dist. mag., Port Louis, May to October, 1886; Mar., 1887, to Jan., 1888; dist. and stip. mag., Moka, July, 1889.

DE SMITH, HENRY, C.M.G. (1901). B.A. (Cape Univ.), F.S.S.—B. 1846; entered the service in Mar., 1865; permanent under col. sec. of the col. of Cape of Good Hope, and perm. head of convicts and prisons dept., holding in addition the following offices: (1) accounting offr., col. sec.'s dept.; (2) controller printg. and statnry.; (3) mem. civ. ser. comsn.; (4) mem. tender bd.; (5) custodian stamps; (6) admnr. food and drugs act; (7) inspr. emrgt. coolie ships. Was specially charged with the duties of dir. of the census of 1891; received congratulations of the govt., and was awarded an honorarium of 500l. specially voted by parlimt. elected fellow of roy. statistical soc., 1892; ass't treasr., July, 1898; ret. 1904.

DEVILLIERS, RT. HON. SIR JOHN, P.C. (1897), K.C.M.G. (1882), KT. BACH. (1877).—B. 1842; justice of the sup. ct., C. of Good Hope, 1874; sup. gen. from 1872 to 1874; for several years mem. of the legis. assem.; pres. of the legis. coun., and mem. of the coun. of the Univ. of C. of Good Hope, 1873; one of the roy. coman. for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal, 1881; represented in Cape at the col. confere., Ottawa, 1884; mem. judicial comtee., H.M. privy coun., 1897.

DEW, ARTHUR TOMKYNES.—Naval cadet, 1879; midshipman, 1889; sub.-lieut., 1890; took part in the expds. to the Linggi and Lukut rivers, 1874, and in suppression of piracy on Malay coast (Indian medal, with Perak clasp); mag. & collr., Perak, 1881; sup. Br. territory of Dinding, 1884; dep. master attendant, shipping mag., Singapore, 1886; is a J.P.; mag., Singapore & Penang; comsnr. ct. of requests; mag. and collr. dist. mag. and habrmbr., Matang, Perak, 1889; dist. mag., Krian, Apr., 1899; ag. dist. mag., Lower Perak, July, 1901; dist. offr., Bau Padang, Perak, Nov., 1902; dist. offr., Lower Perak, 14th Sept., 1904.
DEWAR, ARTHUR R. J.—Capt., 5th Roy. Warwickshire Regt.; comp. comdr., Malay State Guides, Apr., 1902; asst. supt. of pol., Singapore, Sept., 1905.
DEWAR, HAROLD JAMES.—B. 1878; apptd. clk., repatriation, O.R.C., 23rd Apr., 1902; dist. British refugee and, 1st July, 1903; dist. treas., 1st July, 1903; examr. of acts., treasury., 1st Apr., 1904.
DEWDNEY, THE HON. EDGAR.—Lieut.-gov. of N.W. territories, 1881; min. of inter. Canada, 1888; lieut.-gov., Br. Columbia, 1892.

DE WET, THE HON. SIR JACOBUS ALBERTUS, K.C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1840; mem. Cape house assem. and exec. coun.; min. for native affs., 1884; Br. agent, S.A. Republic, June, 1890; ret. on pension, Sept., 1896.

DE WILTON, MAJOR ALBERT ALBERT WALTER-LIEUT., Lanarkshire militia, 1881; lieut., Co. naught Rangers, 1883 to 1886; lieut., India staff corps, 1886 to 1894; capt., ditto, 10th May, 1894; major, ditto, 10th July, 1891; served in Burma campaign, 1885, 1887, 1889 (medal with two clasps); insp.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, Oct., 1898; insp.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Ceylon, 18th Aug., 1902.

DIAS, FELIX REGINALD, M.A., LL.M. Tri-Hall, Camb.—Called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1887; pol. mag. and comsnr. of requests, Gampoa Ceylon, Oct., 1889; crown counsel for the island, July, 1893; additnl. dist. judge, Colombo, July, 1906.

DICK, JOHN NORMAN, M.I.M.E.—B. 1853; govt. engrnr. survr., Penang, 1888; ag. govt. marine survr., Singapore, June, 1895, to June, 1896; govt. mar. survr., and examinr. of engrnr., Singapore, March, 1901; ag. inspr. of marine surveys, S. Sttlmts., July to Nov., 1908.

DICKSON, THOMAS.—B. 1860; ed. Fettes Coll., Edin.; served in Boer war in 1st cont., Ceylon M.I., Jan., 1900, to Jan., 1901 (medal and 3 clasps); and in S.A.C. from Jan., 1901, to June, 1902 (King's medal and 2 clasps); temp. apptd. ch. clk., land settmt. dept., O.R.C., 10th Oct., 1901; astt. sec., land settmt., 12th Aug., 1903; sec., land settmt., 23rd Jan., 1905; ag. dir., land settmt., 14th May to 14th Nov., 1906.

DILLON, ALBERT BARROW.—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad; clk. to inspr. of schls., B. Hond., 1893; ag. clk. to auditor and in treasy. and customs, 1893; clk. to col. engnr., 1895; ag.-insp. of schls., 1895-8; 2nd clk., treasy. and cuat., 1897; inspr. of schls., June, 1898; ag. sup't. bot. station, 28th Sept. to 31st Dec., 1904; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., and clk. of couns., 29th June, 1905, to 14th Jan., 1906.
DILLON, P. L., I.S.O. (1904).—B. 1846; 4th class landing waiter, Barbados, 1866; 3rd class, 1868; senior, 1880; landing survr., 1886; comprtr. of customs, Dec., 1890.

DISCOMBE, JOHN.—Apptd., after compet. exam., 3rd class clk., sup. ct., Gibraltar; 2nd class clk. (supernumerary), Jan., 1899; ag. astt. regnr., Nov., 1899; astt. regnr. and dep. clk. of arraigns., Jan., 1900; ag. regnr. and admstr.-gen., July to Sept., 1901.

DOBIE, EDWARD DAVID.—B. 1857; barrister, etc., sup. ct., Tasmania, admitted July, 1882; joined civ. ser., Tasmania, Mar., 1883; was partly draftsman and afterwards solr.-gen., from Mar. to May, 1887; cr. solr. from 30th May, 1887, to 1895; sec., law dept., to 1898; then recorder, Launceston, to 1st April, 1901; solr.-gen. and crown solr., and grand juror, 1st Apr., 1901.
DOBBS, CECIL MOORE.—Ed. St. Columba's Coll., Rathfarnham, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. Classics (honours); astt. collr., E. Africa Prot., 25th Oct., 1906.

DOBERCK, Dr. W.—Astronomer in charge of Markree Observ., co. Sligo, 1874-1883; dir. Hong Kong Observ. since 1883; has published twenty volumes of observations and researches in Hong Kong, a pamphlet "On the Law of Storms in the Eastern Seas," and over 200 papers on scientific subjects in transactions of learned societies and scientific periodicals in Europe and America.

DOBSON, HON. ALFRED, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1848; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1875; mem. legis. assem., Tasmania, 1877-87; att.-gen. and mem. exec. coun., 1877; speaker legis. assem., July, 1885; solr.-gen., June, 1887; mem. of coun. of educn., and church advoc. for the diocese of Tasmania; agt.-gen. for Tasmania in London, 1901.

DODDS, THE HON. SIR JOHN STOKELL, K.C.M.G. (1901), KT. BACH., C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1848; att.-gen., Tasmania, Dec., 1878, to Oct., 1879, and in the Giblin ministry, Oct., 1879; col. treas. from Dec., 1881, to Aug., 1884; att.-gen. in the Douglas ministry from Aug., 1884, to Mar., 1886; also premier, Mar. to Dec., 1886; judge, sup. ct., 1886; deleg. col. confce., 1887; ch. just., 1888; adminstr. of govt., 1899, and again 1900-1; lt.-gov., 3rd Aug., 1903.
DODDS, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1854; M.D. (Edin.), 1879, gold medallist, M.B. and C.M., 1876; 1st class honours, D.Sc., 1878; inspr. of asyls. and med. supt. of Valkenberg asyl., Cape, 1889.
DOHERTY, C. J.—Puise judge, sup. ot., Quebec, 1891.

DOLBEY, HUGH OWEN.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and Lond. Univ.; called to the bar, June, 1901, Mid. Tem., Oxford circuit; registr., E. Africa Prot., and prin. registr. of documents, 29th Apr., 1904; mag., 1907.
DONALD, RANALD.—Uganda Rly. mag., 9th Oct., 1900; mag., Nairobi, E. Africa Prot., 13th Sept., 1902; inspr.-gen. of pol., 24th Nov., 1906.
DONNAN, J., C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1837; mast. attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, 1863; was comdr. of govt. steamers, "Manchester" and "Pearl," from July, 1869; ret., 1902.

DONNELLY, W. J. S.—Survr.-gen., Newfoundland, to 1882; rec.-gen., 1882; resig., 1889; M.L.A. for Placentia, 1893; rec.-gen. and exec. coun., Apr. to Dec., 1894; M.H.A., Placentia, 1897; rec.-gen., 1898; inspr. of cust., 1902.

DONOVAN, JUSTIN FOLEY, M.D., M.CH.L.M.—Med. offr. and health offr., Killeanh dispensary, co. Cork, 1876 to 1879; surg., R.N., 1879; received thanks of the admiralty, 1882 and 1888, "for zeal and devotion to duty" at R.N. hosps., Port Royal and Malta; and of the med. dir.-gen., R.N., for professional work, 1882-4-6; ret. from the navy 1889; med. attendant, lepers' home, pub. gen. hosp. and constab. training depot, Spanish Town, Jamaica, 1889; med. examr., civ. ser. widows and orphans' pension fund, 1890.

DOUGLAS, ARCHIBALD CAMPBELL.—B. 1872; ed. Radley Coll. and Oxford Military Coll.; apptd. a consulr. astt. on the Niger Coast Protec., Dec., 1894; astt. dist. commr., Feb., 1896; member, Lincoln's Inn, 1898; transport offr. in Qua Ibo dist. punitive exped., Feb. and Mar., 1899 (West African medal); promoted to rank of 2nd cl. offr., S. Nig., Jan., 1901; served as political offr. with Aro field force, Jan. to Feb., 1902 (medal); polit. offr. to Ekele expedn., 1898 (medal and clasp); dist. commr., 1st Apr., 1904.
DOUGLAS, JAMES ARCHIBALD, M.A.—Director of education, So. Nigeria, Apr., 1903.
DOUGLAS, R. STAIR.—Cadet, Sarawak serv., Nov., 1896; astt. rea., June, 1899; rec. 2nd cl., Jan., 1902; 2nd cls. rea., Baram dist., 1904.

DOWER, EDWARD EBENEZER.—Clk., ch. mag.'s off., Kokstad, C. Colony, Oct., 1891; Matatilele, Jan., 1896; nat. affairs offr., Aug., 1896; prin. clk., 1900; ch. clk., July, 1901; also acctng. offr., Prime Minister's dept., July, 1904; astt. sec. to nat. affairs dept., July, 1906; soc. to ditto, Sept., 1907.

DOWNER, THE HON. SIR J. W., K.C.M.G. (1887), K.C.—B. 1844; att.-gen., S. Australia, June, 1881, to June, 1884, and att.-gen. and premier, 1885-7; del. to col. confce., 1887; ch. sec. and premier, 1892-3; mem. of senate, Australian parl., 1901-1903; mem. leg. coun., S. Aust., 1905.

DOWNES, MAJOR-GEN. M. FRANCIS, C.M.G. (1886), late R.A.—B. 1834; served in the Crimea till June, 1866, medal and clasp and Turkish medal; instructor of fortifcn., R.M. Coll. Sandhurst, for two years; commanded artillery Mauritius and St. Helena for four years; instr. of gunnery at Woolwich five years; col.-eq. S. Australia, June, 1877; mem. of roy. e on defences, Sydney, 1881; sec. of defence, Victoria, April, 1885, to Mar., 1888; comdt., S. Australian forces, Apr., 1888-92; ret., 1902.

DOYLE, EDWARD ANGEL GAYNERS.—B. 1859; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond.; obstet. soc., Lond.; mem. Br. med. assoc.; late asst. house surg., Westminster hosp.; joined med. service, Trinidad, 1884; ag. dist. med. offr., 1886; res. surg., San Fernando hosp., Nov., 1892; ag. res. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1893; res. surg., ditto, 1897; D.M.O., S. Naparima, 1906.

DRAKE, THE HON. JAS. GEORGE.—Mem. leg. ass., Queensland, 1888, 1893, 1896, 1899; M.L.C., 12th Dec., 1899; sec. for pub. instrn. and P.M.G., Queensland, Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; senator for Queensland, Commonwealth parlt., Mar., 1901; P.M.G., Commonwealth, Feb., 1901, to Aug., 1903; min. of def., Aug.-Sept., 1903; atty-gen., Sept., 1903; vice-pres. of ex. coun., 1904; ret., 1906.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, RALPH EVELYN.—B. 1875; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, Dulwich Coll. and St. George's hosp.; open scholarship, St. George's hosp., 1st year's proficiency prize and Pollock prize; M.R.C.S. Eng., L.R.C.P. Lond.; apptd. surg.-capt., attached to railway pioneer regt., S.A.F.F., Dec., 1899 (medal and 2 clasps); med. offr., Uganda Prot., 26th Sept., 1900; Nandi expedit., 1900 (medal and clasp); Suk-Turkana expedit., 1901 (ment. in deep, spec. ment. by Sir H. Johnston, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., recd. appreciation of serv. from S. of S. for Foreign Affairs); med. offr., Somaliland Prot., May, 1904; recd. appreciation from S. of S. for the Colonies for serv. during smallpox epidemic in Berbera, Somaliland Prot., 1904-5; passed in Somali language, Dec., 1905.

DRAYSON, MATTHEW JOHN.—B. 1865; Apptd., after compet. exam., a clerk in the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., May, 1883; asst. registr., May, 1892; promoted to higher grade 2nd div., July, 1896; seconded for service as chief clerk, col. sec.'s off., Hong Kong, 11th Jan., 1902; acted as clk. of coun., Aug., 1902; retd. to C.O. as dep. ch. registr., 22nd July, 1905.

DRAYTON, EDWARD RAWLE, C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1859; clk. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1878; 2nd clk. in office of gov.-in-chief of the Windward Is., July, 1879; ag. ch. clk., May, 1880, and May, 1881, to Jan., 1882; ch. clk., Jan., 1882; ag. col. sec., Grenada, Apr., 1883; priv. sec. to Maj.-gen. Browne, July to Oct., 1884; provost-marshal, Grenada, Apr., 1885; ag. col. registr., Sept., 1885; registr. sup. ct., J.P., July, 1886; mem. legis. coun., May, 1887; inspr. of prisons, Mar., 1888; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1888; treas., May, 1890; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1890, to June, 1892; col. sec. and registr.-gen., June, 1892; admstv. govt. of Grenada whenever gov. absent, 1890-1907; govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1893, Feb. to Sept., 1897, and Feb., 1900, to April, 1901; and of Windward Is., Jan., 1897, and Oct., 1907; delegate to W. Indian quarantine conf., Apr., 1894.

DRURY, EDWARD HERBERT MERIVALE.—Trinity Hall, Camb., 1890; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1896; priv. sec. to Hon. Sir R. G. W. Herbert, G.C.B., Mar., 1899, to June, 1900; priv. sec. to Govr. Sir C. A. Moloney, K.C.M.G., Windward Is., July, 1900; ditto, Trinidad, Dec., 1900, to July, 1902; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, St. Helena, Oct., 1902; emigrn. agt., Oct., 1902; clk. to ex. coun., Apr., 1903; J.P., Nov., 1902; schl. attndce. offr., Aug., 1904; collr. rev. rents and taxes, Jan., 1903, to Dec., 1906; asst. stip. mag., Nov., 1904; clk. to re. commr., Bech. Prot., Jan., 1907; ag. ch. clk. and regisr., June, 1907.

DRURY, R. FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E.—Asst. land survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1887; asst. inspr. of bldgs. and asst. sanitary survr., 1888; ag. land survr. and asst. engnr. on extraordinary pub. wks., 1890; annually recurrent wks., 1892 and 1894; duties in connection with black plague, 1894; ag. sanitary engnr., 1895; ag. exec. engnr. for annually recurrent wks., 1897.

DU BOULAY, ED.—Ag. clk. to audr. and treasr., St. Lucia, Jan. to May, 1890; 4th clk., treasr., Mar. to May, 1892; 3rd clk., May to Dec., 1892; 2nd clk., treasr., and accnt., Jan., 1893, to Apr., 1894; ag. ch. clk., treasr., Feb. to Nov., 1893; audit clk., Apr., 1894; ag. audr., St. Lucia, Mar. to Sept., 1901; attached to Somerset house for service in W. African colonies, Dec., 1901.

DUDER, T. C.—J.P., Newfoundland, 1874; M.H.A., Fogo, since 1893; financial sec., 1894; chmn. bd. of wks., 1894; survr.-gen. and chmn. bd. of agricul., 1897; min. agricul. and mined., 1898; stip. mag., Bonne Bay, 1901.

DUPF, HECTOR LIVINGSTONE.—B. 1872; apptd. by F.O., asst. collr., B.C. Africa, 1897; ag. collr., dist. of W. Nyasa, 1898; Zomba, 1899; resig. from serv., 1903; rc.-apptd., Apr., 1904; collr., Manji dist., May, 1904; ag. collr., Blantyre dist., 1905; res. of Manji, Apr., 1906; ag. res., Blantyre, Feb., 1907; author of "Nyassaland under the F.O.," 2nd edition; and a history of Nyassaland in the native dialect, published by the Prot. govt.

DUFF, GRANT, THE RT. HON. SIR M. E., P.C., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S. (1880).—B. 1829; ed. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1850; under-sec. of state for India, 1868 to 1874; lord rector of Aberdeen Univ., 1866 to 1872; partly under-sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1880, to Aug., 1881; M.P. for Elgin dist., 1857 to 1881; gov. of Madras, 1881-6.

DUFF, ROBERT.—Record clk., imigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, Apr., 1883; 4th clk., Aug., 1884; 3rd clk., Oct., 1885; 2nd clk., Mar., 1883; ch. clk., Jan., 1884; imigrn. agt. and ch. clk., July, 1889; sen. imigrn. agt., Jan., 1903; imigrn. agt.-gen., 1st June, 1906; awarded 1st class certif. in Hindustani, Mar., 1885; mem. of ct. of policy and of exec. coun.

DUFFIELD, S.—Clk. to accntg. offr., Cape govt. rly., 1897; bkpr. to ch. acctnt., O.F.S. (afterwards Imp. military) rly., Oct., 1897, to 11th June, 1900; 1st clk. and bkpr. to orphan master, O.R.C., 11th June, 1900, to 1st Apr., 1903; asst. mast. of High Ct., O.R.C., 1st Apr., 1903; ag. mast. of High Ct., Jan. to June, 1906.

DUFFIELD, THOMAS.—Entered crown lands office, S. Australia, Mar., 1867; ch. clk. and imigrn. offr., crown lands and imigrn. dept., 1877; capt. mil. force, 1881; sec. crown lands dept.; mem. of central land and pastoral bds., 1896-1905; sec., crown lands dept., and ch. inspr. of fisheriers, Nov., 1901.

DUFFY, THE HON. JOHN GAVAN.—B. 1844; eldest son of the late Sir Charles Gavan Duffy; ed. at Storshurst; vice-chancellor's prize, English essay, Melbourne Univ.; succeeded his father in representation of Dalhousie in legis. assem. of Victoria, 1874; pres. of bd. of land and wks. of Victoria, 1880; postmr.-gen. of Victoria, 1890; also atty.-gen., 1892; mem. of cabinet without portfolio, 1892; resig. Jan., 1893; again postmr.-gen., 1894 to 1899; rep. Victoria at premiers' confes. at Sydney, 1896; rep. Victoria at Fed. Coun. of Aust., Hobart, 1893, when he was chmn. of standing comitee; rep. Victoria at postal confes., 1891, 1892-5-7-8; rep. Aust. and Fiji at postal congress at Washington, 1897.

DUKE, MANSERGH PACE, M.R.C.P., and L.R.C.S.I., L.M. (1875).—B. 1852; ed. Academic inst., and Meath hosp., Dub.; med. offr., dist. 2, Dominica, 1875 to 1880; asst. surg., Gold Coast, 1880-1; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, May, 1886; offl. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1888; med. offr., dist. 1, and med. offr. of health, Oct., 1889; mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1895; mem. pub. lib. comitee., Oct., 1896; ex officio mem. bd. of health, Jan., 1897; J.P., Apr., 1897; ag. escheator-gen., July, 1898; holds dormant comsnr., July, 1898, to act as comsnr., and has so acted on numerous occasions; resig. dormant comsnr., July, 1907.

DUKES, HUGH ROBSON.—Senr. asst., Boys' Model Sch., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, 1st Feb., 1893; headmaster, Stanger schl., 1st July, 1895; ag. inspr. of schls., 15th May, 1902 to 30th June, 1903; headmaster, Richmond schl., 1st Oct., 1902; inspr. of schls., 1st July, 1903.

DUMARESQ, ROBT. HARE FITZROY.—Ed. at Haileybury Coll., 1885-1890; matric., New Coll., Oxford, 1890; farming in New Zealand, 1892-99; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and two clasps, King's medal and two clasps); inspr., land settm., O.R.C., 1902-1906; sec., dept. of agric., O.R.C., 17th Apr., 1906.

DUNBAR, ARTHUR DUNCAN, M.A. (Aberdeen).—Served during S. African war in S. African Light Horse (Queen's and King's medals and clasps); elk. to dir. of educn., Transvaal and O.R.C., 1902-3; priv. sec. to educn. adviser to high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1904-5; elk., educn. dept., O.R.C., 1905.

DUNCAN, ANDREW, H. F. (late lieut. R.N.).—Joined R.N., July, 1868; served in Mediterranean, S. America, Cape, India, Australia, and W. Coast of Africa; resig. Nov., 1883; passed exam. in theoretical survey, July, 1883, and practical survey, 1884, at the Cape Univ., in honours (first of his year on both occasions); from Apr., 1884, employed by the survr.-gen. of the Cape, and in priv. practice; mem. land comsn. of Br. Bechuanaland, Sept., 1885; survr.-gen. of that col., July, 1886; survr.-gen. to Br. S. Africa Co., 1891.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, C.M.G. (1904).—B. 1870; ed. at Oxford, schol. Ball. Coll.; 1st cls. class. mod., 1891; 1st cls. final class. school, 1893; Craven scholarship, 1890; elk., after compet. exam., int. rev., 1894; priv. sec. to Sir A. Milner; prin. elk., 1898; treas., Transvaal, 1901; col. sec., 1st Dec., 1903; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Oct., 1906.

DUNCOMBE, HENRY FEVESHAM.—Entd. col. serv. as an acctnt., treas., Bahamas, 1891; ag. mag. and collr. in various dists., 1894-5-6; res. mag., Abaco, 1897; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1898; has acted on various occasions as pol. mag., collr. of cust., and treas.; passed govt. exam. in Yoruba language, 1900.

DUNCOMBE, W. KELLSALL.—B. 1878; 2nd elk., tel. dept., Bahamas, Dec., 1895; rev. offr., Inagua, Mar., 1897; asst. res. just., Watlings Island, May, 1900; res. just., Cat Island, July, 1900; supervisor of cust., Lagos, Jan., 1905; ag. collr. of cust. from Aug. to Nov., 1906, and from Apr. to May, 1907.
DUNDAS, KENNETH ROBERT.—Ed. in Germany, studied engrng in Norway; asst. collr. E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1904.

DUNEDIN, 2ND BISHOP OF (founded 1864), Rt. REV. SAMUEL TARRATT NEVILL, D.D.—B. 1837; consec. 1871; Magdal. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1865; curate of Scarisbrick, Lancs., 1860-64; rector of Skelton, Staffs., 1864-71; is also primate of New Zealand.

DUNLOP, J. M. M., B.A., LL.D., D.C.L.—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, and Inner Temple; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1900; ag. pol. mag. 1901-2-3; ag. atty.-gen., 1904; ag. ch. asst. col. sec., 1905; ag. atty.-gen., 1906; ditto, S. Nigeria, 1906; ag. judge of sup. ct., S. Nigeria, 1906; ag. atty.-gen., 1907; ag. puisne judge, 1907.

DUNLOP, COLONEL SAMUEL, R.A., C.M.G. (1884)—B. 1838; ag. comsnr. of pol., S. Sttlmnts., Nov., 1870; ag. pol. mag. and comsnr. of ct. of requests, P.W., Apr., 1871; ag. dep. comsnr. of pol. and coroner, Apr., 1871; ag. dep. comsnr. of pol., Singapore, fire comsnr. and coroner, Aug., 1871; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Dec., 1872; comsnr. to Perak, Jan., 1874. In Nov., 1874, comsnr. with the forces sent to quell the disturbances in the native state of Sungei Ujong; inspr.-gen., Straits pol., May, 1875. In Nov., 1875, on the murder of Mr. Birch, apptd. special comsnr. (temporarily) for Perak affairs; organised the expedn. which captured the Passir Salak stockades, and was present at the capture, Nov., 1875; comsnr. to forces during operations in Perak, Dec., 1875; accompanied Gen. Colborne's force up the Perak river, and across country to Kinta; ag. res. coun., Penang, 1884 to 1885; pres. of Singapore mun. comsn., 1889; ret., 1890.

DUNN, ROBERT ERNST.—Clerk and interp. to the R.M., Umgeni div., Natal, 1880; to R.M., Lions River Mar., 1883; to R.M., Alfred, Oct., 1887; to R.M., Upper Umkomazi, 1894; mag. for Colony, mag., Weenen div., 1901.
DUNN, STEPHEN TROYTE, F.L.S.—Ed. Radley and Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1889; asst. for India, Kew Gdns., 1899; sup. bot. and forestry dept., Hong Kong, 1903; J.P., 1906.
DUNSMUIR, JAMES.—B. 1851; elec. to legislature of B. Columbia, 1898; premier, 1900 to 1902, when he resig.; lieut.-gov. of B. Columbia, 11th May, 1906.

DUPIGNY, E. G. MORSON.—Asst. gov't. off., Dominica, Feb., 1889; elk. comsnrs. of valuats., 1890; elk. mag., dist. F., 1890; do., presdt.'s off., Dom., 1890-92; do. regtr.'s off., 1892; do. treas., Antigua, 1896; stud. Mid. Temp., 1900.
DURMAN, F. J.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to C.O., 15th April, 1901.
DU TOIT, PIETER JOHANNES.—Clk., dept. of agric., Jan., 1890; 2nd cls. elk., May, 1890; 1st cls. elk., June, 1900; ag. chief elk. to ag. dir. of agric., Aug., 1905; apptmt. confirmed, July, 1906.

DU VERGE, LOUIS SEIDE RATHIER, I.S.O. (1903).—Inspr. of distilleries, Mauritius, 1868; supt., inland rev., 1876; ag. curator of vacant estates, 1883-4; acctnt. in bankruptcy, 1888; ch. clk., audit, 1890; asst. rec.gen., 1895; ag. registr.-gen., 1893 and 1896; storekeeper-gen., 1897; ag. rec.gen., 1901-2, 1903-4, and in 1907; collr. of cust., Feb., 1907.
DUVIVIER, AIME.—Clk., archives office, Mauritius, 1st Nov., 1879; custodian of archiv., 1st Jan., 1891.

DYER, JAMES HAROLD.—Ed. King William's Coll., I. of Man, King Edward VI. schl., Berkhamstead; recd. his comsn. in 3rd Batt. S. Lanc. regmt., 1889; obtained his compy., 1893; resig. comsn., 1894; joined Matabele relief force, 1896, and served through the campaign till the force was disbanded in 1897; joined C.M.R., 1897, and served through siege of Mafeking; resig. Cape Police in July, 1901; received medals and clasps; apptd. asst. dist. comsnr. S. Nigeria, 28th Dec., 1901; ag. dist. comsnr., Ediba, 1902.

DYER, THOMAS THEODORE RODNEY.—B. 1869; entd. pub. ser. of St. Vincent as cust. offr., Jan., 1890; ag. clk., treasy., 1891; asst. clk., P.O., 2nd clk. gov. off. to Apr., 1892; ag. 2nd clk. in off. of gov., Windward Islds., May, 1892; transf. to St. Lucia; 4th clk., treasy., May, 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; ag. 2nd clk., Feb. to May, 1893; 2nd clk. govt. off., May, 1893; also supt. of govt. printing; ag. 2nd clk. in off. of adminstr., Wind. Islds., 1894; hurricane relif offr., St. Vincent, Sept. to Dec., 1898; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of comns., St. Lucia, Oct., 1900, to Mar., 1901; apptd. ch. clk. to gov. Windward Is. (Grenada), 1st Apr., 1901; ch. clk., col. sec. s office, and clk. of ex. and leg. couns., 16th Nov., 1901; ag. pol. mag. N. dist.; asst. treas., Carriacou; clmnn., road bd., local health authority and water authority, Carriacou, and pol. mag., dist. III., St. Vincent, Feb. to May, 1904; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of comns.; sec., hurricane loan bd.; keeper of telephone stores and med. registr., St. Vincent, May to Aug., 1904; offl. trustee, Grenada, for emigrts. who desire to deposit earnings in sav. bank.

DYER, SIR WILLIAM T. THISELTON, M.A., F.R.S., K.C.M.G. (1899), C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1843; Chr. Ch., Oxon, 1873; asst. dir., Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, 1873; dir., 1885; ret., 1905; botan. adviser to S. of S. for Col.

DYETT, EDWARD FARLEY.—B. 1869; 2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, Aug., 1886; ad-measurer of vessels, 1889; 1st treasy. offr., June, 1891; comsnr. oaths, Nov., 1895; clk. of waterworks comsnrs., 1896; sec. hd. of health, Jan., 1897; held a comsnr., Dec., 1897, to act as treas. of Montserrat in absence of comsnr., and so acted on numerous occasions until Jan., 1900, when authorised by law to perform duties of treas. in conjunction with those of 1st treasy. offr.; J.P., May, 1898; offl. rec. in bankruptcy, Aug., 1898; holds comsnr., dated Feb., 1899, to admnr. oaths of office to ag. comsnr. in absence of comsnr. and govrn.; mem. legis. coun., Mar., 1900; visiting just. of prison, Nov., 1900; ag. col. postmnr., Antigua, Nov., 1903; provsnl. M.E.C., 10th Nov., 1906; asst. treas., 1st Apr., 1907.

DYETT, HENRY.—Supernumerary, P.O. and treasy., Montserrat, Leeward Is., Mar., 1894, to Feb., 1896; elk. to comsnrs. for assessing taxable value of real estate, 1893 and 1904; comsnr.'s and mag. s clk. and clk. of exec. coun., Feb., 1896; ag. 2nd treasy. offr., Apr., May and Sept., 1897; clk., legis. coun., Mar., 1898; ag. 2nd treasy. and quarantine offr., July, 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. jun. audit clk., Antigua, Oct. Nov., 1903; gov't. offr. and ex-officio rev. and excise offr. and clk. to N. dist. waterworks comsnrs., St. Kitts, Nov., 1906; sanitary inspr., Jun., 1907.

DYETT, RICHARD HENRY KORTRIGHT.—B. 1862; served in pres.s office, Virgin Is., 1879 to 1881, and 1882 to 1884; elk., regist.r's office, Nevis, 1886; 2nd elk., regist.r's office, Antigua, 1887; elk. to trustees, supt. ct. lib., July, 1888; comsnr. to admnr. oaths, 1889; marshal V.-A. ct., L.I., Dec., 1889; elk. to mag., dist. A., 1890; dep. cor., dist. A., 1892; ag. postmnr., May to Sept., 1894; 1st elk. regist.r's off., Jan., 1895; dep. cor., dist. B., Oct., 1896; ag. mag., dist. A., Jan., Feb., Apr. to Nov., 1897; sec. to H.M. Diamond Jubilee comtee., June, 1897; ag. mag., dist. B., Apl., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. mag., dist. B., May to Sept., 1899; and 25th Oct., 1899; ag. mag., dist. A., Nov., 1899, to May, 1900; during which period (Jan. to Apr.) acted also as mag., dist. B.; ag. mag., dist. A., June, 1900, to Mar., 1901; and Apr. to Nov., 1901; also in Mar., 1902; mem. of "Coronation" comtee., and hon. sec. to same, May, 1902; ag. mag., dist. A., Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903; regist.r and prov.-marshal and regist.r of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, 11th June, 1903; mem. of Basseterre town bd., June, 1903; regist.r and prov.-marshal, Antigua, and ch. regist.r., Leeward Is., July, 1905; trustee, sup. ct. library, Aug., 1905; M.L.C., Mar., 1907; chmn., St. John's City comsnrs., May, 1907; chmn., trustees of pub. library, July, 1907.

DYSON, CECIL VENN, B.A. Cantab.—Cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1896; supt. of posts and telegrapha, Negri Sembilan, Jan., 1899; mag., Seremban, Feb., 1900; sec., central census comtee., Nov., 1900; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Jan., 1903; sheriff and dep. regist.r, Penang, Aug., 1906.
EAKIN, JAS. W.—B.A., M.D., M. Ch. Univ., Dublin; gov't. med. offr., Trinidad; dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, S. and consulting surg. Col. hosp., San Fernando; surg.-major, local force.
EARL, ROUT. STEPHEN, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Cantab.), 1891.—B. 1865; med. offr. Lasoye dist., Dominica, July, 1892; med. offr., No. 5 dist. Antigua, 1896; comsnr. Virgin Is.

ERNSHAW, ALBERT.—B. 1865; ed. at Univ. Coll., Durham, and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; held foundtn.; Newby and Univ. class. schlrships., Durham, 1885-1887; 1st cls. classics, 1st year exam., 1886; 1st cls. class. final, 1887; B.A., 1887; M.A., 1890; elected fellow of Durham, 1889; open class. schlr., Pemb. Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1888; 1st cls. class. mods., 1889; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1891; B.A., Oxford, 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1893; went N.E. circuit and W. Riding, Yorks., sessions, etc., 1893-1904; common law lecturer, Yorks. Coll., Leeds, 1899-1900; pol. mag. and coroner, W. div., Grenada, Feb., 1904; ditto S. (or 1st) div., and escheator-gen., Grenada, June, 1905; ag. M.L.C., Grenada, 1905; stip. mag., Georgetown, B. Guiana, Dec., 1906; ag. puisne judge, B. Guiana, Nov.-Dec., 1907.
EASTERBROOK, ARTHUR DOVE.—Asst. collr., B.Cent. Africa Prot., June, 1886; 2nd cls. asst., May, 1902; dist. mag. and collr., N. Nyasa, May, 1904.
EASTWOOD, BENJAMIN.—Ed. at Fleetwood; with John Aird & Sons (now Sir John Aird & Co.) from 1877; chief accntn., Uganda rly., 1897.

EATON, HENRY FRANCIS.—Clik., col. storekeeper's office, Melbourne, Jan., 1853; transf. to civil commissariat, Feb., 1854; accntn., govt. stores, Feb., 1855; transf. to treasy., Mar., 1855; ag. accntn., treasy., 1859-70, 1876, 1886-7; accntn. to treasy., 1887; under treas., Sept., 1889-1895; capt. volr. artillery, 1876; is in the commission of the peace of the Colony.

EDMONDSON, RICHARD.—M.R.C.V.S., 1887; chief veterinary surg. to the Canadian agricultural coal and colonisation company, 1888 to 1891; St. Helen's, 1891 to 1905; veterinary offr., E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1906.

EDWARDS, COL. ALFRED HAMILTON MACKENZIE, C.B.—Served in 1st Dragoon Guards, Jan., 1883, to Apr., 1892; adjutant, Behar Light Horse Volunteers, India, Apr., 1892, to Apr., 1897; 1st and 5th Dragoon Guards and Imperial Light Horse, Apr., 1897, to Oct., 1900; Hazara (N.W. Frontier, India), 1888, ass't prov. marshal (ment. in desp., medal and clasp); commanded Imperial Light Horse, S. African war, Nov., 1899, to June, 1900; dist. comsnr., Potchefstroom and Krugersdorp dists., June to Oct., 1900; commanded "A" division, S.A.C., Oct., 1900, to end of war (ment. in desp., Queen's medal, 4 clasps, King's medal, 2 clasps); substantive lt.-col. in the army, dated 29th Nov., 1900; commdt., Transvaal volunteers; left the service, 1906.

EDWARDS, CHARLES HENRY.—Ed. St. Bees gram. schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; exhibtr. of Queen's Coll., B.A., 1894; B.C.L., 1901; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1898; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, Dec., 1899; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1904.
EGERTON, RICHARD.—Ed. Westminster and Chr. Ch., Oxford; B.A., 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1869; ass't res. mag., Kingston, May, 1892; res. mag., Jamaica, Feb., 1893.

EGERTON, SIR WALTER, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1858; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1880; asst. to the Chinese prot., Jan., 1881; mag., Singapore, Jan., 1881; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1882; collr. of land rev., Penang and P.W., Jan., 1883; 2nd mag. of pol., Penang, May, 1883; also official assignee under the Bankruptcy Ordinance (Sept., 1882); ag. sen. dist. offr., Butterworth, P.W., Apr., 1890; a.J.P. and coroner for S. Sttlmts., and a visiting justice for Penang; offr. in char., Sungei Ujong, July, 1893; ag. res., Pahang, May to July, 1894; offr. assignee and regis. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1896; inspr. of prisons, S. Sttlmts., May, 1897; dep.-pres. of mun. comshrs., Singapore, Mar., 1897; 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Jan., 1898; ag. res. councillor, Malacca, Mar., 1898, and Mar., 1899; 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1898, but continued to act as res. coun.; ag. col. treas., S.S., Nov.-Dec., 1898; ag. col. sec., S.S., Apr., 1900; ag. res. counclr., Penang, Feb. to Apr., 1901; ag. col. treas., S.S., Aug.-Oct., 1902; res., Neg. Semb., Aug., 1902; high comsnr., S. Nigeria, Nov., 1903; and gov., Lagos, 30th July, 1904; gov'r. and comdr-in-chief of the amalgamated colony and prot. of S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906.

EGGETT, W. H.—B. 1862; apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. of the lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Nov., 1880; promoted to higher grade, 2nd div., Apr., 1896; asst. acctnt., 6th Apr., 1899.

EHRHARDT, ALBERT.—B. 1862; ed. King Edward's High Schl., B'ham., and Worc. Coll., Oxford; graduated with honours in mods. and lit. hum., 1886; Inner Tem. schl.rshp., common law, 1888; called to bar, 1889; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1896; travelling comsnr. on the E. bdy., Aug., 1897; ag. res. of Ibadan, 1898; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., June, 1900; ag. att.-gen., Sept., 1900; rlyw. comsnr. at Aro, June, 1901; ag. treas., Aug., 1901, to Mar., 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Mar. to June, 1902; apptd. res., Ibadan, Feb., 1902; apptd. col. treas., May, 1902; atty.-gen., Fiji, mem. ex. and leg. couns., May, 1903; apptd. as comsnr. to produce revised edition of ordinances of the colony, 1903; ag. ch. just., May to Oct., 1904; produced revised edition of ordinances, 1906; ag. ch. jus. and ch. jud. comsnr. of W. Pacific High Comsnr., 1907; chmn. of comsnr. to inquire into customs duties and admtn., 1907.

ELCUM, JOHN BOWEN, B.A., Oxon.—Ed. Highgate Schl. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; scholar of Queen's Coll., 1878; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1884; passed in Malay, 1886; ag. dist. offr., S. Malacca, 1887; dist. offr. Bukit Mertajam, P.W., Jan., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Dec., 1890; sheriff, Penang, Mar., 1892; ag. sheriff, Singapore, June, 1893; 2nd mag., Penang, Nov., 1893; ag. collr. land revenue, Singapore, Dec., 1893, to Feb., 1894; ag. inspr. of schl., S. Sttlmts., June, 1895; also ag. official assignee and regis. of deeds, Singapore, Mar. to June, 1896; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., July, 1897; ag. 1st mag., Singapore, Mar., 1898; inspr. of schl., Aug., 1898, but continued to act as first mag. till Feb., 1899; dir. pub. instrn., Aug., 1901; inspr. of prisons, July, 1904; fin. consmr., F.M.S., Oct., 1904; dir. of educn., S. Sttlmts., and F.M.S., 1906.

ELDRIDGE, A. E. G.—C'lk., P.O., Antigua, Mar., 1880; clk. to pres. and isld. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., 1882; postmtr. of Antigua, Aug., 1891; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. B., 1894, 1896, and 1897; ag. mag. and coroner, Dist. H., Nevis, Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900; mem. St. Kitts-Nevis legis. coun., and gen. legis. coun., 1899; ag. comsnr. of Virgin Is., and ag. asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1903; ag. treas., Antigua, June, 1906; treas. and shipping mast., Apr., 1907.

ELFFERS, PIETER.—T. ii. (Holland), T. i. (O.F.S.); ed. at Normal Training Coll., Nymeyer, Holland; Dutch mast., pub. schl., Stellenbosch, 1882-84; ditto, Grey Coll. Schl., 1884-93; prin., govt. schl., Fauresmith, 1893-1901; Dutch mast., boys' high schl., Wynberg, Cape Colony, 1901-03; ditto, pub. schl., Bethulie, 1903-04; ditto, pub. schl., Winburg, 1904-05; inspr. of schl., O.R.C., 1st July, 1905.

ELGEE, CAPT. C. H.—B. 1871; ed. Lancing and Sandhurst; 1st comsnr., 16th Foot, Feb., 1892; capt., Oct., 1899; active service, Chitral, 1895 (medal with clasp); Ashanti campaign, relief of Kumasi, 1900 (medal); adjutant, Lagos Hausa Force, 1899; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to Sir W. Macgregor, gov'r. of Lagos, 1900 to 1902; and clk. of ex. coun.; ag. insp.-gen., 1902; trav. consmr., Lagos, 1902; ag. col. treas., mem. of ex. and leg. couns., 1902; resident, Ibadan, 1st Jan., 1903; author of "Memo. on Negro Education."

ELGIN AND KINCARDINE, 9th EARL OF (creat. 1633), VICTOR ALEXANDER BRUCE, K.G. (creat. 1899); G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C., LL.D., D.C.L.; Baron Bruce, 1603; Earl of Kincardine and Baron Bruce of Torry, 1647; Baron Elgin (U.K.), 1849.—B. 1849; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford, M.A.; treas. of Household and 1st comsnr. of wks., 1886; Lord-Lieut. of Fifeshire since 1885; Viceroy of India, 1894-99; chrmn. Roy. Coms. on S. African War, 1902; chrmn. Roy. Coms. on Free Churches Controversy in Scotland, 1904; chrmn. Roy. Coms. to administer Scottish Churches Act, 1905; sec. of state for the Colonies, Dec., 1905.

ELLIOT, EDWARD CARLYON.—B. 1870; ed. Bradfield Coll. and Uppingham; apptd. 5th class clk., govt. secretariat, B. Guiana, 1895; seconded ag. clk. to govt. agent, N.W. dist., Br. Guiana, for periods 1896 to 1899; 4th class clk., govt. secretariat, 1899; pay and quartermr. to Hausa force, Gold Coast Col., 1900; apptd. senior asst. treas., Oct., 1902; dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 5th May, 1905; with powers of travelling comsnr., 1906; provincial comsnr., 18th Oct., 1907.

ELLIOT, LAURENCE S., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1845; clk. to mags., W. Australia; landing and tide waiter at Bunbury, 1863; also postmtr. and asst. diregistrar; clerk in col. sec.'s office, 1872; sec. to central bd. of educ., and asst. clerk in legis. coun., 1873; registrar-gen., registrar of deeds, and registrar of brands, 1876; ag. ch. clerk, col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1878, to Jan., 1880; 1st clerk and registrar-gen., col. sec.'s dept., Jan., 1880; apptd. to take the census, Apr., 1881, and to superintend its compilation; ch. clerk and accountant, treas., Apr., 1881; ag. asst., col. sec., Dec., 1889, to Oct., 1890; under treas., Jan., 1891; is a J.P.

ELLARD, JOHN BRANDON.—Ed. at Northampton Gram. Sch.; Thorneycroft's M.I., Jan., 1900; transf., comsnr., Driscoll's Scouts; Queen's medal, 5 bars, King's medal; sec. and acctnt., Repattn. Comsnr., Hoopstaad, O.R.C., to close of Comsnr.; asst. to loc. auditor, B. C. Africa Prot., 1903-04; treas., asst., E. Africa Prot., 28th Sept., 1906.

ELLENBERGER, JULES.—Clerk to asst. commnr., Bech. Prot., 1890; interpreter to concessions commnr., Bech. Prot., 1893; ditto to Sir S. Shippard's boundy. commnr., 1894; J.P., 1893; asst. mag., 1898; served in S. African war, including relief of Mafeking; spec. commnr. for oaths of allegiance in So. dist. of Bech. Prot. to burghers of late S.A.R. and O.F.S.; asst. commnr., So. dist., Bech. Prot., 11th July, 1902.
ELLEY, R. L. J., F.R.S., C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1827; astronomer, Victoria, 1853-95; D. 1907.
ELLIOT, MAJOR SIR HENRY GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1899), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1826; ch. mag. of Tembuland, C. of Good Hope; in command of Tembu levies during Basuto war; ret., 1904.

ELLIOTT, COL. JOHN, R.M.L.I., C.B. (1877), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1824; entered R.N. in 1838; 2nd lieut., June, 1846; col., 1869; served in the China expdn., 1842 (medal); served in H.M.S. "Fox" during the Burmese war, 1852 (officially thanked); mentioned in despatches; comld. the Royal Marines in the steam flotilla on the Irrawaddy (mentioned in gov.-gen.'s despatch; medal with clasp for Pegu). Served at the bombardment and surrender of the forts of Bomarsund, in Aug., 1854 (Baltic medal), and during the siege of Sebastopol in 1855, the expdn. to Kerch and Yeni-Kale (medal with clasp, 5th class of the Medjidie, and Turkish medal); has also the medal for the Kaffir war. Served as maj. of brigade to the force of Royal Marines landed in Mexico in 1863; lieut.-col., Devonshire Rifle Volr. Corps, 1870; in 1871 J.P. for Plymouth; in 1874 called to the bar, Mid. Term., and joined the west-term circuit; in 1876 inspr. of prisons and stip. J.P., Br. Guiana; inspr.-gen. of pol., Barbados, 1881; adminstd. govt. of St. Vincent, 1884 and 1889; acted as col. sec., Barbados, 1886 and 1894; ret. 1895.

ELLIS, FRANCIS ROBT., C.M.G.—B. 1849; writer in the Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1871; additional pol. mag., Kurunegala, 1874; office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., 1876; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, 1878; ag. asst. agt., Matale, 1879; ditto, Mullaitivu, 1880; dist. judge, Ratnapura, 1st Apr., 1883; pol. mag., Colombo, 1885; dir. and inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1891-6; ag. inspr.-gen., pol., May to Nov., 1893; ag. prim. collr. of cust., Jan., 1896; govt. agt., S. Prov., Jan. to Mar., 1897; govt. agt., W. Prov., Sept., 1897; aud.-gen., Mar., 1902; ag. col. sec., 19th Nov., 1903; ret.

ELLIS, FRANK THOMAS.—B. 1881; cadet s. Stltlims, 27th Nov., 1903; attached to Chinese Prot., Penang, Jan., 1904; sent to China to study Hokkien, Aug., 1904; passed final in Chinese, Aug., 1906; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Sept., 1906; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Oct., 1906; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Xmas. Is., Apr., 1907.

ELLIS, WALTER DEVONSHIRE.—B. 1871; ed. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxon; scholar, 1881-85; prox. acc. Hertford Univ. scholarship, 1891; 1st class classical mods., 1892; Univ. jun. Greek testament prize, 1893; 1st class literae humaniores, 1894; B.A., 1894; M.A., 1897; chancellor's priz. Latin essay, 1895; apptd. after compet. exam., to be a clerk in the C.O., Apr., 1895; priv. sec. to Lord Selborne, June, 1897; 1st class clkr., 1898.

ELLIS, WILLIAM GILMOUR, M.D., M.R.C.S.—B. 1860; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Singapore, 1888; ag. also as municipal health offr., Singapore, May, 1889, to Feb., 1892, again 1894; ag. col. surg. in 1901, 1902 and 1903; police surg. since 1897; J.P. for Singapore.

ELY, HERBERT WILLIAM.—B. 1871; clerk in dept. of agt.-gen., for Tasmania; ch. clk., 1895; sec. 1899; acted as priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Sir Edward Braddon, P.C., K.C.M.G., ex-premier of Tasmania, during his visit to England for the Diamond Jubilee celebration, 1897.

EMERSON, GEO. HY., K.C.—Speaker, house of assem., Newfoundland, 1890-94; mem. of official delegation to London, 1890, and of delegation from legislature in 1891 on the fisheries question; mem. exec. coun., 1895; asst. judge, sup. ct., Nov., 1896.
EMMENS, JOHN HOWARD.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., 11th Mar., 1901.
EMMS, C. B.—Clerk, G.P.O., O.R.C., Aug., 1902; clk., treas., Oct., 1903; asst. acctnt., fund dept., July, 1905.

EMTAGE, O. DE C., M.A.—Ed. Harr Coll., Barbados; Barbados scholar, 1886; scholar, Worc. Coll., Oxon, 1887; 1st class math. mods., 2nd cls. finals, 1890; hon. nat. sc. schools, 1891; asst. mast., Harr. Coll., 1891; examiner, 2nd grade schools, Barbados, 1891-92; ditto, grn. school, St. Lucia, 1894-95; ditto, Gren., 1897-98; headmaster, The Lodge School, 1892.

EMTAGE, WM. THOS. ALKER.—B. 1862; M.A., Oxford; Fell. of Phys. Soc., Lond.; ed. Harris Coll., Barbados; scholar, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, 1880; 1st cls. math. mods., 1881; 1st cls. final maths., 1883; 1st cls. nat. sci., 1885; University Coll., Nottingham, math. lecturer, 1885; asst. prof. math. and phys., 1889; prof. math. and phys., 1891; principal, tech. inst., Wandsworth (L.C.C.), 1895; director of pub. instr., Mauritius, 1900; examnr., Oxford Locals, maths., 1888, 1889; examnr., final hon. school nat. sci., Oxford, 1890, 1891; author of "Mathematics of Electricity" (Oxford Press), and other works.

ENGELBACH, A. H. H., I.S.O. (1902).—B. 1830; apptd. 2nd Dec., 1869, after passing an exam. before the civ. ser. commrs., clk. in the accnts. branch in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., having acted in that capacity since Mar., 1868; 1st class asst. clk., 1st Sept., 1879; bookkeeper and gen. asst. to the financial clk., 1884; acted for financial clk., June, 1886, to Apr., 1887; accountant, Apr., 1896.

ENNIS, GEORGE FRANCIS MACDANIEL.—B. 1868; ed. at King's Coll., school and privately; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1892; sec. to gov., B.N. Borneo, 13th Nov., 1894; sessions judge, B.N. Borneo and Labuan, 24th Dec., 1894; judge of genl. ct., Labuan, 1895; judge of ch. ct., B.N. Borneo, 14th Aug., 1895; ag. res., Province Alcock, B.N. Borneo, 13th Dec., 1895; resig., Oct., 1897; ag. asst. judge, Zanzibar, 18th May to 7th July, and 1st Aug. to 24th Oct., 1899; registr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1899; town mag., Mombasa, 20th July, 1900; ag. judge, E. Africa Prot., 6th Sept., 1900; ag. asst. judge, Zanzibar, 25th Jan., 1901; legal vice-consul, Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; judge of high ct. of Uganda, 11th Aug., 1902, and judge of H.B.M. Court of Appeal for Eastern Africa; joint author of "The Registration of Transfers."

ENRAGHT-MOONY, F.—Sub-inspr., Basutoland police, Mar., 1884; acted as govt. sec., Basutoland, 1893-1895; commanded Bech. Prot. native police with rank of capt., 1896; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1897; acted as govt. sec., 1900-1901; mentioned in com.in-chief's desps., 1901; native comsnr., Zoutpansberg, Aug., 1901; commanded Ft. Edward and Spelonken dist., Northern Transvaal, and acted as intell. offr. until close of war; special comsnr., Swaziland, June, 1902; ree. mag. and native comsnr., Swaziland, 1906.

ESNOUF, E. AMAND.—Dist. mag., Seychelles, 1870; Grand Port, Mauritius, 15 May, 1878; dist. mag., Moka, 1883; jun. dist. mag., Port Louis, Aug., 1884; ag. master sup. ct., Mag., 1886; and Dec., 1887, to Jan., 1888; ag. sen. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, Mar., 1890, to Mar., 1892; Dist., 1892, to Feb., 1893; May to Aug., 1896; ag. dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Feb., 1898; ag. dist. and stip. mag., Riv. du Rempart, Mar., 1898; dist. mag., Pt. Louis, 3rd div., Mar., 1899; ag. substitute mast., sup. ct., Aug., 1901.

ESPIE, HENRY PATTULLO.—Ed. at Glasgow Acad.; associate, Chartered Institute of Actuaries, and Actuaries, Glasgow, Apr., 1891; asst. chief acctnt., Imperial Brit. East Africa Compy., 1891; 1st ast. treas., July, 1895; dep. treas., 1st Apr., 1903.

EUSTACE, ROBERT WILLIAM BARRINGTON.—Ed. at Shrewsbury; capt., reserve of officers and late 4th Batt. S. Stafford Regt.; served in the S. African war with Canadian Mounted Rifles and A.S.C. (medal and six clasps); ast. dist. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 10th May, 1904.

EVANS, FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1881), C.V.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered the civ. ser., Admiralty, Somerset house, 13th Dec., 1867; transf. to the sec.'s dept. at Whitehall, Jan., 1872; ast. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, May, 1878; priv. sec. to Sir S. Rowe, K.C.M.G., Feb., 1879 to Feb., 1881; ag. clk. of legis. coun., Sept., 1879; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880; ch. sec. and ch. of the staff to Sir S. Rowe during threatened hostilities with Ashanti, 1881; ast. col. sec., G. Coast, 1882; ag. collr. of customs and treas., Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; ag. col. sec., Aug. to Dec., 1883, and on other occasions; clk. of legis. coun., Feb., 1882, to Apr., 1884; dep. gov., Lagos, July to Aug., 1883, and May, 1885, to Jan., 1886; dep. gov., G. Coast, Sept., 1883, and Apr., 1885; col. sec., Lagos, Jan., 1886; ag. adminstr., July, 1886; col. sec., G. Coast, 1887; Leeward Is., 1888; Jamaica, 1889; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Feb., 1900; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1901.

EVANS, HON. JOHN WM., C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1855; M.L.A. for Kingborough, Tasmania, since 1896; warden and master warden of marine bd. of Hobart for many years; premier and min. of educn., Tasmania, 11th July, 1904, to 10th Oct., 1905; premier and treas., 11th Oct., 1906, to 1st May, 1906; premier and ch. sec. from 1st May, 1906.

EVANS, WILLIAM.—B. 1860; cadet, S. Sttlmtns., Nov., 1882; ed. King's Coll., Camb.; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Dec., 1885; ag. collr. of land rev., Malacca, May, 1886; 2nd ast. prot. of Chinese, May, 1887; ag. asst. prot. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1888; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1888; passed exams. in Chinese and Malay; J.P., ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 1888; ast. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, June, 1890; ag. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1890; ditto, S. Sttlmtns., Nov. and Dec., 1891, to June, 1893; ast. prot. Chinese, Penang, 1893; prot. of Chinese, S. Sttlmtns., Apr., 1895; ag. audr.-gen., S.S., May, 1901, to July, 1902; pres., municipal comsnrs., Singapore, Sept., 1903; seconded for spec. serv. in Transvaal as prot. of Chinese, 25th Dec., 1903; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Mar., 1906; confirmed, ditto, Feb., 1907.
EVELYN, EDWARD A.—2nd clk. to regisr., St. Kitts, 1899; govt. offr., Sandy Point, Apr., 1903; govt. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, June, 1906; clk., regisr.'s off., May, 1906.

EVELYN, EDWARD ERNEST.—B. 1864; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 1883; ast. clk., govt. savings bk., 1883; ch. clk., 1884; rev. offr., St. Lucia, Jan., 1885, to Nov., 1890; accent., P.W.D., Nov., 1890, to Jan., 1893; audit clk., Jan., 1893, to Mar., 1894; ch. clk., govt. off., and clk. of coun., Apr., 1894; ast. col. sec., S. Leone, Nov., 1900; acted as col. sec. for several periods since 1901.
EVELYN, E. F.—Accntnt. of post office, Barbados, Sept., 1880.

EVELYN, MERRIT.—2nd clk. to pres., St. Kitts, Dec., 1878; ch. clk., treasy., Nevis, 1881; ag. treas., Nevis, July, 1882-1883; rev. offr. in charge, Nevis, treasy., 1883; prot. of imgrts., Nevis, 1883; land and house tax comsnr., 1892; ag. mag., Nevis, 1894-5, 1895-6; water comsnr., Nevis, 1901; mem. quarantine bd., Nevis, 1903.

EVERARD, W.—Supernum. col. sec.'s office, Barbados, Sept., 1868; landing waiter, customs, Dec., 1868; ag. inspr. of inl. rev. offrs., Jan., 1884, to Dec., 1885; ch. clk., P.O., Mar., 1885; sen. landing waiter, customs, June, 1886.
EVERETT, COL. SIR W., K.C.M.G.—B. 1844; apptd. one of Brit. comsnrs. in Paris for settlement of W. African affairs, 1895-1898.
EVERY, THE REV. EDWARD FRANCIS, M.A.—Bishop of the Falklands, 1902.

EWART, RAYMOND MAHMAIDUKE.—Ed. at St. David's, Glas.; 1891, Manipur field force, ast. to chief transport offr.; 1892 to 1894, 1st clk. Brit. vice-consulate, Arabia; ag. Brit. vice-consul for Hodeida and Cameron, 1894-95; Aden pol., 1895; supt. of Zanzibar pol.; ast. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 8th Nov., 1895; Mbaruk rebellion, 1896; supt. of pol., 1906.

EWING, Hon. THOMAS THOMSON.—Min. for defence, C. of Aust.; M.P. for Richmond (N.S.W.); vice-pres. of exec. coun., 6th July to 13th Oct., 1906; ag. postmr.-gen., 1906; min. for home affairs, 13th Oct., 1906, to 24th Jan., 1907; by profession a licensed surveyor; M.L.A., for the Richmond, 1885-94; and for Lismore, 1894-1901; was sometime chrmn. of partly. pub. wks. comtee., N.S.W.; chrmn. of royal comsn. on rly. exten. into city of Sydney; ret. to 1st house of reps., C. of Aust., Mar., 1901, re-elected Dec., 1903, and Dec., 1906: joint author of "Progress of Australia during the Nineteenth Century," and of various publications on Australian subjects.

EZECHIEL, P. H.—B. 1875; B.A., Bombay, 1889 (et. 14); first in Woolwich competn., 1893; B.A., London, 1894 (2nd exhibn. at matricn., 1892); major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., 1896; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. elk., C.O., 11th Oct., 1898; passed final exam. for Bar, 1902; sec. to Crown agts. for the Colonies, May, 1905.
FACEY, LUTHER HERBERT.—3rd cl. elk., prisons dept., Jamaica, June, 1877; 2nd cl. elk., June, 1879; 1st cl. elk., police and prisons dept., Nov., 1892.

FAGAN, JOSEPH PATRICK, L.R.C.S.I., L.R.C.P., L.M., D.P.H. (Dublin), Dip. Trop. Med.—B. 1867; med. offr. in Niger coast prot. under F.O., 13th Apr., 1894; in medical charge in operations against Chief Nana, Benin River (spec. ment. in desp., Queen's medal and clasp) 1894; in operations against the King of Benin, with the Aro field force (medal and clasp) 1901 and 1902; sen. med. offr., May, 1903; dep. P.M.O., N. Nigeria, 3rd Aug., 1907.

FAIRBAIRN, JOHN.—B. 1863; temporary elk. in legis. coun. off., Cape Town, Apr., 1881, to 30th Apr., 1889; crown lands off., 31st May, 1890, to 31st July, 1891; elk. to regisr. of mines, Prince Albert, 1st Aug., 1891, to 31st May, 1892; legis. coun. off., 1st June, 1892, to 30th Sept., 1893, and 1st Jan. to 31st May, 1899; elk. of the papers and contree. elk., 1st July, 1899; elk. asst. and taxing offr., 1st Jan., 1904; hon. sec., Central Good Hope Red Cross comtee., Victoria League for concentration camp relief, etc., etc.; is an esquire of the order of St. John of Jerusalem, in England, 1901; S. African War medal and clasp, 1899-1901.

FAIRBAIRN, ROBERT, I.S.O. (1906).—Second master, boys' schl., Perth, W. Australia, 1859; elk. to bench of mags., landing waiter, and postmtr., Vasse, 1862; res. mag., mag. of local ct., and dist. regisr., 1875; Newcastle, 1875; Vasse, 1880; Kimberley, 1883; Fremantle, 1886.

FALCK, DAVID GEORGE ANSON.—Joined post and tel. dept., O.F.S., Nov., 1881; postmtr. and telegraphist, Winburg, Nov., 1881; landdrost's elk., Bethlehem, Aug., 1889; ag. landdrost, Heilbron, Apl., 1890; sec., G.P.O., O.F.S., Aug., 1892; landdrost, Ficksburg, 1894 (not assumed); postmtr.-gen., O.F.S., 1st Jan., 1895; ditto, O.R.C., Mar., 1900; M.L.C., 27th June, 1904.
FALSHAW, PERCY SCOTT.—M.R.C.V.S., Govt. vet. surgeon, Singapore, S. Stthtns., 15th June, 1893.

FARQUHARSON, NORMAN DOUGLAS.—Chief acctnt., refugee camps dept., O.R.C., 1st Sept., 1901; chief asstnt., repatriation (subsequently govt. relief) dept., O.R.C., 13th June, 1902; civil comsnr., Bloemfontein, 1st Sept., 1904.
FARRAR, NICHOLAS.—Supernum., P.W.dept., Br. Guiana, 1882; 3rd cls. elk., G.P.O., 1893; 2nd cls. elk., audit dept., 1900; postmtr.-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., Jan., 1905.

FARRER, ROLAND J.—Ed. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxon.; cadet, S. Stthtns., Nov., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Mar., 1897, to Sep., 1898, Dec., 1898, to Mar., 1900; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, Mar., 1900; ag. dist. offr., Dingdings, May, 1900; ag. supt. educ., Penang, June to Aug., 1900; head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., June, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, May, 1902; confirmed July, 1904; ag. dep. pub. proser., July, 1904; resumed duty as dist. offr., B. Mertajam, Feb., 1905.
FAULKNER, E.—Ed. at the gram. schl., Free town; brigade office elk., S. Leone, 1876 to 1883; ch. elk. col. secretariat, 1883; asst. col. sec., 1889.

FAURE, HON. SIR P. H., K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1848; sec. for native affairs, Cape, July, 1890; col. sec., May, 1893; sec. for agricult., Jan., 1896 to Oct., 1898; col. sec., 1892-04; M.L.A. for Namaqualand, 1904; col. sec., June, 1907.

FAWCETT, WILLIAM, B.Sc. (LOND.), F.L.S.—B. 1851; asst. botanical dept., Br. Museum, 1880; dir. pub. gardens and plantations, Jamaica, 1886; mem. bd. of govts., Jamaica institute, 1887; chrmn., ditto, 1888-89 and 1906-07; nom. M.L.C., 1896; mem. bd. of agric., 1900; dep. chrmn. of agric. soc., 1901.

FAWKES, ARCHIBALD WALTER, K.C.—Ed. Repton schl.; B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1879; registr., sup. ct. comsnr. of ct. of requests and of stamps, Malacca, 1884; ag. registr., sup. ct., Penang, 1885; pol. mag. and coroner, Gibraltar, 1886; acted as atty.-gen., 1890 and 1891; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 1892; puisne judge, O.R.C., 1901.
FEILDEN, CAPT. R. B.—Capt., R.A.—Ed. Wellington Coll.; lieut. R.A., July, 1894; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, May, 1896; to high comsnr., Cyprus, 1892; and to gov., Br. Guiana, 1898.

FELL, T. E., B.A., Cantab., 1897—Apptd. elk., col. sec.'s off., C. Coast, 2nd Oct., 1897; asst. col. sec., 14th Sep., 1899; inspr., prevent. services, 4th Dec., 1899; ag. trav. comsnr., Jan., 1901; trav. comsnr., 16th June, 1902; ag. comsnr. for nat. affairs, 26th June, 1902.

FERGUSON, DONALD.—B. 1839; elected to provincial legislature, Pr. Ed. Is., 1878; appd. to provl. cabinet in 1879, with the portfolio pub. wks.; resig. the latter office in 1880, where was appd. provl. sec. and comsnr. of pub. labs.; served as deleg. to Downing st. in 1883, regarding communication between P. E. Is. and mainland; ret. from provincial govt., 1890; called to the senate of Canada, 1893; mem. of cabinet, ex-officio portfolio, 1894; ditto, 1896; resigned with premier in same year.

FERGUSSON, CLAUD.—Served during S. African war in I.Y. (King's and Queen's medals and clasps, D.C.M.); asst. sec., repatriation comsnr., Philippolis, Aug., 1902; sec., relat. comsnr., Bethulie, July, 1903; admstr., govt. relief dept., Senekal, Nov., 1903; inspr. of accs. govt. relief dept., O.R.C., May, 1904; asst. acctnt., educn. dept., O.R.C., Sept., 1904; asst. acctnt., ditto, 23rd June to 31st Oct., 1906.
FERNANDEZ, HENRY.—Clerical asst. treas. Br. Guiana, 1887; 5th class elk., 1889; 4th class, 1891.

FERNANDO, MARCUS H.—Ed. Royal Coll. Colombo, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; Ceylon Govt. Univ. and Gilchrist scholar, 1883; B.Sc., 1886; M.B., 1888; gold medallist in med. and forensic med.; M.D., 1889; fellow of Univ. Coll., Lond., 1890; col. surg., civil med. dept., Ceylon; registr. of Ceylon Med. Coll., lecturer on pathology, 1891; and analyst to gov't. of Ceylon; reported on epidemics of malarial fever, 1895; also in Bombay, 1897, on epidemics of plague; physician, gen. hosp., Colombo, 1897.
FERREIRA, P. J., C.M.G. (1880).—Was comdt. of local cavalry in the Transvaal against Sikukuni.
FFOLIOTT, Lieut.-Col. O. HUMAN.—Served in Impl. army, 1888-81; mil. sec. col. forces, Cape, June, 1882.
FFRENCH-MULLEN, VINCENT, L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. med. offr., Port Maria, Jamaica, Apr., 1881.

FIDDES, GEORGE VANDELEUR, C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1905), B.A.—B. 1858; ed. at Dulwich Coll., and late scholar of Brasenose Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., 25th Mar., 1881; priv. sec. to Earl of Onslow, Feb., 1887; and to Baron H. de Worms (afterwards Lord Pirbright), Feb., 1888, to Aug., 1892; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, 11th Mar., 1896; 1st class clk., 1896; imperial sec. and acontnt. to Sir A. Milner, high comsnr. for S. Africa, Sept., 1897; political sec. to Lord Roberts at Pretoria, June, 1900; sec. to the Transvaal administration, Dec., 1900; returned to C. O., 1902, as prin. clk.; accounting offr., 1907.

FIDDIAN, ALEXANDER.—B. 1875; ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff, and Oxford; schlr. Pembroke Coll., 1893; 1st class classical honours, 1895; Goldsmiths' co. exhibn., 1895; 1st class lit. hum., 1897; B.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., C.O., Oct. 12th, 1897; asst. priv. sec. to Rt. Hon. Alfred Lyttelton, S. of S. for the colonies, 1st Apr., 1905; ditto to Earl of Elgin, Dec., 1905; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1907.

FIELDING, HON. WILLIAM STEVENS.—B. 1848; mem. for Halifax, Nova Scotia assem., 1882; declined premiership, but entered govt. without office in that year, and continued in that position till May, 1884, when he resign.; premier, provincial sec., and treas., Aug., 1884; min. of finance in Sir W. Laurier's admtn., June, 1896.
FINLAYSON, GEORGE ALEXANDER.—Bacteriologist, Singapore, May, 1903; pathologist, May, 1906.

FIRMSTONE, HAROLD WILLIAM.—B. 1868; ed. at Rugby and Hertford Coll., Oxon (scholar); cadet, S. Sttlnts., 1890; passed final exam. in Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. dist. offr., South Malacca, May, 1894; ag. ast. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1896; dist. offr. P.W., Oct., 1896; ast. protec. Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1897; ast. protec. Chinese, supt. Indian imgrts., and mun. comsnr. for Penang, Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1902, and from Aug., 1902; ag. prot. of Chinese, S.S., Feb. to July, 1902; ag. ast. disto, Penang, Aug., 1902, to Feb., 1903, and from Aug., 1903; collr. of land rev. and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Jan., 1905; mun. comsnr. for Malacca, Apr., 1905; ag. res. coun., Malacca, Sept., 1907.

FISCHER, ABRAHAM.—Attorney, Cape Colony, 7th Dec. 1871; notary, 14th Dec., 1871; attorney and notary, Griqualand West, 29th May, 1872; advocate and attorney, O.F.S., 27th July, 1875; notary, 2nd Mar., 1878; advocate, attorney, etc., O.R.C., 17th Mar., 1903; formerly mem. of Orange Free State Volksraad, with seat on exec. coun.; chrmn. of cent. comte. of Orangia Union, 1906; prime min. and col. sec., O.R.C., 1907.

FISHER, STANLEY.—Ed. at Westminster and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; M.A., 1891; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple and Lincoln's Inn (called 1890); apptd. pros. of dist. ct. of Kyrenia, Cyprus, May, 1902; acted as King's advocate, May to Oct., 1902, from May to Oct., 1905, and from Mar. to July, 1907; ag. puisne judge, Apr., 1906, to Feb., 1907; passed exam. in modern Greek, Dec., 1903; joint comsnr. for compiling revised edition of Statute Laws of Cyprus (published 1907) for which recd. thanks of legis. coun. and S. of S.

FISHER, HON. SYDNEY, B.A., P.C. (Brome).—B. 1850; ed. at High Schl. and McGill Univ., Montreal, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; unsuccessfully contested Brome for seat in H. of Commons, Canada, 1880; elected for Brome at general elections, 1882 and 1887; defeated, 1891; again returned for Brome, 1896, and became min. of agric. in Sir Wilfrid Laurier's govt.; re-elected by acclamation at general elections, 1900 and 1904; organised and directed management of Canadian section of Paris Exhibition, 1900; visited Japan to inquire into openings for Canadian trade, 1903.

FISHER, WILLIAM WOODHOUSE.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1877; ag. crown counsel, N. circuit, Ceylon, Jan., 1885, to Feb., 1886; ag. dist. judge, Matara, June, 1886, to Feb., 1887; ag. crown counsel, Kandy, Feb., 1887; crown counsel, N.W. prov., Ceylon, Mar., 1887; sec. to comte. for drafting code of civ. procedure, June, 1887, to May, 1888; additional crown counsel, N. circuit, Jan., 1890; pres. dist. ct., Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1891; ag. puisne judge, 1894; mag. Jamaica, 1895; puisne judge, S. Sttlnts., Apr., 1905.

FITCHETT, WILLIAM HERBERT.—Ck., agric. dept., Cape Colony, 22nd Feb., 1895; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Wodehouse, 27th Sept., 1898; Port Elizabeth, 23rd Oct., 1899; Colesberg, 16th Mar., 1900; Britstown, 16th Aug., 1901; acted as C.C. and R.M., Britstown, 1902; asst. regir., high ct., and chief clk. to the sheriff of the O.R.C., 29th July, 1902; acted as regir. and sheriff in 1903 and 1904.

FITZGERALD, THE HON. GEORGE PARKER.—B. 1843; mem. of cabinet, Tasmania, without portfolio, 1888-92; while mem. of Tasmanian cabinet was mainly instrumental in obtaining construction of Strahan-Zeehan rly. connecting silver fields with deep water port; was also principal mover in the cause of technical educn.; also chmn. of tech. educn. comte.; was (with Hon. Wm. Hart) apptd. liquidator of the Bank of Van Diemen's Land in Oct., 1891.

FITZPATRICK, HON. CHARLES.—Born in Quebec, 1853; Dufferin medallist, law faculty, Laval Univ., 1876; one of the counsel for the defence of Louis Riel at Regina in 1885; elected to Quebec legislature, 1890; to Dominion parl., 1896, again in 1900 and 1904; solr.gen., Canada, 1886; atty.-gen., 1901; ch. just. of Canada, 1906.

FLEISCHER, THEODORE T. C.—Out-door offr. of customs and treasy. dept., 1st Aug., 1878; 4th clk., customs and treasy. dept., 25th Oct., 1878; 3rd clk., 22nd June, 1879; 2nd clk., 16th May, 1882; apptd. 1st class clk. in the treas. dept. in 1885, on separation of the customs from treasy.; ag. chief clk. and book-keeper, treasy., 9th March, 1899, to 10th Jan., 1900; ag. ch. clk. and cashier, 5th June, 1900, to 31st May, 1901.

FLEMING, Sir FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1842, called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Nov., 1866; crown solr. for Mauritius, 1869; dist. and stip. mag., 1872; acted as dist. judge, Seychelles, 1874; dist. judge, Jamaica, 1876; atty.-gen., Barbados, 1878; acted as ch. just., Sept., 1878, to Mar., 1879; acted as ch. just., St. Lucia, July, 1879; priv. sec. to Sir G. C. Strahan, administering the govt. of the Cape, in 1880. puisne judge, Br. Guiana, 1881; acted as atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1882; Queen's advoc., Ceylon, 1883; atty.-gen., 1884; acted as ch. just., Mar. to Dec., 1885; col. sec., Natal, July, 1886, but did not take up app'tmt., being apptd. col. sec. of Mauritius, Dec., 1886; administ. gov't., July, 1887, to Dec., 1888; col. sec., Hong Kong, 1889; administ. the gov't., Feb. to Dec., 1890; gov't., S. Leone, 1892; ditto, Leeward Is., 1895; ret., 1901.

FLEMING, Sir Sanford, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.E., C.M.G. (1877), LL.D. (St. Andrews), 1884—B. 1827; engrn.-in-ch., Northern Railway, Canada, 1856 to 1863; deleg. to Canadian and Br. gov't. from Red River settlers, 1863, in reference to Pacific rly.; nominated by provs. of Canada, N. Brunswick and N. Scotia; gov't. engrn.-in-ch. inter-col. rly., 1863; engrn.-in-ch., Canadian Pacific rly., 1871 to 1880; elected chancellor, Queen's Univ., Canada, 1880; re-elected 1883, and again 1886; deleg. from Canadian inst. and Amer. meteorological soc., N. York; to internat. geograph. congress at Venice, 1881; representing the Dominion of Canada at internat. prime meridian confoce, Washington, 1884; dir. Hudson Bay Co., Canadian Pacific rly., etc.; author of "The Inter-ool., an Historical Sketch," 1876, "Old to New Westminster," 1884, memoirs on "A Prime Meridian for all Nations," "Universal Time," and various other wks.; deleg. to col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; on special mission to Hawaii, 1894, with Mr. Mercer of the C.O., negotiating for landing place for Pacific cable.

FLETCHER, A. G. M.—Ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Hong Kong, 1901; seconded for famine relief work in Kwang Si, May-June, 1903; ag. asst. reg'rs. gen., 1903-4; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1905-7; priv. sec. to O.A.G., 1907.
FLETCHER, Geo. Wm.—Temporarily employed in Imp. commissariat, Hobart, 1851; clk. col. treasy., Hobart, July, 1852; receiver and paymr., Jan., 1886.

FLETCHER, Stanley Hewitt.—B. 1866; Mem. Inst. Chartd. Acctnts., 1892; 2nd acctnt. Br. C. Africa Prot., June, 1893; collr. and mag., Zomba dist., Apr., 1900; British vice-consul and admtn. agt., Chinde, Jan., 1901 (Cent. Africa medal and clasp).

FLETCHER, Wm., D.S.O.—Surgeon, capt. militia med. staff corps; 6 mos. in 1898 attached to regulars at R.A.M.C. depot, Aldershot; med. off., Niger Coast Prot. (now So. Nigeria), 1898; served with Ashanti exped., 1900 (medal and clasp); Aro exp'dn., 1901-2 (medal and clasp).

FLINT, Thomas Barnard, M.A., LL.B., D.C.L.—B. 1847; ed. at Mt.
