Allison Coll., Sackville, New Brunswick; B.A., 1867; M.A., 1872, and Harvard Univ., Boston; LL.B., 1871; barrister-at-law, 1872; sheriff, Yarmouth Co., 1883-7; asst. clk., House of Assembly, Nova Scotia, 1887 to 1891; elected to H. of C., Canada, 1891; re-elected, 1896 and 1900; clk. of H. of C., Canada, 11th Nov., 1902; rec'd degree of D.C.L. from Mt. Allison Univ., 1903.

FLYNN, Edmund James, K.C., LL.D.—Born 1847, at Percé, Gaspé; ed. Quebec Seminary and Laval Univ. (L.L.L. with honours, 1873), LL.D. (Lav., 1878); Q.C. 1887; called to the bar 1873; prof. of Roman law, Laval Univ., since 1874; mem. legis. assem., Quebec, Apr., 1878; mem. exec. coun. and comsnr. crown lands in Chapleau gov't., Oct., 1879, to July, 1882; comsnr. of rlyw. in Ross gov't., Feb., 1884, and solr.-gen., May, 1885.

FOAKER, Frederic George.—Ed. at United Service Coll., Westward Ho, and King's Coll., London; Impl. Brit. E. Africa Co., Oct., 1889, to Mar., 1894; 2nd cls. asst., Uganda Prot., 26th Aug., 1894; collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1902; Impl. Brit. E. Africa Co., medal, Uganda, 1897-1898.

FORAN, William Robert.—Ed. at Prior Park, Bath, and St. Edmund's Coll., Ware, Herts.; served in Somerset L.I., I.Y., and on transport staff during S. African war, 1899 to 1902; resig., June, 1903; Transvaal civ. ser., 1903 to 1904; inspr. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 16th May, 1904; asst. dist. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 15th Oct., 1904; S. African medals, Queen's and King's, 5 bars.

FORD, Henry Francis.—Entered G.P.O., Cape, 1868; promoted to deeds office, 1872; apptd. to inaugurate deeds office at Kokstad, and served as clk. to ch. mag. Transkei; in charge of deeds office, King William's Town, 1884; ch. clk., insolvency branch, master's office, Capetown, Jan., 1888; registr. and mstr. high ct., Griqualand, July, 1890.

FORD, Joseph Charles.—B. 1852; ed. in France (Academie de Douai), and by private tuition by Dr. Langley at Wolverhampton; sec. inst. of Jamaica, 1889; one of the comsnrs. of Jamaica internat. exhib., 1891, and spec. comsnr. for Bahamas at that exhib.; apptd. supt. of gvt. printing off., Feb., 1891; co-ed. and compiler of "Handbook of Jamaica."

FORD, Sir Theodore Thomas, Kt. Bach. (1888).—B. 1829; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1866; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1874; ag. judge of Penang, 1874, to Apr., 1876; resig. and returned to England; re-apptd. senior puisne judge, 1876; ch. justice, 1886; ret., 1889.

FORDE, Robert Michael.—B. 1861; ast. col. surg., Gold Coast, Nov., 1891; ag. dist. comsnr., Axim, 1892; on special service on Anglo-French bndy. comsnr., Feb. to July, 1892; med. offr. special mission to Kunasi, Dec., 1894, to Jan., 1895; col. surg., Gambia, Feb., 1895; J.P. and comsnr. of ct. of requests, chimn., bd. of health and quarantine bd., health offr. of Bathurst and med. offr., Gambia field force, 1901 (medal and clasp); received thanks of S. of S. for "sleeping sickness" research, 1901; supernumerary M.L.C. on several occasions; ag. ch. mag. and collr. of cust. in 1906; prin. med. offr., S. Leone, Feb., 1907.

FOREMAN, John—Mont., France, L.K.Q.C.P.I. and L.M.; med. offr., Virgin Is., 1878; ag. mag., Virgin Is., 1880; M.L.C., 1889, and mem. ex. coun., 1885; ag. med. offr., Dominica, 1882-1884; med. offr., Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1889-1893; med. supt. leper asylum and Pogson hosp., and med. offr., dist. 5, Sandy Point, St. Kitts, 1893; surg.-capt., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force.

FORESTIER-WALKER, Gen. Sir Frederic William Edward Forestier, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.B. (1894), C.B. (1878), C.M.G. (1886), Knight of Grace of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1844; ed. at Sandhurst; entd. Scots. Guards, 1862; capt. and lieut.-col., 1873; col., 1878; maj.-gen., 1877; lieut.-gen., 1895; gen., 1902; served on staff in Kafir war, 1877-8; mil. sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, 1878-9; in Zulu war, 1879 (ment. in despa.); A.A. and Q.M.G., home district during war in Egypt, 1882; A.A. and Q.M.G., Bechuanaland, 1884; commndd. 2nd brig. at Aldershot, 1889-90; commndd. troops in Egypt, 1890-95; western dist., 1895-99; and troops in S. Africa, 1899; commndd. lines of communication in S. African war, 1899-1901; and troops in Cape Colony, 1900-1; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar 1st Aug., 1906.

FORGET, AMEDEE EMANUEL.—B. 1847; ed. Marieville Coll., Canada; called to the bar, 1871; served as sec. to council of the bar, Quebec; clk. of coun. and priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., N.W. Territ., 1876; aast. comsnr. of Indian affairs for Manitoba and N.W.T., 1888; mem. of coun. of pub. instn., 1893; Indian comsnr., 1895; lieut.-gov., N.W.T., 11th Oct., 1898; lieut.-gov. of Saskatchewan, 1st Sept., 1905.

FORREST, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOHN, P.C. (1897), G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1891), LL.D. (Camb., 1897), F.R.G.S., F.G.S., F.L.S., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1847; Hon. Fellow of the Italian geog. socy.; Hon. Fellow of the imp. geog. societies of Vienna and St. Petersburg; knight of the Italian crown; entered the survey dept. of W. Australia, 1865; commanded exploring expedns. in 1869 into the interior in search of the remains of Dr. Leichhardt; in 1870 from Perth to Adelaide along the S. Coast; and in 1874 from Champion Bay, on the W. Coast; to the overland telegraph between Adelaide and Port Darwin, a journey of nearly 2,000 miles. For these services received thanks of gov. and legis. coun., and gold medal of the Roy. Geog. Socy. of London, 22nd May, 1876, and a grant in fee of 5,000 acres of crown land; dep. survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1876; in 1878 and 1882 conducted the trigonometrical surveys of the Nichol Bay district, and the Gascoyne and Lyons District, in N.W. Australia, 1878; acted as comsnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen.; comsnr. of crown lands and survr.-gen. of W. Australia, 1883 to Dec., 1890, during which time was mem. of the exec. and legis. couns.; in Mar., 1883 and 1886, reported on the Kimberley dist., N. Australia, and selected the towns, etc., of Wyndham in Cambridge Gulf; in Dec., 1890, was sent for to form first ministry under responsible govt., in which he took the position of premier and treas., and which he continuously held for over 10 yrs., resigning on 13th Feb., 1902, to join the 1st Commonwealth Govt. of Australia; was one of the representatives of W. Australia at the National Australian federation convention, held at Sydney, 1891; author of "Explorations in Australia," 1875, "Notes on Western Australia," 1883, 1884, and 1885; deleg. to col. conf., 1887; represented W. Australia at the federal convention at Adelaide, Mar., 1897, and in June of the same year was present as rep. of the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; again rep. W. Australia at fed. convention, Sydney, 1897; Melbourne, 1898; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as postmaster-general, Jan., 1901; defence minister, 1901 to 1903; and min. for home affairs until Apr., 1904; treas. of Commonwealth, July, 1905; resig., July, 1907.

FORTH, J. OGILVIE.—Audit clk., Fiji, Aug., 1883; acctnt. native taxes and clk. nat. accta., June, 1889; ch. clk. audit office, Oct., 1890; col. auditor, Nov., 1891; Oct., 1890, proceeded to Tonga to audit accta. of Tongan govt., for which he received thanks of King George Tubou's cabinet.

FOSBERY, WIDENHAM FRANCIS WIDENHAM, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1869; cons. agt., Niger Coast Prot., 1893; ag. vice-cons., 1894-5; dist. comsnr., 1896; ag. div. comsnr., 1897-8; pol. offr., central division expdn., 1898 (severely wounded); res., Benin City, 1898; pol. offr., Benin Terr. expdn., 1899 (medal with clasp); ag. div. comsnr., 1900; pol. offr., Ishaan expdn., 1901 (medal with clasp); div. comsnr., 1902; pol. offr., Asaba Hint. expdn., 1902; senior div. comsnr., 1903; pol. offr., Igarra expdn., 1903 (clasp); ag. high comsnr., 1903-4; ag. sec. to adminstr., 1904; dep. high comsnr., Sept., 1904, to Feb., 1905, and again in Sept., 1905; prov. comsnr., 1906; ag. col. sec., S. Nigeria, May to June, 1906; ag. gov., June to Aug., 1906; dep. gov. and ag. col. sec., Aug. to Oct., 1906.

FOSTER, THE HON. GEO. E., B.A., D.C.L.—Ed. common and superior schls., King's County, New Bruns.; Douglas gold medallist, Univ. N.B., 1886; B.A., 1868; medallist, ancient rhetoric, Edin., 1873; prof. classics, Univ. N.B., 1873-9; returned to parlmt., 1882; min. of marine and fisheries for Dominion, Dec., 1885; min. of finance, 1888 to 1896; represented Canada at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894.
FOSTER, RICHARD WITTY, M.L.A., S. Aust., 1893.—Comsnr. of pub. wks., 1890; ditto, and min. of industry, 1902-1904; ditto, and min. of agric., 1st March to 26th July, 1905.
FOSTON, EDMUND CHRISTOPHER.—L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.); col. surg., prov. Wellesley (South), S. Sttlmts., 24th Aug., 1891; supt. S. S. emigt. depot, Negapatam, 17th Nov., 1901.

FOWLE, CAPT. HENRY WALTER HAMILTON.—Ed. at Shrewsbury and Clare Coll., Camb.; late 4th Arg. and Suth. Highrs.; ch. sec. for permits, peace presvn. ord., 1902, and regr. of Asiatics, 1903; Roberts' Horse, S.A., war, 1899-1900; seconded for duty under mil. govt., Pretoria, 1900; mil. comsnr. of pol. and asst. prov. marshal, Heidelberg and dist., 1900-1901; aast. comsnr. of pol., Pretoria, 1901; polit. offr. to mil. comsnr. of pol., Johannesburg; mil. intell. offr., Johannesburg and dist.; asst. to dir., mil. intell., army headqrs., and to prov. marshal, army headqrs., 1901-1902; 2 medals, 6 clasps; served on staff of high comsnr., S. Africa since Nov., 1902; is now aast. imp. sec. to high comsnr.; J.P., Transvaal.

FOWLER, FRANK.—B. 1864; asst. clk., pub. wks. dept., Brit. Guiana, Feb., 1878; 3rd clk., Jan., 1881; 2nd clk., Apr., 1884; 1st clk. and draftsman, gov't. ld. dept., Nov., 1884; 3rd govt. survr., May, 1886; senr. govt. survr., Apr., 1896; apptd. J.P., Nov., 1900; asst. comsnr. of lands and mines, Apr., 1902; comsnr. of lands and mines, 1st Apr., 1903; mem. of local govt. bd.; mem. of bd. of examrs. for land survrs.; mem. of bd. of agric.; has held numerous acting appointments.

FOWLER, GEORGE MERRICK, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1852; ed. at Chelt. Coll.; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., 5th Jan., 1874; ag. asst. clk., cust. of cust., Jaffna, 1st July, 1876; offr. of cls. V., 1st Sept., 1876; ag. landing and tide survr., customs, Galle, 25th Sept., 1877; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, 1st Feb., 1878; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Feb., 1878; ag. off. asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., 4th Nov., 1879; ditto, N. Cent. Prov., 9th Feb., 1880; ditto, N. Prov., 10th Mar., 1882; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, 1st Apr., 1883; ag. dist. judge, Chilau, 21st Oct., 1883; ag. pol. mag., Galle, 10th Oct., 1884; ag. dist. judge, Tangalla, 8th Jan., 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Vavuniya-Vilankulam and Mullaittivu, 1st Feb., 1886; offr. of cls. III., 1st May, 1886; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, 15th Apr., 1891; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, 7th Dec., 1894; offr. of cls. II., 16th Dec., 1895; ag. dist. judge, Kalutara, May, 1897; ag. prin. ast. col. sec., 5th July, 1897; ag. dist. judge, Negombo, 23rd Oct., 1897; govt. agt., Ratnapura, 23rd June, 1898; ag. govt. agt., W. prov., 1st June, 1899; offr. of cls. I., 1st Jan., 1900; govt. agt., W. prov., 10th Feb., 1902; ag. aud.-gen., 14th Apr., 1905; ag. col. sec., 8th Sept., 1905; ag. aud.-gen., 3rd Dec., 1906; ag. col. sec., 5th Dec., 1906; controller of rev., 18th May, 1907; ag. col. sec., 11th July, 1907; ret., Sept., 1907.
FOWLIS, Henry George.—Entd. judicial dept., Gambia, 1890; govr.'s off., 1893-1901; col. sec.'s off., 1902-05; ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., 1906.

FOX, Henry Wilson, B.A.—Ed. Charterhouse, Marlborough Coll., Univ. Coll., Lond., Trin. Coll., Cam.; exhibtn., Trin. Coll., 1882; foundation scholar, 1883; natural sci. trip.; exhibtn., Lincoln's Inn, 1888; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1888; priv. sec. to Sir C. Mills, K.C.M.G., 1887 to 1889; admitted to practise in sup. ct., Cape Colony, and high ct., So. Rhodesia, 1894; pub. prosecutor, So. Rhodesia, 1894-1897; manager B.S.A. Co., head office, London, 1898; served during 1896-97 in the Matabeleland and Mashonaland rebellion (medal and clasp and ment. in desps.).
FOX, Walter.—B. 1858; asst. supt., botanic gardens, Singapore, 16th June, 1879; supt., forests and gardens, Penang, 7th Dec., 1903; ag. dir., botanic gardens, S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1907.

FOXON, Cuthbert Colenso.—B. 1867; clk. and Zulu interp. to asst. comsnr. and res. mag. Ndwandwe dist., Zululand, Sept., 1887; transf. to Entonjaneni dist., Apr., 1891; to Lower Umfolosi dist., Feb., 1892; ag. res. mag. there Mar., 1892; ag. sub-inspr. Z'land, pol. for six months, May, 1892, and Apr., 1893; transf. as clk. and Zulu interp. to Nkandhla dist. Nov., 1892; sub-inspr. Z'land pol., Apr., 1893; commanded detachment Z'land pol. with the special comsn. annexation of Sambanas and adjacent territories, May, 1895; Br. res. Amatongaland, Br. Protectorate, June, 1896; deputy comsnr., Sept., 1896; res. mag., Z'land, Dec., 1897; mag., Nkandhla, 1901; J.P. for colony, 1904; mag., Umlalazi, 1905.

FOXON, Frank Ernest.—3rd class clk. civ. serv., Natal, and 2nd clk. and Zulu interp., Umgeni div., Nov., 1880; ag. clk. of ct., regis. circuit ct., and sub-distributor of stamps, Weenen country, 1886; clk. of ct., dep.-clk. of peace, and sub-distributor of stamps, UpperUmkomangti div., May, 1887; admin. native law, Impendlele, Mar., 1889; ditto, Inanda Location, Jan., 1890; ag. mag., Lower Tugela div., Apr. to June, 1890; ag. R.M. at Verulam and Stanger, 1890 to 1894; R.M. for col., and mag., Ndwedwe div., July, 1894; J.P. for col., 1893; served at commencement of Zulu war, 1879, in imp. transport dept., afterwards as lieutenant. Natal N. pioneers (medal and clasp); served as levy leader in command of Sibejpu's contingent and Yamela's mounted men against Dinuzulu in Zululand, 1888; lieut., Natal carbiniers, Apr., 1889, capt., Dec., 1897; mag. Ixopo div., 1897; served through the Boer war, 1899-1900, including the siege of Ladysmith.

FRANCE, Henry Dunlop.—Rev. offr., Anguilla, 1896; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, 1899; tariff clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1901; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1902; supervisor of cust., G. Coast, Sept., 1904.

FRANCIS, Col. Claude Augustus.—Ed. Craven Coll. and Vienna; entd. Impl. Yeo, 1875; C.M.R., 1876; S. African War (medal with clasp) 1877-8; inspr. constab., and armed pol. and J.P., Br. Guiana, 1880; higher grade, 1883; county inspr., 1891; acted as dep. inspr.-gen., 1892, and from Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1896; ag. inspr.-gen., May to Oct., 1896; supt. fire brig., 1895-6; mem. excise, quarantine, and health bds.; author of "A Manual for the Use of Police and Constabulary," "A Criminal Code," 1895, and "A Police Catechism," 1898; sheriff-comdt., armed constab., supt. civ. pol., and J.P., Fiji, 1897; marshal, V.-A. ct.; comdt., European vol. force, which he raised; local col., 1898; M.L.C., 1905; inspr.-gen. of constab., 1906.

FRANCIS, Cyril Gerard Brooke.—Clk. G.P.O., Fiji, 1898; ditto, treasy., 1899; lieut., armed constab., 1901; 2nd command detachment, Nadirivatu, 1902; ag. adjut., A.N.C., and inspr.-pol., 1903; P.S. certif., schl. instruc., Chelsea bks.; passed course musketry and Maxim gun, Hythe, 1905.

FRANKLIN, J. C.—L.R.C.S. and P., Edin., L.F.P. and S., Glasgow; ed. at Queen's Coll., Cork, and Royal Coll. of Surge., Edin.; certif., Lond. Schl. of trop. med.; med. offr. to Impl. forces at home and S. Africa, Dec., 1899, to Sept., 1902; med. offr., Gambia, Apr., 1903; ag. trav. comsnr., McCarthy Prov., Aug. to Dec., 1903; public vaccinator.

FRANKLIN, William.—Asst. regisr., natives, Kimberley and De Beers, Mar., 1877; regisr. deaths, Kimberley and De Beers, 1878; clk. to civil comsnr. and res. mag., Barkly West, Jan., 1879; issuer of passes, Mar., 1879; inspr. of claims, Barkly West, June, 1879; inspr. of Sivonelli's diggings, May-June, 1882; from Oct., 1882, to Aug., 1883, off. valuator of all diamonds exported from the dist. of Barkly West; apptd. regisr. of claims, Barkly West, Sept., 1883; inspr. and regisr. mines and diggings, Barkly West.

FRASER, Charles A.—Ed. at Wellington Coll.; entd. 54th regt., Jan., 1875; resigned, 1877; in colonial forces in S. Africa, 1878 (medal and clasp); asst. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1882; dist. comsnr., 1889; treas., etc., Falkland Is., 1891; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1893; comdt. and prov.-marshal, Bahamas, 1894.

FRASER, Duncan Cameron.—B. 1845; B.A., 1872; called to the bar, 1873; mem. legis. coun., Nova Scotia, 1878; resig. in same year; govt. leader in legis. coun., and mem. of exec. coun., 1888; elected to H.of.C.as mem. for Guysborough, 1891; re-elected 1896 and 1901; judge, sup. ct., Nova Scotia, 1904; lieut.-gov. of Nova Scotia, 27th Mar., 1906.

FRASER, F. Blache.—2nd clk. atty.-gen.'s office, Trinidad, Jan., 1863; promoted to col. sec.'s dept. as 3rd clk., Sept., 1867; 3rd landing waiter, customs dept., Jan., 1875; principal landing waiter, Jan., 1883; ch. clk. customs dept., Apr., 1894.
FRASER, J. J., K.C.—Atty.-gen. and premier, N. Brunswick; ret., 1882; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1883.

FRASER, Malcolm Alexander Clements, F.R.G.S., F.S.S.—B. 1857; ed. at King Edward's schl., Bromsgrove; probation clk. in col. sec.'s office, W. Australia, Apr., 1876; 2nd clk., land and survey dept., June, 1876; transf. to col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1877; clk. and assist. priv. sec. to gov., July, 1878; also asst. clk. to the exec. coun.; clk. to gov., Sir W. C. F. Robinson, K.C.M.G., Apr., 1880; corpdg. clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1881; also meteorological reporter; regisr.-gen., 1891; also regisr. of patents, trade mks., etc.; represented the govt. of W. Aust. at the conference of govt. statisticians apptd. to arrange for a uniform census of Australia, Sydney, Jan., 1900; supt. of census, Western Australia, 1900; govt. statistician, 1901; represented W.A. at conference of statisticians convened to arrange for uniformity in the collection and compilation of statistics throughout the Commonwealth of Aust.

FRASER, MAJOR-GEN. SIR THOMAS, K.C.B. (1900), R.E., C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1840; apptd. C.M.G. for civ. services in connection with the settlement of the Transvaal question; served in expdnry. force in Egypt, 1882, and in the Nile expdnry. force, 1884, with brevet rank 4th class Osmanich, and 3rd class of col.; Medjidie.

FRASER, WILLIAM.—B. 1874; King's student, Aberdeen Univ., 1899-1901; M.A., ditto, 1901; privy coun. certif., 1903; asst. mast., Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, 1901-3; prin., govt. schl., Senekal, 1903-4; inspr. of schl., O.R.C., 1st July, 1904.

FREEER, GERALD DUDLEY, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1888, M.B. (Lond.), 1898, D.P.H. (Lond.), 1904—B. 1866; House surg. gen. hosp., Birmingham; house surg. gen. hosp., Singapore, July, 1890; col. surg. Malacca, Feb., 1893; ag. col. surg. res. Singapore, Oct., 1896; col. surg. res., Penang, 1897; ag. col. surg., Penang, June, 1900; principal, med. schl., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1905; mem. S.S. med. coun., July, 1905.

FREMANTLE, ADMIRAL THE HON. EDMUND ROBERT, R.N., SIR, G.C.B., C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1836; apptd. to the Order of St. M. and St. G. for service during the Ashanti war, 1873-74, having been some time sen. naval offr. on the station.

FRENCH, MAJ.-GEN., SIR GEORGE ARTHUR, R.A., K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1841; ed. at Sandhurst and Woolwich; joined R.A. as lieut. in 1860; proceeded to North America in Dec., 1861, with expeditionary force sent out in consequence of the "Trent Affair"; adjt. R.A., Kingston, from 1862 to 1866; qualified as 1st class gunnery instructor in 1867; 1st class inspr. of warlike stores in 1868; apptd. I.S.W. at Quebec in 1869 on the withdrawal of the Imp. troops; inspr. of artillery, with rank of lieut.-col., in 1870; organised the permanent batteries of artillery in 1871; comsrr. N.W.M.P., and stip. mag. for the territories, in Dec., 1873; raised, organised, and equipped the force; commanded the expdn. sent from the Red River to the base of the Rocky Mountains in 1874; inspr. of warlike stores, Devonport, 1878 to 1883; comdt. Queensland forces, with rank of col., 1883-91; comdt., N. S. Wales forces, 1896.

FRENCH, JOHN KIRIE.—B. 1872; clk., poor law bd., Sandy Point, Mar., 1894; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, Nov., 1897; clk. to regisr. and prov. marshal, Nevis, Sept., 1901; govt. offr., sub-treasy., Sandy Point, St. Kitts, June, 1905; clk., treasy., St. Kitts, Oct., 1907.

FRENCH, SIR SOMERSET RICHARD, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1848; postmr.-gen.; apptd. money order dept., G.P.O., Lond., 30th Aug., 1866; transfld. to sec.'s off. (postal branch), Sept., 1869; in Feb., 1870, asstd. in transfer to State of the undertakings of the telegraph companies in the U.K.; apptd. offr. in charge of intell. branch, telegraphs, and subsequently to control of racing and special arrangements branch of impl. telegraph service; also May to Oct., 1874, supervised surveying branch, home counties dist.; in July, 1878, selected to proceed to Cyprus with expeditionary force under Lieut.-Gen. Sir Garnet Wolseley, to organise a postal and telegraph service on that island. Shortly after return to U.K. was asked to undertake reorganisation of Cape Colony postal service; acted as sec. and acctnt., P.O., Cape Colony, 1st Aug., 1880, to 31st July, 1881, when apptnt. confirmed; introduced P.O. savings bk. system, 1st Jan., 1884; from which date acted as controller of branch in addition to other apptntns.; P.M.G. and gen. man. of telegraphs, Cape Colony and Basutoland, 27th Jan., 1892; also P.M.G., B. Bechuanaaland, 1st Apr., 1893, until 1897, when that territory annexed to Cape Colony; P.M.G., Bechuanaaland Prot., 1897; organised postal and telegraph services of Rhodesia, and acted as gen. man. of Rhodesian telegraph service, 1893-7, and man. Trans-Continental telegraph co., 1893-7; mem. of tender and advisory bds.; agt. gen. for Cape Colony in Lond., 1907.
FRERE, AUBREY TEMPLE.—Cadet, Sarawak service, Oct., 1886; asst. res., May, 1890; res., 2nd class, 4th div., Jan., 1894.

FRERE, BARTLE HENRY TEMPLE, LL.B.—Ed. at Charterhouse school and Trin. Coll., Cam.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1887; pres. dist. court, Cyprus, 1897; transfld. to Gibraltar as pol. mag. and coroner, 1902.

FRERE, HAROLD ARTHUR.—Cadet, Br. North Borneo Co.'s serv., Nov., 1897; asst. supt. of gaols, 2nd May, 1898; ag. supt. of pub. wks., 2nd May, 1898; ag. P.M.G., 16th Sept., 1898; sec. to the commrs., 2nd Aug., 1899; ag. supt. of gaols, 22nd Jan., 1900; editor "British North Borneo Herald," and manager, govt. printing off., 8th May, 1900; inspr. of prisons, 1st Dec., 1901; resign., to take up apptnt. as supt., Georgetown prison, Br. Guiana, June, 1905.

FRETTZ, W. H.—L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Edin.; med. offr., dist. 2, Nevis, Feb., 1882; dist. 3, S. Kitts, May, 1886; dist. 1, S. Kitts, Aug., 1896; official mem. legis. coun., S. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1896; offl. mem., legis. coun., St. K.-N., Nov., 1896, to Dec., 1900; senior med. offr., health offr. and analyser of vital statistics, Feb., 1903; mem., Basseterre town bd., 1903; offl. M.L.C., 1903.

FROST, THE HON. SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1828; M.L.A., C. of G. H., field comdt. of Cape vols., and late of C.M.R.; sec. for agricult., Cape Col., 1893-6; ditto, 1902-1904; M.L.A. for Queenstown, 1904.

FROUDE, ASHLEY A., B.A., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1863; ed. Westminster and Oriel Coll., Oxon; priv. sec. (unpaid) to Sir Robert Herbert at the C.O., Nov., 1886; sec. to roy. comm. for the division of Malta into electoral dists., Jan., 1888; sec. to the Behring Sea comm. at Washington, Jan., 1892; on the staff of the Br. agt., Behring Sea arbitration, 1892-3.

FRY, R. S.—Trigonometrical asst., survey dept., S. Sttlmnts., Jan., 1881; res. Aug., 1884; rejoined July, 1885; dist. survr., Sept., 1885; ch. survr., Dec., 1888; observer for time balls and dep. regist. of shipping, Jan., 1894.

FRY, WM. HERBERT, L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.).—B. 1868; late house surg. and house physician, N.W., Lond. hosp.; asst. electrotherapeutic physician, Charing Cross hosp.; house surg., Liverpool hosp.; house surgeon, S. Sttlmnts., 13th Aug., 1896; supernumerary col. surg., Sing., 1st Apr., 1900; col. surg., prov. Wellesley, South, 17th Nov., 1900; ditto, prov. Wellesley, North. 1st Mar., 1901; asst. col. surg. res., Penang, 18th Mar., 1901; state surg., Pahang, F.M.S., June, 1905.
FULLARTON, R.—Harbourmr., Melbourne, 1877; ch. harbourmr., pres. pilot bd., and chmn. steam navig. bd., 1882; capt. coming, Victorian naval brigade, 1871; mem. of coun. of defence, 1884.
FULLER, A. J.—M.L.A. for Tembuland, Cape Colony, since 1898; sec. for agric., Feb., 1904, to June, 1907.

FULLER, F. C. F. D., C.M.G. (1906).—Cadet, Fiji, 1884; res. comsnr., Rotumah, 1889; discomsnr., Lagos, 1892; res. of Ibadan, 1897; col. treas., Lagos, 1901; asst. sec. to govt., Malta 1902; mem. exec. coun.; ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 1905.

FULLER, Sir THOS. EKINS, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1831; emignt. agt. in Lond. for Cape of Good Hope, 1872; resigned, 1876; served as mem. of house of ass. for 23 years; agent-gen. in Lond. for Cape of Good Hope, 1902-7; J.P., Cape Town; has served on various councils and comiteses, in Cape Colony.

FURLONG, HON. L. O'BRIEN—B. 1856; M.H.A., St. John's, E. Newfied., 1893; mem. exec. coun. and chmn. bd. of wks., Apr. to Dec., 1894; speaker, House of Assem., to 1896, and again from 1899 to 1904; man., govt. savings bk., 1905.

FYSH, HON. SIR PHILIP OAKLEY, K.C.M.G. (1895), D.C.L.—B. 1835; maj. (ret.) Tasmania volr. forces; mem. of exec. coun. and mag., Tasmania; mem. of fed. coun. of Australia, and mem. of Commonwealth parlmt., and P.M.G. in 1st Commonwealth min.; recd. hon. degree, D.C.L. Oxon, on occasion of delegn. to Lond. to present Commonwealth constitution to Impl. parlmt.; was mem. of legis. coun. and House of Assem., premier, ch. sec., and treas. in the ministries of Tasmania, which from time to time held office between 1866 and 1898; agt.-gen. for Tasmania in Lond., 1899-1900.
FYSON, PERCYVAL WILLIFORD.—Asst. govt. printer, Perak, 1896-99; head printer, Br. Cent. Africa Prot., July, 1899.
GABRIEL, J. S., M.R.C.S.E.—Med. offr., Antigua, dist. 6, 1881.

GAGNON, CHARLES ANTOINE ERNEST.—Ed. St. Ann's Coll.; notary public, 1869; since 1885 pres. of Quebec board of notaries; elected to Quebec legislature, 1878-81, and since 1883; prov. sec., 1887; sheriff of Quebec, 1890.

GALBRAITH, MALCOLM STEWART.—Ed. Coll. of Agric., Ontario, Canada (B.S.A., 1904); experimentalist, dept. of agric., O.R.C., 1904-5; ag. farm supt., Grootvlei, 14th Nov., 1904, to 30th June, 1905; agronomist, O.R.C., 1st July, 1905.

GALE, CHARLES HENRY.—Assoc. M.I.C.E.; articled pupil, 1882-5; asst. and resident engr., 1890; ch. draughtsman P.W. dept., Hong Kong, Sept., 1890; asst. engr., Jan., 1900, and ag. exec. engr., May, 1900; exec. engr., Feb., 1901; J.P., 1904.

GALE, WALTER A.—Ed. high schl., Perth, St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide, and Exeter Coll., Oxon; 3rd master, high schl., Perth, 1885; asst. registr. col. sec.'s office, W. Australia, 1886; sec. to cent. bd. of educn., 1888; registr.-gen.; registr. of patents, etc., Sept., 1890; in charge of census for 1891; clk. of legis. assem., 1891; transfld. to Commonwealth govt., 1901.
GALEA, HENRI.—Master, sup. ct., and registr. Mauritius, 4th Feb., 1899.

GALLAGHER, MAURICE.—Ed. at St. Patrick's India; served indentured apprenticeship, Sindh Punjab and Delhi rly., July, 1869 to July, 1875; marine engrn., I.S.F. (now N.W.) rly., May, 1877, to May, 1881; foreman, N.W. rly., to Dec., 1897; Uganda rly.; gen. foreman; asst. loco. supt. (works), 1897; awarded great gold med. and diploma and life mem., Inventor Academy, Paris; certif. ch. engr.

GALLWEY, LT.-COL. HENRY LIONEL, C.M.G. (1899), D.S.O. (1896).—B. 1859; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; entered army, May, 1878; promoted lieut., Mar., 1881; capt., Oct., 1887; brevet-maj., May, 1897; maj., Mar., 1899; and lt.-col., Apr., 1901; apptd. dep. comsnr. and vice-consul, Oil Rivers prot., Mar., 1891; dep. comsnr. and consul, Niger Coast prot., Jan., 1897; and divn. comsnr. Niger Coast prot. (now South. Nigeria), Apr., 1899; ag. consul-gen. Niger Coast prot., Aug. to Nov., 1896; Jan., 1897; Feb., 1898, to Jan., 1899; ag. high comsnr., South. Nigeria, Mar. to Dec., 1900; Brass expedit., 1895 (despa., medal with clasp, D.S.O.); Benin expedit., 1897 (despa., clasp, brevet majority); Aro expedit., as chief political officer, 1901-2 (despa.); gov., St. Helena, 1902.

GALLWEY, SIR MICHAEL HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1888), C.M.G. (1883).—B. 1826; ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; B.A.; barrister, King's Inns, Dub., Trin. term, 1853; Munster circuit; atty.-gen., Natal, 1857; mem. exec. and legis. coun.; pres. Transvaal and Zululand bndry. comsnr., 1878; received the thanks of the sec. of state; ch. just., Natal, 1890; ret. 1902.

GAMBLE, OSWALD.—Asst. paymaster to the forces, Brit. Cent. Africa, 30th July, 1901; paymaster, Somaliland field force, 1902 to 1904; African gen. serv. medal; paymaster, 1st King's African rifles, E. Africa Prot., 1st May, 1905.
GARDINER, EDWARD A.—Asst. supt. of pol., Singapore, July, 1898; ag. supt. of pol., Malacca, Oct., 1901; ditto, Penang, Sept., 1903, to Mar., 1904, and Feb.-Mar., 1907.

GARDINER, JOHN, I.S.O. (1907).—Entd. govt. ser., S. Austral., in ch. sec.'s off., 1866; treas., 1871; clk., engrn.-in-chief's dept., 1874; acctnt., 1876; ch. acctnt., 1888; sec. comsnr. P.W., and chmn. supply and tender bd., 1899; transfld. to serv. of Commonwealth of Aust. as pub. serv. inspr., 1902.

GARLAND, PATRICK JOSEPH.—B. 1867; asst. col. surg., G. Coast, 25th Apr., 1904; ag. dist. comsnr., Kita, 1896; ditto, Axim, 1898 and 1901; apptd. a sen. med. offr., Northern Territories, 1899; served on Fra Fra expedit., under Captain Donald Stewart, June and July, 1899; specially mentioned in despatches; served on Neutral Zone expedit., Feb., 1900; mentioned in despatches; served on Fra Fra expedit., Mar., 1900; served during Ashanti rebellion and siege of Kumasi; acted as P.M.O. to column during retirement of Sir F. M. Hodgson, governor; specially mentioned in despatches (medal and clasp); apptd. dist. comsnr. of Axim Dist., 1901; dep. P.M.O., 6th July, 1904; ag. P.M.O., Aug., 1901, to Feb., 1902, Aug., 1905, and from Jan. to Aug., 1907.
GARLING, HENRY CHAR.—Supt.'s asst., Skerrett's farm and school, Antigua, 18th Apr., 1894; ag. asst. supt. of agric., Feb., 1904; 2nd outdoor offr., treasy., Feb., 1905.

GARNEAU, PIERRE.—Merchant and mem. of bd. of trade, Quebec; late govt. dir. of North Shore railway; mayor, 1870-73; M.L.A., Quebec, 1873-78 and 1881-86; mem. of prov. exec. coun. and ministry as comsnr. of agricult., pub. wks., and crown lands, 1874-78; M.L.C., Quebec, 1887, and again comsnr. of agricult., pub. wks., and crown lands to 1891.
GARNER, CORNELIS ROBERT IRWIN ROSS.—Sub-assistant commr., Hoko, Swaziland, Oct., 1902; to Mar., 1907; asst. commr., ditto, 22nd Mar., 1907.
GARRETT, W. J.—Appointed, after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1907.

GARRARD, C. G.—Ed. Haileybury; dep. reg. sup. ct., Penang, Jan., 1892; ag. dep. reg. sup. ct., Singapore, Feb. to Oct., 1893; ag. reg. sup. ct., Penang, July, 1894; ag. dep. reg. sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, Apr., 1895; also ag. collr., land rev., and offr. in charge of treas., Malacca, Aug., 1895; ag. dep. reg. sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1896; asst. reg. sup. ct., Malacca, 1896.

GARRAWAY, DAVID G., I.S.O. (1906).—Entered the provost-marshal’s office, Grenada, in Jan., 1867; ch. clk., col. sec.’s office, 1868; clk. of couns., 1868 to 1878; dep.-sec., reg. ser., and clk. of the crown, and sec. bd. of educn., 1871 to 1878; commr. of census, 1871; ag. auditor, 1871 to 1872, and 1875; sec. to exec. comte., 1875-6; ag. treas., 1876-7-9, 1880-1-2; and ex-officio a mem. of the legislature: auditor, 1878; notary public, 1880; compiler of census, 1881; ag. pol. mag., St. Vincent, 1883-4; treas., St. Lucia, July, 1884; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; performed duties of col. sec. in conjunction with his own during visit of gov.-in-ch., 1889 and 1896; admnstr. govt., part of Feb., 1899; comp. of customs; Br. Guiana, Sept., 1900; mem. of excise bd.; cent. bd. of hith. and quar. bd., div. pub. offrs. guar. fund.

GARRAWAY, DAVID JULIAN.—B. 1878; entd. pub. serv., St. Lucia, Mar., 1892, as supernumy. clk., treas.; ag. 4th clk., Nov., 1892; clk., P.O., June, 1893; ag. clk. to adminstr.-in-chief, Windward Is., Aug., 1893; 2nd clk. registr. off., June, 1895; ch. clk., Jan., 1898; ag. registr., sheriff and administrator-gen., on 3 occasions, 1901-2-3; rev. offr., Grenada, June, 1903; ag. pol. mag., West Dist., Feb., 1904; additional coroner, West Dist., Mar., 1904; ag. pol. mag., North Dist., for spec. purpose of hearing appeals agst. town bd. assessmts. for 1904; asst. tax offr. in connect. with hearing appeals agst. assessmts. for land and house taxes for 1904; ag. pol. mag., West Dist., May and June, 1905; ag. commr., Carriacou, Aug.-Sept., 1906; ag. road surv., West Dist., Oct., 1906, in addition to duties of substantive office; ch. clk., registr’s off., and dep. registr., Oct., 1907.

GARRAWAY, E. C. F., L.R.C.S.I.—Dist. surg., Millwood, Knysna, Cape Colony, 1888; ditto, Kuruman, Br. Bech., 1891; surg., Bech. Bord. pol., 1892; divnl. med. offr., S.A.C., with rank of major, 1901; prin. med. offr., ditto, Mar., 1905; served in Matabele wars and Boer war (1899-1902).

GARRAWAY, ROBERT FREDERICK.—B. 1860; postmr., Dominica, June, 1881; sec. bd. of hith. and quar. bd., Sept., 1881; ag. dist. mag., dist. G., Apr., 1893; commr. of oaths, Oct., 1893; ag. registr. of sup. ct., Dec., 1898; offr. M.L.C., Dec., 1898; mag., dist. G., and collr. of rev., Portsmouth, Mar., 1896; mem. of quarantine bd., Jan., 1906.

GARRETT, HERBERT LEONARD OFFLEY.—B. 1881; Ed. at Charterhouse and St. John’s Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1902 (2nd cls. hona., classics tripos); asst. mast., The Lodge, Barbados, 1903-4; jun. asst. mast., Queen’s Coll., Hong Kong, 1904.

GARRETT, MARK.—B. 1851; served in the C.M.R. from July, 1876, to Nov., 1879; clk. to civ. com. and rec. mag., King William’s Town, 1st Sept., 1880; clk., col. sec.’s office, 1st May, 1884; transf. to atty.-gen.’s office, 1st Nov., 1894; sec. to the civ. ser. commr., from 1886 to 1898; to the tender bd., 1889-1891; and to the col. med. coun., 1891-3; prin. clk., atty.-gen.’s office, 15th Mar., 1895; re-apptd. sec. to civ. ser. comm., 24th Feb., 1899; sec. to plague advisory bd., 1901, and to the Cape Peninsula commn., 1902; sub-commr., war losses compensation commn., Mar. to Sept., 1903; specially employed on census staff, col. sec.’s office, from Sept., 1903, to July, 1904; resumed duties as prin. clk., atty.-gen’s office, 1st Aug., 1904; detached from Oct., 1904, to Feb., 1905, for spec. ser. upon S.A. nat. affairs commn.

GATT, C.—Supt. govt. printing office, Malta, Feb., 1882; was priv. sec. to Sir Richard Wood, H.M.’s diplomatic agt. and consul-gen., Tunis, Sept., 1868, to May, 1871; asst. sec., Gozo, 1892; rec.-gen., 1899; audr.-gen., 1902; mem. of ex. coun. and of coun. of govt., 1903.

GATT, L., C.M.G. (1901), C.E.—Entered the Malta ser., Mar., 1883, as land survr.; rly. inspr., 1884; asst. engnr., waterwks., 1885; survr., P.W.D., 1888; manager and engnr., Malta rly., 1895; ch. engnr., waterwks., and elect. lighting dept., 1896; supt. of P.W., with a seat in exec. coun., and a seat in coun. of govt., 1897.

GATTY, SIR STEPHEN HERBERT, KT. BACH. (1904), K.C.—Scholar Winchester schl. and New Coll., Oxon; called to bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1874; went the N.E. circuit; atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, June, 1883; ag. ch. justice and local commr., W. Ind. incumbered estates ct. for Antigua, June to Oct., 1884; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, July, 1884; local commr. incumbered estates ct. for St. Kitts, Oct., 1884; atty.-gen., Trinidad, Dec., 1885; chancellor of diocese and admntg. advocr., 1887; chmn. roy. comm. on franchise and electoral dists., 1888, and of royal comm. on Mctayor system in Tobago, 1890; puisne judge, S. Stlntma, 1892; chief just., Gibraltar, 1895; resig., Mar., 1905.
GAVIN, J. C.—Sec. to treas., rec.-gen., and paymr.-gen., New Zealand, Mar., 1860; asst. comptroller and audr.-gen., 1890.
GEAR, HON. HENRY.—M.H.A. for dist. of Burin, Newfoundland, 1900; again elected, 1904; mem. exec. coun., 1903.

GEBERS, H. L.—Student Int. S.N.A. Dep., 1894; clk., Zulu and D. Int. mag. ct., Dundee, 1895; 3rd cls. clk., 1895; 2nd cls. clk., 1900; lent to Transvaal, 1901; clk. of pt. P. Vryheid, 1901; J.P., Vryheid, 1901; A. A. M., Vryheid, 1902; reverted to Natal service, 1903; transf. to mag. ct., Dundee, 1904, senior clk. and A. A. M.
GEBERS, WILHELM FRIEDRICH.—Asst. instr. of native educ., Natal, 1st Mar., 1903; instr., ditto, 1st July, 1904; sub-instr. of schls., 1st July, 1906.

GEIKIE, R.—B. 1874; ed. at Harrow and Cambridge; undergraduate scholarship, King’s Coll., 1893; Winchester prizeman, 1896; 1st class hist. tripus, 1896; elected fellow King’s Coll., 1900; apptd., after open compet. exam., 2nd class clk. Local Govt. Bd., Nov., 1897; 2nd class clk., C.O., Mar., 1899; sec. to rlys. and concessions committee, 1907.
GEMMELL, GEORGE.—Clk. treas. dept., Seychelles, Feb., 1887; clk. to admr., Feb., 1889; clk. of councils, Jan., 1897; 1st customs offr., May, 1900; postmr., June, 1905.

GIBBES, REGINALD PRESCOTT.—Cadet, S. S., Nov., 1889; passed final exam., Tamil, Jan., 1892; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Aug. to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, P. W., Mar., 1894; passed exam. in Malay, 1894; 2nd asst. prot. of imigrts., Penang, May, 1896; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1897; transf'd. to Calcutta as (temp.) asst. emigtn. agt. for Trinidad, Mauritius, Fiji, Jamaica, &c., Aug., 1900; appt. confirmed, Aug., 1901; passed exam. in Hindustani, May, 1901; ag. emigtn. agt., 19th Feb. to 12th Oct., 1902; emigtn. agt., 14th Sept., 1903.
GIBBS, LAWRENCE.—A.M.I.C.E., grad. assoc., and county engr.; ass't engr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 1890; ag. exec. engr., 1896.

GIBRALTAR, LORD BISHOP OF, Rt. REV. WM. ED. COLLINS.—Late Exhib. of Selw. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Jun. Opt.), 1887; Lightfoot schl., 1889; Prince Consort pri., 1890; M.A., 1891; B.D., 1902; D.D., 1903; F.C. of All Hallow's, Barking, Lond., 1890-1; lectr. of Selw. Coll., 1891-3; lectr. of St. John's Coll., Camb., 1891-4; exam. hist. trip, 1894-5; select pr., Camb., 1899-1902; prof. of eccles. hist., King's Coll., Lond., 1893-1904; C. of All Hallows, Barking, 1894; exam. chap. to Bp. of St. Albans (Festing), 1897-1903; Bishop of Gibraltar, 25th Jan., 1904.

GIBSON, HENRY JAMES, C.B. (1902), B.A.—B. 1860; ed. Rossall and St. John's Coll., Oxford; Casberd scholar; 1st cl. class. mods.; clk. War Office, 1885; princ. clk., 1897; asst. acctn.-gen., 1900; dep. acctn.-gen., 1903; asst. dir. of Army finance, 1904; priv. sec. to Mr. Woodall, 1894-95, to Hon. St. John Brodrick, 1895-6; asst. compt. and audr., 1905; is hon. asst. audr. for certain Crown Colonies and Prots., ag. on behalf of the S. of S. for the Colonies.

GIBSON, JAS. YOUNG.—B. 1859; clk. and Zulu interp. to ct. of R.M.; sub-distri. of stamps, and regisr. circuit ct., Newcastle, Natal, Apr., 1882; also dep. clk. of peace, Mar., 1887; asst. comrs. and R.M., Nqutu dist., Zululand, Feb., 1889; ditto, Ndawande dist., May, 1889; mem. of tribal bdny. comsn., 1891, received thanks of H.M.'s govt.; high comrsr.'s interpreter during Swaziland-S.A.R. negotiations, 1894; ag. mag. for several districts in Zululand; ag. ch. mag., Zululand, for trial of certain treason cases, June, 1900; mag. for colony; mag., Umvoti div., 1900; J.P. for colony, 1902; ag. mast., sup. ct., 1904; mag., Up. Unkumanzi, 1905; mag., Mahlabatini div., 3rd May, 1906.
GILES, HAROLD CECIL.—Brit. vice-consul, Beira, Portuguese E. Africa, Apr., 1901; 3rd asst. treas., E. Africa Prot., 20th July, 1904; asst. dir. of transport, 5th July, 1906.

GILES, MORTIMER.—Entd. survey dept., S. Australia, 1864; draughtsman, 1875; clk. and asst. draughtsman, lands titles off., 1876; draughtsman, 1889; draughtsman and 3rd dep. regisr.-gen. of deeds, 1901; regisr.-gen. of deeds and regisr. of buildg. socs., 1904.
GILKISON, THOMAS TRAIN.—Imp. Brit. E. Africa Co., 1890; collr., E. Africa Prot., 1895.

GILL, THOMAS, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1849; entered col. service, S. Australia, Feb., 1865; acctntn., treas., Jan., 1883; under treas., 1894; also regisr. of inscribed stock in Adelaide, 1895, and mem. of supply and tender bd., 1903.
GILL, WALTER, F.L.S., F.R.H.S.—Conservator of forests, S. Australia, July, 1890.
GILLIGAN, GEOFFREY G.—B. 1876; capt., 4th Battn. A. and S. Highrs.; served in S. Africa, 1900-01 (medal with 3 clasps); inspr. of pol., Br. Guiana, 1904; asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Jan., 1906.

GILLOTT, HON. SIR SAMUEL, KT. BACH. (1901).—B. 1838; M.L.A. for East Melbourne, Victoria, since Feb., 1899; min. without portfolio, 1900-01; atty.-gen., 1901-02; Mayor of Melbourne, 1900-02; first Lord Mayor, 1902-3; chief sec. and min. of labour, Victoria, Feb., 1904; has now retired.

GILMAN, EDWARD WILMOT FRANCIS.—B. 1876; ed. at Bradford, and Brasenose Coll., Oxon., B.A., 1898; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Nov., 1901; passed final exam. in Tamil, June, 1902; ag. asst. supt. of Indian immigrants, Penang, Apr., 1902; confirmed, July, 1904; sent on spec. miss. to India in connection with Indian immigration, Sept., 1903; emigtn. agt. in Madras for the S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1907.

GILMORE, HON. GEORGE CROSBY, M.E.C.—B. 1864; B.A., LL.M.; barrister-at-law, Inner Tem.; barrister, solr., attorney and proctor of Tasmania; M.L.A. for George Town, Tasmania, 1893-1900; comnr. of ct. of requests and stip. mag., West Coast, 1900-1903; M.L.A. for Waratah since 1903; atty.-gen. of Tasmania, July, 1904-1907.

GILSON, CAPT. CHAS. HUGH.—D.S.O.; served in B.S.A. police and Natal civ. ser.; served in Matabele rebellion, 1896-7; dist. comdt., S.A.C., Swaziland, 1902 to 1907; asst. comnr., cmdg. Swaziland police, 22nd Mar., 1907.
GIROUARD, DESIRE, K.C.—Ed. Montreal Coll.; called to the bar, Canada, 1860; K.C., 1876; represented Jacques Cartier co., 1878 to 1885; judge sup. ct., Canada, 1895.

GIROUARD, LT.-COL. SIR EDWARD PERCY CRANWILL, K.C.M.G. (1900), D.S.O., R.—Served with Dongola expdny. force, 1896 (desp. brevet-major, British medal, Khedive's medal, 2 clasps); Nile expdny., 1897 (desp., clasp); rlyw. traff. man., Woolwich Arsenal, 1890-5; dir. of Soudan rlyws., 1896-8; pres. Egyptian rlyw. bd., 1898-9 (2nd cls. Medjidiye); S. African war, 1899-1902 (desp.); comnr. of rlyws., Transvaal and O.R.C., 1902; mem. I.C.C.; resig. 1904; high comnr., N. Nigeria, 12th Feb., 1907.

GLASGOW, 7TH EARL OF, THE RIGHT HON. SIR DAVID BOYLE, G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1833; capt. (retired) R.N.; served in the Crimean and China wars, 1857 (medal and clasp); J.P. for Ayrshire, and chmn. of co. coun.; gov. of N.Z., 1892-97.

GLASIER, FRANK BEDFORD.—B. 1872; asst. civ. engr., Bermuda harbr.wks., June, 1894; engr.-in-charge, Coatacoalcos harbr. wks., Mexico, May, 1896; dist. engrn., S. Leone govt. rly., June, 1897; dist. engrn., Tarkwa rly., Gold Coast, Oct., 1898; and gen. manager, S. Leone govt. rly., May, 1899; promoted gen. manager, Lagos govt. rly., Apr., 1901.

GLOVER, H. T.—Apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and temporarily employed by the roy. coman. on the poor lawa, May, 1906, to Feb., 1907; in the B. of T., Feb. to Aug., 1907; at the civ. ser. coman., Sept. to Nov., 1907; assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907.

GLYDE, ADOLPHUS YEVOIL.—Apptd. clk. in land titles dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1882; dep. regisr. of titles, ag. regisr. of deeds, sub. collr. of internal rev., asst. regisr. of titles, 1893; ag. regisr. of deeds, Nov. 1896 to Jan., 1897.
GOBEIL, ANTOINE, I.S.O. (1904).—Law clk., dept. of pub. wks., Canada, 1879 to 1885; then sec., dep. min.of pub. wks., Jan., 1891.

GODBOLT, THOMAS.—Entd. N. S. Wales public serv., July, 1871, as jun. clk., govt. printing off.; asst. paymaster of imperial pensions, 1877; bd. of trade clk. and cashier, govt. shipping office, 1881; emergency offr., col. treasy., 1891; paymaster of imperial pensions and allowances, 1896; paymaster, N. S. Wales Imperial Bushmen's contingent (local pay and allowances), 1900, and chief paymaster (for local pay and allowances), N. S. Wales Naval Contingent to China, 1900.

GODET, FREDERICK LENNOCK.—Ag. clk. to exec. and legis. couns., clk. to gen. bd. of health, and sec. to immigr. bd., Bermuda, in 1871-2; apptd. to above offices, 24th May, 1878; clk. on five occasions to marine cts. of inquiry; sec. to leg. comte., on Queen's Jubilee, 1887; ag. clk., col. sec.'s off., 1889; sec. to Bermuda agric. assoc. since 1888; clk. to bd. of educ., 1st May, 1907.

GODFREY, JOSEPH ED.—B. 1858; M.B. and C.M., Edin., 1882; gov. med. offr., B. Guiana, Feb., 1883; med. inspr. of estates hospitals, May, 1898; deputy regis. of births, deaths and marriages, 1898; deputy chmn., cent. bd. of health, 1902; acted on sev. occasions as surg.-gen., regis.-gen., and chmn. of cent. bd. of health; apptd. surg.-gen., regis.-gen. of b., d. and m., chmn. cent. bd. of health, and offl. mem. of ct. of policy, and comb. ct., July, 1904; dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., 1906; chmn. of poor law comsrs., 1906.
GODFREY, LEOPOLD HENRY.—Active serv. volunteer in 1st batt., Royal Sussex regt., Feb., 1901, to May, 1902; architectural draughtsman, pub. wks. dept., O.R.C., June, 1902.

GODLINGTON, BURT GLANVILLE.—Ed. at Totteridge Park, Herts; served with 1st Brabant's Horse in S. African Campaign, 1899-1901 (Queen's medal and five clasps); clk. to editor of O.R.C. govt. gazette, 1st Nov. 1902; editor, 1st July, 1904; ag. govt. librarian, O.R.C., 14th Nov., 1906, to 14th May, 1907.

GOLDIE, CHARLES.—Apptd., after compet. exam., an examg. offr., H.M. customs, Greenock, Sept., 1863; inspr. of invoices and ch. landing survr. H.M. customs, Jamaica, Oct., 1869; one of the comsrs. to inquire into the pilot service of Jamaica, Apr., 1870; ag. collr. of customs, Kingston, 1873, and 1875 to 1876; acted in 1874 as island treas., and as collr.-gen. of customs, 1875 and 1888; collr. of customs and shipping master, Kingston, 1883.
GOLDIE, WALTER LEIGH MACKINNON.—Ed. at Charterhouse and St. Mary's Hosp.; M.R.C.S., 1902; F.R.C.S., 1905; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prota., June, 1905.

GOLDING-BIRD, VERY REV. C. H.—Dean of the Falkland Is.; b. 1874; ed. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; M.A.; ordained priest, 1898; vicar of St. Barnabas, Dover, 1902-1907; served as chaplain, 1st Cavalry brig.; S. African war, 1900 (medal and five bars); dean of Falkland Is. and govt. inspr. of schls., 1907.

GOLDFEY, SIR JOHN TANKERVILLE, KT. BACH. (1893).—B. 1846; ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Term, 1869, N. circuit; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Apr., 1880; ag. ch. just., May, 1881; puisne judge, Br. Guiana, June, 1883; judge sup. ct., S. Sttlms., Mar., 1887; ch. just., Trinidad, 1892; retired, 1902.

GOLLAN, HENRY COWPER.—B. 1868; ed. at Charterhouse; M.A., Edin.(1887); called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1891, N. circuit; priv. sec. to Col. Lugard, comsrs. and comdt., W.A.F.F., Sept. 1st, 1899; priv. sec. to H.C. of Nor. Nig., 1st Jan.-21st Sept., 1900; ag. sec. to the admnstr., 1st June-31st Aug., 1900; atty.-gen. of Nor. Nig., 21st Sept., 1900; ag. ch. just., 16th Nov., 1900-4th Apr., 1901; ch. just., 4th Nov., 1901; prepared criminal code for N. Nigeria, reed. thanks of S. of S., 1904; compiled proclamations of N. Nigeria, reed. thanks of S. of S., 1905; ch. just., Bermuda, 1904; prepared Cts. Consolidating Act, Bermuda, reed. thanks of S. of S., Dec., 1905; chmn. of produce coman., 1905, and of aliens comte., 1906; chmn. of bd. of educn., Bermuda, July, 1907.

GOMPERTZ, HENRY HERSEY JOHNSTON.—B. 1867; ed. at Bedford schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxon (scholar); 2nd cl. lit. hum., B.A. (1890); cadet, S. Sttlms., 1890; passed final exam., Chinese, Dec., 1893; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Aug., 1894, to Dec., 1896; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 1895; actg. deputy-regist., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1896; passed final exam. in Hokkien, 1897; 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, 1st Jan., 1897; transf. to Hong Kong as asst. regisr.-gen., 1897; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1899; actg. sen. mag., June, 1899, to June, 1900; asst. col. sec., Jan. 1st, 1900; mem. land ct., 1st June, 1900; pres. land ct. 10th Jan., 1902; ag. pol. mag., 1904-5; ag. sen. mag., June, 1906; ag. atty.-gen., 1st Nov., 1906, to 28th July, 1907; ag. puisne judge from 21st Oct. to 6th Dec., 1907; editor of Hong Kong law reports from 1st Feb., 1907; 1st mag., Hong Kong, 29th Oct. 1907.

GOODLIFFE, JOHN HENRY.—Ed. at Repton schl., Univ. Coll., London, and Aberdeen Univ.; M.B., C.M., and M.D. (with commendation), Aberdeen; medallist in midwifery and pathology; civ. surg., Ashanti campaign, 1900 to 1901 (medal); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prota., Oct., 1905.
GOODMAN, CLIFFORD E.—Entered pub. library, Barbados, Sept., 1890; 5th clk., audr.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1891; ch. clk., insp.-gen.'s office, July, 1892; also regisr. of births, dist. A.

GOODMAN, GERALD AUBREY.—Ed. at Lodge schl. and Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Univ. Coll., Lond.; 1st com. law schl., Mid. Temp., Trinity term, 1886; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., June, 1885; ag. judge of petty debt ct., and of asst. ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1889; J.P., 1889; ag. solr.-gen. in 1890-1-2; M.H.A. since Oct., 1889; mem. bd. of educ. since Oct., 1891; solr.-gen., Mar., 1896; ag. atty.-gen., 1891, 1898, 1900-1-2-3-4; K.C. for Barbados, 1903; mem. of quar. bd. and gen. bd. of health, 1902.

GOODMAN SIR WILLIAM MEIGH, KT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1847; ed. at Univ. Coll.; graduated B.A. (honours) at the Univ. of Lond., 1867; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Nov., 1870; went S.E. circ. and Surrey sessions; atty.-gen., Br. Honduras, 1883; ch. just., 1886; comnrr. to revise and consolidate the laws of the col., 1886; atty.-gen. Hong Kong, 1889; ag. col. sec., May, 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Aug., 1895, Jan. to May, 1896, and Apr., 1899, to Feb., 1900; K.C., 1900; ch. just., Hong Kong, 1st April, 1902; ret., 1905.

GOODRIDGE, HON. A. F.—Mem. of house of assom. for Ferryland, Newfoundland, 1880 to 1882, and 1882 to 1885; for Twillingate, 1885 to 1889; returned for same dist., 1893; mem. exec. coun., 1885 (without portfolio) to 1889, and again in 1894; after the resign. of the Whiteway ministry in Apr., 1894, he was called upon to form a ministry, and became premier of the col., which office he held till Dec., 1894; was a mem. of the bd. of wks. and rly. comsrs. under the Thorburn adminstr. (1885-9); he is also a mem. of the Newfoundland chamb. of com., and has been its pres. and vice-pres.
GOOLD, ALBERT WM. FISHER.—Supernum. inspr. of pol., Mauritius, 19th Dec., 1881; inspr., 16th Sept., 1894; ch. offr., 1st Nov., 1898; inspr. gen., 17th Aug., 1902.

GOOLD-ADAMS, MAJOR SIR HAMILTON JOHN.—G.C.M.G. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1902), C.B. (1898), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1858; Roy. Scots fus.; comdg. a troop Bechuana land border police, Aug., 1885; comdt., 1888; major, 1889; again comdt., 1893-5; commanded field force against Matabele, 1893; res. commr. for Bechuana land Protectorate: dep. admr., Orange River Col., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., 7th Aug., 1901; gov., O.R.C., 1st July, 1907.
GORDON, ARTHUR HENRY WYNDHAM.—Supt. of police, Mauritius, 28th Nov., 1889; ag. inspr.-gen., 2nd Aug., 1892; supt. of prisons and reformatory, 25th Oct., 1892.

GORDON, ARTHUR JOHN LEWIS, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1847; priv. sec. to the gov. of Trinidad, 1866 to 1870; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Apr., 1870; priv. sec. to gov. of Mauritius, 1870; priv. sec. to the gov. of Fiji, 1875-80; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Canada (Earl of Aberdeen), 1893.

GORDON, JAMES GEORGE WESTLAND.—B. 1874; served with and attached to Gen. Buller's staff, S. African War (Queen's medal and six clasps, King's medal and two clasps); cont., stationery dept., O.R.C., 17th Nov., 1900.
GORDON, JAMES.—Admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1881; stip. mag., Adelaide, 1892; pol. mag., Adelaide, 1902; mem. of Adelaide licensing bench, 1892; chrmn. ditto, 1901; mem. central bd. of health, 1901.

GORDON, THE HON JOHN HANNAH, K.C.—B. 1850; admitted to S. Aust. bar, 1876; M.L.C., 1888; min. of ed., 1889-90 and 1892; ch. sec., 1893-6; atty.-gen., 1899-1902; atty.-gen. and min. of ed., 1902-3; 3rd judge of sup. ct., 1903; 2nd judge of sup. ct., 1905.

GORDON, BRIG.-GEN. JOSEPH MASIA, C.B. (1901).—B. 1856; lieut. in R.A., 1875; raised S. Australia artil., 1882; comdt., S. Australia, 1892; mil. adviser in England to Austn. cols., 1898-99; served in S. African war, 1899-1900, as chief staff offr. with Austn., Canadian, and oversea volr. troops (ment. in desps., four clasps); comdt., Commonwealth mil. forces of Victoria, 1902-03; ditto, N. S. Wales, 1905.
GORDON, RICHARD WOLF.—Clk. in supreme ct., England, 1892; 3rd asst., B.C. Africa Prot., Apr., 1900; ag. col. sec., Angoniland, 1904; 2nd cls. dis. res., Apr., 1906.

GORDON, WILLIAM MONTGOMERIE.—B. 1855; ed. Elin. Acad.; clk. to consul-gen. of W. Pacific, Aug., 1879; clk. of exec. coun. and ch. clk. of crown lands in col. of Fiji, May, 1880; also acted as sec. to lands comm.; res. commr. and stip. mag. for the island of Rotuma, May, 1882; commr. to inquire into claims of Europeans to land, Nov., 1882; stip. mag., Fiji, Apr., 1886; priv. sec. to gov., Ceylon, June, 1887; ch. clk. col. secy.'s off., Leeward, and clk. of fedl., exc. and legis. couns., and of the local legis. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1889; ag. commr., Montserrat, Dec., 1891, to Mar., 1892, Mar., 1894, to June, 1895, and July to Dec., 1899; ag. col. sec., Leeward Islds., Aug. to Nov., 1900; asst. col. sec., Trinidad, July, 1901; ag. col. sec., Trinidad, Aug.-Sept., 1903, Mar.-Aug., 1904, Apr.-Sept., 1905, Apr.-Sept., 1906, and Feb.-Sept., 1907; admstd. govt., 25th-28th Aug., 1905, and 30th Apr. to 8th May, 1907.

GORE, LIEUT.-COL. JAMES CASAMAJOR.—B. 1852; served in G.P.O., London, 2nd Feb., 1871, to 31st Aug., 1873; entd. army, 1st Sept., 1873; served in Ashanti war, 1873-4 (medal); seconded from army on selection as aud. gen., W. Africa settlmts., 1st May, 1882, to 25th May, 1887; ag. pol. mag. and coroner, etc., Sierra Leone, 1883, to Feb., 1884; with expeditn. agen., Yonnies, W. Coast of Africa, 1887-8 (clasp. in desps.); ag. col. sec., Sierra Leone, 2nd Nov., 1889; ag. treas., ditto, Nov., 1889; 1890; on special serv. in Gold Coast, 9th June to Nov., 1894; col. sec., Sierra Leone, June, 1895; ret. from army, 20th Nov., admstd. govt. of Sierra Leone, 5th Aug.; recvr.-gen. and chief collr. of cust. and Cyprus, 4th Oct., 1901; ag. col. sec., 14th July, 1902, to 14th Feb., 1903, 2nd Dec., 1905, and from May to Nov., 1906.
GOSLING, JOHN THOMAS.—Served in London, 1887 to 1897; ag. P.M.G., B. C., 1897 to 1898; P.M.G., B. C. Africa, 1898; P.M.G., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 1904.
GOSLING, SAMUEL BUCKNELL.—Ser. G.P.O., London, 1889; asst. P.M.G., E. and Uganda Prots., 1904; S. African Med. clamps.

GOTTLIBER, F. H. VALENTINE.—Enter service, S. Sttlmtns., Oct., 1889; ch. clk., Singapore, 1888; called to the bar, May, 1892; dep. regist. sup. ct., Singapore, Aug., ag. asst. regist. sup. ct. and mag., Malaca, 1898; also ag. sheriff, Sep., 1898, to Nov., asst. regist. sup. ct., Penang, July, 1904.
GOUGE, HERBERT DILLON.—B. 1843; land and income tax dept., S. Australia, 1885; pub. actuary, Mar., 1894.

GOUGH, FREDERIC HARRISON.—B. 1858; at Oriel Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. class. mods., B.A. (2nd final class. sch.), 1886; M.A. barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1894; N.E. pol. mag. and coroner, Freetown, S. Lece., 1903; solr.-gen., Oct., 1903; ag. atty.-various occasions, 1903-1907.

GOULBURN, BISHOP OF RIGH CHRISTOPHER GEORGE BARLOW.—D.D., ordained by Bishop of N. Queensland curate of Mackay, 1881-2; vicar of St Charters Towers, 1882-5; mission chaplain, bishop, 1885-6; vicar of St James's pro-canon, Townsville, 1886-91; hon. canon, 1887-9; gen., 1887-8; consecrated 2nd bishop of N. land, 1891; installed Bishop of Goulburn, Apr., 1902.
GOULD, JULIAN BARING.—Cadet, N. serv., Mar., 1897; asst. res., Nov., 1900; ch. clk., Jan., 1902.
GOURDEAU DE BEAULIEU, MAJOR FRÉDÉRIC.—Priv. sec. to Hon. J. Cauchon, of Mackenzien adminstr., 1870; ch. clk. and sec. dept. of marine and fisheries; dep. min. of fisheries of Canada, May, 1896.

GOWAN, SIR JAMES ROBERT, K.C.M.G., C.M.G. (1893), LL.D.—B. 1815; lieu. regt., North York, 1838; called to bar, 1839; judge of Simcoe, 1842-83; ch. of hd. of pub. instruction, 1844 to 1871; for consolidating statute law of Canada; chmn. of hd. of judges, 1869-86; eng. consolidation of the crim. law for Dominion; mem. of "The Law Reform Comm.", 1876; commr. to inquire into charges against the in reference to the Pacific rly. contract; commr. for consolidating the statutes of 1876-77; apptd. to high ct. of just., senator of the Dominion, 1885; for Eng. chmn. of divorce tribunal of the senate of Barrie Collegiate Inst., by annual ass., 1881-87; called to Irish bar, 1889.

GOWDEY, WM. WALLACE.—Solr., Barbados, 1881; mem. house of assem., 1883-93; mem. of comtee. of inquiry into Dodd's reformatory and industrial schl., 1884; mem. bd. of health, 1884-92; J.P., 1885; mem. of financial inquiry comsn., 1885; of general hosp. bd., 1889-92; pol. mag. and judge petty debt ct., Dist. E, Barbados, 1892; receiver of wrecks, Dist. E, July, 1897; pol. mag., Dist. A, Oct., 1901; visiting just., Lazaretto, Jan., 1902; receiver of wrecks, Dist. A, May, 1902; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., May and June, 1902; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal, July, 1902, to Feb., 1903; comsnr. of probates, Aug., 1902; judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., Dec., 1902; ag. coroner, Dist. A, March to May, 1903; ag. judge, asst. ct. of appeal, April, 1903; ag. inspr. of prisons, May, 1903.

GRABHAM, MICHAEL, M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P. Lon., M.A., M.B., B.C., Camb., 1891.—B. 1866; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; St Thomas's Hosp., Lon.; B.A. Camb., 1887; 1st cl. honours nat. sciences, tripods and exhibitioner, St. John's Coll.; asst. med. off., city asylum, Birmingham, 3rd Nov., 1891; surg. to the lying-in hospital and general penitentiary, Kingston, Jamaica, 1st Apr., 1892; re-apptd., 1st July, 1899; served on cattle diseases comsn., 1894; late sec. med. council of Jamaica, 1897; mem. bd. of governors, Institute of Jamaica, 1902; joint author, with F. V. Theobald, S.E. Agric. Coll., "A Monograph on the Mosquitoes of Jamaica," 1906; author of various papers on same subject, 1906 and 1907.

GRAHAM, SIR FREDK., K.C.B. (1907), C.B. (1899).—B. 1848; apptd. clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., after compet. exam., 30th May, 1870; 2nd class clk., 30th Sept., 1872; priv. sec. to Mr. Herbert, 1st Oct., 1872; to the Rt. Hon. M. E. Grant-Duff, M.P., 8th Jan., 1881, to 5th Aug., 1881; to Mr. L. H. Courtney, M.P., 6th Aug., 1881; to the Hon. Evelyn Ashley, 12th May, 1882; and to the Earl of Dunraven, 24th June, 1885; to Sir R. Herbert, 3rd Feb., 1886; and again to the Earl of Dunraven, 3rd Aug., 1886, to Feb., 1887; 1st class clk., Dec., 1887; apg. prin. clk., Feb., 1882; priv. clk., 11th Mar., 1896; asst. under sec. of state, 1st Mar., 1897; ret., 1907.

GRAHAM, SIR JOHN JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1899).—B. 1847; clk. to C.C. and R.M., Albany, 1864; clk. to regis. E. dists. ct., May, 1865; asst. regist., Jan., 1868; asst. regist., sup. ct., Mar., 1872; regist. and master, E. dists. ct., Feb., 1875; ch. clk. to atty.-gen. and clk. of peace, Capetown, Jan., 1878; sec., law dept., July, 1882; high sheriff and taxing off., sup. ct., May, 1884; mem. of the col. tender bd.; a J.P. for the col.; sec. to law dept., 1889.
GRAINGER, HENRY ALLERDALE.—Mem. H. of Ass. S. Austr., 1884, 1890 and 1901; state agent in London for S. Austr., 1901; ag.-gen., 1902-1905; trustee of S. Austr. sav. bank, 1901.
GRANGE, HERBERT CHARLES.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to bd. of educn., Aug., 1901; transf. to C.O., Feb., 1902.

GRANNUM, EDWARD ALLAN.—Passed Barbados, W. I., civ. ser. exam., 1886; 3rd clk. registrar's off., Jan. to Oct., 1885; 2nd clk., Oct., 1885, to Nov., 1887; clk. master in chancery off., Nov., 1887, to June, 1888; honours graduate, diploma Ontario Com. Coll., Canada, affiliated institute chartered accountants, Canada, Dec., 1889; practised public accountant, Barbados, W.I., to Aug., 1897; asst. auditor, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 25th Sept., 1897, to 15th Jan., 1900; acted auditor May to Nov., 1898 asst. auditor, Gold Coast and Lagos, Jan. to March, 1900; auditor, March, 1900; introduced system double entry accounts Gold Coast P.O., July, 1901; prepared scheme for introduction of double entry system of accounts for Accra town council, Aug., 1901.

GRANNUM, EDWARD T.—Representative of Bridgetown, Barbados Assem., 1883 to 1895; J.P., 1886; mem. exec. comtee., 1885-89, and 1890-94; mem. gen. bd. of health, 1885; represented Barbados in negotiations at Washington in connection with McKinley Tariff Act, 1891; audr.-gen., Barbados, 1894; ag. col. sec. and mem. exec. coun., in 1895, 1896, 1897, 1905, 1906 and 1907; M.L.C., 1906; admstd. govt. for a short time in 1907.

GRANNUM, REGINALD CLIFTON.—B. 1872; ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; 2nd clk. to gov. Windward Is., Sept., 1891; ditto, St. Vincent, Apr., 1892; supervisor of customs, G. Coast Col., Oct., 1893; asst. local audr., G. Coast and Lagos, May, 1895; local audr., S. Leone, June, 1896; J.P., S. Leone, 1897; ch. asst. treas., G. Coast, Apl., 1903; ag. treas. and mem. ex. and leg. couns., mem. bd. of educ., July, 1903, to June, 1904, Dec., 1904, to Dec., 1905, June, 1906, to May, 1907; admitted student of Gray's Inn, Aug., 1907.

GRANT, A. W.—Ed. at Aberdeen Gram. Schl., and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; M.A.; Senior Optime Math. Tripoea, 1894; mem. of Lincoln's Inn, 1892; asst. mast., Ware Gram. Schl., 1896; army coach, Aldershot, 1897; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1899; senior grade, 1901; mast. in charge of pol. schl., 1906.

GRANT, CHARLES CAMERON.—King's student, Aberdeen Univ., 1897-8; M.A., ditto, 1899; 2nd asst., Fraserburgh pub. schl., Aberdeenshire, 1899-1901; 1st asst., Aberlour pub. schl., Banffshire, 1901-2; priv. coun. certif., Scotch educn. dept., 1901; teacher, refugee camp, Springfonteine, O.R.C., Nov., 1902; priv., govt. schl., Winburg, Jan., 1903; corrpdce. clk., educn. dept., Sept., 1904; admstd. clk., 1905; sec., Grey Coll. coun., 1906.
GRANT, COLIN.—Apptd. 3rd asst., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1897; 2nd cls. dist. res., Oct., 1905.

GRANT, HENRY EUGENE WALTER.—B. 1855; sec. to the educn. bd., Barbados, 1882; priv. sec. to Sir W. Robinson, 1883; and at Trinidad, 1886 to 1891; went on mission to Venezuela, 1887; sec. to special pub. wks. and road loan bd., Trinidad, 1890-1; priv. sec. to Sir W. B. Griffith, gov. of G. Coast, 1891-2; called to the bar, Inner Tem., Jan., 1896; res. just., Harbour Is., Bahamas, Apr., 1897; ag. ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, and ag. clk. of councils, Br. Honduras, Nov., 1899; appt. confirmed, 1901; ag. atty.-gen., B. Hond., Mar., 1902, to Oct., 1903; ag. col. sec., 14th Dec., 1904, to 21st July, 1905; admstd. govt., 22nd July to 6th Aug., 1905; ag. col. sec., 7th Aug. to 9th Nov., 1905; col. sec., Falklands, 10th Nov., 1905; assumed office, 14th Feb., 1906; admstd. govt., Apr. to Sept., 1907.

GRANT, Lieut.-Gen. HENRY FANE, C.B. (1885).—B. 1848; ed. at Eton; entld. Army, 1868; col., 1888; served in Egyptian army, 1884 (brevet lieut.-col., 3rd cls. Medjidie); with Nile expedit., 1884-85 (desps., C.B., medal with two clasps, Khedive star); commd. 5th div., 2nd army corps, 1903-7; gov. of Malta, 9th Aug., 1907.

GRANT, THOS. ST. JOHN.—B.A., Dublin Univ., 1886; clk. to C. C. and R. M., Komgha, Cape Colony, Dec., 1897; ditto, East London, June, 1898; ditto, Port Elizabeth, Jan., 1899; passed Cape civ. serv. law exam., Feb., 1899; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Cape Town, July, 1899; clk. to R.M., Cape, Oct., 1899; registr. to judge pres. of high ct., Griqualand West, Sept., 1901; transf'd to O.R.C. civ. serv. as ast. res. mag., Winburg, May, 1902; ditto, Bloemfontein, July, 1904; ag. res. mag., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1905, to Mar., 1906.

GRANVILLE, REGINALD KERR.—Ast. dist. commr., Niger Coast Prot., 30th June, 1896; dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1900; Aro expdn., 1901 to 1902; medal and clasp; transf'd to N. Nigeria Prot., 2nd cls. res., 24th Jan., 1903; ment. in desps. for serv. in connection with Burmi expdn., 1905; collr. to the admtn., E. Africa Prot., 26th Sept., 1905; 2nd ast. sec. to the admtn., 1907.

GRAVES, FRANK HELY-HUTCHINSON.—B.B. 1875; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway," 1889; clk., Imperial Ottoman Bank, Alexandria, 1894; clk., Mersey Dock and Harbour Board, 1898; clk. in crown agent's off., 1899 to 1905; acctnt., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, 18th Nov., 1905; ag. ch. acctnt., 4th July to 12th Dec., 1906; offr. in charge of P.W., Sokoga, ag. ch. stkpr. and in charge of works, 16th Aug., 1907.
GRAVES, THE HON. J. H.—M.L.A. for Delatite, Victoria; commr. of trade and customs, Victoria, 9th July, 1881, to Mar., 1883.
GRAVES, SILAS.—Ck., procureur-gen'l's dept., Mauritius, 2nd May, 1859; deputy collr. of cust., 2nd Mar., 1896; ag. collr. in 1899, 1900, 1903 and 1905.
GRAY, E. A. S.—Demy, Magd. Coll., Oxon; B.A. 1892; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, Jan., 1896; ast. dist. commr., Niger Coast prot., Sept., 1899; dist. commr., Feb., 1902.

GRAY, GEORGE SMITH BOOTH ERSKINE.—B. 1865; "Conway," 1879-80; midshipman, R.N.R., Dec., 1880; mercantile marine offr., 1884 to 1894; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1887-1894; Lieut., R.N.R., 1894; H.M.S. "Anson" and naval employment, 1894-1897; joined So. Nigeria marine, Jan., 1900; served Ubium expdn. and Aro campaign (medal); gov. of gaols, 1902; inspr. of prisons, 1906; comdr. R.N.R. (retired), 1907.

GRAY, JOHN.—Served during S. African campaign as offr. comdg. cyclist corps, and staff offr. to Col. Barker's force; prin., E. London refugee camp schl., June, 1902; ast. supt., Brandport refugee camp, July, 1902; supt., ditto, Sept., 1902; prin., Hoopstad schl., June, 1903; corrpdnc. clk., educn. dept., Mar., 1904; statistician, educn. dept., 1st July, 1904.

GRAY, REGINALD.—B. 1831; called to the bar, Inner Tem., 1875; compiled, in 1884, Bermuda laws from 1860-1883; revising offr., Bermuda, 1889-93; compiled, in 1903, Bermuda laws from 1860-1902; counsel to statute law consolidation comtce, since 1900; atty.-gen., Bermuda, 28th May, 1900; ex-officio mem. of exec. coun.; M.L.A., 1895-1897, and 1900-1906.

GRAY, ST. GEORGE, B.A., M.B., B.CH. (Univ. Dub.), L.M. (Rotunda).—B. 1863; ed. at Upper Canada Coll., Toronto, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; ag. col. ast. surg., St. Lucia, 1892; supernumerary ditto, 1894; col. ast. surg. and med. offr. in charge of Yaws hosp., 1898; med. offr. in charge of agric. schl., 1901; med. offr., W. Africa med. staff, Sierra Leone, 1903; sen. med. offr., S. Nigeria, 1907.

GRAY, SIR S. BROWNLLOW, KT. BACH. (1901), C.M.G. (1888).—B. 1823; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1847; inspr. of schls., Bermuda, 1848; atty.-gen. and advoc.-gen., 1861; ch. justice, May, 1900, ret., 1904.

GRAY, THOS. ALEXANDER.—B. 1864; in R.I.C., June, 1882, to Jan., 1899; in charg. div., Leeward Is. pol. and gaol, Montserrat, 1889; mem. of hurricane distress relief comtce. of roads, Northern dist., 1900; inspr. of pol. sci., Dominica, Feb., 1902; J.P. and mem. of telegr. bd., Dominica, May, 1902; lieut., Roseau vol. brigade, July, 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of sci., Dominica, June to Nov., 1903, and again Apr. to Oct., 1904; mil. instr., Dominica dep. reserve, 1st Apr., 1904; mem. of Roseau town council, Jan., 1906; dep. supt. of jail at Mombasa, E. Africa, Jan., 1907.
GREAVES, DUDLEY, A., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Edin.—Ast. med. offr., Virginia, March, 1901; deputy cornr.

GREAVES, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR GEORGE RICHARDSON, K.C.B. (1885), K.C.M.G. (1881), C.B.—B. 1850; has held several important posts: ch. sec. to Cyprus, July, 1878, to July, 1879; adjt.-gen. India, Oct., 1879, to Oct., 1884; ch. of the army at Suakin, 1885; in command of Meerut dist. Indian army to 1892.

GREAVES, KENNETH JAMES.—B. 1850; Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to bar, Inner Temple, 26th Jan., 1900; ag. deputy clk., house of assem., Barbados, May-Sept., 1900; ag. mag., Bridgetown, Oct., 1900, to Feb., 1901; ditto, April, 1901; ditto, St. Thomas and the Virgin Islands, Nov., 1901, to Aug., 1902; appntd. to mag., 1st Sept., 1902; ag. pol. mag., jun. petty debt ct., and recvr. of wrecks of ships, "E" from 5th Oct., 1907, to 28th Feb., 1908.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM HERBERT BACH.—B. 1894, K.C.—B. 1857; B.A.; honours, juris., 1879; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1880; ag. solr.-gen., Barbados, Aug., 1885, to July, 1885, and July, 1886, to Jan., 1887; confirmed; J.P., Sept., 1885; escheator-gen., Q.C. for Barbados, 1890; mem. exec. coun.; acted as asty.-gen., July to Dec., 1887, and to Dec., 1891; mem. of house of assem. and of educn., 1888-1902; asty.-gen., Mar., 1898; just., Apr., 1902.

GREEN, AMAZIAH.—Entd. audit dept., Wales, 1877; registr. of adjustments, audit dept., for two years; bookkeeper, post and telegraph dept., for eight years; acctnt., dept. of jts., 1887; senior inspr., treasy. dept., 1896; mem. staff paymr., 1900; Comwth. pub. serv. inspr., W. Australia, 1902; is also comwth. pub. wks. offr., and registr. for comwth. pub. wks., W. Australia; J.P. for W. Australia and N.S.W.

GREEN, ALFRED PIERCE.—B. 1856; A.M.D., 1878; med. offr., col. forces in India, 1881; regtl. surg., left wing, C.M.R., by med. offr., Cape rly., 1885; ditto, Bechuanaland border pol., 1885; J.P., Mafeking, 1892; capt., Johannesburg mtd. rly., 1901; sen. offr., concentration camp, Wynburg, Oct., 1902; Basutoland war medal, 1901; S.A. war medal and clasps, 1901.

GREEN, CHARLES FRANCIS J.—Cadet, Sttlnts., Nov., 1898; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Mar., 1900; ag. 4th mag., Sing., June; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; asty.-offr., Malacca, Feb., 1902; confirmed, July; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., July, 1906.
GREEN, GEORGE C.—B. 1882; apptd. to compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the serv., and assigned to C.O., 26th Aug., 1891.

GREEN, JOHN FREDERICK NORMAN.—B. 1873; scholar, Emmanuel Coll., Camb., bracketed 15th wrangler, 1883; B.A., 1884; class, Part II., nat. science tripes, 1894; 2nd elk. C.O. after compet. exam., Feb., 1896; priv. sec. to Sir E. Wingfield, Dec., 1898; to Sir M. Ommanney, 1900; 1st class clk., 16th June, 1902; sec. to col. regns. comte., 1907-8.
GREENE, GEOFFREY PHILIP.—B. 1868; gen. mag., Ceylon rlyws., 11th Oct., 1901.

GREENE, GEORGE BALL.—B. 1873; ed. in England; jun. elk. ld. dept., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1887; 5th class elk., Feb., 1888; 4th elk., secretariat, 1891; sec. to comte. for inquiring into post-office, 1893; sec. pilotage coman., and 3rd class, 1895; 2nd class, and despatch elk., 1899; 1st class elk., 1901; ag. prim. elk., 1902, 1903, 1904, 1906 and 1907; priv. sec. to gov. and ag. gov., 1905, 1906 and 1907.

GREENE, JOHN LE SUEUR.—Ed. Roy. Coll.; entered col. sec. office, Mauritius, Jan., 1886; jun. copyist, procur.-gen.'s office, Mar., 1887; sen. ditto, Feb., 1888; gov't. translator of laws, Nov., 1888; 5th class elk., July, 1890; 4th class elk., Mar., 1896.

GREENE, SIR WILLIAM CONYNGHAM, K.C.B. (1900), C.B. (1897).—B. 1854; ed. Harrow, and Pemb. Coll., Ox.; M.A. 1880; entrd. F.O. 1877; ag. 3rd sec. legation at Athens, 1880; ditto Stuttgart, 1883; ag. chargé d'affaires Stuttgart and Darmstadt at various times, 1883-9; 2nd sec. diplomatic service, 1887; 2nd sec. Hague, 1889-91; Brussels, 1891-3; 3rd sec. of legation and chargé d'affaires Teheran, 1893-6; H.M. agt. at Pretoria, S.A. Republic, with rank of chargé d'affaires, 25th Aug., 1896, to 1899.
GREENE, W. H., M.I.C.E. (London).—Asst. engrnr., Victorian rly., 1885; dist. engrnr., 1868; res. engrnr., 1868; chb. engrnr. of existing lines, 1882; Victorian rly. coman., 1887-92.

GREENER, HERBERT.—D.S.O., clk., sec's dept., G.P.O., Cape, 1884; in charge of money order dept., 1886; last clk., admtr.'s office, B. Bechuana land, 1888; recvr. of revenue, Vryburg, and P.M.G., Bechuana land, 1889; examnr. of accts., acctnt. to high coman., Capetown, 1890; paymr. (rank captain), Bech. Br. pol., 1891, combined with prin. cust. offr., for Bech. Prot., 1896; specially employed in charge of finances, Mafeking siege, and financial ast. to mil. gov't., Bloemfontein, 1899-1900; transf'd. to S.A.C. as ch. paymr. and acctnt.-gen. (rank major), Oct., 1900; promoted lt.-col., 1901.

GREENLEES, THOMAS DUNCAN, M.B. Edin.—Surg.-capt., Graham's Town 1st city volrs.; late ast. med. offr., counties asyl., Carlisle, and City of London asyl.; prizeman medico-psychological assn.; med. supt., Graham's Town asyl., and visiting surg. to the chronic sick hospital, Aug., 1890.
GREENWAY, THE HON. THOMAS.—Mem. of legis. assm., Manitoba; formerly pres. of exec. coun.; premier; min. of agric. and immigr. and rly. coman.

GREENWOOD, ALFRED CRAVEN.—Exec. staff, inventions exhib., 1885, and Col. and Ind. exhib., 1886, and Royal Jubilee exhib., Manchester, 1887; sec. to various charitable insttns., 1887 to 1890; priv. sec. to Sir A. Shea, gov. Bahamas, and clk. to coun., 1891-4; treas. of Gibraltar, 1897; J.P. and mem. of sanitary coman., chrmn. at various times, also contract. offr., 1897; ag. capt. of port on various occasions; hosp. coman., 1903; chrmn. of comte. on wharfage tolls and storage waterport wharf, 1902; chrmn. of comte. on management, working and revenue of new comm. mole, 1904.

GREENWOOD, THOMAS JAMES.—B. 1864; supt. of transport, Egyptian campaign, 1882; awarded Queen's medal and Khedive's star; inspr. Cyprus pol., Feb., 1887; ag. local comdt., Limassol, Oct. to Dec., 1894, and Apr. to July, 1895, bronze medal, roy. hum. soc., for saving and attempting to save life during Limassol floods, Nov. 12th, 1894; local comdt., Cyprus pol., 1896; ag. coman., Kyrena, May to Nov., 1900; ditto, Larnaca, Oct. to Dec., 1901.

GREGOROWSKI, REGINALD ADDINGTON.—2nd elk., govt. sec.'s office, O.F.S., Dec., 1896; priv. sec. to state pres., 5th Mar., 1896; landdrost clk., Bethlehem, Dec., 1897; priv. sec. to state pres., July, 1898; landdrost clk., Wepener, Jan., 1899; priv., govt. schl., Reddersburg, O.R.C., 1st Oct., 1900; ast. clk., educn. dept., 1st Mar., 1901; ch. clk. and regir., educn. dept., 1st July, 1901.
GREGORY, HON. HENRY.—B. 1860; M.L.A., for N. Coolgardie, W. Australia, 1897; ditto for Menzies since 1901; min. for mines, 1901-2, and 1902-4; min. for mines and rly., Aug., 1905.

GRENFELL, 1ST BARON OF KILVEY (creat. 1902), GENERAL SIR FRANCIS WALLACE, G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1841; gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Malta, 1898; A.D.C. to Sir A. Cunynghame; also staff offr. to Col. Glyn in the Transkei, 1887-8; dep. ast. adjt. and qrtmr.-gen. in the Kaffir war, 1878; the same in the Zulu war, 1879; ast. qrtmr.-gen. in the Boer war, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882, and the Nile expdn. of 1884; was Sirdar of the Egyptian army from 1885 to 1892; commanded the troops employed at Suakin against the Dervishes; after filling various posts at home, went again to Egypt in 1897 to command the British troops; with Lord Kitchener in the Nile campaign, 1898; gov. of Malta, 1899 to 1903; comdt. 4th Army Corp., 1903-4; G.O.C. forces in Ireland, 1904.

GRENIER, GEORGE.—Employed in Jaffa Kachcheri and registn. dept., 1861-1872; in Queen's advocate's dept., April, 1872, to 1878; second dep. regir. sup. ct., 9th April, 1878; dep.-regir., 15th June, 1885; regir., 1st May, 1894.
GRENIER, JOSEPH.—Dist. judge, Colombo, Ceylon, 23rd April, 1903; has acted on various occasions since 1897 as sol.-gen., coman., of assize, and puisne justice.

GREY, 4TH EARL OF (creat. 1806), VISCOUNT HOWICK, BARON GREY, BART.—G.C.M.G. (1904); ed. Harrow, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.P., 1880-6; dir. B.S.A. Co.; admtr. of Rhodesia, 1896 to 1898; gov.-gen. of Canada, 26th Sept., 1904.

GREY, ROBT. CAMPBELL.—B. 1868; Junior offr. treasry., Selangor, 1st Aug., 1888; ast. in land off., Krian, 14th June, 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, 6th Dec., 1890; ast. mag., Kinta, 24th Dec., 1890; ast. supt. of Penghulun, Lower Perak, 14th June, 1891; supt. ditto, 1st Nov., 1891; ast. mag. and collr. of land rev., Lower Perak, 1st Apr., 1893; ast. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 1st Jan., 1894; ast. sec. to govt., Selangor, 28th Feb., 1895; ast. dist. mag., Krian, 9th Apr., 1896; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1st May, 1897; ditto, Kuala Selangor, 1st Jan., 1899; ditto, Ulu Pahang, 1st Nov., 1899; dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 19th Jan., 1900; ast. sec. to govt., Selangor, 1st Oct. to 30th Dec., 1900; ast. dist. mag., Kinta, 10th Oct., 1901; resumed duties as dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 21st Nov., 1901; sec. to res., Selangor, 1903; ast. Brit. res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1906.

GREY-WILLSON, SIR WM., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1852; ed. at Chelt. Coll.; priv. sec. to Sir William Grey, gov. of Jamaica, 1874; to Lieut.-Gov. Edwd. E. Rushworth, Mar., 1877; admtr., Major-Gen. J. R. Mann, R.E., Aug., 1877; to Lieut.-Gov. F. P. Barlee, Br. Honduras, Sept., 1877; and clk. of the exec. and legis. couns., June, 1878; ag. mag., Orange Walk, in charge of frontier scouts, Dec., 1879, to Apr., 1880, and June, 1880, to Mar., 1881; asst. col. sec. and treas., S. Leone, 1883; special commnr., Sulymah, Dec., 1883, to June, 1884; 4th asst. col. sec., G. Coast Col., 1884; col. sec., St. Helena, July, 1886; ag. gov., 1887; gov., 1890; gov., Falklands, 1897; gov., Bahamas, 5th May, 1904.
GRIESS, WILLIAM MASON.—Ed. at Repton Schl. and Cooper's Hill; senr. asst. engnr., Uganda Rly., 26th Dec., 1895.
GRIFITH, W. L.—Agt. of the Canadian govt. in Wales for some years; sec. to high commr., Mar., 1903.

GRIFITH, Sir Wm. Brandford, Kt. Bach. (1888).—B. 1858; Gilchrist scholar, 1877; B.A. (Lond.), 1880; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1881; ag. Queen's advoc., G. Coast Col., Apr. to Oct., 1884; dist. commr., 1885; ag. puisne judge, Lagos, Jan., and Cape Coast, May, 1885; ag. Queen's advoc., 1886 and 1887; compiled ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1887; res. mag., Jamaica, 1888; R.M., St. Catherine, Feb., 1893; ag. atty.-gen., May to Dec., 1893; ch. just., G. Coast Col., May, 1895; admst. govt. of Lagos, July to Aug., 1896; dep. for gov. G. Coast, Aug. to Nov., 1897; revised ordinances of G. Coast Col., 1898 and 1903.

GRIFITHS, George Hollier, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1837; clk. col. sec.'s off., Mauritius, 1860; elk. of pc. w. dept., Mauritius, 1861; ch. offr. pol. and inspr. of liberated Africans, Seychelles, 1881; ag. ch. as civ. comntr., 1884-87; inspr. of pol., Maur., 1887; ret., 1888; ag. ch. offr. pol., Sey., 1889; inspr. of town prc., and jnt. conservator of crown lds., 1898; cent. dist., 1900; treasr. and collr. of cust., 23rd Dec., 1901; ag. crown prosecutor, Oct., 1906.

GRINDLE, G. E. A.—B. 1869; scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 1st class classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schl., 1891; B.A., 1891; elected fellow of C.C.C., 1891; Chancellor's English Essay, 1892; M.A., 1894; 2nd class clk. in local govt. board, Feb., 1893; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1893; 2nd class clk., after compet. exam., in office of S. of S. for Cola., 10th Feb., 1896; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Nov., 1898; 1st class clk., 30th June, 1900.

GRINLINTON, F. H., C.M.G. (1903), B. 1853, F.R.G.S., A.I.C.E., F.R. Met. S.—Apptd. to Ceylon survey dept., Apr., 1870; dist. survr., Jan., 1877; ch. survr., Nov., 1881; ag. survr.-gen., Aug., 1894; survr.-gen., Dec., 1896; ret. June, 1904; proceeded to F.M.S. to advise govt. on reorganiztn. of survey dept., Oct., 1904.

GRINLINTON, Sir J. K., K.T. Bach.
