(treason branch), Cape Town, Sept., 1900; B.A., Cape Univ., 1900; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., June, 1901; sec. to law dept., Oct., 1904; ag. clk. to legis. coun., May-July, 1906; joint editor of law-book, O.R.C., 1900-1906.

LEFROY, Hon. Henry B., C.M.G. (1903).—Elected M.L.A., W. Australia, 1892; re-elected, 1894 and 1897; min. of educn., May, 1897; min. of mines, May, 1898; ag.-gen. for W. Australia in London, 1901; resigned, 1904.

LEGGE, Charles Arthur Lionel.—B. 1879; clerical asst., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th class offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th class clk., pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut., B. G. Artillery, 1901; attached to No. 32 Coy. R.G.A., Portsmouth, July, 1904; cert. in depression range finding, July, 1904; Hythe musk. cert., Aug., 1904; sub-inspr. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Oct., 1904; promoted capt., B. Guiana Artillery, and seconded, Dec., 1904; mem. local health authority, St. Andrew's parish, Grenada, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. of pol., Grenada, from June, 1907.

LE HUNTE, Sir George Ruthven, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1852; Trin. Coll., Camb., 1873; M.A., 1880; called to the bar, Inner Tem., May, 1881; 2nd priv. sec. to gov., Fiji, 1875; stip. mag., 1875; res. comsnr., Col. Viti Levu, 1877; ag. comdt., A.N.C., 1878-9; ag. col. sec., 1880; asst. col. sec., Jan., 1882; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1882; judicial comsnr. for the high comsnr. to W. Pacific Is., May, 1883; ag. atty.-gen., May, 1884; ag. rec-gen., Jan., 1886; pres. of Dominica, 1887; col. sec., Barbados, with dormant comsnr. as admstr., 1894; col. sec. of Mauritius, 1897; lieut.-govrn. of Br. New Guinea, 1888; gov. of S. Australia, 1903.

LEISK, James Rankine.—Chartered acctnt., Glasgow; asst. recvr. of rev., Johannesburg, 2nd Sept., 1900; ch. clk. to cont. of treasy., Transvaal, 21st May, 1901; asst. sec. treasy., 1st Apr., 1902; sec. to treasy., 1st July, 1903 (Queen's S. African war medal with 3 clasps).
LE JUGE DE SEGRAIS, Paul.—Assoc. M.I.C.E., govt. survr. and supt. of pub. wks., Seychelles, June, 1891; govt. engr. and archt., Mauritius, 14th Sept., 1896; ag. survr.-gen., 1896-97.

LE MESSURIER, Henry Wm.—B. 1848; J.P., Newfoundland, 1879; M.H.A., 1886-89; 3rd landing waiter and asst. tide surr. of cust., Newfild., 1894 to 1897; asst. collr. and sec. to bd. of rev., 1897; dep. min. of cust., with title of asst. collr., 1898; superintended re-orga-nisation of cust. dept., and planned and inaugurated cust. service in connection with Fed Newfild. Co.'s steamer and rly. system, 1898; corrpdnt., C.I.B., B. of T., 1905; served as commissariat clk. under asst. commissaries prior to removal of the Imperial troops.

LEMON, Arthur Henry.—Ed. at Merchant Taylors' Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford (scholar); B.A., 1887; cadet, Straits, 1888; priv. sec. to gov., Sept., 1889, to Jan., 1890; attached to Chinese protectorate, Penang, Aug., 1891; sec. to currency comtee., June, 1893; ag. dist. offr., S. Malacca, Dec., 1893; passed in Malay, 1894; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1894; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1895; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., June, ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Aug., 1895; ag. dist. offr. Malacca, Oct., 1895; dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, June, 1896, but continued to act at Malacca; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1898; ag. ass't. P.M.G., Penang, Aug., 1898; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1902; ag. collr. of land rev. and regist. of deeds, Penang, Apr., 1903; ag. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1906; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Jan., 1907; ag. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1907.

LENNOX, Arbyll Napier Oliver.—Sixth clk., imigrn. dept., Br. Guiana, 1882; 5th clk., 1884; 4th clk., 1885; awarded 2nd class certif. in Hindustani, 1885; acted as 1st clk., 1887, and July, 1889, to Jan., 1890; 3rd class clk., land dept., Jan., 1891; 2nd class clk., Apr., 1891.
LEVESON-GOWER, John.—Subaltern, 3rd K.A.R., 8th May, 1903; asst. coll., E. Africa Prot., 22nd May, 1906.

LEVET, George Collins, C.M.G. (1873).—B.1835; mem. leg. coun., Victoria, 1861-8; was sec. to the Victorian comsnr., and ag. comsnr. in charge of Victorian exhibits at Paris Exhibtn., 1878; sec. Melbourne Exhibtn., 1880-1; exec. comsnr., Victoria Amsterdam Exhibtn., 1883; sec. Adelaide Jubilee Exhibtn., 1887, and Tasmanian Exhibtn., 1894.
LE VEUX, Leon.—Asst. survr., pub. wks. dep., Seychelles, 24th May, 1902; insp. pol. and supt. prisons, July, 1906.

LEWIS, Arthur Kennedy.—Ed. at Wesleyan high schl. and C.M.S. grammar schl., S. Leon; med. dresser attached to Anglo-French bounda-ry comsnr., Dec., 1891, to Apr., 1892 (reed. thanks of govt.); clk. to D.C.; clk. of ct. of requests; registr. of births and deaths, W. dist., 1893 to 1895; 3rd clk. registr. gen.'s dept., 1895 to 1897. 2nd clk., atty.-gen.'s off., 1898; travelled with and clk. to atty.-gen. during trial of insurgents by Judge G. A. Bonner, 1898; transport clk., N.A. dept., 1899; ditto P.W.D., Jan., 1900, to July, 1901; offr. in charge of transport on gov't. tours, 1899-1901; regiam. offr., G.P.O., Freetown, Aug., 1901, to Jan., 1903; asst. postmr., Gambia, Feb., 1903.

LEWIS, EDWARD CORNEWALL LYNCH.—B. 1863; entered Govt. office, St. Vincent, as supernum., May, 1877; clk. to Kingstown pol. mag., 1880; ag. col. registr., 1883; acted as provost-marshal, 1884; ag. col. postmr., 1885; ag. registr., 1886; dep. coroner, Kingstown, 1886; acctnt. of pub. wks. dept., Grenada, 1887; col. postmr., St. Vincent, 1889; ag. col. postmr., Grenada, Jan. to Mar., 1889; ag. registr. sup. court, St. Vincent, Nov., 1889, to Nov., 1890; ex officio inspr. of prisons, registr. of the vice-adm. court, ag. col. postmr., Grenada, Aug., 1891; resumed col. postmr., St. Vincent, Jan., 1892; J.P., 1892; asst. postmr.-gen., Hong Kong, Jan., 1899; J.P., 1899; ag. postmr.-gen., Jan., 1900, to Sept., 1901, and Jan., 1902, to Feb., 1903.

LEWIS, CAPT. E. H.—Joined 21st Lancers in India, 1892; present with that regt. at battle of Omdurman, 1898 (medal and Khedive's medal); served with W.A.F.F. from July, 1899, to May, 1903; served with expdh. for relief of Kumasi, 1900 (Ashanti medal); present at capture of Kontagaro, 1901 (ment. in despa., medal with clasp for N. Nigeria); present at capture of Kano (clasp for N. Nigeria) 1903; apptd. 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, May, 1903.

LEWIS, JOHN CHRISTOPHER.—Ent. Trinidad service, 1861; postmr., San Fernando, Sept., 1869; 5th clk., G.P.O., 1868; ag. 2nd clk., G.P.O., 1868; ag. P.M.G., 1886, 1891, and 1899; recd. thanks of inhabitants of San Fernando and the Naparimas for his zeal in the discharge of his duties, 1882; recd. thanks of Sir Wm. Robinson for the efficient and satisfactory manner in which he had performed the duties of P.M.G.; mem. of mun. coun., San Fernando, 1903; mayor, 1904; mgr. of govt. cent. boys' and girls' schl. since 1890; J.P. and commr. of affidavits.

LEWIS, JOHN EDGAR ANDERSON.—B. 1862; B.A., Caius Coll., Camb., 1883; extra offr., Sarawak civ. ser., Oct., 1886; mag. and manager Sarawak govt. printing off., Aug., 1888; ag. princ. insp. of pol. and prisons, Nov., 1889, to May, 1890; Sept., 1894, Dec., 1895; clk. to gen. coun., July, 1897; res., 2nd class, Sept., 1898; inspr. of govt. schls., Jan., 1906.

LEWIS, JOHN PENRY.—B. 1854; ed. at Mill Hill Schl. and Queen's Univ.; B.A., 1876; M.A., 1882; writer, Ceylon civ. ser., July, 1877; ag. pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1881; ditto, Pt. Pedro, 1883; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Jaffna, N.P., 1883; pol. mag., Gampula, 1884; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., cent. prov., Kandy, 1885; ag. commr. of requests, Colombo, 1885-6; office asst. to govt. agent, C.P., 1887; ag. dist. judge, Matara, ditto, Negombo, 1888; asst. agt., Negombo, 1891; ditto, Vavuniya, Apr., 1896; ag. asst. govt. agt., Chilaw, 1896; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, June; Negombo, Sept., 1896; special offr. under waste lands ordnec., 1897-1901; ag. govt. agt., N. prov., May, 1902; ditto, Cent. prov., Nov., 1902; ditto, N. prov., June, 1903; offr., cls. I., Jan., 1905; govt. agt., Cent. prov., May, 1906; M.L.C.

LEWIS, HON. SIR NEIL ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1901), B.A.—atty.-gen. in Dobson-Hervy ministry, Tasmania, 1892-4; premier and atty.-gen. in Lewis ministry, 1900; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as min. without portfolio, Jan., 1901.

LEWIS, SIR WALTER LLEWELLYN, KT. BACH. (1904), M.A.—B. 1849; gold medallist, Queen's Univ. (Ireland), 1869 and 1871; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1876; South Wales and Chester circuits; stip. justice, Trinidad, Jan., 1885; chmn. of comsm. on agricultural contracts; chmn. of directors, public service widows' and orphans' fund, 1890-1900; puisne judge, 1893; ch. justice, British Honduras, 1900; coronation medal, 1902; ret., 1906.

LEYE, ANDREW K.—Priv. sec. to gov. Labuan, 1882; ag. col. treas. and auditor, 1882 to 1885, and judge gen. ct., 1884-5; mag., ct. of requests, and P.M.G. Sarawak, Oct., 1896; also shipping master and coroner, 1st div.

LEYE, NORMAN MACLEAN.—Ed. at Glasgow Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1900; (distinction midwifery); certif., Liverpool sch. of trop. med., 1901; med. offr., Brit. Cent. Africa, Sept., 1904; E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Sept., 1905.

LEYE, PETER, C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1851; ed. at Univs. of Glasgow and Edinburgh, of which latter he is a graduate in medicine; entered col. service, Labuan, Feb., 1876; administd. the govt. 1881-7; held a comsm. as H.B.M.'s consul-gen. for Borneo; ret., 1889.

LIGHTBOURN, JAMES THOMAS.—Ed. at Bahamas collegiate institute; supernumerary, treasy. and cust. dept., Bahamas, Jan., 1885; 3rd clk., treasy., Dec., 1885; 2nd clk. and bookkeeper, treasy. dept., 1895; has held several acting appointments.
LINCOLN, GABRIEL.—Copyist, col. sec.'s off., Mauritius, 24th June, 1884; clk. to exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1st June, 1901; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., 9th Nov., 1903.

LINTHICUM, 1ST MARQUIS OF (U.K. creat. 1902), 7TH EARL OF HOPETOUN (Scot.), P.C., K.T. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1889), G.C.V.O. (1900), JOHN ADRIAN LOUIS HOPE; also VISCOUNT AITHRE and BARON HOPE (1703, Scotch peerages); BARON HOPEFOWN (1809) and BARON NIDDERY (1814) in peerage of U.K.—B. 1860; lieut., Lanark Yeom. Cav., 1880; lord-in-waiting, 1885-6 and 1888-9; lord high commr. to gen. assem. of Church of Scotland, 1887-8; gov., Victoria, 1889-95; paymr.-gen., 1895; Lord Chamberlain, 1899; governor-general and commander-in-chief of the Commonwealth of Australia, 1900; re-signed, 1902.

LISTER, JOSEPH STORR, I.S.O. (1903).—Asst. to man. of Tukva tea and cinchona estate, Darjeeling, May, 1870; passed lower standard exam. in Hindu-stani and an exam. in land surveying and levelling; apptd. to the forest dept., Punjab, in charge of Changla Manga, Lahore, and Umballa plantations, 1871; transf'd. to Hazara, to assist dep. conservator in exploring and reporting on hill forests of Khagan; on special duty to Kulu and Kangra in charge of Nurpur sub-division; left India owing to ill-health, 1874; supt. of plantations, Cape, Jan., 1875; J.P. and road mag., Aug., 1875; in conjunction with other duties, custodian of ex-chief Langalibalele and ex-king Cetewayo during their detention at Vitvlugt and Oude Moulen; established plantations at Worcester, Beaufort West, Tokai, and Kluitjes Kraal; sec. to sub-comittee, for wood and vegetable products, Indian and Col. Exhibn., 1886; conservator of forests, E. diva., Sept., 1888; ag. ch. conservator forests, Oct., 1905; apptmt. confirmed, 1st Ju. 1906.

LLEWELYN, Sir ROBT. B., K.C.M.G. (1898); C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1845; extra clk., C.O., London, from 1868; reg. in col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, Nov., 1869; was nominated as one of a comsn. apptd. to inquire into prison discipline in Jamaica, 1873; priv. sec. to Sir J. P. Grant, July, 1873; clk. of priv. coun., Jamaica, 1877; comsnr. for Turks Is., 1878; admstr. of Tobago, 1885, admstr., St. Vincent, 1888; ag. admstr., Grenada, 1888; admstr., St. Lucia, 1889, with dormant comsn. as admstr., Windward Is.; admstr. Gambia, 1891; African war medal with two clasps, 1891-2, and 1893-4; gov. Windward Islands, 1900-6.

LLOYD, Augustus GEORGE.—Ch. clk. and cashier, col. treas., S. Leone, Dec., 1896; ag. col. treas., Apr. to July, 1899; ag. asst. col. treas., July, 1899; asst. acctnt., African field force, N. Nigeria, Oct., 1899; asst. treas., N. Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1900; ditto, G. Coast, Sept., 1900; asst. dist. comsnr., Apr., 1902; asst. col. sec., Mar., 1904.
LLOYD, James SIMEON.—Ed. at Codrington Coll., Barbados; 3rd clk., custs., Barbados, Nov., 1876; offr., of custs., on amalgamation of indoor and outdoor staff, Dec., 1890.

LLOYD, John BARCLAY.—B. 1864; ed. at Highgate schl. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford (exhibn.), B.A., 1887; served with C.I.V. in S. African war, 1900; barr.-at-law, Inner Temp.; crown proscr., O.R.C., Sept., 1900; ditto to civ. admstr., Feb., 1901; ditto to spec. crim. ct., July, 1901; advoc., high ct., O.R.C., 1902; asst. law adviser, July, 1902; ag. atty.-gen., 1903, 1905 and 1906-7; M.L.C., 1907.

LOBB, Reginald POPHAM, B.A., F.R.G.S.—Ed. at Clifton and Christ's Coll., Camb.; mod. and medieval languages tripes, 1895; priv. sec. to Sir F. Lugard, high comsnr. of N. Nigeria, Oct., 1900; transf. to polit. dept. as asst. rea., Nupe Prov., Oct., 1901; 3rd cls. rea., Zaria, May, 1902; ag. rea., Zaria, Jan., 1903; 2nd cls. rea., Muri Prov., Oct., 1903; Kano-Sokoto campaign (medal and clasp) 1903, and minor operations; 1903-5; ag. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 1st Jan., 1906.

LOCKE, R. F.—Apptd. cons. agt. in Oil Rivers (now S. Nigeria) Protec., July, 1892; ag. v.-cons. and dep. comsnr., Benin river, May to Nov., 1894; Brohemi expedition, 1894, mentd. in Adml. Bedford's desps., med. and clasp; apptd. dist. comsnr., Apr., 1896; ag. v.-cons. and dep. comsnr., Warri, Sapele, and Benin Riv. Dists., May to Dec., 1896; one of two survivors Benin massacre, Jan., 1897; apptd. dep. comsnr. and v.-cons., Apr., 1897; now divisnl. comsnr., West Div., S. Niger. Protec.

LOCKHART, J. H. STEWART, C.M.G. (1898); F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.—B. 1858; Edin. Univ.; Greek medallist, etc.; apptd., after compet. exam., to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1878; attached to the C.O. for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; supt. opium revenue, Mar., 1883; asst. col. sec. and asst. auditor-gen., Aug., 1883; ag. registr. gen., 1884 and 1885; registr.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; mem. exec. coun., 1891; chairman, bd. of examiners in Chinese, 1891; ditto govern. body Queen's Coll., 1894; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1894; col. sec. and registr.-gen., Mar., 1895; special comsnr. to report on the extension of the bndry. of H. Kong, 1898; representative of Gt. Br. to delimitate bndrys., for both of which recd. thanks of S. S., 1899; rector of coll. of medicine for Chinese, 1895-1902; comsnr. of Weihiwei, 1902.

LOCKHART, Norman.—Fourth revenue offr., Dominica, Feb., 1880; ag. postmtr., 1881; ag. excise offr., May, 1882; 3rd revenue offr., 1883; quarantine offr., 1886; ag. mag., Nevis, 1891; ditto, Dist. E., Dominica, 1892, 1895, 1896; mag., Anguilla, 1895; ag. mag., Dominica, 1897; asst. treas. and regiar. of shipping, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1897; ag. treas., 1899, 1900, 1901 and 1902; treas., Grenada, 1903.
LOCKYER, E.—Examr. of accts., audit dept., O.R.C., 26th June, 1903.

LOGAN, Ewen REGINALD.—Ed. at Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., B.A., 1891; M.A., 1897; student, Inner Temp., Jan., 1894; called to bar, Nov. 1899; joined Middlx. and N. London ses. cent. crim. ct., 1900; serv. in Boer war as 2nd lieut., 65th squad., I.Y., 1901; Queen's medal and four clasps; mines dept., Transvaal govt., Aug., 1901, to Apr., 1902; asst. rea. mag., Transvaal, May, 1902, to May 1904; mag. E. Africa Prot., 4th Aug., 1905.
LONG, THOMAS, I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1897; Topographical survr. in the survr.-gen.'s office, Newfield, Mar., 1872; 1st clk., Sept., 1882; dep. min. agricul. and mines, 1898.

LONGLEY, Hon. J. WILBERFORCE.—B.A., Acadia Coll., N. Scotia, 1871; M.A., 1875; called to the bar, N.S., Sept., 1875; comsnr. sup. ct., 1876; elected to Prov. Parlmt., June, 1882; sworn in mem. of exec., July, 1884; atty.-gen., May, 1886; comsnr. for revising and consolidating the law and framing Judicature Act, Oct., 1882; editorial writer "Acadian Recorder" since 1874.

LORAM, CHAS. TEMPLEMAN.—B. 1879; B.A., LL.B., asst. mast., Pietermaritzburg Coll., Natal, seconded, 31st May, 1901; asst. inspr. of nat. schls., 15th Feb., 1906; asst. inspr. of schls., 1st July, 1906.
LORANGER, L. O., K.C.—Atty.-gen.—prov. of Quebec, Canada; apptd. a judge of sup. ct. of Quebec, 1882.

LORANS, Henri, M.B.C.M., and D.P.H., Edin.—Police and prison surg., Mauritius, 1884; poor law med. offr., Port Louis, 10th Jan., 1884; med. inspr., 15th Nov., 1895; ag. direcrt., med. and health dept., 1900 and 1902; nom. mem. of com. of govt.

LORNE, J., M.A., B.Sc. Edin.—Cadet, S. S., Nov., 1890; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1901; ag. head of Malay Coll., Malacca, May, 1901; ag. asst. registr., sup. ct., and mag. and sheriff, Malacca, Apr., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Jan., 1904.
LOUGHLAND, CHAS. VINCENT.—B. 1871; asst. acctnt., W.A.F.F., N. Nigeria, 27th June, 1900; asst. treas., 1st Apr., 1902.

LOVEGROVE, CHARLES ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Charterhouse; asst. to Mr. W. Wilson, C.E., Westminster, 1879; contractor's engnr., Coleford and Monmouth rlyw., 1881; asst. to Mr. W. Wilson, C.E., 1884; dist. engnr., W. P.W.D., Ceylon, 1886; ag. prov. engr., W. Prov., 1887; ditto, N.W. Prov., 1897; ditto, S. Prov., and mem. mun. coun., Galle, 1898; ag. prov. engr., E. Prov., 1898; ag. asst. dir. of pub. wks., Ceylon, 1900; prov. engr., Uva, 1900; asst. dir., P.W., 1903.

LOVELACE, HARTE LOVELACE.—B. 1858; clk. of the peace, Trinidad, 1876; clk. of cust., 1878; audit offr., 1878; prim. clk., sup. ct., 1887; also comsnr. of oaths; ag. dcp. registr., 1896, 1900, 1903, 1906 and 1907.

LOVELL, Sir FRANCIS HENRY, K.T., B.A.CHESTER (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1844; col. surg., S. Leone, Dec., 1873; med. offr., imgrn. dept., Mauritius, 1878; ch. med. offr. and pres. gen. bd. of health, 1879; mem. coun. of govt., 1880; surg.-gen. and med. offr. of health, and mem. legis... coun., Trinidad, Sept., 1893-1901; also mem. exec. coun., 1894; ret., 1902.

LOW, ALFRED M.—Ed. at Aldenham; open scholarship at Pem. Coll., Camb., 1890; B.A. class. tripos, 1893; ag. prof. of classics, Codrington (Coll., Barbados, 1896; 3rd master, Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad, June, 1897.
LUSWEY, LIONEL DEWE.—Med. offr., Uganda Prot., 1st Mar., 1902.

LUCAS, SIR CHARLES PRESTWOOD, K.C.M.G. (1907), C.B. (1901), B.A. —B. 1853; ed. at Winchester Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; open exhibitioner; 1st class in mods., 1873, and 1st class in final classical schools, June, 1876; Chancellor's prize for Latin essay, 1877; called to the bar by the Soc. of Lincoln's Inn, Easter, 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1877; priv. sec. to Sir R. Herbert, Dec., 1881; Earl Granville, Feb., 1886, and again to Sir R. Herbert, Aug., 1886; also chairman of comttee. of emigrants' information office till 31st Dec., 1896; 1st class clk., Feb., 1892; author of "Historical Geography of the British Colonies"; prin. clk., Sept., 1896; asst. under-sec. of state, June, 1897.

LUCIE-SMITH, A. VAN W.—Ed. at Rugby; solr., Br. Guiana, 1877; called to bar, Mid. Term., 1881; ag. solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1887; pres. dist. ct., Famagusta, Cyprus, 1887; Limasol, 1892; ag. Queen's advoc., 1893; ag. judge, cons. ct., Constantinople, 1896; res. mag., Kingston, Jamaica, Nov., 1896; puisne judge, B.G., Jan., 1898; ag. ch. justice, 1900-01; one of the compilers of the "Rules of Court," 1900.

LUCKIE, D.M., F.S.S.—Trained in a lawyer's office in Montrose, Scotland; in 1862 emigrated to N. Zealand, as editor and part proprietor of the "Nelson Colonist," which he largely popularised; in 1872 was elected mem. of parl. for Nelson City; in 1873 became editor of the "Southern Cross," and when it was amalgamated with the "New Zealand Herald," the leading journal in the col., became ch. editor of the combined papers; asst. comsnr. of gov't. assurance, 1878.

LUCKMAN, ARTHUR OVERTON.—Thames div., Royal Engnrs., submarine miners; asst. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1st May, 1898; transf'd to G. Coast regt.; W.A.F.F., 1900; comsnr., W. Frontier, G. Coast, Dec., 1900, to Sept., 1901; dist. comsnr., N. Territories, G. Coast, 10th Apr., 1902; medal, Ashanti rising, 1900; asst. collr. E. Africa Prot., 24th Nov., 1905.

LUGARD, COL. SIR FREDERICK JOHN DEALTRY, K.C.M.G. (1901), C.B. (1895), D.S.O. (1887).—B. 1858; ed. Rossall and Sandhurst; 1st coman., 9th foot, May, 1878; capt., Aug., 1885; major, Aug., 1896; liout.-col., July, 1899; col., July, 1905; temp. brig.-gen., 1900-07; to India, Sept., 1878; active service, Afghanistan, Sept., 1879, to Sept., 1890; medal: Soudan, Feb. to Nov., 1885; medal, clasps, "Tofrek" Khedive's Star, despatches; 3urmah, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1887; mentioned in despatches, D.S.O. and medal. To Africa, 'eh., 1888; commanding expdn. against slave riders; very severely wounded. To East Africa, Nov., 1889, to Oct., 1902; exploration of Sabakh; admnstr. Uganda; went to Borgu in comd. expdn., July, 1894, and made treaties on behalf of Royal Niger Company, returned, Apr., 1895; (eh., 1896, led expedition across Kalahari for Br. charterland Co.; apptd. comsnr., and comdt., West African frontier force, Aug., 1897; high comsnr. for Northern Nigeria, 29th Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906; gov. of Hong Kong, 1st May, 1907.

LUMB, CHARLES FREDERICK.—Ed. at Liverpool Inst. (Yates exhibitioner), and Downing Coll., Camb. (scholar and prizeman in maths, nat. sci. and law); B.A., math. tripos, 1870; M.A., and LL.M., 1877; LL.D., 1892; barrister of Linc.'s Inn, 1874, and practised on N. circuit; vice-pres. of Liverpool inst., and mem. of Liverpool schl. bd.; prosecutor for the mint, Feb., 1886; 2nd puisne judge, Trinidad, 1887; mem. educn. comsn., 1887; of bd. of educn., 1887; of Roy. Coll. coun., 1888; of library comttee., 1889; drafted dist. cts. and educn. orclces, and rules thereunder; puisne judge, Jamaica, 1892; mem. of bd. of educn., 1893-4; ag. ch. justice, 1895-6; chmn. of the education comsn., 1897-8.

LUTTON, HARRY, M.J.I.E.—B. 1875; ed. Epsom Coll.; articled pupil, City engnr., Canterbury, 1892-6; asst. engnr., Malvern dist. coun., 1896-1900; asst. engnr., Ibadan-Jebba survey, Lagos govt. rly., 1900-1; asst. supt. of wks., Malacca, S. Sttlm., 1902-4; ag. supt. wks. and surveys, Malacca, 1904; J.P. for Malacca, 1905.

LUSHINGTON, CHARLES MORANT.—B. 1854; ed. at Malvern Coll.; writer, Ceylon civ. serv., 1st Apr., 1876; pol. mag., Balapitimodara, 25th Dec., 1877; offr. of cls. V., 1st Feb., 1878; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. prov., 17th May, 1878; offr. of cl. IV., 1st Apr., 1883; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, 12th Mar., 1884; dist. judge, Puttalam, in addition, 1st Jan., 1888; offr. of cls. III., 1st Mar., 1889; asst. govt. agt., Negombo, 13th Oct., 1890; pol. mag., Colombo, 12th Sept., 1891; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, 9th May, 1892; ditto, Hambantota, 7th Nov., 1892; dist. judge, Batticaloa, 7th Nov., 1893; asst. govt. agt., Matara, 24th May, 1894; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, 31st July, 1895; ditto, Trincomalee, 1st Oct., 1896; offr. of cls. II., 15th May, 1898; govt. agt., E. prov., in addition to other duties, 13th June, 1903; offr. of cls. I., grade II., 22nd Sept., 1904; govt. agt., prov. of Uva, 24th Oct., 1906; govt. agt., S. prov., 7th Dec., 1906; govt. agt. and fiscal for S. prov., 22nd Sept., 1907.

LYNCH, G. W. A.—B. 1861; ed. Westminster, Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb. B.A. (1883), and St. Thomas's hospital, Lond.; M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P. Lond., 1885; M.B., B.C., Camb., 1887; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1890; sen. med. offr., 1898; ag. ch. med. offr., Oct., 1899, to Jan., 1900; ditto, and ag. agt.-gen. for imgrn., Mar., 1903, to Jan., 1904.

LYNE, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1844; elected to N. S. Wales parlmt., 1880; sec. pub. wks., 1885-7, and again 1891; mem. exec. coun.; prim. and col. treas., Sept., 1899; joined first Commonwealth ministry as min. of state for home aff., Jan., 1901; ag. min. for defence, 1902; min. for trade and cust., 1903-4; ditto, 1905-6; attended Imp. conf., 1907.

LYONS, CAPT. F. W.—Ed. at Portarlington school, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 80th regt., as 2nd lieut., 15th Feb., 1878; capt., 1884; adjutant, 1885; adjut. of volunteers, 1887-90; capt. reserve of officers, 1890; ag. asst. supt. of pol., S.S., 5th Mar., 1891, to 31st Mar., 1893; apptd. asst. comsnr., Perak Sikhs, 1st Apr., 1893; acted as capt. supt. of pol., and prisons Selangor, Aug., 1893 to Feb., 1895; served in S. Africa, 1878-80, medal with clasp; served in Soudan, 1885-86, medal and Khedive's Star; served in Pahang during disturbances, and recd. thanks of S. of S., 1894; acted as chief pol. offr., Perak, 1900-2; apptd. deputy sup. of pol., Hong Kong, 18th Sept., 1902; ag. capt. supt. of pol., etc., 1903-4.

LYTTELTON, Rt. Hon. Alfred, P.C., M.A., Camb.; Hon. M.A., Oxford; K.C.—B. 1857; ed. at Eton, and Trim. Coll., Camb.; barrister; bencher, Inner Temp., 1899; legal priv. sec. to Sir H. James, attorney-gen., 1882 to 1886; recorder of Hereford, 1894; recorder of Oxford, 1895; dep. high steward of Univ. of Camb., 1899; M.P. for Leamington, Warwick, 1895; chmn. of Transvaal concessions comm., S. Africa, 1900-1; apptd. by Newfd. govt. as arbitrator in case of the Reid railway contract, 1902; S. of S. for the colonies, Oct., 1903, to Dec., 1903.

MAASDORP, Sir ANDRIES FERDINAND STOCKENSTROM, Kt. BACH (1904).—B.A., Lond., 1869; barrister-at-law, Inn. Temp., Nov., 1871; mem. of the house of ass., Cape Colony, 1874 to 1878; solr. gen., Cape Colony, Aug., 1878 to May, 1897; mem. of special (treason) court, Cape Colony, Oct., 1900, to June, 1902; ch. just. of the O.R.C., June, 1902.
MAASDORP, CHRISTIAN GEORGE.—Atty.-gen., Transvaal, Sept., 1878; resig., Jan., 1880; puisne judge, sup. ct., Cape, Nov., 1885; Superior ct., Cape Town, 1896.

MCCALLISTER, ANDREW.—Asst. surv., Oil Rivers Prot. (now So. Nigeria), Sept., 1892; ag. surv.-gen., Feb. to Oct., 1894, and Aug., 1897, to Dec., 1898; survr.-gen., Jan., 1899; dir. of pub. wks., Jan., 1900.

McARTHUR, MALCOLM STEWART HANNIBAL.—B. 1872; ed. at Kelly Coll., Tavistock, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; cadet S. Sttlnts., Nov., 1895; ag. dep. registr. of deeds, Penang, 1896; passed final exam. in Malay, Apr., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., S.S., Jan., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, Aug., 1897; and 3rd mag., Penang, 1898; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1899; ag. asst. sec. to gov't, Selangor, Apr., 1900, to Jan., 1901; dist. offr., Penang, Jan., 1901; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan. to May, 1901; ag. collector of land rev. and regiar. of deeds, Sing.; also ag. sec. to high commr. of F.M.S., May, 1902; ag. commr. of ct. of requests, Sing., Aug., 1903; asst. supt. of Indian immigrts. in addition to other duties, 13th Nov., 1903; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Nov., 1903; confirmed, July, 1904; seconded for spec. serv. under F.O. at Brunei, Apr., 1904; ag. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1905; ag. consul at Brunei, etc., Oct., 1905; H.B.M. consul, Sarawak and B.N. Borneo, Nov., 1905; col. sec., Labuan, and res., Brunei, Jan., 1906; res., Labuan and Brunei, Jan., 1907.
MACARTHUR, WM. HENRY, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1863; Gov't. vet. surgeon, Penang, S. Sttlnts., 21st May, 1892; appt. placed on fixed establishment, 1st Jan., 1897.
MACASKIE, JOHN COCHRANE.—Pres. dist. ct., Cyprus, 1895.
MACAULAY, JOHN DAVIDSON.—Extra elk. col. sec.'s office, S. Leone, 1874; in customs, 1875; tide waiter, 1877; landing waiter, 1879; elk. of customs, 1883.

MCCALLUM, COLONEL SIR HENRY EDWARD, R.F., G.C.M.G. (1904), K.C.M.G. (1898), C.M.G. (1887), A.D.C. to the Queen (1900).—B. 1852; entered the Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich, July, 1868; passed first out of 52 cadets, 1871; after two years' practical training at Chatham, proceeded to Portsmouth (1873), and apptd. supt. of telegraphy, S. dist.; transf. to office of inspr.-gen. of fortifications; priv. sec. to gov't, S. Settlements, Sir William Jervois, and attended him on various missions to the native states of the Malay Peninsula; mentioned in despatches for the assistance given in preparing a project for the defence of Singapore, and again in Dec., 1876, for services in connection with Perak commn. of inquiry; suptding. engnr. admiralty works, Hong Kong, Apr., 1877; in Apr., 1878, selected to proceed to Singapore to adopt measures for the defence of that port; attached to the office of inspr. of wks., roy. arsenal, July, 1879, to July, 1880, when apptd. dep. col. engnr., S. Sttlnts.; is a Pollock medallist of 1871; the annual Fowke medallist for 1874; is an assoc. of instit. of civ. engnrs., etc.; col. engnr. and survr.-gen., S. Sttlnts., 1884; men. of the exec. and legis. couns.; pres. Singapore municipality, Feb., 1881, to July, 1886; apptd. to construct new fortifications of Singapore, Mar., 1885; comdt. Singapore volr. art., 1888; special commr. in Pahang during disturbances, 1891; received thanks of S. of S.; gov. Lagos, 1897; gov. of Newfoundland, 1898; gov. of Natal, 1901; gov. of Ceylon, 1st May, 1907.

MCCARTHY, ROBT. HENRY, C.M.G. (1906).—2nd class elk., impl. customs, Belfast, Feb., 1875; served at Cork and Plymouth; selected for special service with survr.-gen., and in connection with reorganisation of customs statistical dept., 1883; 2nd offr., Folkstone, 1894; collr. of customs, Trinidad, Dec., 1895; mem. legis. coun., 1898; one of the representatives of Trinidad at commercial conferences at Washington and Ottawa, 1899; apptd. to exec. coun. 1903; del. for Trinidad at W. Indian quarantine conf., 1904; mem. West Indian central quarantine authority, 1907.

MCCAY, LT.-COL. THE HON. JAS. WHITESIDE M.A., LL.M., V.D.—B. 1864; mem. legis. ass., Victoria, Nov., 1895, and Oct., 1897, to Dec., 1899; min. of pub. instr. and commr. of trade and cust., Dec., 1899, to Jan., 1900; mem. of Commonwealth parlt., Mar., 1901, and Dec., 1903; min. for defence, ditto, Aug., 1904, to June, 1905.

MCCLELLAN, JOHN WILLIAM TYNDALE.—Ed. at Malvern Col.; and Royal Agric. Coll.; asst. dist. offr., E. Africa Prot., 4th Oct., 1895; Africa gen. serv. medal 1895-6; Africa gen. serv. med. Jubaland, 1898.

MCCLURE, HERBERT REGINALD.—Ed. at Eastman's Royal Naval Acad. and H.M.S. "Britannia"; China medal 1900, relief of Pekin clasp; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 5th Apr., 1901; McCLOINTOCK, SAMUEL ALEXANDER, M.D., Ch.B., EDIN.—B. 1877; certif. trop. disease Edin.; house surg., gen. hosp., Sepoy lines, Aug., 1905; res. med. offr., St. John's Island quarantine station, and asst. port health offr., S. Sttlnts., 6th Dec., 1905; dep. port health offr., Sing., 20th Jan., 1906; res. med. offr., dist. hosp., Penang, 21st June, 1906; dep. health offr., Penang, 4th Aug., 1906; state surg. and head offr., Puket, West Siam, 1st Mar., 1907.

MACCONNAL, WM. GRAECE.—Apptd. asst. acctnt., refugee camps, O.R.C., 24th April, 1902; auditor, refugee camps' accts., attached to ad. dept., 30th Sept., 1902; 2nd examir. of accts. ad. dept., 1st Dec., 1902; clk., treasv., 1st Mar., 1903; 1st clk., ditto, 4th Apr., 1906; asst. treasv., 1st July, 1907.

MCCORD, NEVILLE WM.—5th excise offr., B. Hond., Oct., 1893; 4th ditto, Nov., 1895; 3rd cust. offr., Nov., 1900; 1st excise offr., Jan., 1902; 1st grade cust. and ex. offr., Aug., 1904; ag. keeper, King's warehouses, 1st May, 1905.

MCCOWEN, JOHN R.—I.S.O. (1906), A.D.C., J.P.—B. 1844; served 9 years in R. constab.; apptd. to Newfd. constab., 1871; inst. gen., 1895; recd. thanks of govt. on 5 occasi... for spec. serv.; R.H.S. medal and thanks of govt. for saving life, 1878; gov., penitentiary, 1879; mem. of bd. of health, 1890; hon. sec., fire relief comte., 1892, and spec. thanked for serv.; chrmn. fire commsrs., 1893; spec. medal, 1897, and thanked by govt. for exceptional bravery at a fire; A.D.C. to govr., 1901.

McCREA, JOHN HENRY.—Ed. at United Services Coll., Westward Ho!; sub-inspr., Jamaica constab., 1887; 3rd cls. inspr., 1890; 2nd cls., 1899; 1st cls., 1904; compiled "The Jamaica Constabulary Directory, 1897," and "The Sub-Officers' Guide, 1903."

MACDONALD, A. C., M.R.A.C., F.H.A.S., &c.—Agric. ast., Cape Colony, 1889; resigned 1898; capt., Cape D.M.T., 1901-2; dir. of stock Transvaal, 1902-3; ast. dir. of agric., 1903-7; dir. of agric., E. Africa Prot., July, 1907.

MACDONALD, THE HON. ANDREW ARCHIBALD.—Born at Three Rivers, Prince Edward's Is., 1829; a mem. of prov. assm., 1854 to 1860, and of the legis. coun. from 1863, when it became elective, until July, 1873, when he resig. seat in the legis. and exec. couns., and became prov. postmr.-gen., until 1884, when apptd. lieut.-gov. of the prov., which position he occupied until Sept., 1889; was a deleg. to the Charlottetown and Quebec confce. in 1864, which settled the terms of union for the Dominion of Canada; called to the Senate of the Dominion, May, 1891.

MACDONALD, ERNEST ELDRIDGE.—B. 1870; ed. Sydenham Coll., Kent; supernum. clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, 1887; clk. to atty.-gen., Feb., 1888; clk., col. engrnr.'s dept., Sept., 1888; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, June, 1892; ag. dist. commsr. and pres., bd. of health, The Cayo, Apr., 1896; dist. vaccinator and pres., bd. of health, Stann Creek, Mar., 1896; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, Cyprus, Apr., 1897; ag. asst. sec., 1897-8; sec., loan commsrs., 1898; sec., Central Mejia-i-idareh, 1898; ag. asst. sec., 1899 and 1900; ag. commsr., Kyrenia, 1902 and 1907; coroner, 1902; pres., mun. commsrs., Kyrenia, 1902; pres., Kyrenia hosp. comte., 1902; ag. commsr., Famagusta, 1904; pres., mun. coman., Famagusta, 1904; pres.; Famagusta hosp. bd., and ag. asst. sec., 1904.
MACDONALD, HUGH CAMPBELL—Apptd. clk. in acctn.'s off., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., Sept., 1894; asst. collr., 1896; judl. offr., 1898; 1st cls. ast., May, 1902; dist. mag., Mombera, 1904.

MACDONALD, HON. JAMES, K.C.—Called to the bar of Nova Scotia, 1851; created a Q.C., 1867; chief rly. commsr. of N.S., from June, 1863, to Dec., 1864; financial sec. until the union of 1867; one of the commsrs. (representing N.S.) to open trade relations between W. Indies, Mexico, Brazil, and the B.N.A. provs., 1865-66; sworn of the privy coun. of Canada, Oct., 1878, and apptd. min. of just. of Canada; sat in N.S. assem. from 1859 until 1867, and from 1871 until July, 1872, when returned to the house of commons; ch. just. of N.S.; joint commsr. to inquire into charges agst. atty.-gen. of Jamaica, Dec., 1893.

MACDONALD, JOHN ALEX.—Ed. High School, Inverness; law clk., 1896-1900; joined service co. Cameron Hdra., 1900, and served in S. African campaign; clk., G.P.O., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1900; clk. to crown prosecutor, O.R.C., July, 1901; clk., sec. O.R.C. admtn., Oct., 1901; clk. to col. sec., June, 1902; ag. clk. to exec. coun., Dec., 1906, to Feb., 1907.

MACDONALD, RANDAL.—B. 1868; apptd. clk. 1st of T. offices, Glasgow, Dec., 1888; clk. and dep. supt., Jan., 1892; asst. collr. and clk. to armed forces, Br. Cent. Africa Prot., May, 1897; judl. offr., 1899; 2nd cls. ast. and dir. of cust., Apr., 1901; compt. of cust., Oct., 1906; mag.-in-charge of plague operations, 1st Oct. to 23rd Dec., 1905; Cent. Africa medal, 1898; S. Africa medal and two clasps, 1900.

MACDONALD, W.—Ag. 4th indoor offr., treasy., Antigua, Sept. to Oct., 1899, July to Dec., 1900, and Aug. to Sept., 1902; ag. 2nd clk., P.O., Jan. to July, 1900; 2nd clk., P.O., 1st Jan., 1901; ag. 1st clk., P.O., Sept., 1904, to Feb., 1905, and Aug. to Oct., 1905; ag. postmr., Jan. to Feb., 1905; 3rd outdoor offr., treasy., Feb., 1905; graduated Ontario business coll., Belleville, Canada, 1904.

MACDONALD, WALTER HALLI BURTON.—L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Edin.; L.F.P.S., Glas., 1888; prin. med. offr., Imp. Brit. E. Africa Co., 1889; prin. med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 1895; mem. of the Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1898; Africa gen. ser. medal with clasp, Witu, 1890; clasp, Mwele, 1895; E. and Cent. Africa medal with clasp, Uganda, 1897-98; Africa gen. serv. medal with clasp, Uganda, 1900; Imp. Brit. E. Africa service medal, 1902; med. offr. of health, 1903.

MACDONALD, W. M., M.R.C.S. (England), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—Ag. med. offr., Montserrat, Mar., 1897; ag. med. offr., hosp., Antigua, Apr., 1899; med. offr., Montserrat, 1901; med. offr., burgher refugee camp, Transvaal, Feb. to Dec., 1902; med. offr., Antigua, Sept., 1904; med. offr., St. Kitts, Jan., 1905.

MACDONNELL, JOHN.—Clk. in the immgrn. office of N. S. Wales in 1854; promoted in 1856 sen. clk. to the regiar.-gen.; placed in charge of the compilation of the gen. statistics of the col. in 1858; sec. of the pol. dept. of Queensland on its separation from N.S.W., Feb., 1860; imgnrn. agt., Jan., 1866, inspr. of benevolent asylum and orphanages, also visiting justice to H.M.'s gaol and lun. asyl.; as well as ch. inspr. of distilleries; under-secr. and permanent head of the post and telegraph dept. in 1870; major-comdt. of the Queensland volr. forces, 1877; lieut.-comdt. in 1878; ret., Nov., 1879, and placed on the staff with his rank as lieut.-col.; ag. comdt. of the defence forces in Feb., 1883, to 1884; mem. of immgr. bd. since 1865.
MACDOUGALL, JOHN LORNE, C.M.G. (1897).—R., 1838; Auditor-gen. of Canada, Aug., 1878.

MACDOUGALL, JOSEPH EASTON.—Son of Wm. McDougall, C.B.; ed. U.C. Coll.; called to bar, Feb., 1870; lecturer on criminal law and torts to law society of Ontario, 1879-83; Q.C. 1883; jun. judge cty., York, Mar., 1883; sen. judge, Sept., 1885; judge maritime ct., Ontario, Sept., 1885; chmn. of pol. bd. for Toronto, 1886.

MACDOUGALL, KENNETH.—Imp. Brit. E. Africa Co., 1889; collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st July, 1895; sub-commsr., 1901; Imp. Brit. E. Africa Co. medal, Africa gen. serv. medal (Mauiri Rebellion), Africa gen. serv. medal (Jubaland), 2nd cls. Order of Star of Zanzibar; registg. offr. in connection with slave compensation claims, 1st Sept., 1907.

MACDOUGALL, DONALD KEITH, C. M. G. (1901).—Prin. med. offr. of W.A.F.F.; accompanied Ashanti expdn., 1900, in that capacity; prin. civ. med. offr., Straits Settlements, Mar., 1903, and inspr. of hospitals, F.M.S., 1905.

MEWEN, THOS. SMITH.—Assoc. M.I.C.F.; asst. engrnr., Cape govt. rly., 19th Oct., 1877; ag. dist. engrnr., 9th July, 1877; mainten... engnr., 19th Oct., 1878; ag. res. engrnr., Port Elizabeth, 15th May to 29th Sept., 1881; maintenance engrnr., 30th Sept., 1881; dist. engrnr., 1st Dec., 1881; ag. maintenance engrnr., 20th Mar., 1883; dist. engrnr., Uitenhage, 27th Sept., 1883; ditto, Cradock, 1st Feb., 1889; res. engrnr. in various districts, 1891 to 1897; chief res. engrnr. to supervise lines being constructed by Thames Ironworks and Shipbuilding Co., 1st Apr., 1899; asst. engrnr.-in-chief, 15th Mar., 1901; asst. gen. man., 16th May, 1901; gen. man., 12th Aug., 1902.

MACFARLANE, WM. HOLDSWORTH, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1850; house surg., gen. hosp., Hobart, May, 1874, to June, 1877; asst. med. offr., hosp. for insane, New Norfolk, Nov., 1878, to Sept., 1880; supt. and med. offr., ditto, 1st Oct.,

MCGEE, JOHN J.—Ed. at St. Peter's Coll., Wexford, affiliated with the Catholic Univ., Dublin, was medallist in physical science; employed by the govt. of Canada, on special service in dist. of Algoma, and on various surveys; subsequently professionally engaged under the Dominion govt. in the construction of the inter-colonial rly., and in connection with the rly. comsn. of the house of commons; 1st class clk. in charge of surveys branch of Dominion lands, Dept. of the Interior, 1879; clk. of the privy coun., Canada, May, 1882.

MCGOWAN, HON. JAMES.—B. 1841; first apptd. to New Zealand ministry in Jan., 1900, and has remained continuously in off. as min. of mines and just. since that date; also as min. of industries and commerce, and min. for immigr.; is also min. in charge of Cook and other Islands admtn.
MCGOWAN, JAMES.—B.A., Lond., and fellow of the Inst. of Actuaries, Lond.; acted as examr. to that inst., and engaged for many years in life assurance business; govt. actuary, Cape, 1890.

MACREGOR, D. S.—Fellow of soc. of accnts. and auditor; ch. audit clk., Leewards, Apr., 1895; and federal accnt., Jan., 1896; ag. treas., St. of Leewards and Antigua leg. couns. and Antigua Kitts-Nevis, Dec., 1896; ag. aud.-gen. and mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1897, to Jan., 1898; on special mission to investigate and adjust accnts. of Trinidad, Dec., 1899, to May, 1900; auditor, British Honduras, June, 1900; ag. reg.-gen. and reg. of sup. ct. on five occasions in 1904-5; ag. dist. comnr. of Belize, Mar.-Apr., 1903; aud.-gen., Mauritius, 11th Oct., 1905; chmn., widows' and orphans' fund, May, 1906.
MCGREGOR, JAMES COMYN.—2nd lieut. 7th battn. K.R.R., 1881; lieut., 1881; sub-inspr. Basutoland mtd. pol., 1884; asst. comsnr., 1893.

MACREGOR, SIR WILLIAM, G.C.M.G. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1889), C.M.G. (1881), C.B. (1897).—B. 1847; ed. at Aberdeen, Glasgow, Berlin, Paris and Florence; M.B., Abdn., 1872; M.D., 1874; F.F.P.S.G., 1905; LL.D.; D.Sc.; Watson gold medallist, 1872; formerly res. surg. and res. physician, Glasgow Roy. Infirmary; res. physician, Roy. Lun. Asyl., Aberdeen; asst. govt. med. offr., Seychelles, 1873; supt. lun. asyl., and res. surg., Civil Hosp., Port Louis, Mauritius, 1874; ch. med. offr., Fiji, Mar., 1875; also receiver-gen., and mem. of the exec. and legis. couns., Jan., 1877; has acted as registr.-gen., agtgen. of immigr., and comsnr. of lands; engaged, 1876, in the suppression of the disturbances in the mountains of Viti Levu, for which he was voted a gratuity of 200l.; joint comsnr., 1877, for the settlement of debts due from natives and Europeans, and for the settlement of all pecuniary claims against the late govt. of Fiji; mem. of the Native Regulation Board, 1877; proc. Tonga, 1879, to report on the financial exof that country; ag. col. sec., Nov., 1881- 1884, and Oct., 1874, to Jan., 1875; adm. the govt., and ag. high comsnr. and comfor the W. Pacific, Jan. to Aug., 1875; representative of Fiji at the first session of the Federal of Australasia, at Hobart, Jan., 1880; a medal of the 2nd class (1884), with the gold medal of the Roy. Hum. Soc. of Aust. in 1885, for saving life at sea; dep. adm. Fiji, Sept., 1885, and Aug., 1886; declar- Queen's sovereignty over Br. New Guinea, 1888; admtr., Br. New Guinea, 1888; held, 1895; gov. Lagos, 1899; represented the African colonies and protectorates at Cong. 1902; gov. Newfoundland, 23rd July, 1904.

MCGUIRE, WM.—B. 1864; apptd. afft. compet., clk. in the lower div. of the civ. assigned to the audit office, Nov., 1884; tradvaluation office, Ireland, Nov., 1890, and t. June, 1891; temp. staff officer, 1903.
MCINTOSH, ROBERT.—B. 1868; ins. schls., O.R.C., 17th Mar., 1902; ag. ch. Aug., 1905, to Feb., 1906, and from Apr.

MACINTYRE, DUNCAN CHARLES—C. R.N.R. (retired); b. 1864; ed. at City of L. schl. and Felsted gram. schl.; served in Brit. India Co.; lieut., R.N.R.; promoted to comdr., 1904, in spec. service; offr. and comdng. transports under for 12 years; transport work during S. A. war; commd. depot ship at Taku Forts. China expd. force; R.H.S. medal for life; China and S. African medals; sen. R.N.R. in charge of Hove battery, Sussex; spec. survey of Mutla River by E. India; harbour master and marine mag., Petar- Stlhtns., Sept., 1903; is also man. of govman. of gov. bonded warehouse, chmn. of advisory bd., pres. of pilot bd., recvr. of offr. in charge of imports and exports, Peseconded for spec. serv. for report on Pe- Pulo Wai (Dutch) in the Straits govt.
MCINTYRE, JOHN.—Apptd. puisne Tasmania, 1809.
MCINTYRE, PETER ADOLPHUS, M.D.— gov., Prince Edward Island, Canada, 1899.

McINNIS, LIRUT.-COL. EDWARD BOWARD (9th Lancers), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1847; entd. 1865; adjt. 9th Lancers for several years in campaigns of 1878-9-80; Khyber Pass, un- Gen. Sir F. Maude, K.C.B., 1878; actions Khazi Killa, Dec., 1879; Siah Sung, Dec. others at Kabul; siege of Sherpur under Sir F. Roberts, K.C.B.; with the 9th Lanc. march from Kabul to Kandahar; at Mazra (Kandahar), Sept., 1880; twice mendespatches; bronze star, med. and two inspr.-gen., G. Const. constabty., Mar. mentd. in despatches by Sir R. R. W. K.C.M.G.; comdt. local forces, Trinidad, 1890; mem. exec. coun., Sept., 1890; insp. of Br. Guiana pol., May, 1891; comdt. Br. militia, Nov., 1892, with rank of col.; on duty to Ururan on Venezuelan frontier, Jan. commanded cavalry of the colonial conti- H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee, June, 1897; ret.
MACKAY, GEORGE, M.A. (Hons.), 1892. —B. 1880; asst. master, Victoria Seychelles, 23rd Aug., 1903; ag. headditto, and ag. inspr. of schls., Nov., 1903; master, Victoria schl., and inspr. of schl.

McKEAN, Lieut.-Col. Alexander Chalmers, C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1852; cornet, Inniskilling Dragoons, Oct., 1871; A.D.C., Bengal, 1880; served in Transvaal war, 1881; staff off., Zululand war (Eschwe column), 1883; D.A.A. and Q.M.G., S. Africa, 1884; ag. D.A.A.G., Natal, 1886; survr. to Zululand and New Republic boundary comsn., 1886; asst. comsnr., Zululand, 1887; commr. of Basuto levies in Zulu disturbances, 1888; was honourably mentioned and made brevet lieut.-col. for services; asst. mil. sec. to gov., Malta, 1889.
MACKENZIE, Donald.—Ch. inspr. of pol., Penang, Dec., 1887; ag. supt. pol., Sungei Ujong, Apr., 1890.

MACKENZIE RIVER, Bishop of, The Right Rev. Wm. Day Reeve.—C.M., Coll., Isl., 1865; consec. 1891; hon. D.D., St. John's Coll., Manitoba, 1891; missionry. (C.M.S.) at Fort Simpson, Mackenzie River, &c.; chapl. to Bishop of Athabasca, 1874-84; archd. Chipewyan, 1883-1901; assumed charge of diocese of Athabasca, 1904; asst. Bishop of Toronto, 1907.

MACKEY, Hon. John Emanuel, M.A., LL.B.—M.L.A. for Gippsland W., Victoria, since 1902; barrister-at-law, lecturer in equity in Univ. of Melbourne, late ag. prof. of logic and philosophy; min. without portfolio, Victoria, 1904; is now commr. of crown lands and survey, and pres. of bd. of lands and wks.
McKINLEY, John.—Served with I.Y. in S. African campaign, 1900-1; prin. of govt. schl., prisoners of war camp, Simonstown, Jan., 1902; acctnt., educn. dept., O.R.C., 1st July, 1902.
MACKINNON, Donald Alexander.—B.1863; Mem. of legislature, P.E. Island, 1893-99; atty.-gen., 1899; mem. House of Commons, Canada, 1900-1904; lieut.-gov. of Prince Edward Isl., 3rd Oct., 1904.

McKINSTRY, W. L.—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; ed. Harr. Coll., Barbados; passed civ. ser. test exam., 1882; supernum. col. sec.'s office, 1883; ag. clk. audit office, exec. comtee.'s office, col. sec.'s office, and as sec. to poor law bd., 1886 and 1888; clk. in petty debt ct., Jan., 1889; clk. to inspr.-gen. of pol., June, 1889; ag. inspr. of pol. and asst. of fire brig., 1889-91; int. rev. offr., 1892; transf. to customs, 1896; ag. gov., Glendairy prison, May to Nov., 1904; ag. pol. mag., 1906.
MACLEAN, Adrian John.—Ed. at Selwyn Coll., Camb., B.A., 1895; M.A., 1901; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 14th May, 1901; collr., 1st Apr., 1905.

MCLEAN, The Hon. Allan.—M.L.A., Victoria; pres. of bd. of land and wks. and commr. of crown lands and survey, Nov., 1890, to Feb., 1892; min. of agric., Nov., 1890, to Apr., 1891; ch. sec., Apr., 1891, to Feb., 1892; ch. sec., pres. bd. of land and wks., commr. of lands and survey, Feb., 1892, to Jan., 1893; premier and ch. sec., Dec., 1899, to Nov., 1900; min. for trade and customs, commonwealth parl., Oct., 1904.
MCLELLAN, David.—Mem. of house of assem. and exec. coun., New Brunswick; prov. sec. and rec-gen.; pres. of exec. coun., 1890.

MCLEOD, Hon. Donald.—M.L.A. for Daylesford, Victoria, since 1900; min. without portfolio, 1902-4; min. of mines and water supply, Feb., 1904; min. of mines and forests, Nov., 1904; ag. treas., June to Sept., 1907; mem. of coun. of Melbourne Univ.
MACLEOD, Neil.—Called to the bar, P.E. Isl., 1872; mem. of house of assem. since 1879; prov. sec. and treas., 1879 to 1882; atty.-gen. and premier, 1889.
MACLEOD, Norman Magnus, C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1839; late capt. 74th Highlanders; was commndt. of Swazi contingent against Sikukuni, 1879.

MACLURE, William Grant.—Admtd. Bahamas Bar, 1889; asst. clk., House of Assem., 1890; J.P. for col. and prov. res. just., Gov.'s Harbour, 1894; acted as stip. and circuit mag. and coroner, Is. of New Providence; registr., sup. ct., Bahamas, 1898.

McMILLAN, Barry Richard.—Served in census off., Cape Colony, 1875-6; commr.'s off., May, 1876; 2nd cls. clk., July, 1882; 1st cls. clk., Apr., 1892; in dept. of lands, mines and agric., Sept., 1892; in dept. of agric., Sept., 1898; prin. clk., July, 1899; ag. chief clk., Aug., 1905; also regsr. of brands, July, 1904.

McMILLAN, Sir Daniel Hunter, K.C.M.G. (1902).—B. 1846; ed. Canada; served with Canadian Volunteers on Niagara frontier, 1864; during Fenian raid, 1866; Red River expedit., 1870; N.W. rebellion (medal), 1885; mem. for Winnipeg in Manitoba legislature, 1889; mem. of Manitoba govt., 1889; lt.-gov. of Manitoba, 6th Sept., 1900.

MACMILLAN, Ewen Judson.—B. 1873; ed. Charlottetown Business Coll., Guelph Agric. Coll.; received degree of B.S.A., June, 1900; supt. of govt. experiment farm and lecturer in agric. at Prince of Wales' Coll., Prince Edward Isl., Canada, 1901-4; chief of experimental farms and live stock division, O.R.C., June, 1904; asst. dir. of agric., July, 1905; ag. dir. of agric., from 17th Nov., 1906.

McMILLAN, Robert Furse.—Queen's scholar, Westminster school; scholar, Trin. Hall, Camb.; senior in law tripos, 1879; holder of law studentship at Trin. Hall; Inns of Court studentship and Inner Temple common law scholarship; called to the bar, 1881; joined western circuit and Devon and Exeter sessions; puisne judge, W. Australia, 1903.
MCNAB, Hon. Robert.—Min. of lands and min. for agric., New Zealand.

MCNAIR, J. F. A., C.M.G. (1879).—B. 1828; major, Roy. (late Madras) Artill. (retd.); ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; student at the Schol. of Mines; entered the Madras Artill. in 1845; served in India, the S. Stiltnsts., and Labuan; priv. sec. to the gov. S. S., 1857; and subsequently exec. engnr. and supt. of convicts, Singapore; ch. engrnr., S. S., on their transfer to the crown in 1867; accompanied the gov. to Siam, Aug., 1867; survr.-gen. S. S., 1872; ch. commr. of Perak throughout the disturbances in that country in 1875-6, and engaged in the affair at Kotah Lamah, on the Perak river; special commr. to Selangor to inquire into piracy, and to Perak in connection with the Pangkor treaty, 1874; again to Siam, in Aug. of that year, on special mission; again, in 1875, with Sir A. Clarke, in connection with unsettled state of affairs there; special mission to the State of Sungei Ujong, 1878; sec. to Sir W. Robinson's mission to Siam in Nov., 1878, to present the G.C.M.G. to the king; author of "Perak and the Malays," &c.; ag. heut.-gov. and res. councillor of Penang from Feb., 1871, to Aug., 1884, when he ret.

MACNAGHTEN, T. C.—B. 1872; ed. Charterhouse and Oxford; scholar of Hertford Coll., 1892; 1st class classical mods., 1894; B.A., 1896; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd class clk., Local Govt. Bd., Feb., 1896; 2nd class clk., C.O., Sept., 1896; priv. sec. to Earl of Selborne, Dec., 1899, to E. of Onslow, Nov., 1900, to Duke of Marlborough, July, 1903; 1st cl. clk., 24th Mar., 1904.

McNEIL, Maj.-Gen. Sir John Carstairs, G.C.B. (1882), K.C.M.G. (1880), C.M.G. (1870), D.C.L. — B. 1831; served during campaign of 1857-58 in India as A.D.C. to Sir E. Lugar during the siege and capture of Lucknow; in 1861-6 A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron in N. Zealand; commanded Tipperary flying column during Fenian disturbances, 1866-67; served on the staff of Red River expdn. under Sir G. Wolseley in 1870, and in the Ashanti war, 1873-74; A.D.C. to H.R.H. the Duke of Cambridge, Jan., 1874, and an equerry to Her Majesty in Aug., following; served in the Egyptian expdn., 1872, personally attached to the Duke of Connaught; served in the Soudan expdn., 1885; commanded troops at the action of Tobrek, for which clasp was granted.

McNULTY, Thos. Sydney.—B. 1870; ed. at Mariot Coll., Sydney; clk., acct., branch, wks. dept., Perth, W. Australia, 1896; ch. corresp. clk., ditto, 1897; mem. mun. coun., Claremont, 1901-3; sec. to goldfields water supply admin., 1903.

McOWAN, Islay.—3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Fiji, Sept., 1892; stip. mag., asst. to native consnr., and tax inspr., Lau, May, 1896; stip. mag., and tax inspr., Navua, and consnr., for Namasi, Feb., 1900; stip. mag., Lomaiviti and Tailevu; consnr., and tax inspr., Lomaiviti; offr. in charge, Levuka gaol, Jan., 1904.

MACPHERSON, A.—Ed. at Keith pub. schls., Scotland; served during S. African war in Lord Dunraven's sharpshooters; civilian warrant offr. and paymaster, Imperial remount farm, 1st to 14th Dec., 1902; ch. clk. and sen. examr. of accts., audit dept., O.R.C., 15th Dec., 1902; store acct., govt. stationery and printing off., O.R.C., July, 1904.

McTURK, Michael, C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1843; revenue offr. and asst. survr., Essequibo, 1872; consnr. of taxation, Essequibo dist., 1873; special mag. and supt. crown lands and forests, Essequibo, 1878; govt. gold mining offr., Br. Guiana, 1887; consnr., Essequibo and Pomeroon districts; sen. consnr., B. Guiana bndry. survey; prot. of aboriginal Indians for the col.; trav. mag., 1905.
MACWILLIAM, Donald Kirton.—2nd clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1894; rev. offr. and quarantine offr., Apr., 1894; rev. offr. and harbr. mast., 1906.
MADDEN, W.—Pres. bd. of land and wks., and consnr., crown lands and survey, Victoria, Aug., 1881, to Mar., 1883.

MADLEY, Lewis George.—B. 1844; col., V.D., ret. list of Commonwealth mil. forces; prin. of training coll., educn. dept., S. Aust., 1876; also D.A.A.C., S. Aust. mil. forces, 1895; consnr. of pol., S. Aust., 1896.
MAGRATH, Thos. Henry.—Jun. elk., P.O., Tasmania, 1853; ch. clk., 1869; comptroller of money orders, 1879, and of savings bank, 1882; sec. to P.O., 1888.

MACRO, Prof. Enrico, M.D.—B. 1854; grad. Malta Univ., 1877; examr. in arts and sciences, 1882; examr. in med. and surgery, 1884; prof. of math. and physics, Oct., 1885; examr. in land surveying and archt., Oct., 1885; ag. princ. and sec. to the univ., 1887; ag. asst. dir. of educn., sec. to the senate, 1888-97; ag. inspr. in charge of elemen. schls., Jan., 1898; sec. to local exams. in Malta, 1898; dir. of schls., 1899; mem. exec. coun. and of educn. govt., 1902; dir. of pub. instr. and rectors' Univ., 1904.

MAHAFFY, Arthur Wm.—B. 1889; Marlborough, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1889; B.A., Dublin Univ. (Trinity Coll.), lieutenant, 1st Batt. Royal Munster Fusiliers, 1895; govt. agt. Gilbert Is., Prot., Oct., 1890; res. consmr., Solomon Is., 1897; res. m.d. division, Solomon Is., and dep. consmr., Pacific, 1899; col. sec. and recvr.-gen., Feb., 1904.

MAITLAND, Andrew Gibb.—Rec. educn. as a geologist and civil engnr. at York Coll. of Science, Leeds; graduated, 1881; govt. geologist, Queensland, 1888; his office taking him as far afield as Br. N. Guinea, geol. of W. Australia, 1896; has written works on the geology, features, and resources of Queensland and W. Australia.

MAJOR, Charles.—Called to the Bar Temp., June, 1887; mem. of Leeward, Sept., 1887; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, 1889; nominated mem. of legis. com., 1895 to 1899; nominated mem. of gen. coun., Leewards, Nov., 1896, and pres. of same, 1896 to 1899; mem. fed. ex. couns., Leewards, June, 1897, to June, 1901; vice-pres. legis. Antigua, May, 1897; ag. sol. gen. and atty., Leewards, 1897, 1898, 1899; capt. conf. Antigua united defence force on its estab., 1897; J.P., 1897; ag. 2nd puis. atty., Leewards, June, 1899; atty.-gen. of the colony, 1901; ch. just. of Fiji, and chief judiciaire of Western Pacific, 1902; adm. std. govt., Oct., 1904.

MAJOR, Francis Wm.—B. 1863; sec. treasy., Trinidad, Oct., 1885; 2nd clk. of prisons, Jan., 1886; 9th clk. audir. offr., 1887; 8th clk. ditto, 1888; sec. to road and lands couns., agri. contracts consnr., and comm., Trinidad, between 1887 and 1890; on various occasions as 3rd clk., surg.-gen., 1887 to 1890; 3rd clk. of cust., priv. sec., W. Robinson, 1888; supervisor of cust., 1891; asst. compt. of cust., 1891; asst. audir., Aug., 1891; ag. compt. of cust., on occasions, 1891 to 1895; compt. of cust., 1895; sent by govt. to W. bndry. to inquire into and report on necessity for a prev. ser., 1896; sent by govt. to Volta organise prev. ser., 1897; recvr.-gen., 1st June, 1899; prepared scheme (which was by S. of S.) for improvement of finances of Bermuda.

MALCOLM, Dougal Orme.—B. 1877; Eton and New Coll., Oxford; 1st cl. med., 1st cl. lit. hum., 1890; B.A., 1899; F. All Souls, 1899; M.A., 1902; appd. compet. exam., 2nd cl. elk., C.O. Oct., priv. sec. to Earl of Selborne, high comm., S. Africa, Mar., 1905 to 1907.

MALCOLM, Harcourt Gladstone.—Appd. to Sir Henry Jackson when adminstr. of E. 1892, 1893, 1895; ditto to Sir W. F. Smith, gov. of Bahamas, 1895; called to Lincoln's Inn, June, 1899; ditto, Bahamas, 1899; mem. of house of ass., Bahamas, deputy-speaker, 1901; re-elected, 1904; gen., Aug. to Nov., 1905, and from Sep. to Feb., 1907; thanked by S. of S. for attv.-gen. in drafting bills; thanked for obtaining copies of early records.

MALCOLM, SIR ORMOND DRIMMIE, K.T., B.A.C.H. (1898).—B. 1839; called to the bar of the Bahamas, July, 1861; ag. pol. mag. in 1864 and 1866; elected mem. of house of assem. in 1865; speaker, 1868; ag. judge, court of common pleas, 1875; Q.C. in 1876; ag. atty.gen., Mar., 1882; ag. adminstr., June, 1895; ch. just. of Bahama Is., 1897.

MAILING, CAPT. IRWIN CHAS., C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1841; ed. at Wimbledon schl. and Roy. Mil. Acad.; late 23rd Roy. Welsh Fus.; dep. mag. and collr., Nuddea dist., Bengal, 1857; served throughout the Indian mutiny, 1857-59; present in many engagements (medal); ensign, 1861, 89th Princess Victoria's Regt.; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov., W. Coast of Africa, 1868; lieut. 35th Roy. Sussex Regt., 1870; priv. sec. to the Marquis of Normanby, gov. of Queensland, 1871, and New Zealand, 1874; col. sec. and registr.-gen. of Grenada, 1879; adminstr. the gov't. in 1880, 1882, 1884-7; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is., 1888-9; and represtive at quarantine confce. at Demerara, 1888; ag. adminstr., 1889; adminstr. of St. Vincent, 1889; ret. 1893.

MALONE, CLEMENT.—Ag. jnr. audit clk., Antigua, July and Aug., 1901, and Mar. to Nov., 1902; ag. 2nd clk., registr.'s office, Aug. to Oct., 1902; ag. cashier, St. John's savings bank, Jan. to June, 1903; ag. acctnt., treas'y., Dec., 1902, and July to Dec., 1903; acctnt. of treas'y., Antigua, Dec., 1903; clk. to income and trade tax assessors, Jan., 1905.

MAISCH, CHARLES CHRISTIAN, A.M.I.C.E.—Draughtsman, works dept., admiralty, 1866; ass't engrnr., German admiralty wks., Kiel, 1873; survr. state engrnr. dept., California; surveying Sacramento and Fraser Rivers, etc., California and Br. Columbia, 1875; exploring Formosa for Chinese govt., 1881 to 1883; ass't engrnr., P.W. dept., Hong Kong, 1884; supt. of crown lands, 1892; ag. assessor of rates, 1894; J.P., 1894.

MANN, MAJ.-GEN. J. R., R.E., C.M.G. (1881).—B. 1823; entered R.E., 1840; survr.-gen. of Mauritius, 1856 to 1861; dir. of roads and survr.-gen. of Jamaica, 1867; resig. 1873, and re-apptd. in Feb., 1874; official, M.L.C., 1874; ret., 1886.
MANNING, ALBERT EDWARD.—B. 1865; Copyist in registrar's office, Br. Guiana, 1882; asst. sworn clk., 1887; sworn clk. and notary public, 1898; comntr. to administer oaths to affidavits, 1900.

MANNING, BRIG.-GEN. SIR WM. HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1904); C.B. (1903).—B. 1863; ed. at Camb. and Roy. Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; lieut.; 2nd batt., S. Wales Bord., 24th Foot, 1886; capt., 1897; brevet-major, 1898; brevet-lieut.-col., 1899; brevet-col., 1904; joined Indian army, 1888; 51st Sikhs, frontier force, 1891; 2nd Burmese war (wounded, medal and clasps); Samana and Hazara expds., N.W. frontier of India, 1891 (clasp); C. Africa, 1893-4 (medal); C. Africa and Rhodesia, 1894-98 (clasp); Somaliland campaign (medal and 2 clasps); dep. comntr. and cons.-gen. for B.C. Africa, 1897; acted as comntr. and cons.-gen., Dec., 1897, to Dec., 1898, and also from July, 1900, to Apr., 1901; raised, and comndd. C. Africa regt.; comndd. Somaliland field force, 1902-1903; comndd. 1st brig. Somaliland field force, 1903-4; inspr.-gen., K.A.R., 1901; ag. gov. of Nyasaland Prot., 1907.

MANSEL, GEORGE, C.M.G. (1891).—Served in Weenen Yeomanry through the Langalibalele rebellion in 1873; served as sub-inspr. and inspr. in Natal mounted pol. through Zulu war, 1879 (medal with clasp), and Boer war, 1880 and 1881; raised and commanded the Reserve Territory Carabiners from 1883 to 1887, when the force was changed into the Zululand pol.; served in this force through all disturbances in Zululand from 1883 to 1888; twice mentioned in despatches as having shown conspicuous coolness and gallantry in action; asst. comntr., Natal pol., 1897; ag. commdt., 1902; ch. comntr., pol., Jan., 1903; ret., 1906.

MANSERGH, CORNEWALL LEWIS WARWICKSHIRE, I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1863; clk., crown lands office, Cape Town, May, 1880; 2nd cl. clk. and priv. sec. to comntr. of crown lands and pub. wks., May, 1884; 1st cl. clk., Apr., 1892; ch. clk. to the ast. comntr. of rly. and pub. wks., Oct., 1892; sec. pub. wks., July, 1898.
MANSERGH, JOHN LOFTUS OTWAY.—Ed. at Haileybury Coll.; S. African war, 1899-1902; Queen's and King's medals; 3rd King's African rilles, E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1902; asst. collr., 8th June, 1906.

MANSON, SIR PATRICK, K.C.M.G. (1903); C.M.G. (1900); F.R.S., M.D., F.R.C.P., LL.D.—B. 1844; late dean and lect. on med. coll. of med. for Chinese, Hong Kong; phys. Seamen's Hospital Soc. at Albert Docks branch; lect. on trop. diseases, Charing Cross Hosp., and senior lect., London schl. of trop. med.; apptd. med. adviser to C.O., 1897.

MARKS, OLIVER.—B. 1866; Supt. govt. plants, Perak, Mar., 1891; 2nd cls. mag., Apr., 1894; ag. ast. mag., Kinta, July, 1894; ast. mag. Kinta, July, 1896; ag. ast. sec. to govt., Perak, Sept., 1896; ast. sec. to govt., Perak, May, 1897; passed Malay, Mar., 1898; 1st cls. mag., June, 1898; ast. sec. to res. gen., Fed. Malay States, Nov., 1898; ag. sec. to res. gen., F. M. S., Mar., 1899; sec. to high comntr., F. M. S., Aug., 1903; priv. sec. to gov. S. Sttlmts. in addition, Apr., 1904.

MARLBOROUGH, 9TH DUKE OF (created, 1702), CHARLES RICHARD JOHN SPENCER-CHURCHILL, P.C., K.G.—B. 1871; Chancellor of Primrose League, 1897 and 1898; served with yeomanry cavalry, S. Africa, 1900, and A.D.C. to Lieut.-Gen. Sir Ian Hamilton, K.C.B.; pavm-master-general, 1899; under-secr. of state for the colonies, July, 1903, to Dec., 1905.

MARRIOTT, H., B.A., 1st cls. math. trip., Cantab.—Cadet S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1896; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1897; passed fin. exam. in Malay, May, 1898; dist. offr., Malacca, Feb., 1899; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Feb., 1902; ast. P.M.G., Penang, Sept., 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, July, 1904; ag. off. assignee, Sing., Feb., 1905; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Sing., Mar. 1906.
MARSDEN, ARTHUR.—Ed. at Bowden Coll.; ch. of cust., E. Africa Prot., 6th Aug., 1895; Africa gen. serv. medal, 1895-96.

MARSH, EDWARD HOWARD.—B. 1872; ed. at Westminster and Cambridge; scholar, Trim. Coll., 1891; 1st cl. classical trip., 1893; sen. chancellor's medal for classics, 1895; 1st cl. with distinction classical trip., Part II., and B.A., 1895; M.A., 1897; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Sept. 29, 1896; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, Julv., 1900; to Mr. Lyttelton, Oct., 1903; 1st cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1905; priv. sec. to Mr. Winston Churchill, Jan., 1907; accompanied Mr. Churchill on his visit to E. Africa and Uganda, 1907-8.
MARSH, T. R., C.M.G. (1901), on retirement from post as head of the contract branch, crow agents' office; B. 1847.

MARSHALL, Colonel Robert Seton.—B. 1864; lieut. 3rd brig. Scottish div. R.A., 1882; (lieut. reserve of offrs., 1885); capt., 1885; instructor of artillery, 1887; (capt., res. of offrs. 1896); major Edin. artill. militia, 1896 (hon. major in the army, 1900); hon. lt.-col., 1903; lt.-col., 1903; retired with hon. rank of col., 1903; served with the 1st brig. W. div. R.A., 1885; staff offr. local forces, Trinidad, Sept., 1888, to Dec., 1891; sec. local defence comittee, 1888; command of Trinidad artill. volrs. for purpose of organising the corps, 1888; A.D.C. to offr. adminstrg. govt. of Trin. and Tobago, May to Dec., 1889; ag. commdt. Trinidad local forces, 6th-19th Nov., 1890; adjt. and musketry instr., Br. Guiana pol., Dec., 1891; ag. adjt. Br. G. militia, May to Oct., 1894; and Aug. to Sept., 1897; ag. inspr. of Br. G. pol. during period of special employment at Uruan frontier station, Oct., 1895, to Mar., 1896; adjt. of Br. G. militia and police, Dec., 1897; ag. commdt., Br. G. militia, 9th May to 22nd Aug., 1900; sent to frontier to hand over Amacura and Barima Sands to Venezuela, Oct., 1900; sent to Trinidad for special duty with police, after the riot, 2nd to 27th May, 1903; adjt., B.C. volrs., 26th Feb., 1906; ag. commdt., ditto, in 1906 and 1907; also dep. inspr. of pris., 10th July to 22nd Nov., 1906; and ag. H.M. inspr. of pris. from 6th July, 1907.

MARTIN, Charles MacDermot.—B. 1865; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., London; B.A., Lond. Univ. (honours English language), 1893; inspr. of schls., Jamaica, Apr., 1895; ag. examr., educn. dept., Jamaica, July-Oct., 1898; inspr. of schls., Leeward Is., Apr., 1900.

MARTIN, George Gozzard.—Ck., audit off., South Australia, Mar., 1873; ck., marine bd., Jan., 1874; ck. ch. sec's off., Sept., 1874; ck., land titles off., Aug., 1877; ck., law officers' dept., July, 1880; ch. ck. and acctnt., law officers dept., Nov., 1888; sec. to atty.-gen. and min. of educn., Jan., 1905; sec. to atty.-gen., July, 1905.

MARTIN, H. A.—Ag. ck. to auditor, Grenada, Aug., 1884, to June, 1885; ck. to promarshal, June to July, 1885; ag. ch. to audr., May to Nov., 1887; asst. ck., treasy., Jan., 1889; ag. wks. acctnt., Jan. to Oct., 1890; ch. ck., regisr., Nov., 1891; treasy. acctnt., St. Lucia and Grenada, Apr., 1894; supt. prisons, Feb. to May, 1897; 2nd financial asst., P.W.D., Ceylon, June, 1897; acctnt. and fin. asst., gen. treasy., May, 1905.

MARTIN, James.—Royal Geographical Society's expeds., 1882-1884; Zanzibar govt. serv., 1884-1885; joined Imperial Brit. E. Africa Co., 1885; joined Zanzibar govt. serv., 1892; transport offr. and collr., Uganda Prot., 1894; collr., Ravine, 1894; collr., Entebbe, 1899.
MARTIN, James H.—B. 1848; Apptd., after exam., messenger Admity., Aug., 1876; transf'd. C.O., Sept., 1877, Queen's mess., June, 1897.
MARTIN, Joseph.—Mem. of legis. assem., and exec. coun., Manitoba; atty.-gen. and comsrr. of rlyvs. and lands.
MARTIN, S. B.—Ed. Wesleyan Mission Schl., C. Coast; 6th class offr., customs, G. Coast, Sept., 1890; ck. to dir. of telegraphs and storekeeper, Aug., 1891; ck. and acctnt., July, 1893.

MARTINS, Frederick Germano.—B. 1866; ed. C.M.S. Coll. Inst., Lagos; jun. bailiff, sup. ct., June, 1884; ck. to Queen's advoc., Aug., 1886; storekeeper, P.W. dept., Jan., 1891; audit ck., Jan., 1896; ch. ck., audit off., Jan., 1908.

MARTINS, G. N.—Ed. C.M.S. Grammar Schl., Lagos; messenger, audit, and P.O., 1889; customs, outdoor offr., 1890 to 1893 (during which time attached to revenue office); ag. ck., P.O., 1893; 4th ck. of customs, 1893; 3rd ck. and statistician, Mar., 1894; transf'd. to the revenue dept., 1900; deputy for cashier and ch. ck. on various occasions; 2nd ck. ck., customs, 1901; chief examining offr. of cust., Lagos, 1st Mar., 1905.
MARWICK, Allan Graham.—Servd. in Natal civ. serv., 1895 to 1899; sub. native comsrr., Mbabane, Swaziland, Jan., 1903, to Mar., 1907; asst. comsrr., ditto, 22nd Mar., 1907.

MARWOOD, Horace Richard, M.I.C.E.—B. 1857; junr. asst. engnr., Taff Vale rlyw., 1877; supernumerary 2nd ck. asst. engnr. Cape Govt. rlyws. (Eastern system), 1882; 1st ck. asst. W. system, 1883, also in charge of district, 1884; asst. engnr. rlyw. service of H.H. the Sultan of Selangor, S.S. 1885; chef-de-division, Pyras and Larissa rlyw., Greece, 1890; engnr. of wks. and maintenance, Trinidad govt. rlyws., 1894; and as gen. man. on eight occasions between 1890 and 1906; gen. man. and ch. engnr., 1906.
MASKELL, Thos. Augustus Cornelius.—Cler. asst. to inspr. of schls., B. Guiana, June, 1878; ck., Apr., 1891; 3rd cls. ck., Apr., 1893; sec. to educn. comsrr., 1902.
MASON, Arthur Wier, B.A.—Puisne judge, sup. of Natal, 1896; ag. ch. just., 1900-01; comsrr. of spec. treason et al., 1900-01; pres. of ditto, 1901-02; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1902.

MASON, Clayton T., M.I.C.E., J.P.—Ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham; from 1874 to 1877 was engaged in the rly. branch of the dept. of pub. wks., N. S. Wales; res. engnr. of the Geraldton and Northampton rly., W. Australia, 1877; suptd. the construction of Point Moore Lighthouse and other pub. wks. in the Victoria dist.; afterwards res. engnr. for four years of the E. rly. in the same col.; ag. comsrr. of rlyvs. on two occasions during 1882 and 1883; was provisionally apptd. in 1884 dir. of pub. wks. and comsrr. of rlyvs.; mem. of the exec. and legsn. couns., gen. man. and maintenance engnr., June, 1885; comsrr. of rlyvs. and mem. of exec. coun., Jan., 1890; after the introduction of responsible govt. was apptd. collr. of customs, June, 1891.

MASON, Gerald Boyell.—Ed. Harrow Coll., Barbados; matric., London Univ., 1880; joined London hosp., 1890; passed prelim. scientific, 1892; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; jun. asst. med. offr., St. Marylebone infirmary, 1896; clin. asst., aurul and ophthalmic depts., Lond. hosp., 1897; house physician, Brompton hosp., 1898; ag. med. offr., Three Counties asylum, 1899; civ. surg. in charge, Station hosp., Woking, 1902; apptd. med. offr., No. 4 dist., Antigua, 1903; ag. comsrr., Virgin Is., 1904; surg.-lieut., Antigua def. force, 1904; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla, and dep. judge of sup. ct., Leeward Is., Jan., 1905, to Sept., 1906; med. offr. No. 6 dist., Nevis, 1906; med. offr., dist. "F," and mag., dist. "F," Dominica, 1907.

MASSON, John Leslie.—Acting ck., survr. gen.'s off., Natal, 15th Dec., 1881; 1st cls. ck. and chief ck., survr. gen.'s off., 1st Jan., 1889; asst. survr. gen., 1st July, 1890; survr. gen., 16th Jan., 1894; was chmn. of the civ. surv. bd. of exams.

MATHEWS, Charles Liandaff.—Cadet, G.P.O., S. Australia, Aug., 1873; cadet, off. of comsrr. of pub. wks., Apr., 1875; ck., educn. dept., Sept., 1877; ck., atty.-gen.'s off., Aug., 1881; ck., local cts., Strathalbyn and Wellington, Aug., 1883; ck., local ct., local ct. of insolvency, and licensing bench, Mount Gambier; also dist. regisr. of births, deaths and marriages, Feb., 1898; also clk. representing offr., destitute bd., Aug., 1896; returning offr. for the state, Feb., 1907.

MATHEWS, HENRY ADOLPHUS.—Passed through govt. training instr., B. Guiana; 1st cls. certificated schlstr.; head-master, grant-in-aid schl., B. Guiana, 94 yrs.; educnt. dist. offr., 15th Nov., 1893; acted as 2nd ast. inspr. of schools on various occasions, 1895-1903.

MATHEWS, THOMAS VINCENT, I.S.O.(1903).—Outdoor offr., receiver-gen. and treas.'s dept., Bahamas, Oct., 1874; 3rd clk., Mar., 1875; ag. priv. sec. to admistr., E. B. A. Taylor, from Sept. to Dec., 1879; ag. collr. of customs, 1883; 2nd clerk and book-keeper, 1885; ag. rec-gen. on several occasions, 1898-1904.
MATHIEU, M.—Puisme judge, sup. ct., Quebec, 1881.

MATTEI, CHARLES, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.M., L.F.P.S.G., D.P.H.—Capt., Australian army medical corps, reserve of offrs.; med. offr., N. S. Wales, 1891; ditto, W. Australia, 1897; govt. med. offr., quarantine med. offr., res. mag., chirnn. of quarter sess., chirnn. of local ct., W. Australia, 1899; med. offr., Imperial Tasmanian cont.; S. African war, 1901 (medal and Cape Col. clasp); sec. for permits, East London, S. Africa, 1902; med. offr. in charge of new constructions, C.S.A.R., 1903; med. offr. of health, Malta, 1907.
MATTHEWS, JOHN BROMHEAD.—Called to the bar, 1890; ag. solr.-gen., S. Settlements, Oct., 1902, to May, 1903; M.L.C., S. Settlements, 1901 and 1904; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1907.

MATTHEWS, MAYNARD REGINALD NELSON.—B. 1865; J.P. for Natal; jun. clk. telegraph office, Durban, Natal, 1878; and postmr., Botha's Hill, Verulam, and Stanger, 1879; 3rd cl. clk., R.M.'s office, Durban, 1880; field cornet, Durban, and sec. wreck comsrr. s't., 1886; 2ndclass clk., 1886; ag. clk., mag.'s ct. and registr. circuit ct., Durban and Umzini, 1886-7; clk., mag.'s ct. registr., circuit ct., sub-distributor of stamps, and dep. clk. of peace, Ladysmith, Dec., 1887; J.P., Klip River div., 1884; has frequently acted as R.M., Klip River, 1889-95; ag. mag., Verulam, Feb., 1895; J.P. for col., 1895; ast. mag., Durban, Nov., 1896; mag., Weenen, June, 1895, to May, 1897; mag. and marriage offr. for the div. of Newcastle, 23rd Sept., 1901; mag., Dundee div., 9th Dec. 1901; received thanks of Sir Redvers Buller for services as an intelligence offr., during S. African war, 1899-1902; chairman Dundee cott. hosp. bd., 12th Jan., 1906; acted as chief leader, Dundee militia reserves, and apptd. comdt. of Dundee, under martial law, 4th May, 1906; ag. ch. mag., Durban, 31st May to 11th July, 1907.
MATTHEWS, WM. HENRY.—Inspector of mines, S. Aust., 1899; warden of mines, 1900.
MAUGER, HON. SAMUEL.—B. 1857; M.L.A. of Victoria, 1898-1901; elected to 1st house of rep., Commonwealth of Aust., 1901; re-elected, 1903; min. without portfolio, 13th Oct., 1906.

MAURITIUS, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. FRANCIS AMBROSE GREGORY.—B. 1848; ed. Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mod., 1869; B.A. (3rd cl. lit. hum.), 1871; M.A., 1883; D.D. (Hon. Causa), 1904; deacon, 1873, Win.; priest, 1874, Win.; formerly curate of Cheam, 1873-4; warden of St. Paul's (S.P.G.) Coll. and S.P.G. mission at Ambatocharanana, Madagascar, and chap. to Bp. of Madag., 1874-1900; chanc. of cathedral of St. Laur., Antananarivo, 1889-1901; chap. of St. Jo., Mentone, 1901-4; Lord Bishop of Mauritius, 25th July, 1904.

MAVROGORDATO, ALEXANDER STEPHEN.—B. 1859; apptd. to med. dept., Nicosia, Cyprus, June, 1879; clk. to prin. forest offr., June, 1881; acted as forest offr., May, 1883, to Dec., 1884; was engaged on the locust destruction as paymr., 1883 to 1886; and as ast. to comsrr., Famagusta, 1889-92; examr. of accts., audit dept., May, 1886; transf'd, to rec. gen.'s dept., Apr., 1891; ast. in survey and forest depts., Dec., 1892; pres. surv. delimitation comen.; supt. locust destruction operations, 1895-6; ast. in forest and agricultural depts., June, 1895; financial ast., P.W. Dept., October, 1896; finan. ast., recrv.-gen.'s dept., 1898; supt. of Cyprus census, 1901.
MAVROGORDATO, ARTHUR STEPHEN.—B. 1886; ed. at Oakham Schl., Rutland; clk., pol. headquarters, Cyprus, Nov., 1903; inspr. of mil. pol., Cyprus, May, 1905; in charge of pol. detachment, Kyrenia, 1907.

MAVROGORDATO, THEODORE E.—Ed. at Berlin; clk. to comsrr., Famagusta, 1881; clk. and interp. to Nicosia detachment of pol., 1882; ast. inspr., pol., Limasol, 1883; inspr. pol., Kyrenia, Sept., 1884; joined metrpol. pol., London, for six months' course of instrn., 1894; in charge special pol. force, 1895; local comdt. of pol., ast. comsrr., gov. of prison and dep. cornr., 1895; ag. comsrr., Papho, May to Nov., 1897; ag. comsrr., Limasol, May to Dec., 1900; ast. comsrr. of pol. and J.P., Johannesburg, Dec., 1901; comsmd. to draw up scheme for dest. of locusts in Transvaal, and had charge of locust dest. dept., in addition to other duties, Oct., 1902, to June, 1904; in charge of crim. invstgn. dept. from June, 1903.

MAXWELL, F. M.—Called to the bar, Line's Inn, 1884; 1st cl. studentship, Inns of Ct., 1882; 1st cl. scholarship (Equity), Line's Inn, 1884; M.A. (Oxon), 1891; 1st cl. jurisprudence, 1884; ag. atty.-gen., Br. Hond., Feb. to Dec., 1890, and May to July, 1896; atty.-gen., Br. Hond., July, 1896; K.C., Br. Hond., Apr., 1905; ag. chief just., Br. Hond., 21st June to 29th Dec., 1899, Mar. to Oct., 1902, Apr. to Nov., 1904, and Apr., 1906, to Jan., 1907; chief just., Br. Hond., Jan., 1907; lieut. commdg. mtd. section of Br. Hond.vol. force, Feb., 1905; attended schl. of musk. inst., at Bulford Camp, and recd. certif. of qualification to command a company of M.I., Sept., 1905; qualified at schl. of musk., Hythe, Dec., 1905; capt., Br. Hond. vol. force, Jan., 1906; major, commdg. ditto, Apr., 1906.

MAXWELL, GERALD VERNER.—B. 1877; ed. Cheltenham and Camb. Univ.; cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1898; ag. reg. genl., ag. reg. of titles and ag. dep. comsrr. of stamps, Dec., 1898, to June, 1899; ag. stip. mag., Macuata, Dec., 1900; ag. comsrr., Naitasiri, and stip. mag., Rewa, July, 1901; ag. ch. clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1902; ag. P. L., Nadroga, Feb., 1903; ast. nat. comsrr., Jan., 1904; nat. lands comsrr., 1906; stip. mag., etc., Lautoka, 1906; ag. comsrr., Lomaiviti, 1907.
MAXWELL, JOSEPH P., M.I.C.E.—Pub. wks. dept., N. Zealand, 1874; gen. man., N. Z. rly., 1889; rly. comsrr. under Rly. Act, 1887, Jan., 1889.

MAXWELL, THOMAS DOVETON.—B. 1873; ed. Tiverton, Blundell scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1892; B.A. (honours, jurisprudence), 1896; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1903; ast. dist. comsrr., So. Nigeria, 1902; ag. dist. comsrr., Degema, 1903; ditto, Calabar, Mar., 1904; polit. transport offr., Ibibio patrol, Nov.-Dec., 1904 (African medal); ag. ast. sec., Calabar, 1904. 1906; ag. pol. mag., Calabar, Apr., 1906; comsnr. of lands, Lagos, Aug., 1906.

MAXWELL, WM. GEORGE.—Barrister-at-law, Inner Temp.; junior offr., Perak, F.M.S., 15th Mar., 1891; asst. mag., Kinta, 25th Apr., 1892; ag. collr. and mag., Kuantan, Pahang, 16th Aug., 1895; ag. supt. of Ulu Pahang, 9th Aug., 1896; regis. of cts. Kinta, Perak, 1st Feb., 1899; ag. asst. sec. to govt., Perak, 6th Feb., 1900; ag. collr. of land rev., Larut, regis. of titles, Perak, North, and warden of mines, Perak, North, 8th Mar., 1901; ag. sen. mag., Selangor and Negeri Sembilan, 1st Jan., 1903; ag. sen. mag., Perak, 1st Aug., 1903; ag. sec. to res., Perak, 6th Apr., 1904; ag. comsnr. of the ct. of requesta, Singapore, 3rd Nov., 1904; dist. offr., Dindings, 3rd Aug., 1905; dep. pub. prosecutor, Singapore, 1906; sol.-gen., S. Settmts., 22nd Aug., 1906; compiled "Perak Land Laws (past and present)," and "The Laws of Perak, 1877-1903."
MAY, A. W. J.—B. 1862; entered col. office after exam., May, 1882; asst. clk., May, 1892; asst. to supervisor of copying, Mar., 1900.

MAY, BARRY.—Ck. to res. mag., Vryburg, Brit. Bech., 1st June, 1888; ck. to res. mag., Kuruman, 1889; ck. to civ. comsnr., Vryburg, 1890; acctnt. to recvr.-gen., 1894; acctnt. to res. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1895; and in addition, govt. sec., 1902; is also ch. asst. offr. and mast. of res. comsnr.'s court.

MAY, CECIL.—B. 1868; clerical asst., treasy., B. Guiana; 4th cl. clk., Apr., 1889; 3rd cl. ditto, June, 1892; 2nd cl. ditto, 6th Mar., 1899; ag. 1st cl. clk., cent. bd. of health, Oct.-Dec., 1900; 1st cl. clk., audit dept., 1st Jan., 1901; pay and quartermaster, B.G. pol., 15th Apr., 1901; 2nd lieut., B.G. militia, 29th Dec., 1896; 1st lieut., 18th Nov., 1897; capt., 18th Mar., 1898; ag. adjutant, 8th May to 22nd Aug., 1900; seconded for service with B.G. pol., 7th Aug., 1901; rejoined B.G.M., 24th Feb., 1902; major, 27th Feb., 1902; ag. comdt., 26th Aug. to 25th Oct., 1904.

MAY, CHAS. GALL.—B. 1870; apptd. asst.supt. of works, Straits Settlements, May, 1899; ag. sup. of works and surveys, Malacca, June, 1899, to Sept., 1901, and Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1903; sup. of works and surveys, Sing., July, 1903; ag. dep. col. engr. and survr.-gen., Penang, Nov., 1904, to Feb., 1905; ag. sup. wks. and survey, Malacca, May to Dec., 1906; ag. dep. col. engr. and survr.-gen., Penang, Jan., 1907.

MAY, FRANCIS H., C.M.G. (1895)—B. 1860; ed. at Harrow and Trin.
